raw_text: BOTT, ARTHUR MOORE.—Clerk to attorney-general, British Guiana, 1871; clerk, colonial registry office, July, 1874; 3rd clerk, immigration department, Oct., 1876; stipendiary magistrate's Mar., 1879; compiler of Summary Conviction Ordinances of the Colony, 1837 to 1883; chief police magistrate and sheriff of Demerara; has several times acted as special and auxiliary J.P., ag. asst. pol. mag., Georgetown, 1890, to Jan., 1891, and May, 1893, to Feb., 1891; ag. stip. mag. Essequibo Islands, April, 1891, to 1892.
BOTT, SIR JOSEPH PALMER, K.C.M.G. —Secretary for mines, New South Wales, 1883, to Oct., 1885, when appointed Secretary for Lands; resigned Dec., 1885; speaker of Legislative Assembly, 1892.

events.position: Clerk of petty sessions and crown lands agent, Gunnedah, Liverpool Plains
events.text_span: Clerk of petty sessions and crown lands agent, Gunnedah, Liverpool Plains, N.S.W., 1867
stipendiary magistrate, Sydney Police Court, Sydney, 1884.

raw_text: BOTT, T. KINGSMILL.—Clerk of petty sessions and crown lands agent, Gunnedah, Liverpool Plains, N.S.W., 1867; police magistrate, 1874; Maitland, 1882; stipendiary magistrate, Sydney Police Court, Sydney, 1884.

ACKROYD, EDWARD JAMES.—Clerk, registry supreme court, Mauritius, Sept., 1853; clerk to first puisne judge, April, 1859; chief clerk in registry supreme court, Jan., 1863; chief clerk master's office, Mar., 1864; in July, 1871, obtained from Council of Legal Education an exhibition in common law, and in Nov., 1871, a certificate of honour of the first class; called to the bar, Middle Temple, June, 1872; district and stipendiary magistrate, Riviere du Rempart, Aug., 1873; for many years member of the Council of Education, Mauritius; registrar of the supreme court of Hong Kong, Dec., 1881; appointed on a commission to amend and revise the laws and ordinances of Hong Kong, Aug., 1882; acting puisne judge, Mar. to Dec., 1883; and acting attorney general, 1886-8, and 1890; acting puisne judge, April, 1891; acting chief justice, June, 1891; puisne judge, 1892; retired, 1895.

ACKROYD, JOHN HENRY.—Third clerk, registry, supreme court, April, 1854; clerk to second puisne judge, April, 1855; admitted solicitor of supreme court, Mauritius, April, 1859; police and stipendiary magistrate for the lesser dependencies of Mauritius, 1876; special commissioner to investigate the treatment and state of the liberated Africans at Seychelles, May, 1881; district judge, Seychelles, 1882; district magistrate, Grand Port. Sept., 1884; acting assistant colonial secretary, 1885; district magistrate, Plaines Wilhelms, 1887., police and additional dist. mag., Aug., 1895.
ADAMS, GEORGE P.—Recorder of titles, Tasmania, July, 1876; registrar, supreme court, 1885.
ADAMS, M.—Surveyor-general, province of New Brunswick, Canada.

events.position: Surveyor-general for the colony of New South Wales
events.text_span: Surveyor-general for the colony of New South Wales, 17 March, 1868.

raw_text: ADAMS, P. F.—Surveyor-general for the colony of New South Wales, 17 March, 1868.
ADAMS, ROBERT PATTEN—Solicitor-general, Tasmania, 20 Dec., 1867; puisne judge 1889, formerly chairman of quarter sessions and commr., court of requests.

ADAMS, W. H.—B.A. Pemb. Coll., Oxon; honours in law schools; dist. comsnsr. Wiunebah, G. Coast, Apr., 1891, ag. sheriff, Sept. 1891; dist. comsnsr., Accra, Jan., 1892; ag. priv. sec. to Governor Sir W. B. Griffith, 1893.

ADDERLEY, SIR AUGUSTUS JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1886), C.M.G. (1884).—Formerly a member of the legislative council, Bahamas; special commissioner for the Bahamas and Jamaica at the International Fisheries Exhibition, 1883; royal commissioner, Colonial and Indian Exhibition, 1885, and executive commissioner for West Indies and British Honduras sections.

ADDISON, GLENTWORTH WALSH FRASER.—Born 1831. Assistant gold commissioner, northern list., New South Wales, 1858; police magistrate, Tenterfield, 1869; police magistrate, Maitland, 1875; stipendiary magistrate, Sydney, 1882.
ADDISON, R. H.—Clk. and interp. to R.M. Umbazi, Natal, 1876, to R.M., Lion's River, 1882., Lieut. Zulu Carbineers, 1883, asst. commr. and R.M. Ndwandwe, dist. Zululand, 1887; ditto, Nqutu dist. 1889.

events.position: superintendent of the correspondence branch, general department, and clerk for legal instruments
events.text_span: superintendent of the correspondence branch, general department, and clerk for legal instruments, 1st Oct., 1880

events.position: appointed officer of arms of the Order of St. Michael and St. George
events.text_span: appointed officer of arms of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, May, 1882.

raw_text: ADRIAN, F. O.—Entered colonial office Sept., 1863; assistant clerk, 2nd class, 28th Dec., 1870; 1st class assistant clerk, 26th Jan., 1877; superintendent of the correspondence branch, general department, and clerk for legal instruments, 1st Oct., 1880; appointed officer of arms of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, May, 1882.

ADYE, GENERAL SIR JOHN MILLER, G.C.B. (1882).—Entered the Royal Artillery, Dec., 1836; captain, 1846; colonel, 1867; assistant adjutant-general, R.A., throughout the Crimean War, also during the Indian Mutiny; served in the Sitana campaign of 1863-4; director of artillery, 1870 to 1875; governor of the Royal Military Academy, Woolwich, June, 1875; surveyor-general of ordnance, May, 1880; and a colonel-commandant, R.A., 1881; was chief of the staff in the expedition to Egypt in 1882 (and received thanks of Parliament); is author of "A Review of the Crimean War," "The Defence of Cawnpore;" governor of Gibraltar, 1 Jan., 1883, to Dec., 1886.

AGNEW, THE HON. SIR JAMES WILSON, M.D., K.C.M.G. (1895), Hobart.—Member of the legislative council, 1877-81, and 1884-7; member (without portfolio) of ministries, 1877-8-9-81; premier and chief secretary, Mar., 1886, to Mar., 1887, when he resigned. Resigned seat in legislative council, July, 1887. Is a magistrate for the territory, a member of the council of education, a vice-president of the Royal Society, &c.

AIKINS, THE HON. JAMES COX.—Ed. at Victoria University, Canada; returned to Canadian Assembly in 1854, where he sat until 1861; elected to the legislative council, 1862, and at the union of the B. N. A. Provinces in 1867, was called to the Senate by Royal Proclamation; sworn of the Privy Council, 9th Dec., 1869, and was secretary of state and registrar-general of Canada from that date until the resignation of Sir John A. Macdonald in Nov., 1873; re-appointed to same office in Sir John's new administration, 19th Oct., 1878; became minister of inland revenue; lieut.-governor of Manitoba and Keewatin, Dec., 1882-88.

events.text_span: 3rd class clerk col. sec. office, Jamaica, Feb., 1878

raw_text: AITKEN, C. COLQUHOUN.—3rd class clerk col. sec. office, Jamaica, Feb., 1878; 2nd class clerk, general register office, 1878; 1st class clerk, 1880.

events.text_span: delegate to the South Africa conference on confederation in 1876

raw_text: AKERMAN, SIR JOHN WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1887).—Many years member of the legislative council, Natal: served on the immigration board, council of education, &c.; nominated to the executive council, 1870; delegate to the South Africa conference on confederation in 1876; elected speaker of the legislative council in 1880; member of the native commission of 1882; re-elected speaker 1882; and again to the enlarged council of 1886; and again after dissolution of 1890-1; delegate to the Capetown customs conference, 1888; is a J.P.

raw_text: AKITOYE, DANIEL.—Born at Lagos, 1858; C.M.S. Coll. Institute, Lagos; copyist to Q. Advocate, Lagos, Aug., 1886; registrar of deeds and clerk to registrar-general, Feb., 1887; registrar of instruments affecting land and chief of general registry, July, 1892; dep. registrar of births, deaths and marriages, and aliens, Jan., 1893; registrar Mar. to May, 1895.

raw_text: ALBURY, JOSEPH BENSON, M.D.—Mem. House of Assembly, Bahamas, 1869; J.P. 1870; public vaccinator, 1881; acting surgeon superintendent of the New Providence Asylum, 1882, and public physician, 1883; comsnsr. Col. and Indian Exhibition, 1885; memb. Leg. Coun., 1889; surg. supt., New Providence Asylum and Ward Hospital, 1896.

events.position: acted as inspector of prisons from
events.text_span: acted as inspector of prisons from April to Oct., 1871

events.position: one of the commissions to inquire into the management of prisons

raw_text: ALEXANDER, ARTHUR HARVEY.—E.L. Abdn.; sub-inspector, Jamaica constabulary, Oct., 1867; 3rd class inspector, April, 1868; 2nd class inspector, Nov., 1869; 1st class inspector, Oct., 1870; acted as inspector of prisons from April to Oct., 1871; one of the commissions to inquire into the management of prisons, 1873; acting agent-general of immigration, April, 1875; confirmed Oct., 1873; official member of legislative council, 1882; immigration agent general, Brit. Guiana, 1884.

ALEXANDER, GEORGE HAMILTON.—Lieut., "4th Batt. East Surrey Regt." 1892; Certificate School of Musketry, 1893; Winner of "Militia Cup," 1893; Lieut. April, 1894; Aptd. to "Provisional Batt." Shorncliffe, 1894; Sub-Inspr. "Br. Guiana Police" Nov., 1894.
ALEXANDER, J. F.—Mechanical engr.; Maine State Coll., U.S.A.; Manager Technical Sch., Montserrat, June, 1892; ditto St. Kitts, July 1894.

events.position: and course of instruction for convict service at Wormwood Scrubs
events.text_span: and course of instruction for convict service at Wormwood Scrubs, 1884.

raw_text: ALEXANDER, THOMAS.—Sub-inspector Jamaica constabulary, Jan., 1872; 3rd class inspector, Oct., 1873; 2nd class inspector, Nov., 1876; 1st class, musketry certificate, 1879; and course of instruction for convict service at Wormwood Scrubs, 1884.

events.position: A.D.C. to Sir F. N. Broome, Governor of West Australia

raw_text: ALISON, ARCHIBALD.—Educated at Eton and Sandhurst; formerly lieut. 1st batt., now capt. 5th batt., Rifle Brigade; A.D.C. to Sir F. N. Broome, Governor of West Australia, 1885-8; colonial secretary of Bermuda and registrar general, May, 1888.

ALLAN, ALEX.—Clerk to Colonial Secretary St. Vincent, July 1878; supervisor of customs, Dixcove, Gold Coast, Nov., 1888; transferred to Saltpond, Jan., 1881; acted as district commissioner various districts from Jan., 1881, to May, 1886, when he was appointed assistant treasurer; treasurer and controller of customs, May, 1890, Feb., 1891; suptdt. money order branch and savings bk. dept., Singapore, June, 1892.

events.position: called to the bar of Upper Canada
events.text_span: called to the bar of Upper Canada, 1846

events.position: elected to the Legislative Council of Canada
events.text_span: elected to the Legislative Council of Canada, 1858

events.position: summoned to the Senate of the Dominion of Canada
events.text_span: summoned to the Senate of the Dominion of Canada, 1867

raw_text: ALLAN, THE HON. GEORGE WILLIAM, D.C.L.—Born 1822; educated at the Upper Canada College; called to the bar of Upper Canada, 1846; mayor of Toronto, 1855; elected to the Legislative Council of Canada, 1858; summoned to the Senate of the Dominion of Canada, 1867; lieut.-colonel of the Canadian Militia; Chancellor of the University of Trinity College, Toronto; speaker, Senate, 1889.
ALLAN, GORDON.—Assistant surveyor-general, British Honduras, 1885; surveyor-general, 1886.

ALLARDYCE, W. L.—Clk. and interp.; Provincial Department Fiji, 1879, transferred to Samoa, 1882; stip. mag. July, 1882; asst. native commr.; and stip. mag. and inspr. of native courts, Kadavu, Jan. 1890; native lbs. commsr. memb. leg. coun., 1894; native commsr., 1895; ag. asst. col. sec., April, July, and Aug., 1896.

ALLDRIDGE, THOMAS J., F.R.G.S., F.Z.S.—Ag. U.S. consular agent at S. Leone, 1882; travelling commssr. for colony; hon. mr. of police, and J.P., 1889; on special mission to interior, 1890, and concluded treaties with various chiefs placing their countries under protection; hon. corrpdg. sec. roy. col. inst., dist commissioner and coroner, Br. Sherbro, September, 1893.

ALLEN, SIR JOHN CAMPBELL (KT. BACH., D.D., LL.D.)—Barrister of New Brunswick; was a commissioner, 1845-7, for settling claims to lands in the Madwaska Settlement under the Treaty of Washington of 1842; a member of the House of Assembly of New Brunswick, 1856-65; solicitor general, 1856-7; speaker of the House of Assembly, 1863-5; attorney general, 1865; delegate from the Government of New Brunswick to the British Government on the subject of confederation in that year; a judge of supreme court of New Brunswick, Sept., 1865; chief justice, Oct., 1875; president of the court trial of divorce and matrimonial causes, 1866-1863-65, official reporter of supreme court of province.

ALLWOOD, JAMES.—Second clerk, governor's secretary's office, Jamaica, July, 1862; acting clerk in the executive committee office, Feb., 1865, to May, 1866; clerk immigration department, May to Oct., 1866; first class clerk, finance office, 1866, to Dec., 1869; supervisor of district offices, Oct., 1871, to Mar., 1876; now assistant colonial secretary.

raw_text: ALLWOOD, JAMES.—Solicitor of supreme court, Jamaica, and deputy clerk of the peace, parish of St. Mary, June, 1866; clerk of the peace, parish of St. Elizabeth, Oct., 1868; clerk, Savannah district court, Nov., 1868; ditto, Montego Bay, Jan., 1869; also clerk of the circuit court, July, 1875; clerk, southern district court, Jan., 1880; first class clerk, colonial secretary's office, Nov., 1883; R.M., 1888.

events.position: Third clerk in the colonial secretary's department
events.text_span: Third clerk in the colonial secretary's department, Hong Kong, Jan., 1865

raw_text: ALVES, J. M. S.—Third clerk in the colonial secretary's department, Hong Kong, Jan., 1865; first clerk, 1870; 1st clerk, Feb., 1881.

AMIRAYAN, G. G.—Called to the Bar, Inner Temple, 1894; clerk and translator, commissioner's court, Larnaca, Cyprus, Oct., 1878; translator, high court of justice and court of Temyiz, 1881; registrar, supreme court, 1883; was secretary to commission appointed in 1888 to enquire into organization of Cyprus Police.

events.text_span: Indentured clerk, Pub. Hosp., Bahamas, May, 1885

raw_text: AMPTHILL, 2nd BARON, creat. 1881, OLIVER ARTHUR VILLIERS RUSSELL, son of the 1st Baron. Born 1869, succeeded his father, 1884. Ed. at Eton and New, Coll. Oxon. Rowed in the Oxford eight, 1889-91; presdt. of the Oxford Union, 1891; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, Sec. of State for the Colonies, June, 1895. Is lieut. Royal 1st Devon Yeomanry Cavalry. ANDERSON CHARLES OSBORNE.—Indentured clerk, Pub. Hosp., Bahamas, May, 1885; second clerk, G.P.O., April, 1888; chief clerk, July, 1890; ag. postmr., 1892; elected member leg. ass. 1895.

head master, Royal College School, Port Louis, 1881

raw_text: ANDERSON, DAVID JULIUS.—Ed. Roy. Coll. Mauritius, and Edin. Univ.; assistant master, Royal College, 9th July, 1867; master, 1872; assistant professor, 1873; acting head master, Royal College School, Curepipe, 1878; head master, Royal College School, Port Louis, 1881; superintendent of schools, Mauritius, Jan., 1888.

events.position: Asst. res. surg. col. hosp. Demerara
events.text_span: Asst. res. surg. col. hosp. Demerara, July, 1867

raw_text: ANDERSON, FRANCIS HENRY, M.D., M.C., Edin.—Asst. res. surg. col. hosp. Demerara, July, 1867; res. surg. penal settlement, July, 1870; ditto, col. hosp. July, 1873; med. offr. Buxton dist. Sep. 1875; ditto, Pleasance dist. Nov. 1878; ditto, Georgetown dist. Sep. 1887; acted as med. offr. immigr. dept. July 1877 to Feb. 1878, and July, 1882 to Dec. 1883; and as surg.-general. Dec. 1888 to May 1889.

events.position: served in India as asst. engr., military works dept

raw_text: ANDERSON, Capt. F. J.—Cadet, R.M. Acad., 1876; served in India as asst. engr., military works dept., 1881-8; Burma expdn., 1885 (medal and clasp); dep. col. engr. and surveyor-gen., Penang, April, 1892; acted also as pres. municipal commrs., Penang, 1893-4; ag. col. engr. and surveyor-gen., S.S., Mar., 1896.
ANDERSON, FRANK.—Assist. surveyor, Gold Coast, April, 1885; ag. surveyor-gen., June, 1886; asst. surveyor, Lagos, Mar., 1887, surveyor-gen., Aug., 1888.

ANDERSON, JOHN.—M.A., Aberdeen, 1877; 1st class honours in mathematics; gold medal as most distinguished graduate of year; second class clerk, Colonial Office, 30th June, 1879; Bacon scholar, Gray's Inn, 1887; Inns of Court studentship, 1888; joint commssr. with the late Sir J. F. Dickson, Nov. and Dec., 1891, to inquire into certain matters connected with the registry of the supreme court of Gibraltar; private secy. to Sir R. Meade, 17th Aug., 1892. Attached to the staff of the British agent for the Behring Sea Arbitration in London and Paris, 1892-93; 1st class clerk, 11th Mar., 1896.

events.text_span: Under secretary for public instruction, Queensland, 1 Nov., 1878.

raw_text: ANDERSON, JOHN GERARD, M.A.—Under secretary for public instruction, Queensland, 1 Nov., 1878.

ANDERSON, SIR WILLIAM JOHN, KT BACH. (1896).—Educated at Pembroke Coll., Oxford; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Nov., 1869; judge of the Supreme Court, Turks Islands, Feb., 1874; acting district judge, Jamaica, June, 1882, to May, 1883; and again, April, 1884, confirmed 1886; R.M. Kingston and St. Andrews, 1888; ch justice Br. Honduras, 1890.

events.text_span: C.C. and R.M., Middelburg Division, Cape of Good Hope, Sept., 1870
C.C. and R.M., Beaufort West, Oct., 1884.

raw_text: ANDREWS, C. W.—C.C. and R.M., Middelburg Division, Cape of Good Hope, Sept., 1870; C.C. and R.M. Albert Division, 1875; C.C. and R.M., Beaufort West, Oct., 1884.

events.position: clerk, Eastern Telegraph Co. at Alexandria
events.text_span: clerk, Eastern Telegraph Co. at Alexandria, Aug., 1882

events.position: 1st class clerk, African Direct Telegraph Co., Bathurst
events.text_span: 1st class clerk, African Direct Telegraph Co., Bathurst, June, 1886

events.position: director of telegraphs, G. Coast Colony

raw_text: ANDREWS, MARTINDALE STEWART.—Educated at Merchant Taylors; clerk, Eastern Telegraph Co. at Alexandria, Aug., 1882; 1st class clerk, African Direct Telegraph Co., Bathurst, June, 1886; supt. Accra, Feb., 1887; director of telegraphs, G. Coast Colony, June, 1891.

ANDREWS, WALTER BOYD TATE, J.P.—Deputy registrar-general, 1856, South Australia; registrar-general 1865. ANGELO, LIEUTENANT-COLONEL E. FOX.—Ensign 14th December, 1854; lieutenant, 13th April, 1855; captain, 9th February, 1864; major, 1st October, 1877; lieutenant-colonel, 2nd March, 1878; served with the 28th regiment in the Crimea, from August, 1855, including the siege and fall of Sebastopol (medal with clasp and Turkish medal); adjutant, Decree irregular force, 1860; aide-de-camp to major-general Frank Adams, C.B., 1867; station staff officer, Roorkee, 1871; deputy-assistant quartermaster-general, Saugor district, 1871; brigade-major, Agra, 1874; deputy-assistant adjutant-general, Morar, 1875; assistant adjutant-general, Oude division, 1876; commandant local forces, Tasmania, 1880; inspecting field officer, Western Australia, 1882; government resident, northern division, 1886; R.M., Bunbury, 1889; R.M., Rottnest, 1890. ANGERS, The HON. AUGUSTE RÉAL.—Solr. gen., Quebec, 1874-6; atty. gen., 1876-8; leader of upper house for three years; then puisne judge, superior court, Quebec; lieut.-governor, Quebec, 1887; senator and minister of agriculture, Canada, Dec., 1892 to 1895. ANGOVE, THOMAS.—Probation clerk, audit office, Western Australia, July, 1880; second clerk, Treasury, 1886. ANGUS, JAMES.—Asst. Storekeeper-General, Mauritius, 21st Aug., 1868, has acted on several occasions as Storekeeper General. ANSON, MAJOR GENERAL SIR ARCHIBALD EDWARD HARBORD, R.A., C.M.G. (1876), K.C.M.G. (1882).—Entered the royal military academy, Woolwich, 1841; presented with sword for exemplary conduct; 2nd lieut., R.A., June, 1844; 1st lieutenant 1st April, 1846; 2nd captain, 9th July, 1852; 1st captain, 1st Sept., 1855; brevet major, 21st July, 1864; lieut.-colonel, 12th May, 1866; brevet-colonel, 12th May, 1874; colonel, 23rd Dec., 1875; retired with the honorary rank of major-general 26th Aug., 1879; served at the siege of Sebastopol in 1855; Crimean medal and clasp, 5th class Medjidie, and Turkish medal; inspector-general of police, Mauritius, Sept., 1858; was employed in 1862 as the responsible member of the British Mission to the court of Radama II of Madagascar, on the occasion of his coronation; lieut.-governor of Prince of Wales Island, Feb., 1867; admin. gov. Straits Settlements 1871, 1877 and 1879; conducted the successful military operations for repelling the invasion of the state of Sungei Ujong, 1875-76 (medal and clasp); retired, 1882; J.P. for Sussex. ANSON, FRANK CHARLES MONTRESOR.—Asst. commissary of taxn, B. Guiana, May, 1881; dist. commissary and insp. of weights and measures, June, 1884; secy. to road arrears comsn., 1889; memb. of comsn. to enquire into and assess damages caused by riots, 1890; J.P., 1892; chairman bd. of examiners under "weighers and gaugers ordinance, 1883;" member central bd. of health and excise bd., and act. ch. commissary, 1892-93, 1893-94, 1894-95. ANTELME, Sir CELICOURT, C.M.G. (1880), K.C.M.G. (1890).—Senior unofficial member of the council of government, Mauritius. ANTHONISZ, JAMES OLIVER.—Cadet, Straits Settlements, Oct., 1883; B.A., St. John's College, Cambridge (Senior Optime); assistant Indian immigration agent, Singapore, May, 1886; 3rd magistrate, Singapore, 1888; 2nd ditto, July, 1892; ag. official assignee and regtr. of deeds, Singapore, Oct., 1894; ag. 1st mag., July, 1895. ANTON, C. FALCONER.—Audit clerk, Grevena, Feb. to May, 1877; harbour master Mar. to Dec., 1880; confidential clerk and clerk of court, Jan. 1881; stipendiary magistrate, St. Lucia, 1885; auditor, Mar., 1886; treas. comptroller customs and postm., Grenada, 1890. ANTON, GEORGE.—Clerk, audit office, Grevena, Nov. 1872; 2nd clk. col. secretary's office, May, 1878; ag. chief clerk, April to Sept., 1879; again in 1880; treas. accountant St. Vincent, 1881; ag. auditor, Aug., 1888, to end of 1890; registrar supreme court, Dec. 1891; J.P. July, 1892. ANTROBUS, REGINALD L.—Educated at Chester and at New College, Oxford; fellow of New College, 1872; first class in final moderations, 1874; second class in final school, 1876; B.A., 1876; appointed, after competitive examination, to be a clerk in colonial office, May 3rd, 1877; assistant secretary to the Earl of Kimberley, 5th Dec. to Dec., 1882; private secretary to the Earl of Derby, 16th Dec., 1882, to 24th June, 1885; Colonel the Right Hon. F. A. Stanley, M.P. (Earl of Derby) 24th June, 1885, to 6th Aug., 1886; and to Earl Granville 6th Feb., 1886; administered the govt. of St. Kitts, 5th Nov., 1889, to 8th June, 1890; private secretary to Mr. Mende, 1st Feb., 1892, and to Mr. Buxton, M.P., 17th Aug., 1892; senior clerk, 1893; principal clerk, 1896. APPLEYARD, CHARLES W.—Appointed competitive examination to be a clerk in colonial service, civil service, and assigned to colony, March 14, 1893; matric. Lond., 1895. ARCHER, F. BISSET.—Clerk in Lon. and Westminster Bk., 1884; prin. clk. col. sec. off., 1894; ag. chief clk., Ap., 1894, to Mar., 1895; Oct., 1895 to Jan., 1896, when confirmed as col. sec., Aug. to Oct., 1896; clk. of councils to Oct., 1896; lieut. G. C. rifle volrs.; ag. to Oct., 1896. ARCHER, T., C.M.G. (1884).—Agent for Queensland, 1882 to 1884; reappointed June, 1888; resigned 1890. ARCHIBALD, SIR ADAMS G., K.C.M.G. (1885) (C.M.G.), 1872.—Lieutenant-Governor of Manitoba and North-West Territories, 1871; of Province of Nova Scotia, 1873-83. ARDAGH, W. D.—Deputy attorney-General, Manitoba. ARMBRISTER, PERCY W. D.—Acting provost, prothonotary and clerk of Crown, 1883; 1884; temporary clerk, Treasury, 1885; clerk police court, 1887. ARMOUR, J. D.—President and chief superior court, Ontario. ARMSTRONG, WILLIAM THOMAS.—Late revenue officer, District D, Barbados, Jan., 1870; J.P.; sheriff of St. Joseph's parish; late James troop of yeomanry cavalry, 1859; late police magistrate of District D, April, 1874; 1875; inspector of inland revenue officer, 1879; police magistrate District F, 1884. ARUNACHALAM, P.—Ed. Academy, Colombo, Ceylon, and Christ's Coll., Colombo, which he was a scholar, B.A. 1875; M.A. 1877; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1877; entered Ceylon Civil Service, April, 1875; commr. of requests, police magistrate, 1876; district judge, June, 1887; commr. of requests, 1892.
HBURNHAM, JOHN ANCHITEL.—Registrar r. Justice Shippard, Cape Town, Mar., 1885, to administrator, Br. Bechuanaland. Oct., 1885, tary, 1888.

BLEY, HON. ANTHONY EVELYN MBLEOURNE, n of the 7th Earl of Shaftesbury; born 1836; sted at Harrow and Trinity College, Cam- bre: M.A., 1858. Called to the bar at Lincoln's 1863. Was for some time secretary to the late . Palmerston; is a magistrate for Dorset, and treasurer of county courts of Dorset from to 1874; is 2nd church estates commissioner; amentary secretary to the Board of Trade, 1, 1880; parliamentary under secretary of for the colonies, 12th May, 1882, to 24th , 1885. Sat in the House of Commons for e from May, 1873, to March, 1880, and for the of Wight from April, 1880, to November, . Is author of a life of Lord Palmerston. 3HLEY, E. C.—Master. government schools, ritius, March, 1863; clerk, colonial secretary's 2, July, 1864; chief clerk, railway department, , 1865; corresponding clerk, colonial secretary's e, April, 1866; acting registrar 20th Jan. to Oct., 1873, and 10th Oct. to 21st Nov., 1874; f clerk audit office, July, 1875; officer in charge udit office, Feb 1876, to May, 1877, Mar to ., 1881. and Sept., 1886; ag. auditor-gen., . 1887; chairman public offices inquiry com- ision, June, 1888; acting registrar-gen., April, , to Aug., 1884; collr. of customs, Apr. ; ag. audr.-gen., Jan., 1894; confirmed, Nov.,

ATKINSON, NICHOLAS.—Called to the bar, ser Temple, 11th June, 1867; admitted to practice British Guiana, Feb., 1868; acting solicitor- general, 1869, 1870, and Mar., 1873, to Oct., 1874, when he was confirmed; puisne judge, Aug., 96; acting chief justice 1888 and 1889.

AUBERT, EDGAR, C.M.G. (1892).—Poor law commissioner, Mauritius, 1892; memb. coun. of dn. 1892, of Bd. of Health, 1894; nominated member coun. of Govt. 1896.

AUCHINLECK, W. D.—3rd clerk, treasury, Antigua, 1867; 1st clerk, 1873; landing surveyor, 1874; treasurer and collector of customs, Nevis, 1881; treasurer St. Kitt's and Nevis, and member of Executive and Legislative Councils, 1883; M.L.C., Leeward Islands. AUCKLAND (New Zealand), FIRST BISHOP OF (founded 1869), Right Rev. WILLIAM G. COWIE, D.D.—Was chaplain with Lord Clyde's army at the capture of Lucknow; chaplain to Viceroy of India, 1863; appointed rector of Stafford, 1867.

AUSTIN, JOHN GARDINER, C.M.G. (1876).—Was acting stipendiary magistrate in British Guiana from Nov., 1849, to May, 1850; assistant government secretary from May, 1850, to June, 1851, acting government secretary, June, 1851, to Dec., 1851; assistant government secretary, Dec., 1851, to Feb., 1853; and immigration agent-general, from Feb., 1853; lieut.-governor of Honduras, 1864; colonial secretary of Hong Kong, 1868; administered the government during October, 1874, and from March 11th, 1875. Retired on pension, 1878.
AUSTIN, J. H. — Deputy commissioner of crown lands, Nova Scotia.

Guiana, 1888, and of Demerara River dist. 1892; ag. asst. mr. Queen's Coll., Georgetown. 1889; J.P. for the Colony, 1893.

AYERS, The HON. SIR HENRY, G.C.M.G. (1894), K.C.M.G. (1872).—Was member of the Cabinet of South Australia without office, in Mr. Dutton's ministry, July, 1863; chief secretary, July, 1863, to October 1865, and from May, 1867, to Sept., 1868, and again from Oct. 13, 1868, to Nov. 2, 1868; also from Jan., 1872, to July, 1873, and again in 1876 and 1887; president of the legislative council from 1881 to 1893.

raw_text: BABY, LOUIS FRANCOIS GEORGES.—Born 1834; called to the bar 1857; created a Q.C. 1873; entered Canadian House of Commons, 1872; sworn of the privy council and appointed minister of inland revenue, 26th Oct., 1878; puisne judge of the Court of Queen's Bench, Quebec.
BACKHOUSE, ALFRED P. — Crown prosecutor, N.W. dist., N.S. Wales, Oct. 1878; ditto S. dist., Jan., 1881, ct. Dist. judge, N.W. dist., 1884.

raw_text: BADELEY, FRANCIS JOSEPH.—Ed. Clergy Orphan Asylum School, Canterbury, and Jesus Coll. Camb., Senior opt. math. Tripos 1889; Hong Kong cadet, Oct. 1890; passed cadet, Jan. 1893; ag. dep. supt. of police, June to Dec. 1893; ag. asst. postmr. gen., May to July, 1894; ag. asst. regr. gen., July, 1894; J.P., 1894; dep. suptdt. of police and asst. suptdt. fire brigade, July, 1895.

BADEN-POWELL, SIR GEORGE SMYTH. K.C.M.G. (1888), C.M.G. (1884). M.P.—Educated at Marlborough and Balliol College, Oxford, where he graduated in honours, obtaining the Chancellor's prize English essay in 1876. He is the author of "New Homes for the Old Country," "Protection and Bad Times," and "State Aid and State Interference;" and numerous articles dealing with political subjects. Member of the West Indies Finance Commission, 1882. M.P. for the Kirkdale Division of Liverpool, 1885, and again 1886. Member of commission for delimitation of electoral districts in Malta. Jan., 1888; commissioner in connection with the Bering Sea question, 1891 and 1892.

BAILEY, D. J. S., B.A. (Camb.)—Served in Methuen's Horse; cadet Sarawak Service, Mar., 1888; asst. resdt., May, 1891; resdt., 2nd class, 2nd divn., Jan., 1894. BAILEY EDWD. S.—2nd clk. col. treas. Barbados, 1880; ch. clk. 1892; acted as clk. to asst. ct. of appeal in 1892 and other occasions; ag. sec. emigr. commn., Oct., 1893; ag. col. treas. 1894.

BAILEY, J. B. A.—Appointed a writer to the Ceylon government, 1863; commissioner of requests and police magistrate of Kalpitya, 1865; assistant government agent, Trincomalee, January, 1866; district judge, Batticaloa, acting 2nd assistant colonial secretary, March, 1867. District judge, Batticaloa, Dec. 1867; district judge, &c., Trincomalee, and acting assistant government agent, Puttalam, Jan., 1870; second assistant colonial secretary, May, 1870; fiscal for the central province, April, 1872; asst. govt. agent, Mannár, Sept., 1872; registrar-general, 1875; grain commissioner, 1880; govt. agt. North Central Prov., 1882; ditto Eastn. Prov., 1883; ditto N.W. Prov., 1889; ditto Central Prov., 1895.

BAILEY, WILLIAM H.—Entered the service June, 1875, as third clerk, Customs Department, Barbados; promoted second clerk, Nov., 1876; chief clerk, Colonial Secretary's Office, and clerk of Legislative Council, May, 1882; acted for five months in 1892 as governor, Glendairy Prison; acting auditor-general, June, 1893, to April, 1894. Aug. to Dec., 1895, and July to Nov., 1896.

Canton in connection with Legation, 1883; judge's clk. sup. ct., Mar., 1884; author of several grammatical and school books of Chinese.

BARBADOS, 5th BISHOP OF. (Founded 1679)—RIGHT REV. HERBERT BREE, D.D., ex-officio. 1 Conv., and Caius College, Cambridge, B.A., 1850; M.A., 1853; was curate of Drinkstone, Wool- stone and Long Melford, all in Suffolk; rector of Harkstead, Suffolk, 1858-65, and of Brampton, Mts. 1870, till his consecration in 1882.

raw_text: BARBER, CHARLES—Registrar court of com- mon magistrates, Orange River Sovereignty, 1860; act to civil commissioner and resident magistrate, Acock, and distributor of stamps, Nov., 1858; to Bathurst, Jan., 1860; acting civil com- missioner and resident magistrate, sub-collector of customs and visiting magistrate, Port Alfred, on several occasions until 1874; appointed resi- dent magistrate, sub-collector of customs, harbour and shipping master, Hondeklip Bay, Dec., 1875; acting civil commissioner and resident magistrate, Maqualand, 1877; resident magistrate, sub- inspector, harbour and shipping master, Port Elizabeth, January, 1878; and civil commissioner and resident magistrate, Alexandria, Nov. 1882.

BARFF, SAMUEL—Auditor of railway accounts,ylon, 3rd Nov., 1868; transferred to Hong Kong assistant postmaster-general, 1868; valuer of the rates for 1876 and 1877; a justice of the peace, 1878; acting deputy registrar of the Supreme Court, 1879; acting accountant of the Supreme Court, 1880; acting accountant and acting deputy registrar of the Supreme Court, 1881; acting post- master-general, 1882; now accountant and deputy registrar of the Supreme Court.

BARKER, MAJ.-GEN. G. DIGHT, C.B. (1888).— Gen. 78th Highlanders, 1853; Adj., 1858; Col. of the army, 1887; maj.-gen., 1887; served in Indian war, 1857 (medal with clasp); in Indian Army, 1857-8, including battle of Cawnpore; def. defence, and capture of Lucknow (as A.Q.M.G.) (Mentioned in despatches, medal with 2 clasps, brevet maj., and a year's service). Educated Staff College, 1866; special service at army headquarters, 1867-8; D.A.Q.M.G., southern district, 1867-73; prof. of mil. arts and history at Staff College, 1874-6; assist. director of mili- tary education at army headquarters, 1876-83; commander of troops in China and Hong Kong, 1885; acting gov. of Hong Kong, May to Dec., 1886; governor, Bermuda, 1896.

BARKER, JOHN.—Barrister, Lincoln's Inn, 1843; disputed boundary commissioner, under New South Wales Land Act, 1849; clerk first Legislative Council, Victoria, Oct., 1851; clerk of the Legislative Assembly, Victoria, 18th Nov., 1856, clerk of the Legislative Council and clerk of Parliaments, 1st April, 1882; retired.

events.position: appointed to the rly. dept., N.S. Wales

raw_text: BARLING, JOSEPH.—Ed. at Poole, Dorsetshire, England; appointed to the rly. dept., N.S. Wales, July, 1860; held the offices successively of chief clerk and acctnt. harbors and rivers dept., act. acctnt. rly. dept., and chief clerk pub. works dept.; under secretary for pub. wks., 1888.

BARKLY SIR HENRY, G.C.M.G. (1874), K.C.B. (created 1853).—Was M.P. for Leominster from 1845, to Feb., 1849; governor and com- mander-in-chief of British Guiana, Dec., 1848; lt.-general and governor-in-chief of Jamaica, 1853; governor of Victoria, 1856; received order of the Bath after services in British Guiana governor of Mauritius, August, 1863; governor, Cape of Good Hope, 1870, to 31st March, 1877; retired on pension. Was a member of the Royal Commission on the defence of British pos- sessions and commerce abroad, 8th September, 1879.

BARLOW, G. PRATT.—Cadet, Sarawak Civil Service, May, 1878; resdt. (2nd class), 3rd div., Jan., 1887; resdt., Muka, Oct., 1890; ag. resdt. Baram, July, 1892; of Biutulu, Aug., 1893; resdt. Lower Rejang, Oct., 1894.

BARNARD, MAJOR JOHN HENRY, C.M.G. (1874).—Of the Royal Munster Fusiliers; ap- pointed to the Order of St. Michael and St. George for service in connection with the Ashantee war, 1873-74, under Sir J. H. Glover.

Honduras, June, 1895; is a J.P. for the Colony.

BARNES, JOHN FREDERICK EVELYN, M.I.C.E.; M.I.C.E. Ire.—Asst. county surveyor, Antrim, 1872; on Abercorn Estates, 1873-9; govt. sur- veyor Natal, 1879; Durban boro. engineer 1882 asst. col. engineer, Natal, 1882; engineer, P.W. dept. 1894.

raw_text: BARROW, ROBERT.—Landing-waiter and revenue officer, St. Lucia, Feb., 1882; keeper of Kingstown prison, St. Vincent, Mar., 1883; was for 23 years in 2nd W. I. Regt; served in Baddiboo expedition of 1861; throughout the Ashanti campaign of 1873-4; was awarded the medal for "Distinguished Conduct in the Field," and the medal with annuity for "Long and Meritorious Service."

Cheltenham Coll.; recorder of the high court, Griqua Land West, 1875; appointed temporarily to administer the govern- ment, 1875; member of both councils, 1875; judge in the eastern districts, Cape, 1878; puisne judge, supreme court, 1880.
BARTON, THE HON. E., Q.C.—Member legis- lative assembly, N.S. Wales; attorney general in Dibbs' ministry, 1891-3.

raw_text: BARTROP, GEORGE FREDERICK.—Barrister-at-Law; emigrated to Victoria, 1852; clerk, audit office, Melbourne, 1854; clerk of petty sessions, 1855; clerk of the peace, &c., Sandhurst, 1860; ditto and deputy sheriff, Ballarat, 1868; warden of the gold fields, 1878; clerk of the peace and registrar county court, Melbourne, 1878; author of "The County Court Manual, 1869;" police magistrate, warden, and coroner of Victoria, 1887.

events.position: employed in Victoria water supply department
events.text_span: employed in Victoria water supply department, 1866

raw_text: BASTOW, HENRY R.—Educated as an architect in West of England. Practised several years in Tasmania; employed in Victoria water supply department, 1866; railway department, 1871; architect to education department, 1873; chief architect, public works department, 1884.

BATH, JAMES.—Secretary to board of education, South Australia, 1st Aug., 1867; secretary to minister of justice and education, 1st Aug., 1883. BATHURST (Australia). RIGHT REV. CHARLES EDWARD CAMIDGE, 2nd Bishop of, D.D.—Consecrated Oct., 1887; formerly canon and prebendary of York; rural dean and vicar of Thirsk; proctor in convocation for the archdeaconry of Cleveland; and surrogate for the diocese of York.

raw_text: BATHURST, W. DUNDAS.—Ed. Lancing, Coll.; agent of Congo Free State, 1883-6; appointed to colonial audit branch exchequer and audit department, Jan., 1889; local auditor, Gibraltar under C. and A. G., Jan., 1891.

events.text_span: chief clerk, collector general's office, Jamaica, Dec., 1870

raw_text: BATTEN, R.—In the excise, England, July, 1862; chief clerk, collector general's office, Jamaica, Dec., 1870; supervisor of revenue offices, Nov., 1878; has acted as collector-general several times.

raw_text: BATY, SEBERT CONRAD ELTON, M.A.—Ed. St. Paul's School, and St. Peter's Coll., Camb., B.A. in honours, classical tripos, 1889, M.A., 1892; sworn land surveyor Mauritius and dependencies, 1890; govt. surveyor and suptdt. of public works, Seychelles, Sept., 1892.

BAUMGARTNER, G. A.—Writer Ceylon Civil Service, July, 1871. Attached to Colombo Kachcheri, Oct., 1871; additional police magistrate, Kalutara, July, 1873. Attached to colonial secretary's office, Oct., 1873; police magistrate, Point Pedro, 14th Feb., 1874; acting assistant to government agent, Kandy, April, 1876; assistant to government agent, Jaffna, Sept., 1876; ditto, Kandy, Nov., 1876; acting district judge, Badulla, Feb., 1878; acting assistant to government agent, Jaffna, Feb., 1880; district judge, Tangelle, Feb., 1883; ditto, Badulla, 1885; ditto, Negombo, Dec., 1894; regr. gen., May, 1895.

raw_text: BAUMGARTNER, HARRY PERCY.—Writer in Ceylon service, Oct. 29, 1875; extra office assistant at Colombo to government agent, western province, Jan., 1877; attached to police court, Colombo, Nov., 1877; commissioner of requests and police magistrate, Puttalam and Kalpitiya, Feb., 1878; extra office assistant, Colombo Kachcheri, Feb., 1878; acting assistant government agent, Kalutara, March, 1878; acting office assistant at Colombo, July, 1878; acting landing surveyor and tide surveyor, customs, Galle, Nov., 1878; acting extra assistant at Kalatura to the government agent western province Jan., 1879; acting office assistant at Anuradhapura May, 1879; acting office assistant at Colombo, Oct., 1879; office assistant at Kandy to the government agent central province, Feb., 1883; landing surveyor of customs, Colombo, April, 1884; assistant government agent, Matara, 1891; dep. collr. of customs, May, 1894.

raw_text: BAXENDALE, ARTHUR SALISBURY, M.I.E.E.—Ed. St. Paul's, Stony Stratford, and Leamington Coll., and at Schl. of Elec. Engineering; for six years in service of Eastern and Eastern Exempt Tel. Cos.; supt. of posts and telegraphs, Selangor, Oct., 1888.

raw_text: BAXENDALE, F. R. S.—Ed. at Charterhouse; cadet Fiji civil service, 1884; passed cadet, 1885; European offr. armed native constabulary, 1886; sub. clk. and interpr. provincial dept., 1887; staff mag., 1889; resdt. commissr., Oakandrew Province, 1892; ag. supt. of police and dep. command armed native constabulary, Apl., 1896.

events.position: sub-distributor of stamps, taxing master, and J.P., Vryburg, Trans Bechuanaland
events.text_span: sub-distributor of stamps, taxing master, and J.P., Vryburg, Trans Bechuanaland, Oct., 1885

raw_text: BAXTER, ERNEST CHARLES.—Arrived as clerk; clerk, R.M. court; sub-distributor of stamps, taxing master, and J.P., Vryburg, Trans Bechuanaland, Oct., 1885; examiner of accounts Feb., 1889; collector of customs, 1891.
BAXTER, JACOB.—Member for Haldimand, Ontario Leg. Assembly; speaker thereof.

BAYLY, MAJOR GEORGE C.—(5th batt. 53rd Brigade).—Passed school of instruction, 1879; 1st class extra certificate, Hythe, 1880; instructor of musketry, 1886; asst. instr. G. G., Dec., 1883; dist. commr., Lagos, and Benin, 1884; ditto, Cape Coast, 1885; sub. instr., Honduras Constabulary, Dec., 1888; mag., St. Walk, Nov., 1889, to Nov., 1890; private secretary and aide-de-camp to Sir A. Moloney, Sept., 1891, to Feb., 1892.
BAYLY, LIEUTENANT-COLONEL Z. S., C.M.G. (1880).—Commanding right wing, Cape Mounted Rifles; and acting commdt.-gen. of Cape colonial forces; commandant general, 1881; retired 1892.

BAYNES, EDWARD.—Was private secretary to governor Leeward Islands 1869 to 1873; was clerk in colonial secretary's office; clerk to legislature and executive council, and clerk to legislature of Antigua; acting president, Montserrat, May, 1886, to Oct., 1887; commissr., Montserrat, Mar., 1889; ag. commsr. Dominica, 1890, to 1891, to Mar., 1892, and Mar., 1894, to June, 1894.

events.position: Called to the bar, Middle Temple
events.text_span: Called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1878

events.position: awarded scholarship of one hundred guineas in Real and Personal Property Law
events.text_span: awarded scholarship of one hundred guineas in Real and Personal Property Law, 1880

events.position: crown member of general and local councils
events.text_span: crown member of general and local councils, 1887

raw_text: BAYNES, THOMAS.—Called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1878; awarded scholarship of one hundred guineas in Real and Personal Property Law, 1880; acted as solicitor-general, Leeward Islands, 1885; confirmed May, 1886; crown member of general and local councils, 1887; commr. to revise law of colony; puisne judge, Leewards, 1889.

BEACH, THE RIGHT HON. SIR MURRAY EDWARD HICKS, 9th Baronet (created 1611), K.M.G., M.P.—Born 1837; educated at Eton and Christ Church, Oxon, first class in law and modern history; final examination, July, 1858; B.A., 1858; M.A., 1861; deputy-lieutenant for Gloucestershire, 1863; M.P. for East Gloucestershire, July, 1864; parliamentary secretary to the poor law board, 1866; under-secretary of state for the home department, Aug. to Dec., 1868; chief secretary to the lord-lieutenant of Ireland, and sworn member of the privy council, Feb. 1874; added to a seat in the cabinet, 1876; secretary of state for the colonies, 4th Feb., 1878, to 28th June, 1880; chancellor of the exchequer and leader of the House of Commons, 24th June, 1885, to 10th Feb., 1886; chief secretary to the lord-lieutenant of Ireland, Aug., 1886, to Jan., 1887; president of the board of trade 1888-92; chancellor of the exchequer, 28th June, 1895; M.P. for West Birmingham since 1885.
BEAL, J. C.—Educated St. James' Grammar School, Sydney; superintendent government printing office, Queensland, 1862; acting government printer, Dec., 1866; confirmed, 1867.

events.text_span: Called to the bar, Leeward Islands, Mar., 1875

events.position: awarded D.C.L. by council of legal education, London
events.text_span: awarded D.C.L. by council of legal education, London, 1881

events.position: called to the bar, Middle Temple
events.text_span: called to the bar, Middle Temple, Trin., 1882

raw_text: BEARD, CHARLES HALMAN.—Called to the bar, Leeward Islands, Mar., 1875; awarded D.C.L. by council of legal education, London, 1881; called to the bar, Middle Temple, Trin., 1882; puisne judge, Leeward Islands, 1886; lieutenant-governor, ditto, 1889.

BEAUMONT, W. H.—Ensign 75th (Stirlingshire) regt., August, 1870; lieut. Oct., 1871; served the "Langalibalele Expedition" in 1873; retired Apr., 1875; private secretary to Colonel Miles (ministering the government of Natal), May, 1878; to Sir Benjamin Pine (lieut.-governor), and to the executive council, July, 1873; acting M. Umlazi Division, Aug., 1874; clerk, col. sec. office, Jan., 1875; acting governor's clerk and to the executive council, Oct., 1875; R.M., Newcastle division, Feb., 1878; colonial commandant of District No. 1, Natal, at the commencement of the Zulu War, from 1st to 31st Jan., 1879, during which time he raised levies called the Newcastle Scouts;" R.M., Inanda, May, 1887; M. City divn., 1896.

events.position: Minister of finance and culture, and premier, British Columbia
events.text_span: Minister of finance and culture, and premier, British Columbia, Canada, 1887

raw_text: LEAVEN, ROBERT.—Minister of finance and culture, and premier, British Columbia, Canada, 1887; leader of opposition, 1887.
BECKWITH, A. G.—Government engineer of works, Fredericton, New Brunswick; mem. Can. Soc. C.E.

BELL, ARCHIBALD G.—Assoc. M.I.C.E. Ed. at head and Uppingham; employed on surveys of rly. extensions, 1887-8, and by Jamaica G. Co. after sale of line, 1889-90; asst. to Messrs. Shaw and Hayter, 1891; asst. engr. P.W.D., Guiana, Oct., 1891; ag. asst. col. engr., 1893, M. and 1896.
BELL, CHARLES D. E.—Examining officer of works, Port Elizabeth.

events.text_span: Inspector of police in Guiana, Mar., 1876

events.text_span: acting assistant-commissioner H.M.'s penal settlement, Massaruni, Apr., 1876

ag. inspr.-gen. police, S.S., April, 1894 to Apl., 1895.

raw_text: BELL, EDWARD HORATIO.—Inspector of police in Guiana, Mar., 1876; acting assistant-commissioner H.M.'s penal settlement, Massaruni, Apr., 1876; chief inspector of police, Str. Settls., Apr., 1877; ag. inspr. general, Jan. to April, 1881; is a J.P.; suptdt. of police, Malacca, April, 1881; ditto, Singapore, Mar., 1893; ag. inspr.-gen. police, S.S., April, 1894 to Apl., 1895.

events.position: agent general for the boy in London
events.text_span: agent general for the boy in London, 1881

events.position: apptd. comdr. of legion of honour, for services at Paris exhibition
events.text_span: apptd. comdr. of legion of honour, for services at Paris exhibition, 1889.

raw_text: BELL, THE HON. SIR FRANCIS DILLOX, C.M.G. (1881), KNIGHT BACH. (1873), C.B. (1890).—Formerly Speaker of the House of Representatives, New Zealand; agent general for the boy in London, 1881; resigned, 1891; was commissioner for New Zealand at the exhibition, 1886; legate to colonial conference, 1887; apptd. comdr. of legion of honour, for services at Paris exhibition, 1889.

BELL, HARRY CHARLES PURVIS.—Educated at Etenham College; writer, Ceylon civil service, dy. 1873; police magistrate, Matale, Mar., 1876, riding and tide surveyor, Galle, Jan., 1878; office assistant, Colombo, to the government agent; western province, Feb., 1878; first landing surveyor, customs, Colombo, May, 1883; assistant collector, customs, Galle, April, 1884; assistant commissioner and additional district judge, Kegalla, June, 1885; acting second assistant to colonial secretary, May, 1886; ag. landing surveyor, Colombo, 1887; dist. judge, Ratnapura, November, 1888; to Kegalle, July, 1889; archaeological commissioner, Dec., 1895.

BELL, H. H. J., F.R.G.S.—Educated in Paris; served Col. Service in May, 1882, as 3rd clerk, war-in-chief's office, Barbados; inland revenue dept. St. Andrew's, Grenada, 1883; supervisor of customs, Gold Coast, Jan., 1890; travelling and inspecting supervisor, July, 1890; sr. asst. treasurer, Gold Coast, Aug., 1891; dist. commr., and sheriff, Accra, May, 1892; ag. col. treas. and member ex. and leg. councils, Dec. 1893 to Feb. 1894; is author of "Obeah: Witchcraft in the West Indies," "A Witch's Legacy," "Geography of the Gold Coast," &c.; treasurer and rec.-gen., Bahamas, 1894.
BELL, HUGH HOWARD.—Ed. Abdn. Gram. Schl. and Lond. Univ.; solicitor, supreme court, 1882; dist. commr., Gold Coast, May, 1892.
BELL, THE HON. JAMES.—Minister without portfolio, Victoria, Feb., 1886; minister of defence, 1888-90.

events.position: Chief resident engineer, Jamaica Government Railway
events.text_span: Chief resident engineer, Jamaica Government Railway, 1880-86

raw_text: BELL, VALENTINE G., M.I.C.E.—Chief resident engineer, Jamaica Government Railway, 1880-86; ag.-director of public works, Aug., 1886; confirmed Mar., 1887; ex officio member leg. coun.; member of privy coun. Aug., 1886.

events.text_span: Clerk, real property office, Brisbane, Jan., 1863

events.position: registrar and prothonotary supreme court, registrar and master in lunacy, &c
events.text_span: registrar and prothonotary supreme court, registrar and master in lunacy, &c., Oct., 1882
also Queen's coroner and attorney, July, 1887

raw_text: BELL, WILLIAM.—Clerk, real property office, Brisbane, Jan., 1863; chief clerk, supreme court, Nov., 1873, and also deputy registrar, July, 1876; registrar, Vice-Admiralty Court, Nov., 1877; registrar and prothonotary supreme court, registrar and master in lunacy, &c., Oct., 1882; also Queen's coroner and attorney, July, 1887; is a commissioner for affidavits, a solicitor of the supreme court, &c.

BELLAIRS, MAJ.-GEN. SIR WILLIAM, C.B. (1878), K.C.M.G. (1882).—Entered the army, 1846; adjutant, 1851; captain, 1854; major, 1855; lieut.-col., 1865; colonel, 1873; brig.-general, 1881. Served with the 49th Regiment, and on the staff of the army throughout the Crimean campaign, being present at the siege and fall of Sebastopol, medal with three clasps, Turkish medal, 5th class of Medjidie, knight of legion of honour; served throughout the Kaffir campaign, 1877-8, when he commanded the combined forces on the eastern frontier; also throughout the Zulu campaign 1879. Has also acted as inspector-general of police, Barbados, 1857-8; local inspector of army schools, 1868-73; colonial secretary, Gibraltar, Aug. to Nov. 1872; administrator of Natal April to May, 1880; member of executive council, Transvaal, 1880-1; commander of the forces at Pretoria in the Transvaal, 1880-81; administrator of Transvaal, April to June, 1881.

BELFIELD, HENRY CONWAY.—Ed. Rugby and Oriel col., Oxon.; B.A., 1877; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1880; went western circuit; mag., collr. of ld. rev., and inspr. of schools, Selangor, 1884; ag. chief mag. and commssr. of lds., 1884, confirmed 1888; ag. Br. resdt., June and July, 1889; serr. mag., Perak, 1891; ag. secy. to Govt., Perak, July, 1891, to Jan., 1893; commisnr. of lands, Federated Malay States, July, 1896.

events.text_span: Assistant superintendent Convict Department, Norfolk Island, Aug., 1850
clerk of petty sessions, Westbury, Tasmania, May, 1858

raw_text: BELSTEAD, FRANCIS.—Assistant superintendent Convict Department, Norfolk Island, Aug., 1850; clerk of petty sessions, Westbury, Tasmania, May, 1858; clerk of municipal council, Dec., 1863; commissioner of mines and gold fields, and magistrate and coroner, Launceston, Feb., 1883; secretary and chief commissioner of mines and gold fields for Tasmania, Feb., 1886.
BENJAMIN, SIR BENJAMIN, Kt. BACH. (1889).—Three years Mayor of Melbourne.

events.text_span: Tempy. outdoor offr., S. Leone, July, 1887
chief clk. of customs, Niger Coast Protectorate, 1891.

raw_text: BENNETT, C. K.—Tempy. outdoor offr., S. Leone, July, 1887; warehouseman, 1890; chief clk. of customs, Niger Coast Protectorate, 1891.

events.position: sergt. in charge of Holloway Police Stat
events.text_span: sergt. in charge of Holloway Police Stat., 1887

raw_text: BENNET, JOHN LIGHT.—Ed. Bourton Academy, near Bath, Somerset; joined London Police, Scotland Yard, 1879; warrant officer, Southwark Police Court, 1883; sergt. in charge of Holloway Police Stat., 1887; ag. inspr. 1888; senr. inspr. police, Gibraltar, 1889; chief of police, Gibraltar, 1895; is also supervisor of markets, and admnr. of the Aliens Order in Council.

events.text_span: 3rd clerk, bonded warehouse, British Guiana, June, 1873

raw_text: BENNETT, JAMES FITZMAURICE—3rd clerk, bonded warehouse, British Guiana, June, 1873; 1st clerk, Dec., 1873; 5th clerk, customs, Oct., 1875; 4th clerk, 1879; 2nd class customs officer, 1886.

BENNETT, HENRY JAMES LEE.—M.R.C.S., Eng., 1878; L.R.C.P. and L.M., Edin., 1878; medical officer, Grenada, 1882; late house-surgeon, General Infirmary, Dewsbury, Yorks; junior resident medical officer, Royal Albert Hospital and Eye Infirmary, Devonport, Devon; member British Medical Association; J.P., 1890.

BENNETT, SAMUEL MACKENZIE.—Financial clerk, Immigr. Dept., Jamaica, Nov., 1876; chief clerk, Education Dept., 1882; supervisor of customs, Gold Coast, 1885; acted as dist. commr. at Axim, Saltpond, Anamaboe, and Volta River; assist. col. treas., Sierra Leone, 1888; J.P.; acted as inspr.-gen. of police and col. treasurer, 1889; as treas., 1890, 1891, 1892, and 1893; and as C.C., British Sherbro, 1890 and 1891; M.L.C. and mem. ex. coun. and bd. of education; ag. D.C., Sherbro, June, 1893 to Jan., 1894; ag. judge ct. of requests and pol. mag., Freetown, also ag. col. treas., Oct., 1894, to June, 1895.
BENNETT, THOMAS.—Speaker, House of Assembly, Newfoundland, 1869; dist. judge, 1873.

raw_text: BENNETT, WILLIAM HART—Appointed after a competitive examination, a clerk of the lower division, and assigned to the colonial office, 21st Nov., 1878; chief clerk, chief secretary's office, Cyprus, Jan., 1884; acting assistant to chief secretary, Aug., 1886; acted as chief secy. July to Oct. 1893; ag. commisar. Papho, May to July, 1894; asst. secy. to Govt. June, 1895; ag. chief sec. and member exec. coun. July, 1895.
BENT, THE HON. THOMAS.—Commissioner of railways, Victoria, 9th July, 1881; also vice-president of board of land and works; afterwards speaker of legislative assembly.

events.text_span: Revenue officer, Leeward District, St. Vincent, Oct., 1883

events.text_span: clerk of councils, and chief clerk to governor, Windward Islands, Nov., 1889.

raw_text: BERESFORD, M. H. DE LA POER.—Revenue officer, Leeward District, St. Vincent, Oct., 1883; acted as police magistrate and coroner, Windward District, Dec., 1884; confidential clerk and clerk of councils, Grenada, Jan., 1885, and in conjunction therewith private secretary to governor, Sir Walter J. Sendall, K.C.M.G., Nov., 1887; acting inspr. of prisons, Jan. to Mar., 1889; clerk of councils, and chief clerk to governor, Windward Islands, Nov., 1889.

BERKELEY, SIR GEORGE, K.C.M.G. (1881), C.M.G. (1874).—Appointed (1845) colonial secretary and controller of customs, Honduras; administered the government of Dominica from Apr. 1860 to Dec., 1861; lieutenant-governor of St. Vincent, April, 1864; acting administrator, Lara, Dec. 1872; governor, West Africa Settlements, 1873; governor, Leeward Islands, 1874; retro 1881.

BERKELEY, SIR HENRY SPENCER K BACH.—Called to the bar, Inner Temple, Jan. 1873; ag. attorney-general of the Leeward Islands, Aug., 1877; solicitor-general, Leeward Islands, June, 1878; colonial secretary, Leeward Islands (acting) 1883; attorney-general, Fiji, 1884; ag. chief justice, and chief judicial commissioner, Western Pacific, May, 1887, to July, 1888; high commissioner for W. P., Dec., 1887, to Feb., 1888; chief justice of Fiji and chief judicial commissioner for W. P., Feb., 1889; administered the government of on several occasions.

events.position: joined Sir T. Sidgreaves, chief justice
events.text_span: joined Sir T. Sidgreaves, chief justice, S.S., 1885
in charge of Stiawan District, Nov., 1889
magistrate and collector, Upper Perak, May, 1891.

raw_text: BERKELEY, HUBERT.—Ed. at the Charter School, Edgbaston, and H.M.S. "Conway"; shipman, R.N.R., 15th Dec., 1881; served H.M.S. "President" and "Trincomalee"; joined Sir T. Sidgreaves, chief justice, S.S., 1885; appointed to S.S. police, June, 1886; suptdt. of Penghulus Perak, Jan., 1889; in charge of Stiawan District, Nov., 1889; magistrate and collector, Upper Perak, May, 1891.

BERKELEY, JOHN HART HARDMAN.—Sign, 83rd Regt., Sept., 1869; lieut. and adj., June, 1871; A.D.C. to Sir William Cairns, 1875; A.D.C. and private secretary to Sir Mary O'Connell, 1877; A.D.C. to Sir Arthur Kean, governor of Queensland, 1877-78; member legislative Council, Nevis, and general legislative council, Leeward Island, May, 1882; elected president of latter council May, 1882, and sec., 1883 and 1886; nominated member legislative council of St. Kitts, Feb., 1883; member executive council, June, 1883.

BERKELEY, MAURICE J.—Barrister-at-Law (Lincoln's Inn); clerk to the chief justice, Barbados, 1878-83; acting police magistrate, Windward District, Tobago, April to Dec., 1883; acting inland revenue officer, District "C," Barbados, on several occasions; confirmed 1883; acting police magistrate, District "C," Barbados, 1883-84 and 1885-6; acting police magistrate, Windward District, St. Vincent, July, 1886; pol. mag. and petty debt ct. judge, dist. "D," St. Vincent, 1887; clerk to the quarter sessions, St. Vincent, 1888; appointed clerk to the quarter sessions, St. Vincent, 1889; acting clerk of the quarter sessions, St. Vincent, 1890; appointed clerk of the quarter sessions, St. Vincent, 1891; acting clerk of the quarter sessions, St. Vincent, 1892; appointed clerk of the quarter sessions, St. Vincent, 1893; acting clerk of the quarter sessions, St. Vincent, 1894; appointed clerk of the quarter sessions, St. Vincent, 1895.

BERNAYS, LEWIS ADOLPHUS, C.M.G., F.L.S., F.R.G.S., and corresponding member of various scientific societies.—Educated at King's Coll. and St. Thomas' Hospital, and was a member of Parliament in N.S. Wales, 1853-9; clerk of Legislative Assembly, Queensland, 1859; secretary of the Brisbane Water Supply, and local director of various public institutions.
flags: name_block_extra:and corresponding member of various scie

raw_text: BERRIDGE, T. PROBYN.—Member of assembly, St. Christopher, from 1843 till 1851, when he was appointed clerk to the legislative council; accompanied Prince Alfred when His Royal Highness visited St. Christopher; appointed to the magistracy, 1862; one of the clerks of the general legislative council of the Leeward Islands, 1872; acting master, St. Christopher, 1872; commissioner of cemeteries, and member of the Board of the Nunningham Hospital, 1886.

raw_text: ERRINGTON, ARTHUR T. D.—Educated at College and Christ Church, Oxford; B.A., 4; called Inner Temple, 1879; Oxford circuit; and D.L. for Monmouthshire; private secretary to Sir H. Bulwer in Cyprus, Oct., 1888, and Sir Frederick Dickson in the Straits Settlements, May, 1890; acting chief magistrate, Selangor, Nov., 1890; confirmed 1891; snr. mag., k. July, 1896.

ELRY, THE HON. SIR GRAHAM, K.C.M.G. —Member of the Victorian Legis. Assembly, in 1875 formed his first administration, taking post of chief secretary and treasurer; resigned; a premier in 1877; in Dec., 1878, proceeded to land to procure a settlement of the dead lock between the two Houses of Legislature; chief secretary and postmaster general in Mr. Service's stry, 1883; agent-general for Victoria, 1886-87; delegate to the Colonial Conference, 1887; commander. of legion of honour for services Paris Exhibition, 1889; treasurer in Shiels stry in 1892; speaker of assembly, 1894.

events.position: member of executive and legislative council
events.text_span: member of executive and legislative council, 1883.

raw_text: ELRY, JOHN.—Entered survey department, W., 1866; survey department, Fiji, 1877; surveyor-general, 1879; commissioner of works and surveys, 1882; member of executive and legislative council, 1883.

ETRAM, LOUIS J.—Educated Victoria College, 1870-7; appointed to exchequer and department, March, 1878; assistant auditor, Gold Coast Colony, Oct., 1884; acting auditor, 1886, 1887; auditor, 1888. Prepared scheme for accounts introduced into Gold Coast and West Africa, 1888; auditor of Windward Islands, 1893; Leeward Is., 1896; ditto, Jamaica, 1897.

events.text_span: clerk magistrate of Port Louis, May, 1854

events.position: acting ditto and stipendiary magistrate at Black River
events.text_span: acting ditto and stipendiary magistrate at Black River, 1856

events.position: acting treasurer and collector of land revenues
events.text_span: acting treasurer and collector of land revenues, October, 1873

raw_text: EYTS, H. N. DUVERGER, C.M.G. (1881).—Settler at Flacq, Mauritius, April, 1862; clerk magistrate of Port Louis, May, 1854; acting ditto and stipendiary magistrate at Black River, 1856; at Grand Port, March, 1857; district judge at Flacq, March, 1858; acting protector emigrants, May, 1859, and confirmed July, 1861; member of the legislative council, 1867; chairman, law commission, 1869; census commissioner, 1871; acting treasurer and collector of land revenues, October, 1873; member of executive council, October, 1873; acting receiver-general, January, 1874; confirmed as such, 1877; has acted several occasions as colonial secretary, and also for a short time administered the government of the colony; retired, 1889.
KENELL, H. J.—Police magistrate, Kingston, Jamaica, Mar., 1860; acting judge, eastern district, Oct., 1882; R.M., St. Catherine, April,

KENELL, W. A.—Ch. clk. col. sec. office, Singapore, Sep. 1885; auditor, Penang, July, 1888. KIDULPH, GEN. SIR ROBERT, R.A., K.C.M.G. (1886), K.C.B.—Born 1839; appointed to Royal Artillery, 1853; served Crimean Campaign, 1854-56; Indian Mutiny campaign, 1857-59; China War, 1860. Staff Services.—Deputy assistant adjutant-general in India, 1860; military secretary in China, 1860-61; military secretary, Madras, 1861-65; deputy assistant quartermaster-general, Woolwich, 1868-71; private secretary to Mr. Cardwell when secretary of state for war, 1871-73; assistant adjutant-general, War Office, 1873-78; special service, Cyprus, 1878; commanding the troops, Cyprus, 1879; was assistant boundary commissioner for the Parliamentary Reform Act of 1867. Was appointed, 1879, H.M.'s Commissioner for arranging payment to be made to the Porte under the Treaty to the Convention of 4th June, 1878; and High Commissioner for Cyprus, 1879; inspector general of recruiting, 1886; director general of military education, 1st Mar., 1888; quartermaster-general, Jan., 1893; governor and commander-in-chief, Gibraltar, Oct., 1893.

events.text_span: 2nd clerk to R.M., Weenen County, Natal, 8 May, 1882

raw_text: BIGGS, JOHN W. FORBES.—Lieut. 109th regt., 1871; enlisted in Natal mounted police, 1879; 2nd clerk to R.M., Weenen County, Natal, 8 May, 1882; clk. to inspr. of prisons, 1893; clk. to R. M. Estcourt, 1894.

BIGGS, REV. LOUIS COUTIER.—Ordained deacon 1864, priest 1866; B.A. (3rd class in Lit. Hum.) of St. Edmund Hall, Oxford, 1863; M.A. 1866; curate of Grendon Northants, 1864; rector of Parracombe, North Devon, 1868; Chickerell, Dorset, 1870; chaplain of Malacca, 1874; of Penang, 1885.

BIRCH, SIR ARTHUR N., K.C.M.G. (1886), C.M.G. (1875).—Clerk in the colonial office, Feb., 1855; promoted to 3rd class, Oct., 1859; assistant private secretary to Sir E. Bulwer Lytton, 1858; private secretary to Mr. Chichester Fortescue, from Sept., 1859, until Feb., 1864, when he was permitted, while still remaining on the establishment of the colonial office, to accept the appointment of colonial secretary of British Columbia; was senior member of the executive council, and ex officio clerk of the same; also president of the legislative council; was administrator of the government of the colony from Sept., 1865, to Nov., 1866, promoted to be assistant senior clerk, colonial office, July 5, 1866; resumed his duties in colonial office in September, 1867; acting lieut.-governor of Penang and Province of Wellesley, Feb., 1871; returned to England, July, 1872; 1st class clerk, colonial office, 30th September, 1872; colonial secretary, Ceylon, June, 1873; administered the government during the spring and summer of 1874 and 1875-6; lieut.-governor, Ceylon, 1876; resigned, June, 1878; is agent of the Western Branch of the Bank of England, Burlington Gardens; executive commissioner for Ceylon at the Colonial and Indian Exhibition, 1886.

BIRCH, ERNEST WOODFORD.—Ed. Harrow; cadet, S.S., Jan., 1876; employed in the colonial office, Downing street, to July, 1878, secretary to the police commission, 1879; passed final examination in Malay, Aug., 1880; J.P. and a magistrate, Malacca; took charge Malacca land office, Feb., 1881; acting collector of land revenue, Singapore 1881, 2nd assistant colonial secretary, 1882; on special mission in H.M.S. "Espoir," to report on the Cocos-Keeling Islands, 1885; magistrate and collector of land revenue, Malacca, 1888; ag. Br. resident, Selangor, May, 1892, to Jan., 1893; secy. to govt., Ferak, July, 1893; ag. resdt., Perak, Sept., 1895, to July, 1896.

BIRCH, JAMES KORTRIGHT.—Cadet, Str. Sett., 1872; passed final examination in Malay, Dec., 1873; assistant magistrate, coroner, and collector land revenue, P.W., Sept., 1874; collector land revenue, Penang and P.W., and settlement officer Trans Krian, Sept., 1877; collector of land revenue, Singapore, June, 1880; acting pol. mag. and comsr., ct. of requests, P.W., Dec., 1881; confirmed, May, 1882; sen. dist. officer, P.W., 1888; 1st mag. and comsr., ct. of requests, and supt. of prisons, Penang, June, 1890; ag. treasurer, S.S., Feb., 1894; 1st mag. Singapore, April, 1895, but continued to act as treas.

events.text_span: Has represented Franklin District in House of Assembly, Tasmania, since 1882

raw_text: BIRD, THE HON. B. STAFFORD.—Has represented Franklin District in House of Assembly, Tasmania, since 1882; Member of Commissions on Education and on Lunatic Asylums, 1883; treasurer of the colony in the Fysh Government, 1887-92.

BIRD, CHRISTOPHER.—Clerk, Engineer's Department, Natal, Jan., 1874; 2nd clerk, colonial secretary's office, Jan., 1880; 1st clerk, Aug., 1881; clerk, Executive Council, April, 1883, to July, 1885; acting asst. col. sec. April, 1884, to 1886, J.P., 1887; asst. col. secy., Jan., 1888; apptd. principal under sec. on establishment of responsible govt., Oct., 1893; member of civil service bd., 1894.
BLACK, G. G.—Ed. High Schl., Glasgow; Univ. Coll. Schl.; London and Glasgow Univ.; clk., audit dept., W. Australia, 1892; audit exmur., 1894; chief clk., P. W. Dept., 1896.

BLACK, THOMAS GORDON DALLAS.—Served in 2nd Dragoons, "Scots Greys," 1858-68. Ensign 4th W. I. Regiment, Oct., 1868; transferred to 3rd W. I. Regiment, April, 1869; sub-inspector, Jamaica constabulary, Feb., 1870; acting inspector, Aug., 1871; 3rd class inspector (and in charge of detective department), Oct., 1872; 1st class inspector, temporarily, Feb., 1885; 2nd class inspector, Dec., 1875; 1st class ditto, Aug., 1886.

BLACKMORE, EDWIN GORDON.—Educated at King Edward VI Grammar School, Bath; served with the Taranaki Rifle Volunteers in the New Zealand war, 1863-64, and was present, in reserve at the action of Poutoko, 2nd Oct., 1863, and at the storming and capture of the rebel Maori strongholds at Ahuahu and Kaitake, March, 1864 (medal); parliamentary librarian to the legislature of South Australia, Oct., 1864; clerk assistant and serjeant-at-arms, House of Assembly, Dec., 1869; clerk of the House of Assembly, May, 1886; clerk Leg. Coun. and clerk of Parlts., May, 1887; author of "The Decisions of Mr. Speaker Denison and Mr. Speaker Brand, on Points of Order, Rules of Debate, and the General Practice of the House of Commons, 1857-84," "The Decisions of Mr. Speaker Peel, 1884-90," "Manual of the Practice, Procedure, and Usage of the House of Assembly of S.A.," "Manual of the Practice, Procedure, and Usage of the Legislative Council of S.A."; and the "Law of the Constitution of S.A."

raw_text: BLACKWELL, LIONEL NORTON.—2nd lieut. 4th Bn. King's Shropshire L.I., Nov., 1888; lieut., Sept., 1889; transferred to Thames Mil. Divsn., R.E., Mar., 1894; instructor of musketry, Apl., 1895; asst. inspr., Lagos Housa Force, Nov., 1895; ag. dist. commsr., Oct., 1896.

South Malacca June, 1890; 3rd mag., Penang, Mar., 1892; dist. offr. S. Malacca, 1893; ag. collr. ld. rev. and officer in charge of treas., Malacca, Dec., 1893 to Mar., 1894; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Nov., 1895; ag. 2nd mag. Singapore, Nov., 1895.

raw_text: BLAIR, A.G.—Member for York in New Brunswick House of Assembly; premier and attorney-general from 1883 to 1896; minister of rlys. and canals in Mr. Laurier's govt., June, 1896.

BLAIR, WM.—Sec. to Bd. of Edn., and inspr. of schools, Bahamas, 1868; inspr. of schools, Ceylon, 1879; ag. dir. of ednn 8 1882 and 1884; inspr. of schools, Br. Guiana, 1890; is author of a school arithmetic in English, Sinhalese, and Tamil.

BLAKE, THE HON. EDWARD, Q.C. M.P.—Born 1833; called to the bar of Upper Canada, 1856; created a Q.C., 1864; entered Canadian parliament 1867, and has also sat in Ontario assembly; was prime minister of Ontario from 20th Dec., 1871, until 25th Oct., 1872, when he resigned; sworn of the privy council, 7th Nov., 1873, but did not hold office; re-entered the cabinet, 19th May, 1875, as minister of justice, a position he exchanged in Sept., 1877, for that of president of the council; retired from the cabinet, 31st Jan., 1878; declined the chancellorship of Ontario, Dec., 1869, also the chief justiceship of the supreme court of the Dominion, May, 1875, and the K.C.M.G. in May, 1877; is chancellor of the University of Toronto, and President of the Law Society of Upper Canada; leader of the Liberal party in Canada, 1880-7; elected to English Parliament, 1892.

BLAKE, ERNEST EDWARD.—Appointed to clerkship in the colonial office, 4th July, 1897, after a competitive examination; 1st junior clk., 20th Nov., 1869; 2nd class clerk, 30th Sept., 1872; assist. priv. sec. to the Earl of Kimberley, 1st Oct., 1872; priv. sec., 27th Jan., 1874; class clerk and head of the general department, 1st May, 1879; crown agent for the colonies, 1st Jan., 1881.

BLAKE, SIR HENRY ARTHUR K.C.M.G., D.C.M.G. (1887), F.R.G.S.—Cadet, Royal Irish Constabulary, Feb., 1859; sub-inspector, Mar., 1872; appointed resident magistrate, Feb., 1876; in 1882, was one of the five special resident magistrates selected by Government to concert and carry out measures for the pacification of a rebellious portion of Ireland; appointed governor of the Bahamas, Jan., 1884; Newfoundland, 1887; Queensland, Nov., 1888, but did not take up appointment; governor, Jamaica, Dec., 1888.

events.text_span: transferred to sheriff's office, Brisbane, Feb., 1860

raw_text: BLAKENEY, WILLIAM THEOPHILUS.—Sheriff's office at Sydney, Feb., 1856; transferred to sheriff's office, Brisbane, Feb., 1860; under sheriff, Mar., 1862; deputy regis.-gen., Dec., 1865; now regis.-gen., comsr. of stamps, regis. of patents, designs and trade marks, and of friendly societies.

raw_text: BLAKEWAY, CAPT. JOHN EDWIN CHAS.—F. St. Peter's Coll., Camb.; lieut. 4th Batt. Essex Regt., Sept., 1886; capt. Feb., 1895; qualified commsn. in the line, Sept., 1889; Hythe certificate, 1894; Aldershot and Shoeburyness, 1st tactics and military topography, 1894; asst. ins. S. Leone frontier police, Sept., 1895; is now on leave for the colony.

BLANCHARD, VICTOR P.—Clk., Govt. Office, Roseau, Dominica, 1889; clerk to valuer, comsrs. and comsrs. of assessed taxes, 1895; ag. chief outdoor officer, treaty and quarantine officer, July, 1895, to 1896; ag. sec. quarantine bd., 1896.

raw_text: BLANCHET, J., Q.C.—Provincial secretary and registrar, Quebec; puisne judge, Quebec Bench divn., supreme ct., Quebec, 1891.

raw_text: BLAND, ROBERT NORMAN.—Ed. St. Paul's School and Cheltenham Coll., B.A. Trin. Coll. Dub.; Cadet, Straits S., Oct., 1882; passed Malay, May, 1884; collector and magistrate at Menanti, June, 1886; district officer southern district, P. W., 1888; collr. of land rev., Penang, 1889; ditto, Singapore, 1890; offr. in charge of Sunjei Ujong, Dec. 1894; ditto, Negri Sembilan, Jan. to April, 1895; ag. offr. assignee of debt, Singapore, June, 1896.

BLISSETT, HENRY FREDERICK, C.M.G. (1897)—Clerk, Royal Ordnance Factories, at Portsmouth, Jan., 1867; assistant commissary, African Commissariat, Aug., 1869; private secretary, 1870-1; administrator of the Gold Coast, and clerk of court, 1872; served throughout the Ashanti Expedition, 1873-4, as control officer with Capt. Glover's force. mentioned in despatches, and medal; acted on several occasions as auditor-general of the W. A. Colonies from 1875 to 1881; commissioner to audit the accounts of the Falkland Islands; sent on special service to the Gold Coast as commissioner of accounts, 1884.

events.position: under secy. for railways (in addition to P. W.)

raw_text: LOW, HORATIO JOHN HOOPER.—Born London, arrived N. Zealand, 1871; entered govt. service (P. W. dept.), 1873; record clk. 1878; ag. under secy., Jan. 1885; asst. under secy., 1885; ag. under secy., June, 1891; under secy. for railways (in addition to P. W.), Jan., 1895.
DAK, THE HON. ROBERT.—President of legislative council, Nova Scotia.

raw_text: OHAM, JOSEPH ALFRED.—Wardman Elmina p. 1881; dispenser and clk. Cape Coast hosp., senr. dispr. and gen. storekeeper med. dept. m. 1889.

DISRAGON, CAPT. A. M.—Asst. inspr. G. st constab.; served with 1st batt. Roy. Irish in Nile expedn., 1884-5 (medal and clasp, and five's star); commanded escort Anglo-French boundary commn., on western boundary G. Coast, to June, 1892, received thanks of sec. of state; special mission to Krobo and Shai Hills, 1892, received thanks of governor; comdt. constab., Niger Coast Prot., 1894.
OLDERO, CAPT. A. H.—Retired R.N.; dep. attendant, Singapore, May, 1896.

OND, HON. ROBERT.—Speaker, Newfoundland house of assembly, 1884; member of exec. coun. col. secy., 1889 to 1894; official delegate to London on fisheries question, 1890, and to Washington on reciprocity question in the same year; n col. sec. 1895; delegate to Ottawa in connection with the confederation negotiations, and subsequently negotiated the loan for the colony in London in that year.

raw_text: UNSER, SIR J. W., M.A., KT. BACH. (1894).—Formerly fellow of Christ's College, Cambridge, or classic in 1870; called to the bar, Lincoln's 18th Nov., 1872; attorney-general, Straits Settlements, 1883; chief justice, 1893; chief justice, Ceylon, 1893.
OOTH, LEONARD WM.—Writer, Ceylon Service, 1878; police magistrate, Avisawella, 1880; assistant to government agent, Uva, 1886; o. Manaar, 1891; pol. mag., Kandy, 1895.

raw_text: BOOTHBY, JOSIAH, C.M.G. (1878).—Clerk, colonial secretary's office, South Australia, 1853; clerk in audit office, 1854; chief clerk, 1856; chief clerk in chief secretary's office, 1859; also government statist and superintendent of census, 1860; assistant secretary and government statist, South Australia, 1866; and under-secretary and government statist, 1868 to 1880; elected corresponding member of the Statistical Society, London, 1869; also trustee, savings bank South Australia, 1869; a commissioner for international exhibitions, 1872; joint editor of a work "South Australia; its History, Resources, and Productions," published by authority of government, 1876; executive commissioner representing South Australia at the Paris Universal Exhibition of 1878; elected hon. member of Royal Geographical Societies of Berlin and Vienna, 1878.

BORDEN, THE HON. FREDERICK WILLIAM.—Born at Cornwallis, King's County, May 14, 1847; graduate of Harvard Medical School; has represented King's in the House of Commons since 1874, with the exception of one term, being defeated at the general elections of 1882; minister of Militia and Defence in Mr. Laurier's govt., June, 1896.

raw_text: BOSMAN, JOHANNES JACOBUS.—Examiner of diagrams, surveyor general's office, Cape, Aug., 1892.

BOUCAUT, THE HON. JAMES PENN.—Entered Parliament, South Australia, 1862. Held office in several ministries, as attorney-general, October, 1865, to March, 1866, and thence to May, 1867; as attorney-general and premier, and again as attorney-general, in 1872; as premier and commissioner of crown lands and public works, from June, 1875, to June, 1876; as premier and treasurer, from 26th October, 1877, to 25th September, 1878, when he accepted a seat on the bench of the Supreme Court.

BOURINOT, JOHN GEORGE, M.A., LL.D., Lit.D., D.C.L., C.M.G. (1890).—Educated at Trinity College, Toronto. Officer of the Senate of Canada, 1868; clerk assistant of the House of Commons of Canada, 1873; honorary secretary of the royal society of Canada; presdt. of the Society, 1893-4, and then again secy.; clerk of the House in 1880; member of coun. of American Historical Assn., and of the Amer. Academy of Political and Social Science; hon. foreign memb. Amer. Antiquarian Soc.; vice-presdt. in Canada of Egypt Explor. Fund; is author of a large work on Parliamentary Procedure in Canada, of a manual on the Constitutional History of Canada, and of other works on the history and constitution of Canada, and of numerous essays relating to the intellectual and material progress of the dominion, which have appeared in the transactions of English and Amer. societies, Economic Association, and in the pages of "Blackwood," "Edinburgh," and the "Quarterly Review;" lecturer on political science in Trinity University, Toronto; hon. LL.D. from Queen's University, Kingston, and hon. D.C.L. from Trinity University, Toronto, King's Coll. N.S., Bishop's Coll., Quebec; Docteur-ès-Lettres, from Laval Univ., Quebec.
