raw_text: BOURNE, HUGH CLARENCE, M.A. (Balliol College, Oxford).— Assist. secretary, Charity Organization Society, 1881-84; called to the bar (Inner Temple), 1884; registrar-general and examiner of titles, Trinidad, 1893; has acted in addition at various times as regr. sup. ct., and marshal as dep. judge of dist.-ct. and as S.J.P. Pt. of Spain; member of rlys. and rds. commsn., 1894; as judge sup. ct., 1896.
BOURNE, J. O.—Registrar of titles, Queensland, 1890.

BOVELL, HENRY ALLEYNE.—Gilchrist scholar (Lond. Univ.), Jan., 1873; LL.B. (Lond.), Jan., 1877, with second class honours in jurisprudence and Roman law; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Nov., 1876; acted as solicitor-general, as escheator-general and attorney-general of Barbados, 1881 and 1882; solicitor-general and escheator-general of Barbados, and J.P., Mar., 1882; M.L.C., July, 1883; member of commission to revise laws of Barbados, Mar., 1886; chancellor of the diocese, 1884; acting attorney-general, June, 1884, to July, 1885; att.-gen., Oct., 1886; atty.-gen. Br. Guiana, 1896.

BOVELL, JOHN R., F.L.S., F.C.S.—Parochial treas. and collr. of rates, parish of St. John, Barbados, April, 1882; suptdt. Reformatory and Industrial School, April, 1883; member local committee Col. and Ind. Exhibn. 1885; suptd. Botan. Stan., 1886; went to Antigua 1890 to start Skerrett's Training School; vice presdt. Windward dist. Agri. Soc. 1890; member of commsn. to enquire into sugar cane borers, Jan., 1893; J.P., Mar., 1893; chairman Emigr. Comtee., Apl. 1895.

BOWDLER, EDWARD.—Articled to city surveyor and architect, York, 1850; employed temporarily on civil staff, R.E., on fortifications on the Humber, July, 1855; supernumerary clerk of works, R.E., to superintend works at the Royal Military College and the erection of the Royal Staff College, Sandhurst, April, 1859; assistant surveyor-general and civil engineer, Mauritius, June, 1862; acting surveyor-general, 1868-9; assistant surveyor-general, Hong Kong, July, 1874; acting surveyor-general, April, 1878; J.P., Dec., 1878, acting surveyor-general, 1880-2 special engineer, Praya Reclamation Works, 1889.

BOWELL, THE HON. SIR MACKENZIE, K.C.M.G., 1895.—Emigrated from Suffolk to Canada in 1833; well-known in Canada as a journalist; has sat in Canadian House of Commons since 1867; sworn of the privy council, 19th Oct., 1878, and appointed minister of customs; minister of militia and defence, 1892; minister of trade and commerce, Dec., 1892; on special mission to Australia in connection with the development of inter-colonial trade, 1893; president of Colonial Conference at Ottawa, 1894; Premier of Canada and Vice-President of Council, 1894-96.

events.text_span: Financial asst. police dept., Penang, April, 1890

events.position: ag. asst. offl. assignee and regr. of deeds, Penang
events.text_span: ag. asst. offl. assignee and regr. of deeds, Penang, Mar., 1895

events.position: sheriff and dep. regr. sup. ct., Penang
events.text_span: sheriff and dep. regr. sup. ct., Penang, Feb., 1896

raw_text: BOWEN, ADDERLEY FRED.—Financial asst. police dept., Penang, April, 1890; ag. asst. offl. assignee and regr. of deeds, Penang, Mar., 1895; sheriff and dep. regr. sup. ct., Penang, Feb., 1896; ag. asst. andr. gen., Mar., 1896.

events.position: physician to Children's Hosp., Philadelphia, U S.A
events.text_span: physician to Children's Hosp., Philadelphia, U S.A., 1888

events.position: junr. res. surg. Gen. Hosp., Bridgeton
events.text_span: junr. res. surg. Gen. Hosp., Bridgeton, Barbados, 1889

raw_text: BOWEN, CUTHBERT, M.A., M.D.—Ed. Harrison Coll. and Codrington Coll., Barbados; B.A. (Hon. classic and lit.), Dur., 1882; super. clk. P.O., Barbados, 1883; asst. master Combermere school, 1884; M.D., 1887; physician to Children's Hosp., Philadelphia, U S.A., 1888; junr. res. surg. Gen. Hosp., Bridgeton, Barbados, 1889; senr. ditto, Feb., 1890.
BOWEN, ERNEST F. S.—Entered Colonial Service, Feb., 1892; supt. Pub. Wks. Barbados, Feb., 1895.

BOWEN, RIGHT HON. SIR GEORGE FERGUSON (G.C.M.G. 1860), C.M.G. in 1855, K.C.M.G. in 181, Hon. D.C.L. (Oxon.), Hon. L.L.D. (Camb.)—Ed. Charterhouse, and Trin. Coll., Ox.; scholar in 184, 1st class in classics, 1844; fellow of Brasenose College, and member of Lincoln's Inn; chief secretary to the government of the Ionian Islands 1854-59; first governor of Queensland, 1859-68; governor of New Zealand, 1868-73; of Victoria, 1873-79; of Mauritius, 1879-83; of Hong Kong, 1883; retired 1887. Is author of "Ithaca in 1890" "Mount Athos, Thessaly, and Epirus," &c.; member of the privy council, 1886; commissioner for delimitation of electoral districts in May, Jan., 1888.

events.text_span: Temporary clerk, receiver general's office, Trinidad, Feb., 1857

events.position: went to Diego Martin Ward Union
events.text_span: went to Diego Martin Ward Union, Sept., 1859

raw_text: BOWEN, G. F.—Temporary clerk, receiver general's office, Trinidad, Feb., 1857; went to Diego Martin Ward Union, Sept., 1859; 4th receiver-general's office, Oct., 1873; 3rd July, 1874; 2nd clerk, Feb., 1877; chief clerk accountant, Jan., 1884.

BOWER, SIR GRAHAM JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1890), C.M.G. (1886).— Retired commander, R.N.; entered navy, 1861, and served on Mediterranean East Coast of Africa, East Indian, Home, and Australian stations, until 1880, when he was appointed private secretary to Sir Hercules Robinson, governor, Cape of Good Hope, and high commissioner for South Africa; imperial secretary and assistant to the high commissioner, April, 1884.

events.position: provisional revenue officer and keeper of the spirit warehouse
events.text_span: provisional revenue officer and keeper of the spirit warehouse, Nov., 1884

raw_text: BOWHILL, JAMES ALLEN PURSER.—Inspector of schools, Tobago, Sept., 1884; provisional revenue officer and keeper of the spirit warehouse, Nov., 1884; ag. inland revenue officer, April, 1893; ag. auditor and registrar, 8th June, 1893.

BOWKER, COL. JAMES HENRY, F.R.G.S., F.Z.S., F.S.St., Lond. (Gold Medalist).—Served in Kaffir war of 1846 and 1847, and in Kaffir war of 1851; inspector, frontier armed mounted police, 1855; served in the Transkei expedition, 1858, and remained in command until the withdrawal of the police in 1865; served in expedition to Basutoland, 1868, and was high commissioner's agent at the final annexation of Basutoland to the Cape Colony; commandant of the frontier armed mounted police, 1870; commanded expedition to Hope Town and the Diamond Fields 1871; was one of the three commissioners for the administration of the government at the Diamond Fields, and for some time chief commissioner; commanded expedition to Tembuland, 1875; governor's agent, British Basutoland, 1877; retd. 1878 with hon. rank of colonel. J.P. for the Cape Colony; one of the commissioners for Natal for the Indian and Colonial Exhibition.

events.text_span: Analyst, chemist, medical department, Jamaica, June, 1893

raw_text: BOWREY, J. J., F.C.S., F.I.C., &c.—Analyst, chemist, medical department, Jamaica, June, 1893; is also curator of the Institute of Jamaica.

BOYCE, ERNEST PEDDER.—Supernumerary clerk, G.P.O., Barbados, June, 1878; notarial and colonial secretary's office, Apr., 1879; secretary to commissioners to inquire into the working of the G.P.O., 1879; chief clerk, public library, 1881; acting librarian, Sept., 1882; asst. clk., petty debts court of St. Michael's, Nov., 1882; acting chief clerk, May, 1883, to June, 1884; Inland revenue officer, 1885.

events.position: Collector and inspector of customs, Hobart
events.text_span: Collector and inspector of customs, Hobart, Tasmania, 1st Mar., 1883.

raw_text: BOYES, E. TH.—Collector and inspector of customs, Hobart, Tasmania, 1st Mar., 1883.

events.position: Civil commissioner and resident magistrate, Namaqualand division
events.text_span: Civil commissioner and resident magistrate, Namaqualand division, Cape of Good Hope, 1st Dec., 1860

raw_text: BOYES, LORENZO.—Civil commissioner and resident magistrate, Namaqualand division, Cape of Good Hope, 1st Dec., 1860; C.C. and L.D. Somerset, 1st July, 1878.

BOYES, R. C. R.—Civil commissioner and resident magistrate of Caledon division, Cape Colony. 1868. Was clerk to resident magistrate Albany, 1845 to 1850 clerk to the civil commissioner of Cradock, June, 1850; clerk to ditto, Dec., 1855. OYLE, CAVENDISH, C.M.G. (1889).—Educated Harterhouse; was assistant clerk of seats, principal registry, court of probate, 1869-74; district judge, Leeward Islands, Dec. 1879; magistrate and coroner, District E, and registrar-general, Dominica, Feb., 1880; member of legislative assembly, June, 1880; inspector of gaols and prisons, Nov., 1880; deputy-marshal, V.A. court, 1880; colonial secretary of Bermuda, member legislative and executive councils, and registrar-general, 1882; col. secy., Gibraltar, 1888; is also chairman of hospital board, and member of board of health, crown land board, and Gavino's sum trust, and chairman of sanitary commission; awarded vellum certificate by Royal Society, and received the special thanks of the Board of Trade, and certificate and medal from the Italian Government for services in connection with the wreck of the "Utopia" on Mar., 1891; colonial secy., Br. Guiana, 1894; act. Govt. 1894, and 1895-6.

RABANT, EDWARD YEWD, C.M.G (1879).—Member of the legislative assembly for East London, Cape of Good Hope; field commandant of Cape volunteers, and late of H.M.'s Cape Mounted Rifles. RACE, T. E. D.—Dispenser N. P. Asylum, Jamaica, Sep., 1882; clerk police office, Aug., 1883; 2nd clk. col. sec. off., Sep. 1887; has acted several occasions as chief clk. and clk. bd. of wks. RADBERY, F.—Joined the Straits service, 23 1864, as chief officer of the "Pluto;" 17 Mar., deputy master-attendant, Singapore; 29 Aug.,wright surveyor, member of the Pilot Board, registrar of boats; received thanks of Admiral for services in connection with attack onigor, 1871; harbour master, Penang, 1886; J.P. and mag. of Police.

ADBURY, JOHN SWANWICK, B.A.—Ed. Webster Schl. and Brasenose Coll., Oxon. Mar. 1891; Goldsmiths Co. Exhibn., 1892; Senr. Exhibn., 1894; 1st class. Class Mods., 1895; Miss Lit. Human., 1895; 1st class Modern Hist., 1896; B.A., 1895; Librarian, Oxford Univ. Society, 1895; 2nd class clk. Colonial Office, Oct., 1896.

ADDON, THE HON. SIR E. N. C., K.C.M.G. —Assistant commissioner, Santhal Pergunnahs, India, 1857; supdt. of excise, Oudh, in addition to this appointment, was inspector-general of registration and superintendent of statistics for several years, and during seven months secretary to financial commissioner; retired, Mar., 1877, and settled in Tasmania; elected M.P. for West Devon, 1879, and subsequently four times elected for same district; leader of opposition, 1886; minister of lands and forests and minister of education in Fysh administration, Mar., 1887; also member of the Federal Parliament of Australasia; agent-gen. for Tasmania, 1893; premier of Tasmania, 1894.
BRAIN, ROBERT S.—Govt. printer, Victoria, Aug., 1887.

BRAITHWAITE, H. W.—Petroleum locker and assistant excise locker, San Fernando, Trinidad, 1873; chief clerk, sub-treasury, 1874; suptdt. registrar of births, 1878, J.P.; elected municipal councillor, 1888.

BRAMSTON, JOHN, D.C.L., C.B. (1886).—Graduated B.A., at Balliol College, Oxford, 1854; elected fellow of All Souls', 1855; D.C.L., 1863; barrister, Middle Temple, 1857; went to Queensland, 1859, as private secretary to Sir George Bowen; resigned, 1861. Was member of the legislative council of Queensland, 1863 to 1869 and of the executive council, 1863 to 1866. While in England, in 1867, was assistant boundary commissioner for Devon and Cornwall, under the Reform Act of that year. Returned to Queensland, 1868, and became attorney-general, 3rd May, 1870; resigned, 31st Dec., 1873; represented the Burnett district in the legislative assembly of Queensland, April, 1871, to Dec., 1873; appointed attorney-general of Hong Kong, 1873; was ex-officio member of the legislative and executive councils; acted as judge of the supreme court of Hong Kong, Feb. to May, 1874; appointed an assist. under-secretary of state, Colonial Office, 30th June, 1876; employed on a mission to Berlin in connection with the Angra Pequena negotiations, July, 1886; registrar of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, Feb., 1892.
BRANCH, W. J., M.D.—Inspector of hospitals and medical officer, District No. 1. St. Kitts; is health officer, Basseterre, and M.L.C.

BRELAND, PASCAL.—Has been for many years member of the North West Council and of the Legislative Assembly of Manitoba. Is a half-breed, and exercises a great and useful influence in the N.W. Territories.

events.position: Entered the army as ensign in the 76th regiment
events.text_span: Entered the army as ensign in the 76th regiment, May, 1840

events.position: private secretary to Sir J. G. Le Marchant, governor of Malta
events.text_span: private secretary to Sir J. G. Le Marchant, governor of Malta, April, 1858

events.position: was also assistant military secretary to Sir J. G. Le Marchant, at Madras
events.text_span: was also assistant military secretary to Sir J. G. Le Marchant, at Madras, 1865 to 1886.

raw_text: BRETT, MAJOR SIR WILFORD, K.C.M.G. (1864).—Entered the army as ensign in the 76th regiment, May, 1840; lieutenant, Jan., 1843, and captain, June, 1855; private secretary to Sir J. G. Le Marchant, governor of Malta, April, 1858; was also assistant military secretary to Sir J. G. Le Marchant, at Madras, 1865 to 1886.

events.position: clerical asst. gov. off. G. Coast
events.text_span: clerical asst. gov. off. G. Coast, Apr., 1887

raw_text: BREW, SAM. H.—Ed. Wesleyan High School, Cape Coast; clerical asst. gov. off. G. Coast, Apr., 1887; junr. clk. Jan. 1891; 3rd clk. Jan., 1893.

events.text_span: ag. asst. regr.-gen., May, 1891 to Mar., 1894

raw_text: BREWIN, ARTHUR WIMBOLT.—Ed. at Winchester; cadet, Hong Kong, 1888; passed cadet, Dec. 1890; ag. asst. regr.-gen., May, 1891 to Mar., 1894; J.P., 1894; ag. asst. regr. gen., 1895.

events.position: state commissr. of lands, and regr. of mines
events.text_span: state commissr. of lands, and regr. of mines, Jan., 1892

raw_text: BREWSTER, EDWARD JOHN.—Educated at Brewood and Cheltenham; asst. dist. officer, Matang, Perak, 1878; asst. supert., Lower Perak, 1878; asst. mag., Krian, 1879; ditto, Salama, 1880; actg. dist. officer, Krian, 1883-4; ditto, Larut, 1885; ditto, Kuita, 1888; dist. officer, Krian, 1889; mag., Krian, 1889; state commissr. of lands, and regr. of mines, Jan., 1892; ag. state auditor, Apr., 1893; dist. mgr. Lower Perak, Aug., 1893.
BRIGGS, N. F., B.A.—Judge of petty debt court, Barbados.

BRIGHT, CHARLES E., C.M.G. (1883).—A leading merchant in Victoria; has been chairman of the harbour trust and commissioner for the Melbourne Exhibition; was also commissioner for Victoria at some of the exhibitions in Europe.

events.position: RIGHT REV. WILLIAM THOMAS THORNHILL WEBBER, D.D., consecrated
events.text_span: RIGHT REV. WILLIAM THOMAS THORNHILL WEBBER, D.D., consecrated 1885.

raw_text: BRISBANE, 3RD BISHOP (Australia), 1859.— RIGHT REV. WILLIAM THOMAS THORNHILL WEBBER, D.D., consecrated 1885.

BRISTOWE, LINDSAY W.—Clerk, surveyor-general's office, British Honduras, 1880; in treas. and cust. dept., 1883-1884; secretary Crown Lands Board, 1884; qualified land surveyor, clerk, registrar's and record office, clerk of the petty debt court, July, 1884; acted as clerk of the legislative council, 1885; as registrar V.A. Court, 1886; as provost marshal, 1886-7; as registrar and keeper of records 1887-8-9-90; dist. commissr. Toledo dist., 1892; Belize dist., 1893; notary and commissr. of the sup. ct.; editor Br. Honduras handbook, 1888-93; dist. commissr. G. Coast, 1893; assigned to treas., April, 1894; ag. chief asst. treasurer, Aug., 1894, to Jan., 1895; commsnr. Elmina, Oct., 1895, to July, 1896; commsnr. Accra, July, 1896; attached to secretariat, Accra, Aug., 1896.

events.position: clk. lower divn. H.M.'s office of works
events.text_span: clk. lower divn. H.M.'s office of works, Nov., 1884
dist. offr., Nebong Tebal, P.W., June, 1890

events.position: also ag. 2nd asst. prot. of immigrants
events.text_span: also ag. 2nd asst. prot. of immigrants, May, 1896.

raw_text: BROADRICK, Edwd. Geo.—Ed. at Sherborne Schl.; clk. lower divn. H.M.'s office of works, Nov., 1884; Cadet, S.S., 1887; passed Tamil final July, 1889; dist. offr., Nebong Tebal, P.W., June, 1890; passed in Malay, Jan. 1892; ag. 2nd mag. Penang, July, 1895; also ag. 2nd asst. prot. of immigrants, May, 1896.

events.position: ag. coll. of land rev., Penang
events.text_span: ag. coll. of land rev., Penang, May, 1889
ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Feb. to Oct., 1892
ag. coll. ld. rev., Penang, July, 1895
also ag. collr. ld. rev., Singapore, June, 1896.

raw_text: BROCKMAN, Edwd. Lewis.—Cadet, S. S., 1886; passed in Malay, 1888; actg. supt. of education, Penang, 1888; 3rd Mag. Penang, June, 1890; ag. coll. of land rev., Penang, May, 1889; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Feb. to Oct., 1892; dist. offr. Bukit Mertajam, Mar., 1892; ag. coll. ld. rev., Penang, July, 1895; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Nov., 1895; confirmed, June, 1896; also ag. collr. ld. rev., Singapore, June, 1896.

events.position: assistant to government agent N.W. Provinces
events.text_span: assistant to government agent N.W. Provinces, 1885

raw_text: BRODHURST, HENRY WM. FRED. COTTINGHAM.—Educated at Cheltenham College; writer, Ceylon service, 1877; police magistrate, Matale, 1880; assistant to government agent N.W. Provinces, 1885; ditto. Kegalla, 1891; asst. govt. agt., Kalutara, 1891.

BROOK, HERBERT ARTHUR.—Acting clerk police office, Nassau, May to Oct., 1874; librarian Nassau public library, reading room, and museum, Oct., 1875, to Oct., 1879; clerk police office Oct., 1879, acting chief clerk col. sec. dept. May to Nov., 1881; and Oct. 1882; acting private secretary to Governor, Mar. to April, 1883; chief clerk, col. sec. dept., June, 1883; and clerk, board of public works; registrar of records, Sept., 1887; suptdt., Bahamas census, 1891; is a J.P., and member of board of education.
BROOKE, HIS HIGHNESS SIR CHARLES JOHNSON, G.C.M.G. (1888).—Rajah of Sarawak.

BROOKING, J. S.—Entered the survey department, Western Australia, 1871; acting inspecting surveyor, 1874; confirmed, 1876; acted for surveyor-general and commissioner of Crown lands, 1881, 1884, 1885, and 1887; inspector of plans and surveys, 1885.

BROOKS, EDWARD PAKENHAM.—Cadet, R.N., Dec., 1862; sub-lieut., 1870; resigned, 1871; asst. district clerk, Grand Port, Mauritius, Feb., 1874; in July, 1874, special district and stipendiary magistrate to inquire into the treatment of the labourers employed in the lesser depots of the colony; Feb., 1876, corresponding assistant col. sec. office, Aug., 1879, joint district commissioner assistant officer of the civil Status Riviere du Nord; April, 1880, ditto Savanne; Dec., 1881, sec. pol. mag., &c., Falkland Islands; acting gov., July, 1889, to Feb., 1890; retired 21st Dec., 1891; treasurer, Grenada, June, 1892; administered government, Grenada, in Sept., 1892.

BROOKS, G. H.—Clerk of customs, Apr., 1868; warehousekeeper, Cape Coast Castle, Aug., 1870; served as issuer during Ashanti campaign, Oct. 1873 to 1874; removed with quarter's staff to Accra, April, 1877; transferred back to Cape Coast, Oct., 1881, as clerk and warehouse keeper.

events.text_span: General storekeeper for Government railways, Aug., 1879.

raw_text: BROOKS, J. M.—General storekeeper for Government railways, Aug., 1879.

events.position: clerk, executive council, and secretary defence committee
events.text_span: clerk, executive council, and secretary defence committee, 1878

events.position: acted several times assistant colonial secretary
events.text_span: acted several times assistant colonial secretary, 1886-88

raw_text: BROOME, WILLIAM.—Entered Natal civil service, 1875; clerk, executive council, and secretary defence committee, 1878; chief clerk, attorney general's office, 1879; advocate, supreme court, 1882; secretary, council of education, 1885; Newcastle division, 1889; acted several times assistant colonial secretary, 1886-88; J.P. for the colony (1889); member of council of education, 1891; master and registrar, supreme court.

events.position: Deputy master Her Majesty's Mint, chief officer of the Melbourne Branch
events.text_span: Deputy master Her Majesty's Mint, chief officer of the Melbourne Branch, 8th Nov., 1877

raw_text: BROUGHTON, V. DELVES.—Deputy master Her Majesty's Mint, chief officer of the Melbourne Branch, 8th Nov., 1877; was for many years clerk in the Treasury, Whitehall.
BROWN, C. CUTHBERT.—Acting chief clerk and book-keeper, treasury department, Gold Coast Colony, 1st March, 1876, confirmed 18th July, 1876.
BROWN, CHARLES GAGE, M.D., C.M.G.—Has been for many years medical adviser to the Colonial Office.

events.text_span: assistant master, Scotch college, Melbourne, Victoria, Apr. 1857
head master, national grammar school, Melbourne, Jan., 1858

events.position: organising master and inspector of national schools
events.text_span: organising master and inspector of national schools, May, 1859
organising inspector, board of education, Sept., 1862
clerk the executive council, Victoria, April, 1889

raw_text: BROWN, G. WILSON, M.A.—Educated Emmanuel College, Cambridge; scholar, B.A. (Math. Tripos), 1855; M.A., 1881; assistant master, Scotch college, Melbourne, Victoria, Apr. 1857; head master, national grammar school, Melbourne, Jan., 1858; organising master and inspector of national schools, May, 1859; organising inspector, board of education, Sept., 1862; inspector general, education department, Jan., 1873; secretary for public instruction, Mar., 1878; clerk the executive council, Victoria, April, 1889; retired 1893.
BROWN, H. V. L.—Government engineer South Australia, Dec., 1882.

events.position: resident engineer, western and midland systems of railway
events.text_span: resident engineer, western and midland systems of railway, 1884

raw_text: BROWN, JOHN.—M. Inst. C.E.; assistant engineer, Cape, 1873; maintenance engineer, western system, 1882; resident engineer, western and midland systems of railway, 1884; acting engineer-in-chief, June to Nov., 1888; chief engineer (open lines) Dec., 1890.

events.text_span: director-general of forests, N. S. W., 1890.

raw_text: BROWN, JOHN EDWD. J.P., F.L.S., F.R.G.S., F.R.H.S.—Son of the late Dr. Jas. Brown, late of "The Forester;" conservator of forests, Aust., 1878; director-general of forests, N. S. W., 1890.

BROWN, JOHN JAMES.—Ed. Liverpool Collegiate Institution and Royal Coll., Manchester; volunteer colonial secretary's office, Mar., 1863; 2nd clerk to senior stipendiary magistrate, Aug., 1863; clerk to the chief judge, 1864; curator of vacant estates, Oct., 1874; settlement agent before the Forest Lands Purchase Commission in conjunction with his office of clerk, Nov., 1881, till Dec., 1882, and alone from 1882, to Feb., 1884; is also marshal of the admiralty court since Feb., 1873; asst. receiver, Dec., 1888; ag. collr. of customs May to and Mar., 1892; to Nov., 1893; Jan. to July, 1894; and Aug., 1894; confirmed, Jan., 1895.
DOWN, MAITLAND.—Govt. resident, Geraldton, West Australia, 1883.

DOWN, THE HON. N. J.—Member, House of Assembly, Tasmania, 1875; minister of lands and works, Oct., 1877, to Dec., 1878, and Dec., 1882 to 1883; was joint representative to Federation Convention at Sydney, 1883; and member of the Federal Council of Australia.

DOWN, RICHARD MYLES.—Ed. Liverpool Institute, and Royal Coll., Mauritius (1st Queen's Scholarship); called to the bar, Middle Temple, June, 1869; counsel to government, Seychelles, 1872 to 1877; practised before supreme court, Mauritius, 1877 to 1884; member of commission on laws and rules affecting civil jurisdiction and procedure of district courts in Mauritius, April, 1882; district judge, Seychelles, Nov., 1884; judge of Seychelles and senior member executive and legislative councils, 1889; administered government, Seychelles, Nov., 1891, to Nov., 1892; member of the legislature, &c.

events.position: Entered civil service, New Zealand, as clerk to the bench
events.text_span: Entered civil service, New Zealand, as clerk to the bench, Mar., 1863

raw_text: DOWN, W. R. E.—Entered civil service, New Zealand, as clerk to the bench, Mar., 1863; state secretary to premier and secretary to premier; registrar-general, Nov., 1873; concurrent commissioner—clerk to district court, 1866-8; inspector of bankruptcy, 1886-7; curator of intestate estates, 1872; and registrar of friendly societies, 1878-84.

DOWNE, ALBERT.—Appointed, after a competitive examination, to be a clerk of the lower (now land) division of the civil service, and assigned to Colonial Office, 4th Sept., 1877, to 20th Oct., 1880; clerk in chief secretary's office, Cyprus, 21st Nov., 1880; returned to duty in Colonial Office, Jan., 1883; appointed to act for three years as assistant to the high commissioner for South Africa, April, 1891; acts also as auditor, Basutoland, from Oct., 1892; member of board of enquiry into postal and telegraph administration, Transvaal, April, 1892; ag. Imperial secy., to Mar., 1895; and (jointly with Capt.kins), Mar. to Aug., 1896; asst. to Imperial v. and accountant, Aug., 1895.
DOWNE, DODWELL F.—District judge, Ceylon, 1893.

events.position: Prof. of Eng. St. Mary's Coll., of Spain
events.text_span: Prof. of Eng. St. Mary's Coll., of Spain, Trinidad, Nov., 1864

raw_text: DOWNE, F. J.—Prof. of Eng. St. Mary's Coll., of Spain, Trinidad, Nov., 1864; Prof. of Eng. Math., 1867; Dean of Coll. 1870; presdt., 1876.

DOWNE, GEORGE.—Associate to the Judges of the Supreme Court of Tasmania, registrar of supreme court in bankruptcy, curator of intestate estates, and marshal of the vice-admiralty court, 1866; was clerk of the peace, deputy sheriff, registrar of the court of requests and of insolvency, Launceston, 1863; recorder of titles, acting registrar, supreme court, and collector of probate duties, 1875; was private secretary to administrators of the government, June, 1874-75; was acting state secretary to Governor Weld, 1876, 1877-79; was appointed private secretary, 1880; private secretary to governor, Straits, 1881, to Mar., 1884; to the administrator of Tasmania, April, 1884; acting private secretary to Sir G. C. Strahan, governor Tasmania, July, 1886, and to administrator Sir W. L. Dobson, 1886-7.

DOWNE, T. NEWTON.—Junior clerk in Colonial secretary's office, St. Vincent, 1866; clerk to colonial secretary, 1871; confidential clerk to governor, and clerk of councils and postmaster till 1881, when resigned latter office; chief clerk, governor's office, St. Lucia, 1882; acted as stipendiary magistrate of the first district of St. Lucia in 1883; inspector of police and inland revenue, officer, Tobago, 1884; stip. mag. for Tobago; inspr. of police and marshal of prison, 1892; clerk of the peace, Cty. Caroni, Trinidad, 1895; ag. S.J.P. Cty. Caroni, 1895; is a J.P. for Tobago.

BRUCE, SIR CHARLES, K.C.M.G. (1889), C.M.G. (1881).—Educated at Harrow; author of "Die Geschichte von Nala und Damayanti" (Sanskrit text, published by the Imperial Academy of St. Petersburg), 1862; poems, 1866, and other works; assistant librarian, British Museum, 1863, after competitive examination in modern languages and Sanskrit; professor of Sanskrit, King's College, 1865; rector Royal College, Mauritius, 1868; director of public instruction, Ceylon, 1878; colonial secretary, Mauritius, 1882; administered the government in 1883; lieut.-governor and government secretary, British Guiana, 1885; Governor, Windward Islands, 1893; Mauritius, 1897.

events.text_span: 3rd officer, customs, and ass. harbourmaster, St. Helena, Dec., 1878

raw_text: BRUCE, GEORGE C.—3rd officer, customs, and ass. harbourmaster, St. Helena, Dec., 1878; 2nd offr., harbourmaster, emigr. agt., 1890; harbour master, St. Lucia, 1891.

BRUMELL, JOHN.—Admitted to Middle Temple, Oct., 1872; called to the bar 7th June, 1875, practised his profession in British Guiana until 21st April, 1882, when he was appointed a stipendiary justice of the peace, stipendiary justice for New Amsterdam, and sheriff for the county of Berbice, 1883; acted on several occasions as public prosecutor, and on two occasions as judge of the inferior criminal court.

W., 1889; dist. offr. Dindings, June, 1890; ag. collr. of ld. rev. and offr. in charge of treas. Malacca, Mar., 1894; ag. 1st mag., Penang, Aug., 1895.

J.—Called to the bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1873; advocate of supreme court of Cape Colony, of high court of province of Griqualand West, and of vice-admiralty court, Cape Colony; elected member for Worcester in house of assembly, 1877; acting attorney-general of Griqualand West, Nov., 1879; appointed a puisne judge of the supreme court of the Cape Colony April, 1880; assigned to the court of the eastern district, May, 1880, and to supreme court, June, 1887.

Camb., B.A. (1888); 2nd class classical tripos, cadet; Hong Kong, 1890; passed cadet, Jan., 1893; ag. dep. supt. of police, July, 1892 to June, 1893; ag. asst. col. secy., June, 1893 to Feb. 1894; ag. clk. of councils ap., 1894; J.P., 1894; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of councils, Mar., 1895.

raw_text: BUDGE, ALEX. CAMPBELL, J.P.—Entered the civil service of N. S. Wales, Nov., 1858; clerk, executive council, Oct., 1863; secretary to the Australian conference for the last 15 years.

BURT, ALFRED EARLE. - Clerk to chief justice, Western Australia, June, 1871; clerk, col. secretary's office, Dec., 1873; to governor, in audit office, Jan., 1875; draughtsman, in survey department, 1875; and in Crown Land department, Sept., 1877; acting as registrar, 1878. titles, Jan., 1880, to Mar., 1881; registrar of deeds, June, 1890. URT. OCTAVUS.—Clerk in governor's office, Australia, May, 1872; Jan., 1874, clerk to executive council and private secretary to acting governor; April, 1874, to Dec., 1874, private secretary to Governor Weld; May, 1875, clerk to executive council and private secretary to Governor Wm. Robinson. K.C.M.G.; Sept., 1877, chief and keeper of records in survey office; resident magistrate, Newcastle, April, 1880; ditto April, 1887; asst. col. sec., April, 1887; col. sec., 1890, with seat in exec. and leg. councils, under sec. for W. Australia under responsible govt., 1891. RT. THE HON. SEPTIMUS, Q.C.—Member of legislative council of Western Australia from 1890, when responsible government was instituted; acting attorney-general and member of executive council from March, 1886, to Sept. when he resigned; delegate to colonial conference in London, 1887; vice-consul for Denmark; first attorney-genl. under responsible government 18th Dec., 1890; appointed on special mission to London to open office of agent general Western Australia, and to act as agent-general from April to Oct., 1891.

SHE, G. F.—Fourth clerk in colonial secretary's office, Trinidad, Oct., 1873, third clerk, Jan., 1875; second clerk, 1877; private secretary to the administrator, May to Oct., 1874; private secretary lieutenant-Governor Des Vœux, Feb., 1877, to 1878; assistant to the sub-intendant and commissioner, northern province, has acted on several occasions as commissioner and sub-intendant.

events.position: was 27th wrangler in the mathematical tripos

raw_text: SHE, ROBERT GERVASE.—Educated at King's College, Cambridge, where he obtained an exhibition entrance; elected to a foundation scholarship, 1874; was 27th wrangler in the mathematical tripos, 1875; graduated B.A., 1875; 2nd master in's Royal College, Trinidad, 1878; inspector of schools, 1890.

SHE, I. J. SCOTT.—Assoc. M.I.C.E., Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad, Malvern Coll., Lancing Coll.; articled pupil to J. E.ner, M.I.C.E., 1879-82, ag. and special draftsman, P. W. Dept., Trinidad, various occasions, 1886; draftsman light railway Feb., 1880; surveyor of loan wks., Grenada, Jan., 1899; director, pub. wks., Mar., 1891; engaged on works, Trinidad, 1893; and in charge of river division, P.W.D., 1894-6; draftsman, 1897.

Kuala Kangsar, Jan., 1887; 1st asst. collr. mag., Kinta, Jan., 1888; ag. state commsr. lands, Nov., 1888; collr. and mag., Kuala Kangsar, June, 1889; commsr. of lands, Aug., 1890; ag. state Audr., Sept., 1895.
TLER, CAPT. P. J. H.—Late 3rd Bat., E. es. Regt.; entered Natal Civil Service, Jan., 1881, as looker and tide waiter, Customs dept.; lieut.-at-arms, House of Assembly, Natal, Nov., 1883.

raw_text: TLER, CAPT. VERE ALBAN.—Late R.M.L.I., 7th Roy. Lanc. Militia; 2nd class inspr. of police, Mauritius, June, 1880; pol. officer, with mag. powers, Diego Garcia, Sept., 1885; reverted to her post on abolition of latter, Mar., 1889; ag. class inspr. of police, Jan., 1892; inspr., t., 1894.

UTTERFIELD, AUBREY G.—Clerk in British Consulate, New York, April, 1862; paid viceconsul, Key West, Florida, October, 1862 to 1868 on as unpaid vice-consul to 1871; treasury clerk Bermuda, 1871; colonial postmaster, 1880.

events.text_span: Govt. storekeeper, Br. N. Guinea, Oct., 1890

raw_text: BUTTERWORTH, ARCHIBALD WILLIAM.—Govt. storekeeper, Br. N. Guinea, Oct., 1890; head-qrs. officer, armed constabulary, Feb., 1891; also port and harbour master, and native mag.

BUXTON, SYDNEY CHARLES, M.P.—Born Oct., 1853; educated at Clifton College and Trinity College, Cambridge; was a member of the London school board from 1876 to 1882; honorary secretary to Mr. Tuke's Irish emigration fund, 1882-4; author of the "Handbook to Political Question," the "Political Manual," "Finance and Politics," "An Historical Study, 1783-1883," &c., and editor of the Imperial Parliament Series; M.P. for Peterborough from June, 1883, to Nov., 1885, when he was unsuccessful; contested Croydon unsuccessfully in Jan., 1886; elected July, 1886, and re-elected in 1892, for Poplar (Tower Hamlets), for which he still sits; parliamentary under-secretary of state for the colonies, 17th Aug., 1892, to 28th June, 1895.

events.position: Lieut.-Col., 2nd Tower Hamlets Rifle Volr. Brigade
events.text_span: Lieut.-Col., 2nd Tower Hamlets Rifle Volr. Brigade, 1864

raw_text: BUXTON, SIR THOMAS FOWELL, BART., K.C.M.G. (1895).—Born 1837; 3rd Bart.; M.A., Camb.; Dep. Lieut. and J.P. for Essex and Norfolk; Sheriff, 1875; M.P. for King's Lynn, 1865-68; Lieut.-Col., 2nd Tower Hamlets Rifle Volr. Brigade, 1864; Hon. Col. since 1884; Governor of South Australia, 1895.
BYNOE, A. D.—Clerk, G.P.O., Barbados, 1874; revenue officer, Jan., 1879; landing waiter, May, 1879; 2nd clerk, customs, June, 1887; chief clerk, customs, Dec., 1890.

events.text_span: govt. agent, N. Central Prov., Dec., 1895.

raw_text: BYRDE, MACERLEY DURAND EVAN.—Educated at Queen Elizabeth's college, Guernsey; writer, Ceylon Civil Service, 1867; police magistrate, Kalpitiya, Dec., 1871; acting landing surveyor, Galle, April, 1873; acting commissioner of requests, Colombo, 1874; police magistrate, Colombo, 1876; district judge, Matara, 1877; assistant adjutant, &c., Mannar, Jan., 1885; district judge, Negombo, Jan., 1886; fiscal, cent. prov., 1891; dist. judge, Batticaloa, Dec., 1891; govt. agent, N. Central Prov., Dec., 1895.

BYRNE, A.—Governor's clerk, Natal, Mar., 1878; June, acted sec., secret defence committee; July, acting clerk, executive council; during 1880-81 was private sec. to the various officers administering the government; Feb. 9, 1881, private sec. to Sir G. Pomeroy-Colley, and on his personal staff during Transvaal war, Mar., 1882; clerk, executive council; 1885 to 1886, private secretary to Sir C. B. H. Mitchell, administrator, and priv. sec. to gov. Natal, Feb., 1890; accompanied governor to England when he went to discuss affairs of Natal with S. of S., Jan., 1893; priv. sec. admr., July, 1893; ag. priv. sec. to gov., July, 1896.

BYRNE, J.—Clerk in the audit office, Natal, 1859; clerk in treasury, 1863; first clerk and warehouse-keeper Customs department, 1873; asst. collr. of Customs and landing surveyor, 1889; collr. of customs; regr. of shipping and emigr. officer, 1894. J.P. for the colony, 1894.
BYRNE, M.—Secretary to law department, Victoria, Oct., 1893. Formerly chief clerk in same department.

CADDY, DUNCAN JAMES, M.D., F.R.G.S., M.B.—Govt. medl. offr., Carriacon, 1892; ed., Merchant Taylors' and King's Coll, Lond. and Dub. Univ.; member Pathol. Soc. Lond., British Med. Assocn., and other learned societies; has contributed papers on medical subjects to the Lancet, Br. Med. Journal and other journals. CADE. CHARLES SHERWOOD.—Captain in the West Cork Artillery Militia; served through the Zulu War. 1878-9; present at the battle of Ulundi; assistant-inspector, Gold Coast Constabulary, Mar., 1880; inspector, 11th Dec., 1882; adjutant and musketry instructor of the Gold Coast Constabulary, Sept., 1882, to May, 1883; local commandant of police, Cyprus, 29th Sept., 1883; also assistant to commissioner, Nicosia, and governor, central prison, Oct., 1884; has acted several times as commissioner.

CADOGAN, 5TH EARL (Great Britain), George Henry Cadogan, eldest son of the 4th Earl; born, 1840; succeeded his father in 1873; was M.P. for Bath for a few months previous to his succession to the title; Parliamentary Under Secretary for War, May, 1875, to 2nd March, 1878; Parliamentary Under Secretary for the Colonies, 2nd March, 1878, to 28th April, 1880; Lord Privy Seal in Lord Salisbury's 1st and 2nd ministries; Lord Lieut. of Ireland, 28 June, 1895. CALLCOTT. JOHN HOPE—Sup. of works and surveys, S.S., 1883; has four times acted as Dep. Col. Engineer and Surveyor-General, Penang. Is a J.P. CALDER. CHARLES MACLEAR.—Admitted solr., sup. ct., Jamaica, 1881; cl. of cts., par. of Portland, Apl., 1888; ditto, par. of St. Mary's, Feb., 1889. CALDER. WILLIAM McCAUL.—Magistrate, Berbice River, Br. Guiana, 1880; stip. mag., 1886.

events.position: Clerk, col. sec's. office, N.S. Wales
events.text_span: Clerk, col. sec's. office, N.S. Wales, 1853

events.position: clerk of Select Committees, legislative assembly
events.text_span: clerk of Select Committees, legislative assembly, May, 1856

raw_text: CALVERT, JOHN J.—Clerk, col. sec's. office, N.S. Wales, 1853; clerk of Select Committees, legislative assembly, May, 1856; first clerk legislative council, April, 1859; clerk assistant, Jan., 1860; clerk of the Parliaments, April, 1871.

CAMERON, MAJ.-GEN. DONALD RODERICK, C.M.G. (1877).—Entered R.A. 1856; capt. 1866, maj. 1875, lieut.-col. 1882, col. 1886, retired 1888; instr. in gunnery, 1859-62, 15th Bde. R.A.; served throughout Bhootan campaign, 1864-66, as adjutant and as staff officer of officer commanding R.A. Dooar Field Force (medal with clasp, thrice mentioned in despatches); adjutant 1st Bde. R.A. 1867-8; accompanied the Hon. W. McDougall, C.B., to Fort Garry, as a member of the executive council, N.W. Territories, 1869; was commissioner international boundary commission, and superintended the expedition which marked the international boundary from the Lake of the Woods to the summit of the Rocky Mountains, 1872-6; reported on the British Alaskan boundary, 1874; secretary to Canadian delegation at the Paris international conference, 1883, for the protection of submarine cables; reported on the eastern boundary of British Columbia, 1884; and again on the Alaskan boundary in 1886; private secretary to Sir C. Tupper, Canadian plenipotentiary to the fishery confer., 1887; principal, Royal Military College, Kingston, Canada, 1888. Retired 1896.

events.text_span: Clerical asst. Inland rev. B. Guiana, 1890

raw_text: CAMERON, DONALD CHARLES.—Clerical asst. Inland rev. B. Guiana, 1890; 5th cl. clk. secretariat, 1891; 4th ditto, 1893; sec. to bd. of enquiry shipping casualties, Ordce, 1893; to special commissioner to convict settlt., 1893; to spirits comsn., 1894; minor industries comtee., 1895; and to select comtee. of legislature on railway tariff; 3rd class and despatch clk., 1895; sec. special committee on loans to planters on sugar crops, 1895; priv. sec. to ag. governor, Oct. to Nov., 1896.

CAMERON, EDWARD J.—Ed. at Shrewsbury Schl., Clifton Coll., and at Merton Coll., Oxon.; private secretary to Sir C. C. Lees, governor, Bahamas, Jan., 1882; and as governor of the Leeward Islands, Jan., 1884; assistant colonial sec. and treasurer, Sierra Leone, Nov., 1881; J.P. for the Settlement, and visiting justice, Freetown Gaol; acting dist. sec. and treas., Dec., 1885, to July, 1886; president, Virgin Islands, Feb., 1887; member Leeward Islands Leg. Coun., 1887, and Ex. Coun., 1888; commissioner, Turks Islands, March, 1890; ass. judge sup. ct., May to Dec., 1893, and Jan. to June, 1895.

raw_text: CAMERON, H. H.—Educated at Charterhouse and Univ. Coll., Oxf.; writer Ceylon civil service, 1868; commissioner of requests, &c., Hampden; acting assistant government agent, Kandy, 1871; confirmed April, 1872; acting assistant government agent, &c., Nuwarakalawiya, Nov., 1872; acting second assistant colonial secretary, 1873; private secretary to the governor, 1874; fiscal, central province, 1875; police magistrate, Colombo, 1875; assistant government agent, Anuradhapura, 1876; ditto, Nuwara Eliya, 1879; acting agent, Badulla, 1881; and acting government agent, western province, 1886; chairman municipal council and mayor Colombo, Nov., 1887; ass. govt. agt., Puttalam, 1893; govt. agt. N. Central Prov., 1894; dist. judge, Jaffna, 1895.

CAMERON, MAJOR MAURICE ALEXANDER R.E.—First commission in Royal Engineers, Aug., 1874; deputy colonial engineer, West African Settlements, Dec., 1883, to April, 1892; during which acted for about two years and a half as colonial engineer and surveyor-general, and was a member of executive and legislative councils; appointed crown agent for the colonies, 18th July, 1895.
CAMERON, SIR RODERICK WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1883).—Commissioner for Canada to the Australian International Exhibitions.
CAMPBELL, CAPT. ARCHIBALD DAVID.—Ireland; S. Leone police, Mar., 1891, wounded at action, severely; ag. inspr.-gen., Sept., 1896.

events.text_span: R.M., Hopetown District, Cape Colony, Dec., 1882

raw_text: CAMPBELL, C. C.—R.M., Hopetown District, Cape Colony, Dec., 1882; ditto, Colesberg, 1884.
CAMPBELL, ERNEST D.—Sub-inspector constabulary, British Honduras, Dec., 1886.

CAMPBELL, SIR GEORGE W. R., K.C.M.G. (1891), C.M.G. (1887)—Ensign, Argyll and Sutherland Rifles, Jan., 1855; lieutenant same year, and assistant-superintendent, Bombay revenue police, 1856; on the outbreak of the mutiny in 1857, appointed adjutant of the Ahmedabad Field Corps, assistant superintendent of police, and assistant magistrate (has mutiny medal), superintendent of police, and magistrate of the 1st class and commandant of the Rutnagherry district, Dec., 1859; promoted in 1863, and selected to reorganize the Canara police force; in 1864 command of the Belgaum police. Received numerous commendations from the Bombay Government and from the governors-general and secretaries of state for India for special services, and received two special donations of 3,500 rupees and 7,500 rupees for special service. Passed examinations Hindostanee, Goojerathi, Marathi, and Gujarati. In 1866 was selected to take police charge of Ceylon and to reorganise its constabulary, received special gratuity of 1,000l. for this service. In 1868 confirmed as inspector-general; ag. lieut. gov. of Penang, 1872-3. Cholera commissioner to Java in 1866, and again in 1877; inspr.-gen. of police from 1st Jan., 1885; was in charge of Egyptian exiles. Retired 1891.

events.text_span: Second clerk in the land registry office, Natal, April, 1861

raw_text: CAMPBELL, H. C.—Second clerk in the land registry office, Natal, April, 1861; second in audit office, January, 1863; first clerk, High Court, county of Pietermaritzburg, Mar., 1865; R.M., division of Ixopo, Mar., 1876; act. R.M. division of Umpeni, Nov., 1876; R.M., Ixopo, 1878; for colony, 1880; master and registrar, sup. rt. 1882; colonial auditor, April, 1889; has acted judge native high court, and as 2nd puisne judge and atty. general; auditor general, 1894; man civil service board.

CAMPBELL, JOHN WILLIAM. — Appointed bl. 1852, to the customs at Hobart, Tasmania, ctor of customs, Launceston, registrar of ship- g. and warden, marine board; is a magistrate a commissioner of the supreme court.

events.position: lieut. and staff adjt. of volunteers
events.text_span: lieut. and staff adjt. of volunteers, 1886.

raw_text: CAMPBELL, JOSEPH ALEXANDER. — Late ser- nt-major. Cameron Highlanders; served in the an campaigns 1858-9, and in Egyptian cam- p. 1882; medal for Indian service and medal clasp for Tel-el-Kebir and Khedive's star; bis for long service and for distinguished act; drill instructor, Western Australia, l; lieut. and staff adjt. of volunteers, 1886.
CAMPBELL, WILLIAM BENJAMIN. — In postal, rev. and harbour depts., Sierra Leone, since l; chief clerk, survey dept., 1879.

events.position: THE RIGHT REV. JAM WEST JONES, D.D. — Consecrated

raw_text: APE TOWN, 2nd Bishop. — THE RIGHT REV. JAM WEST JONES, D.D. — Consecrated, 1874; roplitan of the church of the province of th Africa; formerly fellow of St. John's L. Oxon, preacher at Chapel Royal, Whitehall, rural dean of Oxford.

APPER, ALFRED HOUSTON. — Ed. Royal col. Armagh, and Trin. Coll., Dub., scholar- in classics; B.A. in 1883; cadet, S. S., Nov., l; J.P. and magistrate, May, 1884; passed l examination in Chinese, and attached to best councillor's office, Penang, 1886; 2nd st. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, 1888; district sr. 1889; ag. sen. dist. off., Butterworth, P. W., , 1889; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., April, 1890; protector of Chinese, Singapore, 1893; ag.. Mag. Penang, Sep. 1893; ag. 2nd asst. col. Sept., 1894; ag. asst. col. sec., Nov., 1894, to fl. 1895; asst. protr. of Chinese, Penang, April, l.
APPER, THOMAS. — Ed. Trin. Coll., Camb.; c. (Lond.), 1876; B.A., Camb. (Math. and Nat. Triposes), 1879; inspector of schools, Jamaica, , 1880; official member of leg. coun., 1882.
ARRERY, JOSEPH, M.B.C.M. — Assistant colo- surgeon, Ceylon, 1867.

ARBONE, SIR JOSEPH, L.L.D. (1862), LG. (1887), K.C.M.G. (1891). — Received at the ha bar, 1863; reporter, court of commerce, 1868 advocate and govt. legal adviser, 1880; member be executive and legislative councils ex officio ; of justice, Malta, 1895.
ARDEW, COL. FRED., C.M.G. (1894). — vernor S. Leone, 1894; formerly residentistrate in Zululand.

events.position: Special agent to interior tribes Vitu Levu
events.text_span: Special agent to interior tribes Vitu Levu, Fiji, Nov., 1874

events.position: land titles commissr. and after native regulations board
events.text_span: land titles commissr. and after native regulations board, 1877

raw_text: AREW, W. S. — Special agent to interior tribes Vitu Levu, Fiji, Nov., 1874; stipendiaryistrate, 1875; land titles commissr. and after native regulations board, 1877; native boundary commissr., Nov., 1880; member coun., Sept., 1882; is now rest. commr., Colo n. and stipendiary magistrate, Rewa.

ARLILE, EDWARD. gold medallist in law, Melbourne Univ., 1868. — Clerk, census office, Victoria, 20th May, 1861; registrar general's be. 1st Jan., 1862; crown law offices, 1st Mar., 65; called to the bar, Victoria, 1871; parliament- and professional assistant to crown law officers 73; parliamentary draftsman, July, 1879; clerk- assistant, legislative assembly, April, 1882; re- pointed parliamentary draftsman with higher ans, April, 1889; author of "Notes on Victoria Institution," "Victorian Year Book, 1883-4."

events.position: elected to Dominion house of commons on confederation
events.text_span: elected to Dominion house of commons on confederation, 1867
minister of works and agriculture, Ontario, 1867-71

events.position: sworn of the privy council and made postmaster-general in Sir J. MacDonald's government
events.text_span: sworn of the privy council and made postmaster-general in Sir J. MacDonald's government, 1882

raw_text: CARLING, THE HON. SIR JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1893). — Born 1828; member for London, Ontario, 1857-74; elected to Dominion house of commons on confederation, 1867; receiver-general of Canada, 1862; minister of works and agriculture, Ontario, 1867-71; again elected for London, 1878; sworn of the privy council and made postmaster-general in Sir J. MacDonald's government, 1882; minister of agriculture, 1885 to 1892; member of cabinet without portfolio, 1892; retired, 1894.

CARLINGFORD, BARON (United Kingdom) 1874), CHICHESTER PARKINSON FORTESCUE. — 1st baron, P.C. (England 1864, Ireland 1865). — Educated at Christ Church, Oxford, first class in classics in 1844, and obtained the chancellor's prize for the English essay in 1846; was a lord of the Treasury from Jan., 1854, to April, 1855; under secretary of state for the colonies, May, 1857, to Feb., 1858, and re-appointed June, 1859. M.P. for eo. Louth 1847 to 1874. Privy councillor, 1864; chief secretary for Ireland, Nov., 1865, and again 9th Dec., 1868; president of Board of Trade, Dec., 1870, to Feb., 1874; lord privy seal, 1881; lord president of the council, 1883, to June, 1885.

CARMODY, P. — Government analyst, Trinidad, and professor of chemistry, Queen's Roy. Coll., and Coll. of the Immaculate Conception, June, 1890; member central agricultural bd., 1890; member bd. of health, 1891; entered imperial service after open competition, 1876; inland rev. laboratory scholarship, 1880; diploma (1st class) Roy. Schl. of Mines; asst. Analyst and subsequently analyst, Somerset House, 1881-90. Is F.I.C., F.C.S., member of soc. of chem. industry, and author of prize paper on "Recent Progress of Invention and Science," and of other published papers on scientific subjects.
CARON, L. B. — Puisne judge, Superior Court, Quebec, 1874.

CARON, THE HON. SIR J. P. R. A., K.C.M.G. (1885), Q.C. — Born 1843; called to the bar of Lower Canada (now Province of Quebec) 1865; created a Queen's counsel, 1876; entered the Canadian House of Commons, 1873, where he still sits: minister of militia and defence for the Dominion of Canada, 8th Nov., 1880; postmaster-general, 1892; one of representatives of Canada at Colonial conference, Ottawa, 1894.

CARR, CAPTAIN GEORGE LYON, R.N. — Superintendent of ports, Malta, 1880.

raw_text: CARR, MARK WILLIAM, F.R. Inst., C.E. — Born 1847; Ed. King's Coll., London; employed on Indian Rlws., 1866-76; dist. eng., Natal Rlys., Aug., 1876; resident eng., April, 1882; chief redt. eng. constrn. and maintenance, Aug., 1888; eng.-in-chief of railways, Sept. 1889.

CARRINGTON, EARL (created 1895), CHARLES ROBERT CARRINGTON (THIRD BARON), P.C., born 1842; G.C.M.G. — joint hereditary Lord Great Chamberlain of England; late captain Royal Horse Guards, lieut.-colonel 3rd battalion, Oxford Light Infantry; captain Queen's Body Guard, 1881; governor of N. S. Wales, 1885-90; Lord Chamberlain, 1892.

CARRINGTON, MAJOR-GEN. SIR FREDERICK. K.C.M.G. (1887), C.M.G. (1880).—Ed. Cheltenham Coll.; 24th Regt.: commander of the "Frontier Light Horse" in the Transkei War, South Africa, 1877-78; commanded Transvaal volunteers against Sikukuni, 1878-79; commanded left wing C. M. R., 1879 to 1883; commanded colonial forces in Basuto war, severely wounded; commanded 2nd Mounted Rifles, Bechuanaland Field Force, 1884; commandant of police, British Bechuanaland, 1885; commandt. native forces, Zululand, in 1888; again in command Bechuanaland Police, Dec., 1888-93; and again on special service, Nov., 1893, in connection with Matabele war; commanding brigade at Gibraltar, 1895; commanded forces in Matabele war, 1896.

CARRINGTON, JOHN WORRELL. Q.C., F.R.G.S., C.M.G. (1888). D.C.L., LL.D.—Ed. Codrington Coll., Barbados (S.C. C., 1866), and Linc. Coll., Oxon (B.A., 1872; M.A., 1891); called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, June, 1872; acted as judge, assistant court of appeal, Barbados, 1874-75; J.P., 1874; member house of assembly, 1874-8, and again 1881; M.L.C., 1878-81; solicitor-general, 1878; again acted as judge, assistant court of appeal, 1879; and as attorney-general, 1880; Hon. D.C.L., Durham, 1879; Hon. LL.D., St. Andrews, 1894. Was presdt. of the education board, and of the board of health; and received the thanks of govt. for services in connection with education; chief justice, St. Lucia and Tobago, 1882; on special duty as ag. col. sec., Grenada, 1882; administered govt. of Tobago, 1883 to 1885; prepared revised edition, Tobago laws; acting chief justice of Grenada, 1886; in 1887-9 prepared a new edition of the Laws of St. Lucia; att.-gen. Br. Guiana, Dec., 1888, chancellor of the diocese, 1890; engaged, 1891-2, in drafting for the govt. of B. Guiana bills relating to the courts, criminal law and procedure, &c., of that colony; chairman central board of health, 1889, of the opening-up-the-country commission, 1891-2, and of the spirits ordinance commission, 1893-4; major in the Br. Guiana militia, 1893. In 1894 prepared a new and revised ed. of the laws of Br. Guiana; chief justice, Hong Kong, 1896.
CARRINGTON, J. A.—Sub-inspector of schools. Barbados, Jan., 1879; inspected schools, St. Lucia, July, 1885.

CARTER, THE HON. SIR F. B. T., K.C.M.G. (1878).—Barrister, Newfoundland, 1842; Q.C., 1859; member house of assembly, 1855 to 1878; speaker, 1861 to 1865; premier and attor.-gen., 1865 to 1870, and from Feb., 1874, to April, 1878; delegate to Quebec, 1864, in arranging terms for establishing the dominion of Canada; again at Ottawa, 1869, for the admission of Newfoundland into the dominion; and several delegations respecting treaty rights of the trade on the Newfoundland coast; chief justice, 1878; administrator by Royal Sign Manual, 30th Sept., 1878, and on several occasions since during the absence of and vacancies in office of governor.

CARTER, SIR GILBERT T., K.C.M.G. (1893); C.M.G. (1890).—Entered Navy 1864; paymaster of Colonial steamer "Sherbro," Sierra Leone, Aug., 1870; served on the Gold Coast during a part of the Ashanti campaign; was one of the commissioners for valuing the ordnance and stores left behind by the Dutch at the transfer of Elmina; private secretary to governor Leeward Islands, Sept., 1875; collector of customs and treasurer, Gold Coast, Aug., 1879; treasurer and postmaster, Gambia, Oct., 1882; administered the government several times; administrator of Gambia on separation from S. Leone, Nov., 1890; governor, Lagos, 1890, retired, 1896.

CARTWRIGHT, CAPT. J. T.—Liut. in army, 1856; capt., 1862; sold out 1868; agent of immigration, Jamaica, 1872; stipendiary magistrate, 1874; dt. British Guiana, 1888.

CARTWRIGHT, THE HON. SIR RICHARD JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1879).—Born 1835; entered Canadian Parliament, 1863; sworn of the privy council, and was minister of finance, 7th Nov., 1873, until the resignation of the Macdonald cabinet, Oct., 1878; Minister of Trade and Commerce in Mr. Laurier's govt., June, 1896.

Is Knight Officer of the Legion of Honour and of the Crown of Italy.

CATT, THE HON. A.—Commissioner of crown lands, South Australia, 24th June, 1881, to June 11, 1884; commissioner of public works June 11, 1884; chairman of committees, leg. ass., 1890.
CAVE-BROWNE-CAVE, BERNARD.—Served 39th regiment, 1882-1890; lieut. 99th regiment, 1890; inspr. S. Leole frontier police, 1895; J.P. colony.

ELESTIN, LOUIS ALBERT.—Ed. govt. normal school, Mauritius; teacher in Edn. Dept., 1874; ag. pr. of schls., Nov., 1886, to Aug., 1889; dist. iter Dec., 1889, to Nov., 1893; chief insp. of mills, Nov., 1893; ag. supt. of distilleries, 1894 to Oct., 1895.

HADWICK, JOHN COURTENAY CHASMAN.—Rank and interpreter to the R.M., Inanda division, al, Jan., 1868; clerk of the court Oct., 1870; and as R.M. and administrator of native law on bus occasions, 1872-7, at Inanda; J.P. for Inanda division, Aug., 1875; registrar of native high court, and sec. to native administration board Nov., 1876; admitted an attorney of supreme court, 1878; first class interpreter on staff of Maj.-Gen. Newdigate during Zulu War 1879 (medal and clasp); administrator of native and border agent, Upper Tugela, 1880; R.M., as River division, and J.P. for the colony, 1882; L. and admtr. of native law, Ixopo, Sept., 1883.

raw_text: HADWICK, JOHN.—Assistant-superintendent instrument room, telegraph department, Cape Town, March, 1879; superintendent of telegraphs, Jan., Natal, July, 1879; general sub-manager telegraphs, Natal, 1880; postmaster-general, 1883; postmaster-general and general manager telegraphs, Jan., 1884.

HALMERS, SIR DAVID PATRICK, KNT. BACH. (E.D.).—Member of the Scottish Faculty of Advocates, and called to the bar, 1860; appointed advocate of the Gambia, 1867; magistrate of the Gold Coast and judicial assessor to the native courts, 1869; queen's advocate of Sierra Leone, 1872; queen's advocate, Gold Coast, 1874; prepared codes of civil and criminal procedure, measures for the abolition of slavery, and other important measures necessary on the foundation of Gold Coast Colony; in 1876, knighted, in recognition of his services on the West Coast of Africa; chief justice, Gold Coast Colony, 1876; chief justice, Br. Guiana, 1878; mem. of commiss. to enquire into charges against the attorney-general of Jamaica, 1893; retired 1894.

raw_text: HALMERS, WILLIAM.—Ag. asst. cr. surveyor revenue officer in charge of rivers and creeks, Demerara, Nov., 1866; asst. cr. surveyor, cty. time, Dec., 1866; junior asst. cr. surveyor, May, 1873; senior do., Dec., 1877; asst. cr. surveyor, Dec., 1886; cr. surveyor, Feb., 1888.

CHAMBERLAIN, THE R. HON. JOSEPH, P.C., P. Hon. LL.D. (Camb.).—Eldest son of the late Mr. Joseph Chamberlain; born in London, 1836, and educated at University College School. after which he was engaged in business in Birmingham till 1874, when he retired. In 1870 he was elected a member of the Birmingham town council, and in the same year a member of the school board, of which he became chairman in 1873. In each of the three following years, 1874-5-6, he was elected mayor of the borough, and in June, 1876, on the retirement of Mr. Dixon, one of the three representatives of Birmingham, he was returned to Parliament. At the general election of 1880 he was again returned with the late Mr. John Bright and Mr. Philip Muntz as his colleagues, and on the formation of Mr. Gladstone's administration he was appointed President of the Board of Trade with a seat in the cabinet. While at the Board of Trade he prepared and succeeded in passing through parliament the bankruptcy act which is now in force, the patents act of 1883, and other measures. After the redistribution of seats in 1885 he was returned as member for West Birmingham, and joined Mr. Gladstone's third administration in 1886, as president of the local government board, but resigned in consequence of his disagreement with the premier's home rule policy. At the general election of 1886 he was returned unopposed for West Birmingham, and at the end of 1887 proceeded to the United States as plenipotentiary to negotiate, in conjunction with H.M.'s minister there and Sir Charles Tupper, a treaty for the settlement of the Atlantic fisheries question. The result of these negotiations was the convention of 1888, and though it was rejected by the U.S. senate, the modus verendi established by it still regulates the fisheries. On the defeat of Lord Rosebery's government in June, 1895, Mr. Chamberlain joined Lord Salisbury's government as secretary of state for the colonies. At the general election of 1895, in which he took a very prominent part, he was again returned for West Birmingham.

CHAMBERLAYNE, MAJOR TANKERVILLE JAMES.—Ed. Cheltenham Coll.; Ensign, Ceylon Rifles, June, 1868; transferred to 80th regt., Oct., 1873; fort adjutant, Hong Kong, June, 1875, to Mar., 1877; acting-brigade-major, Nov., 1875, to Feb., 1876; officiated twice as assistant military secretary, China command; served in Transvaal and throughout Zulu war, 1878-79; present at Ulundi (medal and clasp, 1878-79); A.D.C. to Governor of Natal (Sir H. Bulwer, G.C.M.G.), 3rd Feb., 1882; private secretary, Mar., 1882; and also acting clerk executive council, July, 1885; private secretary to high commissioner of Cyprus, and clerk, executive council, Mar., 1886; local comdt. of police, 1892; col. of customs and excise and asst. to commr. Larnaca, 1894; ag. commr., Larnaca, June, 1895.

CHAMBERLIN, BROWN, LT.-COL., C.M.G. (1870).—Member House of Commons of Canada for county of Missisquoi, 1867 to 1870; in command 60th Bat. Canadian Active Militia, 1869 to 1871; in 1870 commanded battalion, and for a time the whole force, engaged in repelling Fenian invasion at Eccles Hill, for which created C.M.G.; appointed Queen's printer, and retired from Parliament, 1870; Queen's printer and controller of stationery, with rank as Deputy Minister, July, 1886.
CHAMBERS, C. PEARSON, A.I.E.E.—Supt. telephones, Leeward Islands (Antigua), 1889.

raw_text: CHAMBERS, JOHN RATCLIFFE—2nd clerk to president St. Kitts, May, 1875; 3rd landing waiter, treasury, Dec., 1878; 1st clerk to registrar and provost marshal, April, 1881; secretary of the commission to enquire into the general condition of Dominica, Nov., 1881; private secretary to governor, Leeward Islands, 1882 to 1883; clerk to magistrate, district C, St. Kitts, July, 1883; deputy coroner May, 1885; quartermaster with St. Kitts' Militia, June, 1885.

CHANCELLOR, W. G.—Second officer of customs, Maryborough, Jan., 1861; locker, Brisbane, April, 1861; landing waiter, April, 1862; chief landing waiter, Jan., 1867; landing surveyor, Nov., 1869; chief inspector, distilleries, and excise April, 1886. Is a magistrate for the colony.

CHANDLER, WILLIAM KELLMAN, B.A., LL.D.—St. John's Coll., Cambridge (1875-79); law tripos, 1879-1880; called to the bar, Inner Temple, June, 1879; acting escheator-general of Barbados, 1880-81; J.P., 1880; acting police magistrate of Bridgetown, Aug. to Nov., 1880; acting solicitor-general, Nov., 1880, to Mar., 1881; acting judge of assistant court of appeal, Mar., 1881, to June, 1883; master-in-chancery, May, 1882; judge of assistant court of appeal, June, 1883; member of the House of Assembly, Mar., 1881; represented the parish of St. Peter until Nov., 1884; M.L.C., 1884; represented Barbados in negotiations at Washington in connexion with McKinley Tariff Act, 1891.

CHANTRELL, HENRY WILLIAM.—Auditor-general, Trinidad, and member exec. and leg. councils, Feb., 1882. Entered service as secretary and accountant, Roy. Coll., Mauritius, May, 1863; senior clerk and accountant, treasury, April, 1864; head accountant, Nov., 1875; acting storekeeper-general, 1877-80; promoted to Trinidad, Feb., 1882; acting rec.-gen., April, 1885; member bd. of edn., 1887-90; ag. col. secy., Nov., 1886, to Nov., 1887, Sept. to Nov., 1888, May to Dec., 1889; Mar. to Oct., 1890; Sept. to Nov., 1891, June to Nov., 1892, May to Oct., 1893, and July to Nov., 1895. Is a J.P. for Port of Spain and Western district of co. St. George.

CHAPLEAU, THE HON. SIR J. A., K.C.M.G. (1895), D.C.L., and Q.C.—Born 1840; called to the bar of Lower Canada, 1861; created a Q.C., 1864; entered the Quebec assembly, 1867; was solicitor-gen. from 1873 to 1874; provincial secretary from 1875 to 1878; became premier of Quebec, 30th Oct., 1879, a position he retained until July, 1882, when he resigned, was sworn of the privy council, and appointed secretary of state for Canada; knight commander of the Order of St. Gregory the Great, 1881, and commander of the Legion of Honour, 1882; is president of the credit foncier, France-Canadien, and director of the Montreal and Western, and of the Pacific Junction railways; minister of customs, 1892; lieut.-governor, Quebec, Dec., 1892.
CHAPMAN, ARTHUR.—Assessor of taxes, Hong Kong, 1889; ag. asst. suptdt., fire brigade, 1891-2; J.P., 1894; lieut. Hong Kong volrs.

raw_text: CHAPMAN, GENERAL SIR FRED. EDWARD, G.C.B. (1877) K.C.B. (1867), R.E.—Entered the army as lieut., 1835; became captain, R.E., 1846; lieut.-colonel in that corps, April, 1859; colonel in the army, 1855; proceeded on a special mission to Constantinople, Jan., 1854; was present at the battles of the Alma and Inkerman; served throughout the siege of Sebastopol, during the early part of which he was director of the left attack, and in the latter part was executive engineer to the forces; was several times mentioned in despatches (medal with three clasps); made officer of the legion of honour; received the Sardinian and Turkish medals, and the third class of the Medjidie; created, for his military services, C.B., 1855; K.C.B., 1867 and G.C.B., 1877; Governor and Commander-in-chief at Bermuda, 1857; and major-general same year. Inspector-general fortifications and director of works, 1870 to 1876; director of military intelligence, 1890-6.

HENRY JOE CHITTY HARPER, D.D.—Educated at Hyde Park, Winchester, and at Queen's College, Oxford, B.A., 1826, M.A., 1840; officiated for many years as "conduct" or chaplain to Eton College; vicar of Stratfield Mortimer; 1856, first Bishop of Christchurch.

CHRISTIE, JAMES JOHN.—Clerk to C.C. R.M., Fort Beaufort, and distributor of stamps, 1866; served in customs department Cape Town, 1870 to 1877; secretary to special commissioner Trans-Gariep tribes, April, 1876, to May, 1877; charge in Damaraland, Dec., 1876, to April, 1878; C.C., Cathcart Dec., 1879; C.C. and R.M., If Jan., 1881.
CHURCH, THE HON. C. E.—Member of Lunenburgh in Nova Scotian Leg. Assembly, commr. for public works and mines.

raw_text: LARK, ANDREW INGLIS.—Attorney-general of mania in the Fysh government, 29th Mar., 1892; member of the federal council of Austral., Jan., 1888; and of the federation convention, 1.

LARK, FRANCIS W., M.B., D.P.H. (Camb.), B.C.S., M.R.C.P.—Ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Middlesex Hosp., med. offr. of health and suptdt. er Hospital, Lowestoft, 1893; health offr., Hong Kong, May, 1895; memb. sanit. bd.; J.P., 1896.
LARK, HENRY JAMES.—Government printer, Bridad, Oct., 1873; govt. statistician, Feb., 1887; author of "Trinidad—A Field for Emigration," 1893; is fellow of the Royal Statistical Society.

LARK, JAMES HENRY, M.R.C.P., R.C.S., L.M., L.S.A.—Prizeman St. Thomas's Hospital, hon. Jan., 1870; government medical officer, St. Kitts (1870 to 1877), Jamaica; G.M.O. (1877-1878), Mandeville District of Manchester, in charge of hospital, prison and constabulary; medical superintendent lepers' house, parochial hospital alms house, Spanish Town, District of St. Catherine's, Mar., 1878; transferred to Kingston hospital, Sept., 1878, to April, 1879 (acting senior medical officer); acting medical officer, Kingston constabulary force, (1878 to 1879); member quarantine board; re-appointed G.M.O., Santa Cruz, District of St. Elizabeth, 1st May, 1879.

raw_text: LARK, WM.—Ed. Roy. High Schl., Edin.; called to the bar (Mid. Temp.) June, 1883; scholarships intern. and constit. law, 1882, and common and criminal law, 1882; admitted Br. Guinna bar, 1883; acted on several occasions as crown prosecutor; revising barrister, Oct., 1892; master sup. and regr. gen. S. Leone, Sept., 1894; J.P., Jan., 1895; dep. judge sup. ct., Feb., 1895; ag. just., Mar., 1895; pol. mag., coroner and judge of requests, May, 1895; cr. prostr., Jan., 1896; dep. judge, Jan., 1897.

CLARKE, LIEUT.-GENERAL SIR ANDREW, R.E., G.C.M.G. (1885), C.B. (1869), C.I.E. (1876).—Entered R.E. 1844; A.D.C. to Sir William Denison when governor of Van Diemen's Land, 1846; served in New Zealand during 1848-9, and was at the close of the war sent on a mission to Hiki; in the same year, rejoined Sir William Denison as private secretary; M.L.C., 1851; surveyor-general of Victoria, 1853; on the proclamation of the new constitution he was returned to the assembly for the city of Melbourne, and joined Mr. Haines' cabinet as minister for public lands, resigning with it in 1857; on its second resignation, in 1858, he declined to form a new administration, and returned to Europe. He was commanding royal engineer of the eastern and midland districts till 1863, when he proceeded on special service to the West Coast of Africa; director of works of the navy, 1864 to 1875; governor of the Straits Settlements, 1873, when he initiated the operations which led to the pacification of the Malay peninsula; public works member of Council of India, March, 1875; commandant of school of military engineering, Chatham, 1881-2; inspector-general of fortifications and director of works, 1882; ag. agent-gen. for Victoria, 1892-4 and 1897.
CLARKE, ERNEST D.—Dep. regr., Sup. Ct., Trinidad.

CLARKE, SIR FIELDING, Kt. BACH. (1894) LL.B.—Called to the bar, Middle Temple, Easter Term, 1876, north-eastern circuit; atty.-gen. of Fiji, 1881; acting chief justice and chief judicial commissioner for the Western Pacific, 1882 to 1883, and again 1884, confirmed 1885; puisne judge, Hong Kong, 1888; chief justice, 1891; chief justice, Jamaica, 1896.

CLARKE, LIEUT.-COL. SIR GEORGE S., R.E., K.C.M.G. (1893), C.M.G. (1887).—Entered R.E., 1868; Capt., 1880; Major, 1887; served in Egypt, 1882 (Medal and Star); in Suakin Expedition, 1885, mentioned in despatches (clasp); Sec. Colonial Defence Committee, 1885-92.

raw_text: CLARKE, JOSEPH.—Formerly a non-commissioned officer in the Army; served three years on the West Coast of Africa; intendant and dispenser colonial hospital, British Honduras, Jan., 1872; quarantine officer, and in charge of the station, 1874; chief warder, 1876; acting keeper of the common gaol, 1877; sub-inspector of police, 1878; keeper of the common gaol, 1878; clerk, colonial engineer's office, 1883, and keeper of cemeteries, 1885; inspector of nuisances, 1885.

CLARKE, LIEUT.-COL. SIR MARSHAL JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1886), C.M.G., 1880. (late R.A.).—Resident magistrate, county Pietermaritzburg, Natal, 1874; A.D.C. to Sir T. Shepstone, H.M.'s special commissioner, South Africa, 1876; political officer and special commissioner, Lydenburg, 1877; served in Transvaal campaign, 1880-1, in command of Landdrost's office, Potchefstroom; mentioned in despatches; resident magistrate, Quithin, Basutoland, 1881; commissioner, Cape police, King William's Town, 1882; colonel commanding Turkish regiment, Egyptian gendarmerie, 1882; third class order of Medjidie; retired from the army, with rank of lieutenant-colonel, 1883; resident commissioner, Basutoland, 1884; resdt. commissioner and chief mag., Zululand, 1893.

raw_text: CLARKE, WILLIAM JAMES.—Joined Natal Mounted Police, 1878; served through Zulu war of 1879 (medal with clasp); accompanied ex-Empress Eugenie to Zululand, 1880; served in Transvaal Campaign, 1881; present at battle of Laing's Nek; accompanied mission to Pondoland, 1887; mission to Tongaland, 1888, and Portuguese- Swaziland Boundary Commission, 1888; acting R.M. and administrator native law. Exopo division, 1889-90; Criminal Investigation offr., 1895.

raw_text: CLARKE, SIR W. JOHN, BARONET, 1882.—A distinguished and wealthy colonist of Victoria; member of the legislative council; president of the commission and executive committee for the Melbourne International Exhibition, 1880.

CLAVIER, ALEXANDER. — Ed. Stonyhurst; super. clerk col. sec. office, St. Lucia, Mar., 1860; clerk, audit office, Jan., 1862; comptroller and auditor, June, 1868; also registrar royal court and of deeds and mortgages, Mar., 1870, with the title of deputy registrar and accountant; registrar and accountant, July, 1875; prothonotary and registrar, Oct., 1879; also sheriff, April, 1886; is also registrar-general of births and deaths, a member of the legislative council, Castries town board, and a J.P.

CLIFFORD, HUGH CHARLES.—Joined Perak service, 1883; passed in Malay, Feb., 1885; collr. land revenue, Kwala Kangsar, March, 1885; on special service to Pahang, 1887; act. governor's agent there, 1887-8; collr. and mag., 1889; supt. Ulu Pahang, 1889; ag. Br. resdt. Pahang, Sept., 1890 to March, 1891, Ap. to Dec., 1891, and from Feb., 1893; sec. to govt. Selangor, Dec., 1894, but continued to act as resdt. Pahang. Resdt. Pahang, July, 1896.

COBLEY, WALTER HENRY, Assor. M. Inst. C.E.—Assistant engineer, Natal railways, 5th Feb., 1876; acting maintenance engineer, Jan. 1880; 1881; maintenance engineer, 1882; district engineer, 1883.

C.E.—Graduate Trinity College, Dublin, 1868; was employed railway works in South America, 1871-74; assistant engineer on the Cape Government railways, 1875; acting district engineer, 1877; engineer in public works department, Trinidad, 1878; engineer in charge of surveys, 1879.
COCKBURN, THE HON. JOHN ALF., M.L. (Lond.)—Member of House of Assembly, S. Australia; minister of education, 1886, to Nov., 1887; premier, 1889-90; again minister of education and agriculture, 1893.

CODE, A.—Land surveyor, St. Lucia, May, 1882.
COHEN, THE HON. H. E.—Colonial treasurer New South Wales, 18th December, 1877, to 31st Dec., 1878; Judge, Metropolitan Court, May, 1881; minister of justice, 5th Jan., 1883.

A.—Assistant clerk, treasury, Gambia, May, 1873; 2nd landing waiter, customs, Aug., 1875; assistant clerk, treasury, Feb., 1877; chief clerk, treasury and post office, Mar., 1880; acting gov't. clk.; clk. to leg. com. and dep. com., June to Nov., 1883; cashier, Jan., 1887; charge of the treasury, June to Nov., 1892.

COLE, G.—Inspector of public schools, Feb., 1892, and secretary to the education board, and Sept., 1882.

COLE, JOSEPH W.—Temporary clerk in colonial secretary's office, Sierra Leone, Nov., 1866; was clerk to the queen's advocate from Jan., 1868, to July, 1869, transferred to the customs department as second clerk and locker, in July, 1869.

COLE, SAMUEL SILVANUS.—Government printer, Lagos, 20th January, 1865; acted as printer from December, 1866, to February, 1867, and again from May to October, 1874; government head printer, Gold Coast, March, 1876.

COLES, THE HON. SIR JENKIN, K.C.M.G. (1894).—Speaker leg. assembly, S. Australia, 1894.

COLL, A. M.—Ed. at Haileybury and Trinity Coll., Oxon; B.A., 1885; M.A. and B.C.L., 1890; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1893; admitted to the Gibraltar bar; administrator general, 1891; acting police magistrate, 1893. police magistrate, Aug., 1892; inspr. of schools, 1894; acting attorney-general, Queen's State, and commissioner in bankruptcy, 1894-5 and 6; ag. ch. justice, 1894; ag. col. sec., 1895; ag. col. sec. 1895-1896. DOLLARD, J. HEWLETT.—Clerk third class, colonial secretary's office, Cape, Jan., 1858; second class, Feb., 1859; first class, Jan., 1867 C.C. and D.C., Prince Albert, Jan., 1870; Tulbagh, Dec., 1870; deputy assistant-treasurer, Feb., 1879. DOLLENS, JAMES HENRY.—Superintendent of Normal and Boys' Model Schools, Port of Spain, Trinidad, 1878; lieutenant Trinidad Volunteers, 1880; capt., 1885; ag. inspr. of schools, to Nov., 1886; elected on first bd. of directors Civil Service, Widows and Orphans Fund, 1887; author of "Guide to Trinidad," and other works; ag. suptdt. Govt. Printing dept., Dec., 1888, to Nov., 1891. DOLLET, WILFRED.—Ed. at Univ. Coll. Sch., Lond.; 1st class hon. inter., LL.B., Lond., 1889; colonial 2nd clerk, native department, Fiji, 1891; inspector of immigrants and assistant commissioner, Jan., 1883; secretary W. African High Commission, Oct., 1884. DOLLER, FREDERICK WILLIAM.—Third clerk colonial secretary's office, British Guiana, Nov., 1870; second clerk, Dec., 1870; record clerk, Jan., 1871; chief clerk and secretary to the quarantine bd., Dec., 1879; has acted as assistant government secretary, and clerk to the court of policy the combined court on several occasions since 1880; postmaster-general, 1888. DOLLINGWOOD, MAJOR-GENERAL WILLIAM C.M.G. (1880); for services in the Zulu war. DOLINS, A. E.—Educated at the City of London School and Cambridge; Lionel Rothschild scholarship and foundation scholar of Trinity College; prizeman 1891-2; 1st class classical tripos, 1893; appd. after competitive examn. for the office of S. of S. for the Colonies, 11th, 1894. DOLINS, ROBT. MUIRHEAD.—Retd. lieut. Royal Navy, 1866; served in flying squadrons in 1868 and 1872; sub-lieut., June, 1872; served on Channel and Australian stations 1875; lieut., June, 1876; appointed to Victorian Naval Forces (Australia) Dec. 1878; commander ocean Naval Forces, 1881; secy for Defence Victoria, 1888. DOLINSON, EDWN. DE LISLE.—Ed. Winter and Univ. Coll. Oxon. (scholar); called to bar (Inner Temp.), 1890; admitted Lagos, 1893; dist. comsnr., Lagos, 1895. DOLYER, E. W., Appointed after competitive exam. Clerk, 2nd divn., Civil Service, and sent to Admalty, 17 Feb. to 21 Oct., 1896, to 5 Nov., 1896. DOLYER, W. R.—Acting chief justice, Sierra Leone, 1879; acting puisne judge, Gold Coast, 1880, and acting Queen's advocate, 1880; Queen's advocate, Cyprus, 1881; puisne judge, Straits Settlements, 1892; att. gen., Straits Settlements, 1893; puisne judge, Oct., 1894, to April, 1895. DOLLYMORE, CHARLES H.—Audit clerk, Barbados, 1874; treas. account., St. Vincent, July, 1879; chief clerk, col. sec.'s office, Grenada; and reg. registrar of births and deaths for St. Vincent, Feb., 1881; dep. regr. and sec. sup. ct., 1881, to Dec., 1882; acting provost marshal, 1884, to May, 1885; atg. clk of councils, 1886 to Nov. 1887, and May to Nov., 1888; audr. Aug. to Nov., 1887, and June to Dec., 1888; keeper immign. records, Feb., 1890; postmaster, Grenada, 1893; Regr. Sup. Ct., 1896.

COLLYMORE, H. W.—Supernumerary, general post office, Barbados, October, 1876; acting third clerk, 1878; third clerk, record branch, colonial secretary's office, March, 1879; third clerk, correspondence branch, January, 1881; acting second clerk, 1883; acting public librarian, 1884; second clerk, correspondence branch, colonial secretary's office, July, 1884; ag. chief clk., June, 1893, to April, 1894; Aug. to Dec., 1895, and July to Nov., 1896; secy. to Highways Commn. 1893-6.

CORNWALL, C. F.—Born 1836; graduated B.A. Magdalen College, Cambridge, 1857; called to bar of Inner Temple, 1861; many years member of legislature of colony of British Columbia; on confederation of that province with Canada, in 1871, summoned by Her Majesty to the Senate of the Dominion; lieut.-governor of British Columbia, 1881-6.

CORNEY, BOLTON GLANVILL.—Educated at Fontainebleau, in London, and at Schwerin; St. Thomas's Hospital, 1870; M.R.C.S., England, 1874; entered the colonial service in 1877 as government medical officer, Fiji; also health officer, Suva, 1877; medical officer for the immigration department, 1881; acting chief medical officer, 1882, 1883, 1884, 1885, and since June, 1887; acting agent-general of immigration, Jan., 1885, to Mar., 1887; M.L.C. and of native regulative board, 1885; confirmed as ch. med. offr., June, 1887; M.L.C., June, 1888; ag. supdt. lun. asylum, 1887; ag. rec. gen. Jan. to Mar., 1892; commsr. to enquire into decrease of native popn. 1893; commssnr. to enquire into misgovernment of Prov. Tailevu, May, 1895.

COTTLE, H. C.—2nd asst. govt. printer, Ceylon, Nov., 1885; asst. govt. printer, 1888.

COURTNEY, RT. HON. LEONARD H., P.C.—St. John's Coll., Cam.; B.A. (2nd wrt, 1855); called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1859; represented Liskeard, Dec., 1876, to Dec., 1885, when borough was merged; Parliamentary under-secretary for the Home Department, Dec., 1880; Parliamentary under-secretary for the Colonies, 1881; secretary to the Treasury, Mar., 1885; resigned, 1885; chairman of ways and means, 1886-92.

Perth, 1891.

COWARD, CAPT. GEORGE F.—Sub-agent, immigration, Jamaica, 1873; inspector of stabolary, 1873; 2nd-class inspector, 1881.

COWPER, SYDNEY.—Clerk in office of commissioners, exhibn. of 1851, in 1871, in edn. dept., S. Kensington, 1875; 3rd-class clerk in col. off., Capetown, 1879; priv. sec. to premier, 1880; 2nd-class clk., 1881; priv. sec. to premier, 1882; 1st-class clk., 1885; sec. to prime minister, 1887; retired on abolition of office, 1891; priv. sec. dept. of agri., 1892; ag. sec. to pre (office re-established), 1896. Is a J.P. for District.
