raw_text: COWPER, VERY REV. WILLIAM MACQUORN Magd. Hall, Oxon. — B.A., 1833; M.A., 1836; d. 1833; p. 1834; dean and archdeacon of Sydney, 1858; vicar-general, 1877; formerly c. of St. Peter's, Dartmouth, 1833-6; chapl. to A. A. Co., R. Stephen's, 1836-56; principal Moore Coll., Liverpool, 1856; I. of St. John's, Bishopsthorpe, 1859; I. of St. Philip's, Sydney, 1858.

COX, CHARLES T.—Third clerk, governor's secretary's office, British Guiana, 1st July, 1874; second clerk, 1876; record clerk, 1879; senior clerk, 1883; chief clerk and secretary to the quarantine board, 1888; was secretary to the commissioners appointed in Jan., 1882, to inquire into the state of the administrator-general's department of British Guiana; student Mid. Temp., 1888; compiled "Index to Laws of B. Guiana," 1890 (2nd ed. 1891), also "Some Notes for Govt. Officers," 1894; asst. govt. secy. and clerk to the policy and combined ct., 1889; also clerk of the coun., 1892; ag. govt. secy., Mar. to July, 1893. Dec., 1894, to Jan., 1895, with seat in exec. and legislature.

HAMER, CAPT. J. H. — Capt. 3rd Bat. Highld. Served in Roy. Canadian mtd. infantry, 1888; d. to G. Coast constab., 1891; served in Ata- r expdn., 1893-4 in command of guns, and thanks of S. of S.; gunnery instructor and licence offr., 1895.

(1888), M.D., C.S., Eng., F.R.G.S. — Assistant-surgeon medos," hospital ship, convict establishment, India, July, 1880; entered the service of Her Majesty's commissioners of emigration as surgeon superintendent of an emigrant ship, Aug., 1885; con-general, Trinidad consulting surgeon general hospital, April, 1871; member of general bd. of health, May, 1871; member of quarantine bd., Dec., 1871; and medical officer of health for colony, Jan., 1872; one of the directors of the nic gardens, July, 1874; J.P., 1884; chief medical officer, Jamaica, 1893.

raw_text: RAWFORD, HENRY LEIGHTON. — Ed. at Eton and Corp. Christi Coll., Camb. (scholar); mr. Ceylon service, 1877; police magistrate, Pitiya, 1883; ditto, Gampola, 1883; office assnt to government agent, Western Province, 1884; second asst. col. secy. and clerk of legn., 1890; principal asst. col. sec., 1896.

RAWFORD, WALLACE. — 4th clerk, immigration department, British Guiana, Sept., 1880; 3rd clk., Aug., 1884; 2nd clerk, Oct., 1885; acted as agent of immigration, June, 1883, to June, 1884, and Oct., 1885, to Jan., 1886; Sept., 1886, to Vr., 1888; July, 1889, to Mar., 1890; ag. emigr. agt., Barbados, Oct., 1890, to Nov., 1891; ag. senr. agent, July, 1892; awarded 1st certificate for proficiency in Hindustani, Mar., 1885.

REAGH, C. V., C.M.G. (1892). — Barrister-at-law (Middle Temple, called May, 1892); was assistant superintendent of the first class in the Pjaub police; in 1866 passed the highest police examination, and the second standard in oriental languages; in 1867 raised the Sikh police for the Hong Kong govt.; deputy superintendent, 1867; C. in 1868; sheriff in 1874; received thanks govt. for services in typhoon, Sept., 1874; acted as super. of police in 1869-70 and 1877-78; acting A.D.C. in 1878; super. of fire brigade, 1878 to 1880; passed with credit the six examinations in Chinese colloquial prescribed by the Government; in 1879, member of committees public gambling, and on the defence of Hong Kong; arbitrator under the Opium Ordinance (7 & 1879); assistant British resident, judge, and member of state council, Perak, Mar., 1883; governor and commr.-in-chief and chief judicial officer, Br. N. Borneo, Mar., 1888 to 1895; also gov., Labuan, 1890 to 1895, when he retired.

CREASE, SIR HENRY PERIM PELLEW, Kt. BACH. (1896). — Graduated at Clare College, Cambridge, B.A., 1847; called to the bar at the Middle Temple, June, 1849; admitted to the bar, British Columbia, 1858; attorney-general, 1869; puisne judge, from 1870 to 1896, when he retired; served on various legal commissions.

raw_text: CREASY, E. — Private secretary to his father Sir Edward Creasy, Chief Justice of Ceylon, in 1868; assistant to the surveyor general, 1871; district surveyor, 1876; acting police magistrate, commissioner of court of requests, and deputy fiscal at Balapeta-Modera, 1878; assistant super. in the Ceylon police force, 1879; passed the prescribed examination in the Singhalese language, 1882; acting head-quarter superintendent, and superintendent of police of the northern, eastern, and north-western provinces, and J.P. for those provinces in 1882-5; act. assistant supt., central province, 1885; supt. of police, Colombo, Aug., 1892.

CREASY LEONARD, A.M.I.C.E. — Pioneer offr., Ceylon, 1874; superintending offr., 1877; district engineer, Feb., 1886; provl. engr. N. Prov., July, 1890; financial and office asst., Nov., 1894; prov. engr. E. Prov., Jan. 1895; ditto Prov. Uva. May, 1895; on deputation to Seychelles, Oct., 1896, to Feb., 1897.

CRESWELL, MARGARET S. — Aptd. post-mistress of Gibraltar and suptdt. of govt. telegraph imperial P.O., Sept., 1877, as a mark of the estimation in which the services of her late father, the dep. postmr.-gen. at Gibraltar, had been held; transferred to col. govt. in Jan., 1886, when the colony assumed the control of the local P.O.

CRISSON, JOHN C. — Cashier of the public bank, Turks Islands, Mar., 1864; clerk of the crown and prothonotary, and clerk, colonial secretary's office, June, 1867; postmaster, July, 1868; auditor, 1869; acting assistant commissioner, April, 1884, to Sept., 1886; registrar of shipping, Aug., 1885; is a J.P.

CROFTON, FRANCIS BLAKE, B.A., Trinity College, Dublin, 1862. — Provincial librarian, Nova Scotia, Jan., 1882; author of "The Major's Big-Talk Stories," "Haliburton, the Man and the Writer," "Hairbreadth Escapes of Major Mendex," &c.

raw_text: CRONIN, J. D. — Probationer, treasury, Sydney, Feb., 1854; third class clerk, 1855; second ditto, 1857; first clerk, pay branch, 1864; paymaster, 1865; acted suptdt., money order office in 1865.

Engineering School, 1888; gained 1st prize for civil engineering. City of London Coll., 1889; asst. engr. water and drainage department, Hong Kong, 1891; in charge of new sewerage works, city of Victoria, 1891-94; sanitary surveyor, 1892; executive eng. P. W. D., 1893; and water authority during absence of dir. of pub. wks, 1893-94.

CROPPER, ROBERT P. — Superintendent of emigration, Barbados, 1873; protector of immigrants, St. Lucia, 1878.

raw_text: CROSBY, JAMES MICHAEL. — Clerk to resident magistrate, Simon's Town, Cape Colony, 1857; clerk to C.C. and R.M., Prince Albert, 1860; 2nd clerk, attorney-general's office, 1864; master and registrar eastern districts court, 1869; C.C. and R.M., Humansdorp, 1872; chief clerk, attorney-general's office, and clerk of the peace for Cape Town, Dec., 1872; appointed high sheriff of the colony, Oct., 1877; taxing officer, supreme court, Jan., 1882; R.M., Cape Town, and district and visiting magistrate to Breakwater, Clifton, and Tokai convict stations, May, 1884.

CROSS, J. W. — Clerk, Zulu interpreter, and sub-distributor of stamps in the resident magistrate's office, Ixopo, Natal, 23rd Oct., 1876; deputy-clerk of the peace for Ixopo, 2nd Mar., 1877; and on 3rd Mar., 1879, appointed in command of the Ixopo, Natal native foot contingent, 800 strong; and subsequently transferred to command of the mounted contingent during the Zulu war; acted on several occasions as resident magistrate of Ixopo; clerk to R.M., Weenen, July, 1886; admnr. of native law, Pagadi's locn., 1889; magistrate, Lower Tugela divn., 1894; R.M., Alfred Divn., 1895.
CROSSKILL, HERBERT. —Deputy provincial secretary, Nova Scotia, 1868-78; and again, 1882.

CUDDEFORD, WM. —Employed on financial mission in Bahamas, May, 1885, to May, 1886; supervisor of customs, Gold Coast, Jan., 1887; local auditor, Lagos, under C. and A.G., London, Dec., 1889; ditto, S. Leone and Gambia, Sep. 1893.

CULBERT, JOHN ALEXANDER. —Tidewaiter, Nassau, Oct., 1853; acted as warehouse keeper, Feb., 1856; second clerk, receiver-general and treasurer's department, Dec., 1856; acting market clerk, Oct., 1859; warehouse keeper from Aug., 1863; also examining officer, 1869; collector, Mar., 1870; acted as president justice, Inagua, from Dec., 1873, to June, 1874; member House of Assembly for Harbour Island, May, 1875; acting registrar of records, June to Dec., 1883, and Jan. to Feb., 1884; ag. rec. gen. and treas. July to Oct., 1887, and June, 1888, to May, 1889.

raw_text: CUMMINGS, HENRY. —Ed. at Derby and Ober Lalinstein, Germany; admitted solr. supreme ct., May, 1880; dist. commsr. G. Coast Colony, May, 1891; asst. Queen's advocate, Accra, May to Aug., 1891.

CUNNINGHAM, HENRY HUTT. —Educated at Charterhouse; obtained open scholarship (nat. sci.) Clare Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (math. tripos), 1874; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1876; stipendiary magistrate, British Guiana, 1884.
CUNYNGHAME, PERCY F. —Cadet, Sarawak service, July, 1886; asst. resdt., May, 1890; resdt., 2nd class, 4th divn., March, 1896.

CURREY, CHARLES. —Entered the col. sec.'s office, Cape, June, 1871; appointed 3rd class clerk, Aug., 1872; 2nd class, July, 1874; promoted dept. of crown lands and public works, Oct., 1879; chief clerk, office of crown lands and public works, April, 1881; secretary to the diamond mining commission, Sept., 1881; from 1882 has frequently acted as asst. commissioner of crown lands and public works asst. commissioner of crown lands and public works, Apr., 1892; secy. for agriculture, 1893.

CURRIE, SIR DONALD, K.C.M.G. (1877), C.M.G. (1877). —Chairman of the Castle Mail Packets Co. In July, 1876, at the time of the negotiations between the Earl of Carnarvon and President Brand, he assisted by his good offices bringing about the success of these negotiations and thus ending the long dispute between the British Government and the Orange Free State in reference to the Diamond Fields; has been M.P. for Perthshire since 1880.
CURTIS, ALFRED P. —Third class clerk Jn 1864, in public works department, Western Australia; removed to survey office, 1865; senior class clerk, post office, 1872; 1st class clerk, 1890.

Consul at Samoa, Jan., 1890; H.M. deputy commissioner for the Western part at Samoa, May, 1890; proceeded in H.M.S. "Curaçoa" to Union Group to report on claims, Aug., 1892; deputy commissioner for Samoan and Union Groups, and authorised to perform the duties of resident in the Union Group Oct., 1892; special judicial commr., April, 1893; apptd. to license and superintend employment of Gilbert Islds. natives in Samoa, Jan., 1894.

A. —Senior Sophister, Trn Coll., Dub; captain, 4th Royal Dublin Fusiliers; assistant inspector of constabulary, Gold Coast, 1879; civil comm., Tacquah, 1881; ch. inspr. pol., Singapore, 1883; asst. suptdt. of pol., Perak, 1884; act. pol. supnt., Malacca, 1889, and Singapore, 1891; asst. supdt. of pol., prov. Wellesley, 1893.

DAVIES, REV. JOHN HENRY.—Late Exhib. of Jesus Coll., Oxford; math. honours in mods., 1876, and in finals, 1878; B.A., 1879; M.A., 1881; D., 1879; P., 1880 St. D.; curate of Aberystwyth, 1879-86; curate in charge of Merthyr, Carmarthenshire, 1886; curate of St. Martin, Birmingham, 1887-88; author of "Letters on Christianity and Mahomedanism," "Intermediate Education," &c., &c.; col. chaplain, Accra, Gold Coast; secy. to Board of Education, registrar of deaths, and manager of govt. schools.

DAVIES, HON. LOUIS HENRY.—Born Charlottetown (P.E.I.), May 4, 1845; called to the Bar, 1866; Q.C. in 1880; he was one of the Canadian counsel before the International Fishery Commission at Halifax in 1877; solicitor-general of P.E. Island, 1869, and again in 1872-73; led the Opposition in P.E.I. Legislature until Sept., 1876, when he became premier and attorney-general; resigned in March, 1879, and was defeated at the general elections which followed; he was first returned to the House of Commons at the general elections in 1882, and has since sat for Queen's; minister of marine and fisheries in Mr. Laurier's govt., June., 1896.

DAVIS, NICHOLAS DARNELL, C.M.G. (1895).—Was for some years in Grenada, in the private sec.'s office; private sec. to Administrator Baynes for a short time. In British Guiana served as a clerk in several departments; Aug., 1870, to Feb., 1871, secretary to the commissioners of inquiry into the treatment of immigrants; secretary to the royal commissioners of inquiry into the treatment of immigrants in Mauritius, Feb., 1872; commandant, British Sherbro, Sierra Leone, 1874; postmaster-general, British Guiana, 1876; acted as receiver-general, Dec., 1880, to Sept., 1881; comptroller of customs, 1st Oct., 1881; acting auditor-general, a member of the court of policy, and senior commissioner of the Vlissengen Estate, 1882; member, court of policy, again in 1886.
DAVIS, S. SPENCER.—Extra Rev. offr. St. Kitts, 1893; 4th ditto, 1895.

Camb., 1862; M.A., 1866; student interpreter Hong Kong, Mar. 28, 1862; extra A.D.C. to Sir Hercules Robinson, 1864; passed as interpreter, 1865; private secretary to administrator, 1865; J.P., 1865; registrar-general, June, 1865; captain superintendent of police, July, 1868; wounded on duty, 1878; acting colonial treasurer, 1881; colonial secretary, 1881 and 1882; member of coun., 1887, and of leg. coun., 1888; retired 1892.

DEMPSTER, T. ERSKINE.—Educated at Whide House Academy, Jersey, and Hartley Institute, Southampton; entered the "non-regulation commission," Bengal, 1872, as extra assistant commissioner, Hayaribagh; in 1874, on trial duty during Bengal famine, deputy magistrate and deputy collector, Durbhunga. shoot; in charge treasury judicial and revenue brk. 1875, extra assistant commissioner, Nya, maka; Sonthal, Pergunnahs; the same year on trial duty as assistant settlement officer during mthal settlement, determining measurements d adjudicating land disputes; 1876, sub-isional officer in charge Jauntara, Sonthal ergunnahs; stipendiary mag. Mauritius, 1877.

DENTON, GEORGE CHARDIN, C.M.G. (1891).—Ensign, 57th Regt., Oct., 1869; lieut., May, 1871, lit., Aug., 1876; capt., Jan., 1878; chief of police, t. Vincent, April, 1880; appointed one of the commissioners to enquire into the police force, Barbados, Oct., 1880; member of executive council, Oct., 81; represented St. Vincent at the telegraphic conference at Barbados, May, 1882; administered govt., St. Vincent, May to July, 1885; again in 86, and from Nov., 1887, to Mar., 1888; acting sl. sect., 1886 to 1888; col. sect., Lagos, Mar., 1888; admin. govt., 1889, 1890, 1891, 1893, and 1895.

DE PETRI, EMILIO.—Entered the Malta civil service, after competitive examination, as clerk, Dec., 1857; assistant secretary to government, and clerk to the council of government, Jan., 1886; acting chief secretary, May to Sept., 1886, Oct. to Dec., 1887, June to Oct., 1890, July to Sept., 1892; rec.-gen. and director of contracts, July, 1894, with a seat in exec. coun. and in coun. of govt.
DE PIRO, GIUSEPPE LORENZO, MARCHESINO, C.M.G. (1887).—Late hon. secretary, Committee of Privileges, Maltese Nobility.

DERBY, 16th EARL of (England, created 1485); BARON STANLEY, 1832 (United Kingdom); BART. 1627 (England); BARON STANLEY OF PRESTON, 1886; G.C.B. (1886); A.D.C.—The RIGHT HON. FREDERICK ARTHUR STANLEY, younger son of the 14th Earl of Derby; born 1841; ed. at Eton; entd. the Grenadier Guards in 1858, and was appointed lieutenant and captain in 1862; retired from the army, and entered Parliament as member for Preston in 1865; was a Lord of the Admiralty from Aug. to Dec., 1868; elected member for North Lancashire at the general election of 1868, and again in 1874, 1880, and 1885; was financial secretary for war from Feb., 1874, to Aug., 1877, when he was appointed financial secretary to the treasury; Mar., 1878, secretary of state for war, which office he held till the retirement of Lord Beaconsfield's administration in 1880; secretary of state for the colonies, June, 1885, to Feb., 1886; resigned Feb., 1886; president of the Board of Trade in Lord Salisbury's second administration, Aug., 1886; governor-general of Canada, 1888-93; succeeded his brother as Earl of Derby, 1893; Lord Mayor of Liverpool, 1895.
DERHAM, THE HON. FREDERICK THOMAS.—Postmaster-General, Victoria, 18th Feb., 1886-90.

DE SARAM, J. H.—Writer to the government of Ceylon, 1865, police magistrate of Balapitimodara, 1866; district judge, Badulla, Oct., 1867; commissioner of requests, Colombo, 1868; district judge, Kurunégala, 1875; regr. gen., Colombo, 1886; dist. judge, Kalutara, Dec., 1883; regr.-gen., May, 1886; dist. judge, Jaffna, Aug., 1891; ditto Galle, Dec., 1891; ditto Kandy, Aug., 1893.

DESHON, EDWARD.—Member of land board, Queensland; ensign in 68th Light Infantry, 6th June, 1854; lieutenant, 1st Dec., 1854; served with the 68th Light Infantry at siege and fall of Sebastopol, from 14 Nov., 1854, to the end of the war; medal and clasp, and Turkish medal; instructor of musketry to the 68th Light Infantry from Nov., 1857, to Oct., 1861; passed competitive examination for admission to Staff College, in July, 1861; retired by sale of commission in Oct., 1861; auditor gen., Queensland, 1890.

DESMARAIS, HENRY EUGENE.—Called to the bar, Middle Temple, April, 1866; police magistrate, Rodrigues, June, 1875; district judge and stipendiary magistrate, Seychelles, Nov., 1879; acting chief civil commissioner on several occasions; acting magistrate, Moka, April to Sept., 1882; district magistrate, Moka, Aug., 1884; acting police and additional magistrate, Port Louis, Nov., 1884; district magistrate, Moka, Dec., 1885; acting junior district magistrate, Port Louis, May to Oct., 1886; Mar., 1887, to Jan., 1888; district and stipendiary magistrate, Moka, July, 1889.

DE SMIDT, HENRY, F.S.S.—Permanent under colonial secretary of the colony of Cape of Good Hope, and accounting officer, colonial secretary's department. Holds in addition the offices of controller of printing and stationery, member of tender board, and custodian of the main stock of stamps. Was specially charged with the duties of director of the census of 1891, on the successful accomplishment of which, received the congratulations of the government, and was awarded an honorarium of 5000L, specially voted by parliament. Entered the service in Mar., 1865, as a clerk in the colonial office, in which department he has served ever since. Elected fellow of Royal Statistical Society, 1892.

DE VERTEUIL, SIR LOUIS ANTOINE AIMÉ, K.C.M.G. (1895), C.M.G. (1889).—Senior unofficial member of the legislative council, Trinidad. DES VŒUX, SIR GEORGE W., G.C.M.G. (1893), K.C.M.G. (1883), C.M.G. (1877).—Ed. Charter House and Ball. Coll., Ox. Called to the bar of Upper Canada, 1861; stipendiary magistrate, Br. Guiana, 1863; administrator of the government, St. Lucia, 1869; act. gov. of Trinidad, Jan., 1877, to Jan., 1878; acting governor of Fiji, June, 1878, to Sept., 1879; governor, Bahamas, 1880; governor Fiji, 1880-6; Assist. High Commissioner Western Pacific, 1880, and High Commissioner, 1882-5; governor of Newfoundland, 1886; of Hong Kong, 1887; retired 1891.

events.position: Civil commissioner and resident magistrate, Victoria West
events.text_span: Civil commissioner and resident magistrate, Victoria West, Cape of Good Hope, 1st Sept., 1878.

raw_text: DE VILLIERS, J. N. P.—Civil commissioner and resident magistrate, Victoria West, Cape of Good Hope, 1st Sept., 1878.

events.position: Chief justice of the supreme court
events.text_span: Chief justice of the supreme court, Cape of Good Hope, 1874

events.position: president of the legislative council and member of the council of the university of the Cape of Good Hope in
events.text_span: president of the legislative council and member of the council of the university of the Cape of Good Hope in 1873

events.position: one of the royal commission for the settlement of the affairs of the Transvaal
events.text_span: one of the royal commission for the settlement of the affairs of the Transvaal 1881

events.position: represented the Cape at the Colonial conference
events.text_span: represented the Cape at the Colonial conference, Ottawa, 1894.

raw_text: DE VILLIERS, SIR JOHN, KNT. BACH. (1877), K.C.M.G. (1882).—Chief justice of the supreme court, Cape of Good Hope, 1874; attorney-general from 1872 to 1874; for several years member of the legislative assembly; president of the legislative council and member of the council of the university of the Cape of Good Hope in 1873; one of the royal commission for the settlement of the affairs of the Transvaal 1881; represented the Cape at the Colonial conference, Ottawa, 1894.

events.position: took part in the expeditions to the Linggi and Lukut Rivers in
events.text_span: took part in the expeditions to the Linggi and Lukut Rivers in 1874

events.position: deputy master attendant, shipping master, &c. Singapore
events.text_span: deputy master attendant, shipping master, &c. Singapore, 1886

events.position: mag. for Singapore and Penaung, commissioner Ct. of Requests, S.S. mag. and collr., and hrbr. mr. Matang and Port Weld, Perak
events.text_span: mag. for Singapore and Penaung, commissioner Ct. of Requests, S.S. mag. and collr., and hrbr. mr. Matang and Port Weld, Perak, 1888.

raw_text: DEW, ARTHUR TOMKYNS.—Naval cadet, 1867; took part in the expeditions to the Linggi and Lukut Rivers in 1874 (Indian medal, with Perak clasp); left the Navy, 1876; magistrate and collr., Perak, 1881; superintendent of the Bindings, 1884; deputy master attendant, shipping master, &c. Singapore, 1886; is a J.P.; mag. for Singapore and Penaung, commissioner Ct. of Requests, S.S. mag. and collr., and hrbr. mr. Matang and Port Weld, Perak, 1888.
DEWDNEY, THE HON. EDGAR.—Lieutenant-governor of North West Territories, 1881; minister of interior, Canada, 1888; lieut.-governor, Br. Columbia, 1892.

DE WET, THE HON. SIR JACOBUS ALBERTUS, K.C.M.G. (1890).—Member Cape house of assembly and exec. council; minister for native affairs, 1884; British Resdt., S.A. Republic, June, 1890; retired on pension, Sept., 1896.

DE WET, SIR JACOBUS PETRUS, KNT., 1883.—educated at the South African College, Capetown, and University College, London (B.A., 1860); barrister, Inner Temple, 1863; solicitor-general, Cape, 1873; recorder, Griqualand West, 1878; chief justice, Transvaal, 1880 to 1881; acting chief justice of Ceylon, 1882 to 1883, when he retired.

events.position: served in the Crimea, and was military attaché at the embassy at Constantinople
events.text_span: served in the Crimea, and was military attaché at the embassy at Constantinople, 1877-8

events.position: under secy. to the governor-gen. of Canada
events.text_span: under secy. to the governor-gen. of Canada, 1878 to 1880

events.position: commissioned to inquire into Swazi affairs
events.text_span: commissioned to inquire into Swazi affairs, 1889

events.position: comptroller of the household of the Duke of York
events.text_span: comptroller of the household of the Duke of York, Feb., 1892.

raw_text: DE WINTON, MAJOR-GENERAL SIR FRANCIS WALTER, R.A., G.C.M.G. (1893), K.C.M.G. (1884), C.M.G. (1882).—Entered Royal Artillery in 1854; served in the Crimea, and was military attaché at the embassy at Constantinople, 1877-8; under secy. to the governor-gen. of Canada, 1878 to 1880; administrator-general, Congo Free State, 1884; commander, Order of Leopold, 1886; commanded expedition against the Yonnies, 1887; commissioned to inquire into Swazi affairs, 1889; comptroller of the household of the Duke of York, Feb., 1892.

DE WOLF, JAN. A.—Medical offr., St. John, Trinidad, 1876; ag. health offr. of shipping, &c., inspr. of immigrants, Pt. of Spain, 1879 to 1883-4; ag. surg.-gen., Aug., 1884, to June, 1890; health offr. of shipping, med. inspr. of immigrants, &c., Aug., 1890.
DEWS, ALBERT, M.A.—Classical master, Brixington Coll., Barbados, 1893.

D'HOTMAN, HENRI JULES JOSEPH.—Entered Mauritius civil service in 1865 as volunteer; examiner general's department; March, 1866, passed examination, and appointed extra mechanical clerk (same office); 1866, acting junior clerk, magistracy office; 1867, acting clerk, police court; Mar., 1870, acting assistant-clerk, magistrate's office; 1872, second clerk, magistracy, Plaines Wilhems; May, 1876, acting chief clerk; August, 1876, reappointed and clerk (same office), 1878; clerk and court interpreter to R.M., Inanda, Natal, 1878; secretary pollution commission, 1880; clerk of court, 1884; registrar, Victoria county, 1884; senior clerk, R. M. Durban, 1887; 1st clerk to R.M., registrar, circuit court, Mar., 1891.

events.position: Called to the bar, Inner Temple
events.text_span: Called to the bar, Inner Temple, May, 1887

events.position: pol. mag. and commr. of requests, Galle
events.text_span: pol. mag. and commr. of requests, Galle, Ceylon, Oct., 1889
crown counsel for the island, July, 1893.

raw_text: DIAS, FELIX REGINALD, M.A., I.I.M., Trinity Hall, Camb.—Called to the bar, Inner Temple, May, 1887; pol. mag. and commr. of requests, Galle, Ceylon, Oct., 1889; crown counsel for the island, July, 1893.
DIAS, SIR HARRY, KNT. BACH., 1893.—Barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1847; jun. puisne judge, Ceylon, 5th July, 1879; retired, July, 1892.
DIAS, W., M.D., M.R.C.S., L.S.A., London—colonial surgeon, Ceylon, 14th Oct., 1862.

DIBBS, THE HON. SIR GEORGE RICHARD, K.C.M.G., (1892).—Colonial treasr., New South Wales, Jan., 1883, to Oct., 1885; premier and colonial secy., Oct., 1885; treasr. and premier, Oct., 1885; colonial secy. and premier, Feb., 1886, to Feb., 1887, and Jan. and Feb., 1889, and premier and colonial secy., 1891-4; one of the representatives of N.S.W. at federation conference, 1891.

DICK, GEO. ROYER, M.A.—Educated at Trinity Coll., Mauritius, and Gonville and Caius, Camb. (late Fellow); called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Mar., term 1869; professor of mathematics at Trinity Coll., Mauritius, 1879; registrar-gen., 1880; member, leg. coun., 1890; census commissioner, 1890; ag. auditor-gen., 1893; ag. rec.-gen., 1894, to Oct., 1895, and April, 1896.
DICK, JOHN NORMAN.—Govt. engineer surveyor, Penang, 1888; ag. Govt. Marine Surveyor, June, 1895, to June, 1896.

DICK, THE HON. THOMAS.—Colonial secy., New Zealand, 5th Mar., 1880; and still holding office, minister of education, 15th October, 1880; minister of justice, 23rd April, 1881, and master-general and commissioner of telegraphs, 21st April, 1882, resigning ministry of justice, resigned post office and telegraphs, 11th October, 1882.

DICKEN, CHARLES SHORTT, C.M.G. (1893).—Educated at Charterhouse; ensign 87th Royal Fusiliers, July, 1859; lieutenant, 1861; first certificate, school of musketry, Hythe, Nov., 1861; retired in 1864; acting sub-inspector of police, 1865; appointed police magistrate, 1866; retired, 1893. Queensland, June, 1866; clerk of Petty Sessions, Springure, Oct., 1867; police magistrate, Springure, July, 1872; gold commissioner and police magistrate, Ravenswood, July, 1874; police magistrate and Gold Fields' warden, Charters Towers, May, 1875; police magistrate, Townsville, Dec., 1878. to May, 1880; called to the bar at the Middle Temple, June, 1883; hon. secretary to the Queensland Commission in London, Indian and Colonial Exhibition, 1886; J.P. of the colony, and secretary, Queensland government office, London; ag. agent general, Oct., 1895, to Mar., 1896; and again Dec., 1896, to May, 1897.

DICKSON, GEORGE WORKMAN, A.M.I.C.E. (1878).—Graduate of Arts and licentiate of civil engineering, Trinity College, Dublin, 1869; junior engineer on different sewerage, canal, tramway, and waterworks in Norfolk and Suffolk, 1869-73; engineer for the Odessa waterworks, Russia; the Whbury waterworks, Berkshire, and the Eastern and Midlands railway, Norfolk, 1873-79; asst. rector, public works, Trinidad, 1879; acted as rector of public works and general supt. of railways, 1882, 1883, 1885, and 1888; acting M.L.C., 1888; Colonial engineer, Br. Guiana, 1891.

DICKSON, JAMES ROBERT.—Born at Plymouth, 1832; educated at Glasgow; has been extensively engaged in mercantile pursuits in Victoria and Queensland; entered Queensland legislative assembly, 1873; secretary for public lands and works, 1876; colonial treasurer, 1876 to 1879; led opposition during part of 1881; colonial treasurer, 1883 to 1887; member of the federal parliam. of Australasia, Jan. and Feb., 1886 and 1887; offered speakership of assembly in 1893, but declined; represents Bulimba in assembly.

events.position: Acting clerk in the district magistrate's office, Bridgetown
events.text_span: Acting clerk in the district magistrate's office, Bridgetown, Barbados, January, 1864

events.position: 4th class landing waiter in the customs, after a competitive examination
events.text_span: 4th class landing waiter in the customs, after a competitive examination, January, 1867

raw_text: DICKSON, ROBERT.—Acting clerk in the district magistrate's office, Bridgetown, Barbados, January, 1864; confirmed, March, 1864; 4th class landing waiter in the customs, after a competitive examination, January, 1867; 3rd class landing waiter, September, 1869; 2nd clerk, May, 1871; 1st clerk and warehouse keeper, November, 1876; landing surveyor, Dec., 1890.

DIDIER, ST. AMAND E.—Called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1864; district stipendiary magistrate, Mauritius, Ang., 1869; has acted at different times as substitute to the master of the supreme court and to the procureur and advocate-general; senior district magistrate, Port Louis, Mar., 1881; chairman, forest lands purchase commission, 1883-4; senior district magistrate, 1884-85; master supreme court, June, 1886; ag. puisne judge, Feb., 1891; May, 1886; Feb., 1890, to Mar., 1892, and May, 1894.

DIFFORD, ABRAHAM.—Somerset and Dorset Railway, 1855 to 1877, of which he was secy. updt., and goods manager; traffic manager Eastern Railways, Cape, Ang., 1878; ditto, Western Railways, Aug., 1882; secretary, railway dept., Oct. 1884; agent at Johannesburg, 1886.

events.text_span: Deputy clerk to magistrates, Trelawny, Jamaica, 1861

raw_text: DIGNUM, ANDREW, B.—Deputy clerk to magistrates, Trelawny, Jamaica, 1861; clerk, Falmouth district court, Jan., 1867; ditto, St. Ann's Bay, 1870; ditto, northern district, Jan., 1880; notary public, May, 1868; R.M., 1888.
DILLON, P. L.—4th class landing waiter, Barbados, 1866; 3rd class, 1868; senior, 1880; landing surveyor, 1886; comptroller of customs, Dec., 1890.

events.position: elected member of the council of Malta
events.text_span: elected member of the council of Malta, 1849

events.position: crown advocate, legal adviser of the government, and ex officio member of council
events.text_span: crown advocate, legal adviser of the government, and ex officio member of council, Jan., 1854

events.position: chief justice and President of the Court of Appeal
events.text_span: chief justice and President of the Court of Appeal, 1880

raw_text: DINGLI, SIR ADRIANO, G.C.M.G. (1860); K.B. (1859).—LL.D. (1836); received at Malta Mar., 1837; elected member of the council of Malta, 1849; crown advocate, legal adviser of the government, and ex officio member of council, Jan., 1854; chief justice and President of the Court of Appeal, 1880; vice-president of council of govt., 1893; retired, 1894.

DIX, THOMAS H.—Supernumerary clerk, colonial secretary's office, St. Lucia, Jan., 1862; acting chief excise department, May, 1867; acting stipendiary magistrate, Jan., 1871; magistrate, Dominica, Sept., 1871; stipendiary magistrate, 2nd district, St. Lucia, May, 1872; is ex officio judge of the court of requests, chairman poor law committee and district coroner, member of road committee; called to the bar Jan., 1879; member leg. coun. 1883; is local corresponding agent for Imperial Institute; promoted to 1st district, Nov., 1892; was chairman quarantine bd., Nov., 1892, to Aug., 1894.

events.position: Crown solicitor, Tasmania (also clerk of the peace and registrar of building societies)
events.text_span: Crown solicitor, Tasmania (also clerk of the peace and registrar of building societies), May, 1887
barrister, &c., supreme court, Tasmania, admitted July, 1882

events.position: was parliamentary draftsman and afterwards solicitor-general from
events.text_span: was parliamentary draftsman and afterwards solicitor-general from 25th Mar. to 30th May, 1887.

raw_text: DOBBIE, EDWARD DAVID.—Crown solicitor, Tasmania (also clerk of the peace and registrar of building societies), May, 1887; barrister, &c., supreme court, Tasmania, admitted July, 1882; joined civil service, Tasmania, Mar., 1883; was parliamentary draftsman and afterwards solicitor-general from 25th Mar. to 30th May, 1887.

DOBELL, HON. RICHARD REID.—Born at Liverpool, 1837, his father being a prominent merchant of that city; went to Quebec in 1857, and, entering into the lumber business, became the head of one of the largest firms in the country; elected member of House of Commons for Quebec West, and became member of Mr. Laurier's govt. (without portfolio), June, 1896.

events.position: Called to the bar, Inner Temple
events.text_span: Called to the bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1875

raw_text: DOBSON, ALFRED.—Called to the bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1875; member, legislative assembly, Tasmania, 1877-87; attorney-general and member executive council, 1877; speaker, legislative assembly, July, 1885; solicitor-general, June, 1887; member of council of education, and church advocate for the diocese of Tasmania.

events.position: Called to the bar of the Middle Temple, June 6
events.text_span: Called to the bar of the Middle Temple, June 6, 1856

raw_text: DOBSON, THE HON. SIR WILLIAM LAMBERT, Kt. BACH. (1886).—Called to the bar of the Middle Temple, June 6, 1856; attorney-general, Tasmania, Feb. 11, 1861; also member of the executive council; puisne judge, 1870; chief justice, 1885.

DOBSON, THE HON. FRANK STANLEY, B.A., LL.D. (Camb.).—Barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, Q.C., F.L.S. (Lond.); M.L.C., Victoria, since 1870; member executive council; solicitor-general, July, 1881, to Mar., 1883; lecturer on law, Melbourne University; trustee Melbourne public library and national gallery; official visitor of the observatory; chairman of committees, leg. coun.

events.text_span: District Court judge, New South Wales, 7th June, 1884.

raw_text: DOCKER, E. B.—District Court judge, New South Wales, 7th June, 1884.
DODD, J. H.—Engineer, Eastern District, Jamaica, 4th Dec., 1880.

DODDS, THE HON. JOHN STOKELL, C.M.G. (1889).—Attorney-general, Tasmania, 20 Dec., 1878, to 29 Oct., 1879, and in the Giblin ministry, 30 Oct., 1879; colonial treasurer from 1st Dec., 1881, to 15th Aug., 1884; attorney-general in the Douglas ministry from 15th Aug., 1884, to 8th Mar., 1886, also premier, Mar. to Dec., 1886; judge, supreme court, 1886; delegate, colonial conference, 1887.

events.position: Inspector of asylums and med. supt. of new asylum, Mowbray
events.text_span: Inspector of asylums and med. supt. of new asylum, Mowbray, Cape, 1889.

raw_text: DODDS, WILLIAM JOHN, M.D. (Edin.), 1879, gold medallist, M.B. and C.M., 1876, 1st class honours, D. Sc., 1878.—Inspector of asylums and med. supt. of new asylum, Mowbray, Cape, 1889.
DOHERTY, C. J.—Puisne judge, superior court, Quebec, 1891.

events.position: was commander of government steamers "Manchester" and "Pearl," from
events.text_span: was commander of government steamers "Manchester" and "Pearl," from July. 1859.

raw_text: DONNAN, J.—Master attendant, Colombo, Ceylon, 1863; was commander of government steamers "Manchester" and "Pearl," from July. 1859.

events.text_span: receiver-general and member exec. coun., April to December, 1894.

raw_text: DONNELLY, Hon. W. J. S.—Surveyor-general, Newfoundland, to 1882; receiver-general, 1882; resigned, 1889; M.L.A. for Placentia, 1893; receiver-general and member exec. coun., April to December, 1894.

DONOUGHMORE, 5TH EARL OF, K.C.M.G. (1879.)—JOHN LUKE GEORGE HELY HUTCHINSON, Educated at Balliol College, Oxford; second class in law and modern history, 1870; honorary private secretary to the Earl of Carnarvon, May, 1876, to Feb., 1878. Deputy-Lieutenant for the county of Tipperary. Assistant-commissioner to Sir H. Drummond Wolff, G.C.M.G., H.M.'s commissioner on the European commission for the organization of Eastern Roumelia under the 18th Article of the Treaty of Berlin in 1878.

DONOVAN, JUSTIN FOLEY, M.D., M.CH.L.M.—Medical officer and health officer, Killeagh dispensary, co. Cork, 1876 to 1879; surgeon, R.N., 1879; received the thanks of the admiralty, 1882 and 1888, "for zeal and devotion to duty" at R.N. hospitals, Port Royal and Malta; and of the medical director general, Royal Navy, for professional work, 1882, 1884 and 1886. Retired from the navy 1889; medical attendant, lepers' home, public general hospital and constabulary training depot, Spanish Town, Jamaica, 1889; medical examiner, civil service, widows and orphans pension fund, 1890.

DOUGLAS, THE HON. JOHN, C.M.G. (1877).—Born 1828; educated at Rugby and Durham University; has filled many important political offices in Queensland, including those of agent general and prime minister; special commissioner for British New Guinea, 1885-8, when he returned to duty as resident magistrate, Thursday Island.

events.position: Clerk assistant and librarian, Natal, leg. council
events.text_span: Clerk assistant and librarian, Natal, leg. council, Feb., 1889

raw_text: DOUGLAS-DE-FENZI, C. W. P.—Clerk assistant and librarian, Natal, leg. council, Feb., 1889; clerk, leg. council, 1893.

events.position: Minister of lands and agriculture, president board of lands and works
events.text_span: Minister of lands and agriculture, president board of lands and works, Victoria, Feb., 1886-90.

raw_text: DOW, THE HON. JOHN LAMONT.—Minister of lands and agriculture, president board of lands and works, Victoria, Feb., 1886-90.

DOWNER, THE HON. SIR J. W., K.C.M.G. (1887), Q.C.—Attorney-general, South Australia, 24 June, 1881, to 16 June, 1884, and att.-gen. and premier, 1885-7; delegate to Colonial Conference, 1887; chief secretary and premier, 1892-3.

DOWNES, MAJOR-GEN. M. FRANCIS, C.M.G. (1885), late R.A.—Retired, Oct., 1884; served in the Crimea till June, 1856, medal and clasp and Turkish medal; instructor of fortification, R.M. Coll., Sandhurst, for two years; commanded artillery in Mauritius and St. Helena for four years; instructor of gunnery at Woolwich, five years; colonel commandant, South Australia, June, 1877; member of Royal commission on defences, Sydney, 1881; secretary of defence, Victoria, April, 1885, to Mar., 1888; commandant, S. Australian forces, April, 1888-92.

DOWSON, R. W.—Accountant, Mauritius railways, 19th Sept., 1867; junr. audit. examiner, June, 1878; chief clerk rly. dept., April, 1882; ag. asst. manager, July, 1884, to April, 1885; head agent, Sept., 1891; ag. gen. manager, July to Sept., 1896.
DRAGE, FRED. S.—Cadet Sarawak Service, Jan., 1886; resdt. 2nd class, 3rd divn., June, 1892.

events.position: Appointed after competitive examination, a clerk in the lower (now second) division of the civil service, and assigned to the colonial office
events.text_span: Appointed after competitive examination, a clerk in the lower (now second) division of the civil service, and assigned to the colonial office, 16th May, 1883.

raw_text: DRAYSON, MATTHEW JOHN.—Appointed after competitive examination, a clerk in the lower (now second) division of the civil service, and assigned to the colonial office, 16th May, 1883.

DRAYTON, EDWARD RAWLE.—Clerk, colonial secretary's office, Barbados, Sept., 1878; served clerk in office of governor-in-chief of the Windward Islands, July, 1879; acting chief clerk, Nov., 1880, and May, 1881, to Jan., 1882; chief clerk, Jan., 1882; acting colonial secretary, Grenada, April, 1883; private secretary to Major-General Browne, July to Oct., 1884, provost marshal, Canada, April, 1885; acting colonial registrar, Sept., 1885; registrar supreme court, J.P., July, 1886; member leg. coun., May, 1887; inspr. of prisons, Mar., 1888; member ex. council, Nov., 1888; treasurer, May, 1890; ag. col. sec., Oct., 1891; June, 1892; administered govt. in 1891, 1893, 1894, and 1895, and govt. of St. Vincent, June, 1893; col. sec. and regr. gen., Jan., 1892.

DREW, W. L. G., C.M.G. (1890).—Served in Royal Navy, Feb., 1842, to June, 1856; subsequently employed under the government of N. S. Wales under treasurer of Queensland, Feb., 1866; auditor-general, Oct., 1877; chairman Civil Service Board, 1890; holds the rank of fleet paymaster, R.N.

events.text_span: head master, Govt. school, Seychelles, Jan., 1891

events.position: auditor, inspr. of schools, and secretary of councils
events.text_span: auditor, inspr. of schools, and secretary of councils, Sept., 1893.

raw_text: DRIVER, J.—Ed. at St. Mark's College (Chelsea) and Owen's College (Man.).—Matric. Lond. Univ., 1878; Int. B.A., 1880; head master, Govt. school, Seychelles, Jan., 1891; auditor, inspr. of schools, and secretary of councils, Sept., 1893.

DRURY, ALBERT VICTOR.—Supernumerary clerk, War Office, Feb., 1856, to Oct., 1861; clerk, colonial secretary's office, Queensland, Mar., 1862; transferred to executive council, Oct., 1863; clerk to executive council, Jan., 1867; private secretary to Gov. Sir W. W. Cairns, 1875-7; and to Adv. Sir A. H. Palmer, May to Dec., 1886.

DUBERLY, FREDERIC.—Educated at Chatham College, served in Eastern Extension Telegraph Company, and Straits Settlements, July, 1875, to July, 1882, when appointed cadet in the service of the British North Borneo Company; resigned May, 1883; chief clerk treasury, Perak, Nov., 1884; assistant-collector and magistrate, Krian, July, 1885; ag. collr. land rev., Larut, July, 1887; asst. to sec. to govt., June, 1892; ag. commsr. of lands, April, 1894; dist. mag. K. Kangsar, Feb., 1895, but continued to act as commsr. of lands.

DUFF, BENJAMIN.—Postmaster, Swellendam, Cape, April, 1863; telegraphist in charge of Swellendam, July, 1864; chief clerk and accountant, telegraph department, Mar., 1875; active service in Transkei, as lieut. P.A.O., Artillery, Nov., 1880, to April, 1881; acting general manager of telegraphs, June, 1883, to Feb., 1884; chief clerk, postal department, and suptdt. of telegraphs, Jan., 1886; acting secretary, general post office, June, 1890, to Jan., 1891; secretary, general post office, and suptdt. of telegraphs, 1892. Is member of the institution of electrical engineers.

DUFF, GRANT, THE RT. HON. M. E., P.C., G.C.S.I., C.I.E., F.R.S. (1880).—Educated at Balliol College, Oxford, B.A. 1850 under-secretary of state for India, 1868 to 1874; lord rector of Aberdeen University, 1866 to 1872; parliamentary under secretary of state for the colonies, April 29, 1880, to Aug. 5, 1881; M.P. for Har district, 1857 to 1881; governor of Madras, 1881-6.

events.text_span: Record clerk, immigration department, British Guiana, April, 1883
first class rate in Hindustani, Mar., 1885

raw_text: FF, ROBERT.—Record clerk, immigration department, British Guiana, April, 1883; 4th clerk, 1884; 3rd clerk, Oct., 1885; first class rate in Hindustani, Mar., 1885; 2nd clerk, 1893; chief clerk, Jan., 1894; has acted as immigr. agt. on several occasions, and also as sub immigr. agt.

FFERIN AND AVA, 1st MARQUIS of (created );—Viscount Clandeboye, 1871 (United Kingdom); Baron Dufferin, 1800; Baron Clandeboye, (Ireland); Baron Clandeboye, 1850 (United Kingdom); Bart., 1863; Privy Councillor, 1868; K.P., 1863; G.C.M.G. (1876); G.C.B. (civil); G.M.S.I.; G.M.I.E.; Frederick Temple Blackwood, appointed governor-general of Canada, and governor of Prince Edward Island, 1872, Ambassador at the Court at St. Petersburg, Feb., 1879; ambassador at Constantinople, 1882; proceeded to Egypt on special service, 1882; admiral of Ulster, 1884; viceroy of India, 1888; chancellor of the Royal University, Ireland, 1888; ambassador to Italy, 1888; ambassador at and warden of the Cinque Ports, 1891-1896.

events.position: Entered Crown Lands e. S. Australia
events.text_span: Entered Crown Lands e. S. Australia, Mar., 1867

events.position: secy. to commissr. of crown lands
events.text_span: secy. to commissr. of crown lands, Mar., 1896.

raw_text: FFIELD, THOMAS.—Entered Crown Lands e. S. Australia, Mar., 1867; chief clerk, July, 1867; secy. to commissr. of crown lands, Mar., 1896.

UFFY, THE HON. SIR CHARLES GAVAN, M.G. (1877), (Kt. BACH. 1873).—Was called to Irish bar in 1845, but was early engrossed duties as editor of the "Nation" newspaper, one of the leaders of the national party known Young Ireland. Was a state prisoner along with O'Connell in 1844, and Smith-O'Brien in 1848. Member for New Ross in 1852, defeating Thomas Redington, chief secretary for Ireland. Resigned his seat and emigrated to Australia at the commencement of 1856, and practised for a short time at the bar in Melbourne. Was elected a member of the first parliament of Victoria, and in 1857 became minister of public works in the first responsible government. In 1858 became president of the board of land and works. In 1861 was recalled to the home office. In 1867, after two years' absence in Europe, was re-elected to Parliament for the first time, and shortly afterwards was appointed chairman of a royal commission to devise the best means for effecting a federation of the Australian colonies. In 1871 became prime minister; and in the same year was chairman of an intercolonial conference of Australian cabinet ministers to urge on the colonial office the removal of restrictions on colonial legislation, which change has since been effected. In 1876, after two years' absence in Europe, was again re-elected to Parliament by one of the largest constituencies in the colony without personally visiting the district, and on the assembly being dissolved in May, 1877, was unanimously elected to the office of speaker. After three years' service he resigned, in order to return to Europe. Has since published "Young Ireland—a Fragment of History," "Four Years of Irish History," and "The League of North and South."

DUFFY, THE HON. JOHN GAVAN.—Eldest son of the above (Sir Charles Gavan Duffy). Educated at Stonyhurst. Vice-chancellor's prize, English essay, Melbourne Univ. Succeeded his father in representation of Dalhousie in legislative assembly of Victoria, 1874. President of board of land and works of Victoria, 1889; postmaster-general of Victoria, 1890; also attorney-general, 1892; member of cabinet without portfolio, 1892; resigned Jan., 1893; again postmaster-general, 1894.
DUFFY, WILLIAM.—Engineer of roads, Tasmania, 19th February, 1883.

DUNLOP, COLONEL SAMUEL, R.A., C.M.G. (1884)—Acting commissioner of police, S. S. Nov., 1870; acting police magistrate and commissr. of Ct. of Requests, P. W., April, 1871; acting deputy commissioner of police and coroner, Apr., 1871; acting deputy commissioner of police, Singapore, fire commissioner and deputy coroner, Aug., 1871; acting inspector-general of police, Dec., 1872; commissioner to Perak, Jan., 1874. In Nov., 1874, commissioner with the forces sent to quell the disturbances in the native state of Sungei Ujong; inspector-general, Straits police, May, 1875. In Nov., 1875, on the murder of Mr. Birch, appointed special commissioner (temporarily) for Perak affairs; organized the expedition which captured the Passir Salak stockades, and was present at the capture, Nov., 1875; commissioner to forces during operations in Perak, Dec., 1875; accompanied General Colborne's force up the Perak River, and across country to Kinta; acting resident councillor, Penang, 1884 to 1885; president of Singapore municipal commission, 1889; ret'd., 1890.

ELLIOTT, COLONEL JOHN, R.M.L.I., C.B. (1877), C.M.G. (1891).—Entered the Royal Navy in 8, second lieutenant, June, 1846; colonel, 1869; served in the China expedition, 1842 (medal); served in H.M.S. "Fox" during the Burmese war, 2 (officially thanked); mentioned in despatches; commanded the Royal Marines in the steam flotilla the Irrawaddy (mentioned in governor-general's despatch, medal with clasp for Pegu). Served at bombardment and surrender of the forts of Marsund, in Aug., 1854 (Baltic medal), and during the siege of Sebastopol in 1855, the expedition to Kertch and Yeni-Kale (medal with sp. 5th class of the Medjidie, and Turkish gal); has also the medal for the Kaffir war. Served as major of brigade to the force of Royal Marines landed in Mexico in 1863; lieut.-col., 1871 J.P. for Plymouth; in 1874 called to the bar, Middle Temple, and joined the western circuit; 1876 inspector of prisons and stipendiary P. British Guiana; inspector-general of police, Barbados, 1881; admind. govt. of St. Vincent 84 and 1889; and acted as col. secy., Barbados, 89, and again 1894; retired, 1895.

ERGUSSON, RIGHT HON. SIR JAMES, BART., M.P., G.C.S.I. (1885), K.C.M.G. (1874), E. (1880).—Educated at Rugby and Univer- College, Oxford; was lieutenant and captain be grenadier guards; served in the Crimeanaign, including the battles of Alma and ermann (wounded), and siege of Sebastopol, M.P. for Ayrshire 1854-57, and 1859-68; un- esfully contested Sandwich, 1859, Frome, 1875; Greenock, 1878; was under secretary of state for India and the home departments in Lord Derby's third, and Mr. Disraeli's first administration; governor South Australia, 1863; and governor of New Zealand, 1873; resigned 1874; governor of Bombay, March, 1880. to Mar., 1885; M.P. for Manchester, 1885 and 1886; under secretary of state foreign affairs, 3rd Aug., 1886; postmaster-general, 1891-92.

FINNEMORE, ROBERT ISAAC, F.R.G.S., F.Z.S., F.S.S., F.S.Sc., F.R.C.I., Hon. Corres. Mem. Vic. Ins.—Entered civil service, Natal, 1858; 2nd clerk surveyor-general's department, Mar., 1859; govt. land surveyor, 1863; chief clerk and draughtsman, 1864; transferred to the attorney-general's department, Dec., 1865; admitted to the bar, Jan., 1868; postmaster-general, 1876; R.M., city division, 1877 to 1878; acting colonial treasurer, 1877; has acted also as surveyor-general; edited Natal Almanac and Register, 1876-78; digest of decisions of supreme court; author Natal Law Reports from 1879, &c.; master and registrar, supreme court, and registrar, vice-admiralty court, 1878; R.M. and administrator of native law, Durban, March, 1881; J.P., 1881; acting puisne judge, supreme court, 1883; collr. of customs, regr. of shipping, emigration offr. and harbour comissnr., 1889; dep. chairman Natal harbour board; crown solic. and parliam. draftsman, 1894; mem. of civ. service bd.; is F.R.A.S., Roy. Meteor. Soc., Roy. Hist. Soc., mem. Soc. Arts. and various other British and foreign scientific societies; puisne judge, sup. ct., Natal, 1896.

FLEMING, SIR FRANCIS, K.C.M.G., C.I.E. (1887).—Called to the bar, Yd Temple, Nov., 1866; crown solicitor for Mauritius, 1869; district and stipendiary magistrate, 1872; acted as district judge, Seychelles, 1873; district judge, Jamaica, 1876; attorney-general, Barbados, 1878; acted as chief justice, St. Lucia, July, 1879; private secretary to Sir J. Strahan, administering the government of Cape, in 1880; puisne judge, British Guiana, 1881; acted as attorney-general, Aug. to Nov., 1882; Queen's advocate, Ceylon, 1883; attorney general, 1884; acted as chief justice, W. Dec., 1885; colonial secretary, Natal, Jan., 1886, but did not take up appointment, being appointed colonial secretary of Mauritius, Dec., 1886, administered govt., July, 1887, to Dec., 1888; col. sec., Hong Kong, 1889; administered the Feb. to Dec., 1890; governor, S. Leeward Islands, ditto, Leeward Islands, 1895.

FLEMING, SANFORD, C.E., C.M.G., LL.D. (St. Andrew's), 1884.—Engineer Northern Railway, Canada, 1856 to 1863; agent to Canadian and British Government for River Settlers, 1863, in reference to claims nominated by provinces of Canada, N. Brunswick and N. Scotia Government engineer-in-chief, colonial railway, 1863; engineer-in-chief, Canadian Pacific Railway, 1871 to 1880; chancellor, Queen's Univ., Canada, 1880; re-elected 1883, and again 1886; delegate from Canadian Institute and American Meteorological Society, York, to international geographical congress, Venice, 1881; representing the Dominion of Canada at international prime meridian conference, Washington, 1884; director Hudson Bay Company, Canadian Pacific railway, &c.; author of "Inter-colonial, an Historical Sketch," 1876, "From New Westminster," 1884, memoirs of "Prime Meridian for all Nations," "The Time," and various other works; delegate to colonial conference, 1887; representative of Canada, colonial conference, Ottawa, 1894; on mission to Hawaii, 1894, with Mr. Mercer; colonial office, negotiating for landing Pacific cable.

FLYNN, EDMUND JAMES, Q.C., LL.D.—Born 1847, at Percé, Gaspé; educated Quebec Seminary and Laval Univ. (L.L.L. with honours, LL.D. (Lav., 1878); Q.C. 1887; called to bar, 1873; professor of Roman law, Laval Univ., 1874; member Leg. Ass., Quebec, April, 1879; member ex. coun. and commissioner Crown in Chapeau Govt., Oct., 1879, to July, 1882; commissioner of railways in Ross Govt., Feb., 1883, and solicitor-gen., May, 1885.

FORD, CHARLES F.L.S.—Suptdt. Botanic Afforestation department, Hong Kong, 1871. 5th, 1862; 2nd secretary in Her Majesty's diplomatic service, Oct. 1st, 1862; chargé d'affaires at Carlsruhe from Oct. 15th, 1862, till Sept. 26th, 1864; transferred to Vienna, June 25th, 1864; sent to Buenos Ayres, Aug. 10th, 1865, where he was in charge of the mission from Dec. 1865, till Oct. 13th, 1866; transferred to Copenhagen, June 26th, 1866, where he was acting chargé d'affaires from Mar. 3rd till May 18th, 1867; sent to Washington, Mar. 28th, 1867, where he was acting chargé d'affaires from Sept. 1867, till Feb. 8th, 1868; secretary of embassy at Petersburg, Mar. 30th, 1871, where he was charged d'affaires from Nov. 16th, 1871, till 1872; transferred to Vienna, Oct. 26th, 1872; chargé d'affaires at Carlsruhe and Darmstadt, 11th, 1873; appointed Her Majesty's agent to attend the commission at Halifax, under the 22nd and 23rd Articles of the Treaty of Washington of 8th, 1871, July 26th, 1873; H.M.'s envoy ordinary and minister plenipotentiary to the United Mexican Republic, 22nd Feb., 1878; ditto to the Emperor of Brazil, 14th June 1879; ditto to the King of the Hellenes, 5th Mar., 1881; sent on special commission to Paris, and subsequently to Constantinople with Mr. Pennell of the colonial office in connection with the Newfoundland fisheries question, 1884; envoy extraordinary and plenipotentiary to the King of Spain, 1884; negotiated the commercial convention between Great Britain and Spain, signed, 1886; made a privy councillor, 1888; ambassador at Madrid, 1888; at Constantinople, 1892; at Rome, Comsn., Feb. to July, 1892; med. offr. special mission to Kumasi, Dec. 1894 to Jan. 1895; col. surg., Gambia, Feb., 1895.

events.position: acting master and registrar, supreme court

raw_text: FORDER, JAMES.—B.A., Cambridge, in 1860; head-master High School, Pietermaritzburg, July, 1868; acting master and registrar, supreme court, Mar., 1878; acting chief clerk, colonial office, 1878; resident magistrate 1880; J.P., 1880; now R.M. of Umgeni division, Pietermaritzburg.

FORREST, THE HON. SIR JOHN, K.C.M.G (1891), F.R.G.S., F.G.S., F.L.S., C.M.G. (1882). —Honorary Fellow of the Italian Geographical Society; Honorary Fellow of the Imperial Geographical Societies of Vienna and St. Petersburg; knight of the Italian crown; entered the survey department of Western Australia, 1865. Commanded exploring expeditions in 1869 into the interior in search of the remains of Dr. Leichhardt; in 1870 from Perth to Adelaide along the south coast; and in 1874 from Champion Bay, on the west coast, to the overland telegraph between Adelaide and Port Darwin, a journey of nearly 2,000 miles. For these services received the thanks of the governor and legislative council, the gold medal of the Royal Geographical Society of London, 22nd May, 1876, and a grant in fee of 5,000 acres of crown land; deputy surveyor-general of Western Australia, 1876; in 1878 and 1882 conducted the trigonometrical surveys of the Nichol Bay District, and the Gascoyne and Lyons District, in North-Western Australia, 1878; acted as Commissioner of crown lands; and surveyor-general; commissioner of crown lands, and surveyor-general of Western Australia, 1883, to Dec., 1890, during which time was member of the executive and legislative councils; in March, 1883, reported on the Kimberley district; in Dec., 1890, was sent for to form first ministry under responsible gov., in which he took the position of premier and treasurer; was one of the representatives of W. Australia at the National Australian Federation Convention, held at Sydney, 1891; author of "Explorations in Australia," 1865, "Notes on Western Australia," 1883, 1884, and 1885; delegate to colonial conference, 1887.

raw_text: ORD, HENRY FRANCIS.—Entered G.P.O., 1868; promoted to deeds office, 1872; appointed to inaugurate deeds office at Kokstad, served as clerk to chief mag. Transkei; in charge of deeds office, King Williamstown, 1884; clerk, insolvency branch, master's office, town, Jan., 1888; registrar and master, high court, Griqualand, July, 1890.

ORD, RICHARD.—Ed. at Collegiate, Shaw St., Liverpool; served for three years with a firm of stockbrokers in that town; emigrated to Australia, 1852, and employed for 2 years in aitor's office in Melbourne; returned to England, 1854, and employed for 3 yrs. by the Liverpool Trust; returned to Australia in 1858, and 3 yrs. in a solicitor's office in Ballarat, commenced business as a sharebroker at Daylesford, subsequently as an accountant, auditor and managing agent at Ballarat; elected auditor to the council and town council there by a large majority, and afterwards chosen as town clerk and treasurer successively; after six years' service, on creation of the Melbourne harbour trust, he was chosen as secy., and received numerous testimonials and valuable testimonials on leaving Ballarat, as delegate on behalf of the trust successfully floated loan of 250,000l. in London in 1883, soon after his return appointed one of the managers of the line, and received testimonials from members of the trust, their officers, and the press, on his transfer; resigned, 1892.

ORD, SIR THEODORE THOMAS, KT. BACH. —Called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1866; made judge, Straits Settlements, Mar., 1874; acting judge of Penang, 1874, to April, 1876; retired and returned to England; re-appointed master of the rolls, 1876; chief justice, 1886; retired, 1889.
FORDE, ROBERT MICHAEL.—Asst. col. surg., Madras, Nov., 1891; ag. dist. commsr. Axim, on special service on Anglo-French Bdy.

FORTESCUE, CAPTAIN EDMUND.—Ensign Rifle Brigade, Feb. 1855; lieut., June, 1855; capt., Sept., 1861; served in Indian mutiny, including siege and capture of Lucknow, affair of Koorslee and taking of Fort Oomereah (medal and clasp); served on north-west frontier in 1863-64, including action of Shubkhudder (medal and clasp); passed P.H. in Hindustani, July, 1863; station staff officer Nowshera, and cantonment magistrate, Jan., 1865; brigade major, Mooltan, Oct. 1865; Umballa, Oct., 1867, to Sept. 1871; supt. penal settlement, Massaruni, Br. Guiana, July, 1878; ag. inspr. of prisons, April to Sept., 1879; inspr. of prisons, 1881; also managing director, orphan asylum, and inspr., government school for vagrants and youthful offenders, 1883; inspr. commdt. police, Trinidad; inspr. wts. and measures, and capt. vol. fire brigade, and ex officio J.P. for the island, 1888.

FORTH, J. OGILVIE.—Audit Clerk, Fiji, Aug. 1883; acct. native taxes and clk. native accounts, June, 1889; chief clk. and off., Oct., 1890; col. audr., Nov., 1891; Oct. 1890 proceeded to Tonga to audit accounts of Tongan govt., for which he received thanks of King George Tubou's cabinet.

events.text_span: Inspector-general of police, New South Wales, 7th Oct., 1874.

raw_text: FOSBERY, E.—Inspector-general of police, New South Wales, 7th Oct., 1874.

FOSTER, THE HON. GEO. E., B.A., D.C.L.—Born 1847. Educated common and superior schools, King's County, N. Brunswick; Douglas gold medallist Univ., N. B., 1866, B.A., 1868; medallist, ancient rhetoric, Edin., 1873; prof. classics, Univ. N. B. 1873-9; returned to parliament, 1882; minister of marine and fisheries for Dominion, Dec., 1885; minister of finance, 1888 to 1896; represented Canada at Colonial Confce., Ottawa, 1894.

raw_text: FOSTER, JOSEPH.—Clerk to R.M., Port Elizabeth, 1872; first clerk to C.C., Oudtshoorn, 1874; at George, 1876; chief clerk to master sup. ct., 1881; sec. to law dept. and permanent head att.-gen.'s div., 1884; registrar of deeds, 1889; one of the editors revised edn. Cape Statutes, 1886; author of "The Executor's Reference," "Legal Forms for Common Use," and "The Deeds Registry Practice," Cape Colony; J.P. for the colony.

FOSTER, WILLIAM JOHN.—Admitted to the bar, N.S. Wales, 1858; crown prosecutor, 1859-62 1864-77; member of leg. conn., 1877-80; attorney-general, 1877-89; member of executive council, 1878; M.L.A., 1880-82; minister of justice, 1881-3; again M.L.A., 1885-8; Q.C., 1886; again attorney-general, 1887; puisne judge, 1888.

events.position: Assistant clerk, petty civil court of Port of Spain
events.text_span: Assistant clerk, petty civil court of Port of Spain, Trinidad, Aug., 1874.

raw_text: FOURNIER, A.—Assistant clerk, petty civil court of Port of Spain, Trinidad, Aug., 1874.

FOURNIER, THE HON. TELESPHORE.—Born 1824; called to the bar of Lower Canada, 1846; created a Q.C. 1863; sat in the House of Commons and the Quebec Assembly for several years; sworn of the privy council, 7th November, 1873; and was minister of inland revenue from that date until 8th July, 1874, when appointed minister of justice; transferred to postmaster-generalship, 19th May, 1875; retired from government on his appointment as a puisne judge of the supreme court of the dominion, 8th October, same year; the supreme court act and the insolvency act, 1875, were carried by him as minister of justice; resigned 1895.

events.position: Clerical Asst., pub. wks. office. Br. Guiana

raw_text: FOWLER, F.—Clerical Asst., pub. wks. office. Br. Guiana, Feb., 1878; 3rd clerk, Jan., 1881; 1st clerk and draughtsman, crown lds. dept., Nov., 1884; 3rd Govt. Surveyor, May, 1886.

FOWLER, GEO. MERRICK.—Ed. Cheltenham coll.; writer, Ceylon service, Jan., 1874; pol. mag., Galagedara, Sept., 1876; landing surveyor, Galle, Feb., 1878; pol. mag., Navalapitiya, Jan., 1880; assist. coll. customs, Galle, June, 1880; dist. judge, Matara, May, 1886; assist. govt. agt., Matara, 1887; ditto, Kalutara, 1888; ditto, N'Eliya, 1891; ditto, Trincomalee, 1895.

FOXON, FRANK ERNEST.—Third class clk. Civil Service, Natal, and second clk. and Zulu intrp., Umgeni div., Nov., 1880; ag. clk. of court, registrar circuit court, and sub-distributor of stamps, Weenen country, 1886; ag. clk. of court, deputy clk. of peace, and sub-distributor of stamps, Upper Umkomangi div., on 2nd April, 1887, confirmed May, 1887; administrator native law, Impendle, Mar., 1889; ditto, Inanda Location, Jan., 1890; ag. res. mag., Lower Tugela div., April to June, 1890; acted frequently as R.M. at Verulam and Stanger, 1890 to 1894; R.M. for colony, and mag., Ndwelwe div., July, 1894; J.P. for colony, 1893; served at commencement of Zulu war, 1879, in Imperial Transport dept., afterwards as lieut. Natal N. Pioneers (medal and clasp); served as levy leader in command of Sibepu's contingent and Yamela's mounted men against Dinuzulu in Zululand, 1888; lieut., Natal Carbineers, April, 1889.

FRANCIS, CLAUDE A.—Inspector of police, British Guiana, 1880; promoted to higher grade, 1883; and to county inspr., 1891; acted as dep. inspr. gen. for some months in 1892 and Feb. to Sept., 1895, Nov., 1895, to April, 1896; as inspecgen., May to Aug., 1896; as suptdr. George fire brigade, Nov., 1895, to Oct., 1896; ex J.P. for the colony; served with the UX through the Galeka war and Gaika revt 1887-8 (medal and clasp); author of a "Ms for the use of the police and constabulary in Guiana," 1891; and a "Criminal Code for R. 1895; suptdt. of police, Fiji, 1897.

FRASER, CHARLES A.—Educated at Wells College; entered 54th Regt., Jan., 1875; ret'd 1877; in colonial forces in South Africa (medal and clasp); assistant inspector, Gold constabulary, 1882; dist. commr., 1889; treas. &c., Falkland Islands, 1891.

FRASER, J. J., Q.G.—Attorney-general premier, New Brunswick; retired, 1882; judge, supreme court, 1883.
FRASER, J. O.—Surveyor-general, Newfoundland, 19th Dec., 1882; postmaster-gen 1885.

raw_text: FRASER, THE HON. SIR MALCOLM, K.C.M.G. (1887), C.M.G. (1881).—Employed in various departments of the government, New Zealand, 1857-70; surveyor-general, Western Australia, and member of the executive and legislative councils, 1870; col. secretary and senior member, legislative council, Jan., 1883; represented the colony at the colonial Conference, Sydney, 1877; commissioner, Melbourne International Exhibition, representative to the Australasian Conference, Sydney, 1883; executive commissioner, Paris Exhibition, 1886; delegate, intercolonial conference, Sydney, 1888; administered government, 1889 to Oct., 1890; ret'd., 1890; agent general the Colony, 1892.

raw_text: FRASER, MALCOLM A. C.—Educated at St. Edward's School, Bromsgrove; probation in colonial secretary's office, Western Australia, April, 1876; second clerk, land and survey department, June, 1876; transferred to colonial secretary's office, Aug., 1877; clerk and assistant secretary to governor, July, 1878; also assistant clerk to the executive council; clerk to governor, Sir W. C. F. Robinson, K.C.M.G., April, 1881; is also meteorological reporter.

FRASER, SIMON JAMES.—Barrister-at-law, stipendiary magistrate of Tobago and J.P., 1868; coroner for the island, July, 1868; has on several occasions as attorney-general from 1872, changed to Windward magistracy, 4th April, 1875, acted as inspector of immigrants, 1875 and inspector of schools, 1876 to 1884; acting attorney-general, Oct., 1883; commissioner of district, No. 1, Dec., 1883; police magistrate, Windward district, Nov., 1884; chairman Metawar commission, 2nd Dec., 1884; member board of education and chairman board of health, Aug., 1886, of quarantine bd., Sept., 1887; judge of petty
FREELAND, J., M.R.C.S.E.—Medical officer, district 3, Antigua, Jan., 1871.

BEER, GERALD DUDLEY, L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.C.S. (Eng.). 1888.—House surg. general hosp., Singapore; house surg. gen. hosp., Singapore, 1890; col. surg. Malacca, Feb., 1893. REISLICH, J. G.—Resident magistrate, Xosa district, Cape Colony, 19th Dec., 1882; C.C. R.M., Middleburg, 15th Sept., 1884. REMANTLE, GENERAL SIR ARTHUR J. LYON, K.G. (1894), C.B.—Lt. Col. commanding Isream Guards, 1878; A.D.C. to the Duke of Edinburgh, 1881; commanded at Suakin, 1884-5 (1st class Medjidie); chief of staff, Egypt, Nov., 1882; D.A.G. for auxiliary forces, 1886; commanding troops in Scotland, 1892; Governor, Malta, 1894.

REMANTE, VICE-ADMIRAL THE HON ROBERT, R.N., C.B., C.M.G. (1874).—Selected to the Order of St. Michael and George, for service during the Ashantee war 1874, having been some time senior naval officer on the station; naval aide-de-camp to the Queen, 19th Jan., 1881; now commander-in-chief, Cape station.

events.position: Clerk to the police magistrates, District D

raw_text: RENOCH, C. W.—Clerk to the police magistrates, District D, St. Kitts, 1866.

RENOCH, MAJ.-GEN. GEORGE ARTHUR, R.A., C.G. (1877).—Educated at Sandhurst and Woolwich; joined R.A. as lieut. in 1860; proceeded to North America in Dec., 1861, with expeditionary force sent out in consequence of the "Trent Affair"; served R.A., Kingston, from 1862 to 1866; qualified as 1st class gunnery instructor in 1867; 1st inspector of warlike stores in 1868; appointed W. at Quebec in 1869 on the withdrawal of Imperial troops; inspector of artillery, with rank of lieut.-col., in 1870; organized the present batteries of artillery in 1871; commissioner North West Mounted Police, and stipendiary magistrate for the territories, in Dec., 1873; raised, organized, and equipped the force; commanded expedition sent from the Red River to the base at the Rocky Mountains in 1874; inspector of lake stores, Devonport, 1878 to 1883; commandant Queensland forces, with rank of col., 1891; commandant, N. S. Wales forces, 1893.

events.position: Secretary and controller, general post office
events.text_span: Secretary and controller, general post office, Cape, 1st Aug., 1890

raw_text: RENOCH, S. R., C.M.G. (1895).—Secretary and controller, general post office, Cape, 1st Aug., 1890; controller post office savings bank, 1884; master-general, 1892.
RERE, AUBREY TEMPLE.—Cadet, Sarawak force, Oct., 1886; asst. resdt., May, 1890; resdt., 4th class, 4th divn., Jan., 1894.
ROST, THE HON. JOHN, C.M.G. (1879).—L.A., Cape of Good Hope, field commandant of Boer volunteers, and late of Cape mounted rifles; commissioner for agriculture, Cape Colony, 1893.

(1892).—1 Westminster and Oriel Coll., Oxon.; private secretary (unpaid) to Sir Robert Herbert at the Colonial Office, Nov., 1886; secy. to royal commission for the division of Malta into electoral districts, Jan., 1888; secy. to the Behring Sea commission, June, 1891, and to the joint Behring Sea commission at Washington, Jan., 1892; on the staff of the Br. agent, Behring Sea arbitration, 1892-3.

FULLER, F. C.—Cadet, Fiji, 1884; resident commissioner, Rotumah, 1889; district commissioner, Lagos, 1892.
FULLER, THOMAS.—Architect, Parliamentary buildings, Canada, October, 1859, to July, 1867; chief architect, public works, 9th December, 1881.

raw_text: FYNN, HENRY FRANCIS.—Clerk and interpreter, Newcastle division, 8th Mar., 1864; Umkomasi division, 7th Aug., 1865; camp-master, Zulu coronation expedition, August, 1873; interpreter to pursuing column through the Maluti mountains, Langalibalele expedition, November, 1873; administrator of native law, Umsinga division, 22nd June, 1874; justice of the peace for the counties of Weenen and Klip River, June, 1874; R.M., Umsinga division, 2nd Feb., 1876; in charge of the native levies in Umsinga division during the war, 1879; resident with Cetywayo, 1882; resumed his duties in Natal, 1883; advocate of supreme court, Nov., 1884.

FYSH, THE HON. SIR PHILIP OAKLEY, K.C.M.G. (1896).—Premier and chief sec., Tasmania, 1887 to 1892; member legislative council for district of Buckingham; treasurer in Mr. Kennersley's ministry, 1873-5, and without portfolio to July, 1876; premier without office Aug., 1877 to Mar., 1878, and without office in Mr. Giblin's 1st administration; member exec. coun.; magistrate of the colony; pres. central bd. of health; major commanding Tasmanian Volunteer Rifle Regt., 1880-4.
GABRIEL, J. S., M.R.C.S.E.—Medical officer, Antigua, district 6, 1881.

GAFFNEY, F. SEBASTIAN BURKE.—Assist. supt. of works and surveys, S.S., July, 1890; ag. supt. of wks. and surveys, Malacca, July, 1891; confirmed Oct., 1892; ag. supt. of works and surveys, Singapore, May, 1894, to Apl., 1895; and from Mar., 1896.

events.text_span: chief draftsman, P.W. dept., Hong Kong, Sept., 1890.

raw_text: GALE, CHARLES HENRY.—Assoc. M.I.C.E.; chief draftsman, P.W. dept., Hong Kong, Sept., 1890.

GAGNON, CHARLES ANTOINE ERNEST.—Educated at St. Ann's Coll.; notary public, 1869; since 1885 pres. of Quebec board of notaries; elected to Quebec legislature, 1878-81, and since 1883; prov. sec., 1887; sheriff of Quebec, 1890.

raw_text: GALE, WALTER A.—Ed. High Sch., Perth, St. Peter's Coll., Adelaide, and Exet. Coll., Oxon.; 3rd master, High Sch., Perth, 1885; asst. regr., col. sec.'s office, West Aust., 1886; sec. to Central Bd. of Edn., 1888; ag. regr. gen.; regr. of patents, &c., Nov., 1889; confirmed Sept., 1890; in charge of census for 1891.

GALGEY, OTHO, L.R.C.S. IreI., M.K. and Q.C.P.I., L.M.—Medical officer, No. 5 District, St. Vincent, Feb., 1872; assistant colonial surgeon, Castries, St. Lucia, Nov., 1878; acted in 1888, 1893, and 1894 as col. surgeon, St. Lucia.

events.position: barrister, King's Inns, Dub., Trinity Term

raw_text: GALLWEY, SIR MICHAEL HENRY, K.C.M.G. (1888), C.M.G. (1883).—Educated Trin. Coll., Dub.; B.A.; barrister, King's Inns, Dub., Trinity Term, 1853; Munster circuit; attorney-general, Natal, 1857; member ex. and leg. councils; president, Transvaal and Zululand boundary comm., 1878; received the thanks of the S. of S.; ch. justice, Natal, 1890.

GALLWEY, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR THOMAS LIONEL J., R.E., K.C.M.G. (1889).—2nd lieut., R.E., Mar., 1839; served in the West Indies from Jan., 1842, to June, 1845; employed in Ireland during the famine, 1846-47, as inspecting officer, under the Board of Works; served in Canada, 1849-58; employed 1858-62 in the construction of fortified position, Gosport; member of the ordnance select committee, 1862-65; member of special military commission to the United States during the war of 1864; employed as C.R.E. in building works of defence at Quebec, 1865-68; commandant, school of military engineering at Chatham, 1868-75; commanding royal engineers and colonel on staff, Gibraltar, 1877-79; inspector-general of fortifications, 1880-82; governor and commander-in-chief of Bermuda, 1882-88.
GALT, SIR THOS., KT. BACH. (1888).—Ch. Just., Common Pleas, Ontario; retired, 1894.
GANADO, LUIGI.—LL.D., Malta University, 1856; admitted to Malta bar, 1856; examiner in law to university, 1881; judge of supreme court, 1886; also member of university senate, 1887.

raw_text: GARCIA, A. H.—Inspector-general of war department, Cape of Good Hope, 1 July, 1882; C.C. and R.M., Stockenstrom, 1884.

GARCIA, GEORGE LEWIS.—Ed. Stonyhurst Coll., First B.A., Univ. of Loud., 1866; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Hilary, 1868; member leg. coun., Trinidad, 1882; agc. solicitor-general, April to Dec., 1885, Feb. to May, 1886, June to July, 1888; confirmed Aug., 1888; attorney-general, 1892; member of the royal commission on the franchise and division of the colony into electoral districts, Jan., 1888.

GARNEAU, PIERRE.—Merchant and member of board of trade Quebec; late govt. director of North Shore Railway; Mayor, 1870-73; M.L.A., Quebec, 1873-78 and 1881-86; member of provincial ex. council and ministry as commr. of agric., pub. wks. and crown lands, 1874-78; M.L.C., Quebec, 1887, and again commr. of agric., pub. wks. and crown lands to 1891.

GARNETT, T. TAYLOR.—Clerk of consolidated board, Barbados, Oct., 1878, to Mar., 1881; clerk Lock Hospital, July, 1880, to Mar., 1881; clerk of market and inspector weights and measures, Mar., 1881; acting clerk, executive committee, June, 1883; acting secretary poor law board, Mar. to Oct., 1884; inspector of seamen's lodging houses, 1884; secretary to finance commission, 1885.

raw_text: GARRARD, C. G.—Ed. Haileybury; dep. regr. sup. ct., Penang, Jan., 1892; ag. dep. regr. sup. ct., Singapore, Feb. to Oct. 1893; ag. regr. supt. ct., Penang, July, 1894; ag. dep. regr. sup. ct. and mag., Malacca, April, 1895; also ag. collr., ld. rev., and offr. in charge of treasury, Malacca, Aug., 1895; ag. dep. regr., sup. ct., Penang, Mar., 1896; asst. regr. and mag., Malacca, 1896.

GARRAWAY, DAVID G.—Entered the provost-marshall's office, Grenada, in Jan., 1867; chief clerk, colonial secretary's office, 1868; clerk of councils, 1868 to 1878; deputy-secretary, registrar and clerk of the crown, and secretary, board of education, 1871 to 1878; commissioner of census, 1871; acting auditor, 1871 to 1872, and 1873; secretary to executive committee, 1875-6; actg. treasurer 1876, 1877, 1879, 1880, 1881, 1882; ex officio a member of the legislature; author, 1878; notary public, 1880; compiler of census, 1881; acting treasurer, St. Lucia, 1882 to 1883; acting police magistrate, St. Vincent, 1883 to 1884; treasurer, St. Lucia, July, 1884.

raw_text: GARRAWAY, R. F.—Postmaster, Dominica, June, 1881; secretary, board of health and quarantine board, Sept., 1881; ag. dist. mag., Dist. 5, April, 1893.

GARRETT, MARK—In F.A.M.P., Cape, Feb. 1876, to Nov., 1879; clerk to C.C. and R.M. Ser-Williamstown, Sept., 1880; clerk col. secy.'s office, Capetown, May 1884; secy. to civil service commission, Mar., 1886.

GARRICK, THE HON. SIR J. F., Q.C., K.M.G. (1886), C.M.G. (1885).—Secretary for public lands and mines, Queensland, 6th Feb., 1878; attorney-general, 7th Dec., 1878, to 21st Jan., 1879; colonial treasurer and postmaster-general, 13th Feb. to 31st Dec., 1883, when he resigned the first office; agent-general for that colony in London, June, 1884, to June, 1888; was delegate to the Colonial conference, 1887; again agent-general 1890-5.

GATT, C.—Suptdt. govt. printing office, Malta, Feb., 1882; was private secretary to Sir Edward Wood, H.M.'s diplomatic agent and consul-general Tunis, Sept., 1868, to May, 1871; assistant secretary, Gozo, 1892.
GATT, MAJOR-GEN. SAVERIO, C.M.G. (1885)—Ensign, R.M.F.A., 1825; lieutenant and adjutant, 1837; captain, 1847; major, 1858; lieutenant colonel, 1861; colonel, 1866; hon. major-general, 1877.

raw_text: GATTY, STEPHEN HERBERT, Q.C.—Sch. Winch. Schl. and New Coll., Oxon.; called to the bar, Middle Temple, Nov., 1874; went the N. circuit; attorney-general, Leeward Islands, June 1883; acting chief justice, and local commissioner West India Incumbered Estates Court for Antigua, June to Oct., 1884; chancellor of the diocese of Antigua, July, 1884; local commissioner Incumbered Estates Court for St. Kitt's, Oct., 1884; attorney general, Trinidad, Dec., 1885; chancellor of diocese and admgt. advocate, 1887; chairman, Roy. Commission on franchise and electoral districts, 1889, and of Royal comsn. on Metayer System in Tobago, 1890; puisne judge, S S., 1892; ch. jus., Gibraltar, 1895.

raw_text: GAVIN, J. C.—Secretary to treasury, receiver general and paymaster-general, New Zealand, 1st Mar., 1860; asst. controller and audr.-general, 1890.
