raw_text: GAYLEARD, CHRISTOPHER—Ed. Crank grammar school, and Guy's hospital; surgeon to Bahia railway coy.; resident medical officer, Kingston hospital, Jamaica, 1865-6; lazaretto, 1868; parochial medical officer, 1870; special commissioner to Panama to report on Jamaicans there, 1887; district medical officer, Kingston, 1888.

events.position: Member of legislative council, Tasmania, since
events.text_span: Member of legislative council, Tasmania, since 1871

raw_text: GELLIBRAND, HON. W. A. B.—Member of legislative council, Tasmania, since 1871; president legislative council, July, 1884.

GENIS, MICHAEL CHRISTIAN.—Clerk to solicitor, Transvaal, 1874; clerk to R.M., prosecutor, and postmaster, Christiana, 1876; clerk to R.M., Christiana, 1880; ditto, Rustenburg, July, 1880; pensioned upon retrocession in 1881; registrar of deeds, Stellaland, Sept., 1884; re-appointed, treasurer and distributor of stamps; clerk to land commission, British Bechuanaland, Nov., 1885; registry clerk and J.P., Vryburg, July, 1886. GENNADIUS, P., director of agriculture, Cyprus, 1896. GIBBES, REGINALD PRESCOTT.—Cadet, S. S., Nov. 1889; passed final exam., Tamil, Jan., 1892; ag. 3rd Mag., Singapore, Aug. to Nov., 1892; ag. st. offir., Nebong Tebal, P. W., Mar., 1894; 2nd st. prot. of immigrants, Penang. May, 1896. GIBRALTAR, 4TH BISHOP.—See SANDFORD. GIBSON, FRED. ALBAN.—Clk. Mauritius Civil service after exam. Oct. 1875; clk. despatch branch, p. 1878; ag. précis writer, Nov. 1882 to June, 1883; ag. chf. clk. and sec. to exec. and leg. coun., Mar. 1890; clk. exec. and leg. coun., Sep., 1892; ag. junr. asst. col. sec., Sep., 1892; chf. clk., Mar., 1894; ag. asst. col. sec., June, 1894, to Jan., 1895; June to Dec., 1895, and Mar. to Sept., 1896. GIBSON, JAS. YOUNG.—Clerk and Zulu interp. Ct. of R.M.; sub. distrib. of stps., and regr. circuit ct., Newcastle, Natal, April, 1882; also p. clerk of peace, Mar., 1887; asst. comsr. and M., Nqutu dist., Zululand, Feb., 1889; ditto, twandwo dist., May, 1889. GIDDY, RICHARD WILLIAM SHEPSTONE.—A., LL.M. (Cantab.); acting asst. law. adviser, re, 1886; confirmed, 1888; is also J.P., and airman of gold commission, Knysna. GILES, MAJOR JAMES.—Commandant of volunteers, with rank of major, 1873, and R.M.,ired County, Natal, 1874; educated at Eton, and rin. Coll., Camb.; as cornet in 14th Light dragoons, 1853; served in Persian campaign under r James Outram, 1857, and in Indian mutiny campaign under Sir Hugh Rose and Sir Robert Napier; engaged in several skirmishes and general actions, and at the siege and capture of Jhansi, 5th pl., 1858; received Central Indian medal and asp. assistant clerk legislative council, Natal, 1880-89; magistrate, Upper Umkomanzi divn., 1889. GILL, C. I.—Puisne judge, superior court, Montreal, province of Quebec, 1879. GILL, THOMAS.—Entered colonial service, S. Australia, Feb., 1865; accountant, treasury, Jan., 1883; under treasurer, 1894. GILLIES, THE HON. DUNCAN.—Premier and treasurer of Victoria, also minister of railways and minister of mines; resigned Nov., 1890; president the board of land and works, and commissioner of crown lands and surveys, May, 1868; held the fee of commissioner of railways and roads, 10th June, 1872, to 7th Aug., 1875; was commissioner of crown lands, &c., 20th Oct., 1875, to 21st May, 1877; commissioner of railways, &c., 5th Mar., 1880, also Mar., 1883, to Feb., 1886, when he took office as premier and treasurer on the retirement of Mr. Service; resigned 1891; was one of the representatives of Victoria at the Federation Convention, 1891; agt.-gen. for Victoria, 1894 to 1897. GILKARD, M. A.—Began his political life with the organization of Manitoba as a province of the Dominion; was a member of the local government under the first three lieutenant-governors, and occupied the positions of provincial treasurer, provincial secretary, and minister of agriculture; in 1872 appointed senior member of the north west council and a senator of Canada, which latter position he still holds. GIROUARD, DESIRÉ, Q.C.—Born 1836. Ed. Montreal Coll., called to the bar, Canada, 1860; B.L. 1876; represented Jacques Cartier County, 1878 to 1895; judge supreme ct., Canada, 1895.
GISBORNE, HARTLEY, A.M., Cam., C.E., M.A.I.E.E.—Ed. at Mercers school and Windsor Coll., Canada; asst. geological surveyor, Canada, 1876; telegraph engineer, 1880; dist. tel. supert. Manitoba, 1882.

GISBORNE, WILLIAM.—Was commissioner of crown lands, New Zealand, from 1848 to 1853; from 1853 to 1869, under secretary; was colonial secretary, and for some time also minister of public works, with seats first in the legislative council, and afterwards in the House of Representatives, from 1869 to 1872; N. Z. Government insurance commissioner from 1870 to 1875; became, in 1877, again a member of the House of Representatives; was a member of the Grey Ministry from July to Oct., 1879.

GLADSTONE, RIGHT HON. WILLIAM EWART.—Was educated at Eton, and at Christ Church, Oxford, where he attained a double first class in 1831; graduated M.A. 1834; and received the honorary degree of D.C.L. in 1848; was a lord of the treasury in Dec. 1834; under-secretary for the colonies from Jan. to April, 1835; vice-president of the board of trade, and master of the mint, from Sept. 1841, to May, 1843, when he became president of the board of trade, retaining the office of master of the mint; resigned both Feb. 1845; was secretary of state for the colonies from Dec., 1845, to July, 1846; chancellor of the exchequer from Jan. 1853, to Feb. 1855; lord high commissioner extraordinary to the Ionian Islands, Nov., 1858; reappointed chancellor of the exchequer, June, 1859; is author of 'The State in its Relations with the Church,' 'Church Principles considered in their Results,' and other works; was M.P. for Newark from 1832 to Jan. 1846, and sat for the university of Oxford from 1847; defeated 1865, and returned for South Lancashire; at the general election of 1868 was defeated for South Lancashire, and was returned as M.P. for Greenwich; first lord of the treasury, Dec., 9, 1868; chancellor of the exchequer and master of the mint, in conjunction with other appointments, 1873; resigned Feb. 1874; was returned as M.P. for Midlothian, and became first lord of the treasury and chancellor of the Exchequer, April, 1880; resigned the latter office, 16th Dec., 1882; resigned on defeat of the budget proposals, June, 1885; again first lord of the treasury, Feb. to Aug., 1886; and 17th Aug., 1892, to April, 1894.

events.position: Third clerk in the immigration department
events.text_span: Third clerk in the immigration department, British Guiana, 1st Jan., 1873

raw_text: GLADWIN, HENRY JAMES.—Third clerk in the immigration department, British Guiana, 1st Jan., 1873; second clerk, 1st Aug., 1873; chief clerk, 1884; sub-immigration agent, 1885.

events.position: served in the Crimean and China wars
events.text_span: served in the Crimean and China wars, 1857

raw_text: GLASGOW, 7TH EARL OF, THE RIGHT HON. SIR DAVID BOYLE, G.C.M.G. (1892).—Captain (retired) R.N.; served in the Crimean and China wars, 1857 (medal and clasp); J.P. for Ayrshire, and chairman of the county council; governor of New Zealand, 1892-1897.
flags: name_block_extra:THE RIGHT HON. SIR DAVID BOYLE

raw_text: GLASS, DAVID, Q.C.—Born 20th July, 1829; Ed. Gram. Sch., Lond. (Ont.); called to the bar; Q.C. for Ontario, 1875; elected alderman in 1855; and mayor in 1858-64-65; member of commons, Canada, for E. Middlesex, 1872; has been pol. mag., recorder, and also dep. judge of Middlesex, and bencher of law soc. Ont.; removed to Manitoba, 1882; solr. of Winnipeg, 1884; member leg. ass., Winnipeg, 1886; unanimously elected speaker, 1887 to 1888.

events.text_span: acted as commissioner, Kyrenia, July to Sept., 1892

events.position: comr. and local commdt. of police, Kyrenia
events.text_span: comr. and local commdt. of police, Kyrenia, 1894.

raw_text: GLOSSOP, FRANK G.—Clerk, War Office, Nov., 1878; clerk, auditor-general, Cyprus, Mar., 1879; first clerk to receiver-general, Sept., 1879; island treasurer, April 1885; acting auditor, July, 1889 to Dec., 1890; British delegate of Evkaf, July, 1889; supdt. of census, 1891; acted as commissioner, Kyrenia, July to Sept., 1892; ag. rec. gen. 1892; ag. comr., Kyrenia and Larnaca, 1893; comr. and local commdt. of police, Kyrenia, 1894.

events.text_span: 3rd class clerk, police department, Western Australia, 1864

raw_text: GLYDE, GEORGE FREDERICK.—3rd class clerk, police department, Western Australia, 1864; 2nd class clerk, 1875; accountant, treasury, 1878; accountant, crown lands, 1885.

events.text_span: Secretary, department of public works, Canada, Jan., 1885.

raw_text: GOBEIL, ANTOINE.—Secretary, department of public works, Canada, Jan., 1885.

GOBLE, BLAKE.—Gazetted ensign 3rd West India Regiment, 1852; lieutenant, 1853; transferred to 57th regiment, on service in the Crimea, 1855; justice of the peace, Natal, 1860; surveyor-general department, 1874; 1875, transferred to the port establishment; in the same year to the excise department, 1877; inspector of Lazaretto; appointed to the customs department, 1880.
GOLDBY, WALTER ERNEST.—Clk. Colonial audit branch; E. and A. dept., Feb. 1890; local auditor, Zululand, July, 1893.

GOLDIE, CHARLES.—Appointed, after a competitive examination, an examining officer H.M. customs, Greenock, Sept., 1863; inspector of invoices and chief landing surveyor H.M. customs, Jamaica, Oct., 1869; one of the commissioners to inquire into the pilot service of Jamaica, April, 1870; acting collector of customs, Kingston, 1873, and 1875 to 1876; acted in 1874 as island treasurer and as collr.-genl. of customs, 1875 and 1885; collr. of customs and shipping master, Kingston, 1883.

GOLDNEY, SIR JOHN TANKERVILLE, KT. B. (1873).—Educated Harrow and Trinity College, Cambridge; called to the Bar, Inner Temple, 1869, Northern Circuit; Attorney-general, Leeward Islands, April, 1880; acting chief justice, May, 1881; puisne judge, British Guiana, June, 1883; judge, supreme court, Straits Settlements, Mar., 1887; chief justice, Trinidad, 1892.

GOLDSWORTHY, MAJOR JOSIAH WEBBE.—Served in 86th Royal Regt., 1867 to 1875; acting A.D.C. to Sir Henry Barkly, K.C.B., Governor of the Cape, 1872; inspector, drill and musketry instructor, frontier armed and mounted police, 1877; J.P. eastern districts, Cape Colony, 1877; agt. adjt. F.A.M.P., 1878; field adjt. to Col. C. D. Griffith, C.M.G., during Gaika and Galeka rebellion, 1877-8 (mentioned in despatches); capt. and adjt. Cape Mounted Riflemen, 1878-82, field adjt. to Col. Bayly C.M.G., at the final storming and taking of Moirosi's Mountain, 1879 (mentioned in despatches); honorary A.D.C. to Governor Sir Bartle Frere, 1878 (S. African war medal and clasp, 1877-8-9); detached for special service at training depôt, King William's Town, 1880-81; served in Basutoland, Jan. to July, 1882; major Cape Infantry Regiment, 1882-6; major and adjt., Duke of Edinburgh Volunteer Rifles (Cape Town), Jan., 1890.

GOLDSWORTHY, SIR R. T., K.C.M.G. (1889), C.M.G. (1874).—Served during the Indian mutiny in the volunteer cavalry of Havelock's forces; present in the actions of Oonao, Busserutgunj, and recapture of Busserutgunj; for these services he received a commission in the 17th Lancers; present at the taking of Bithoor, action of Boorbrakechowkee, and first relief of Lucknow; subsequently served as assistant field engineer in defence of the residency of Lucknow until its final relief (twice specially mentioned in despatches); was staff officer to a flying column in Central India; medal with clasp; passed the examination for the Staff College, inspector-general of police, Sierra Leone, 1868; inspector of Houssa police and district magistrate, Lagos, 1879; collector of customs, Gold Coast, 1873; second in command to Sir John Glover's force on the Volta, 1873; was left by Sir John Glover, in command of a large native force, to finish the war with the Ahoonahs, whom he engaged and defeated on three occasions; president of Nevis, May, 1876; colonial secretary of Western Australia, 1877, and senior member of legislative council; administrator of the Government, and colonial secretary of St. Lucia, 1881; governor British Honduras, 1884; governor Falkland Islands, 1891.

events.position: ag. assis. prot. of Chinese, Penang
events.text_span: ag. assis. prot. of Chinese, Penang, Mar., 1894

raw_text: GOMPERTZ, H. H. J.—Ed. at Bedford School and Exeter College, Oxon. (scholar); 2nd class Lit. Hum., B.A. (1890); cadet Str. Settls., 1893; passed final exam. Chinese, Dec. 1893; ag. assis. prot. of Chinese, Penang, Mar., 1894; 3rd mag., Penang, Aug. 1895.
GOODMAN, CLIFFORD E.—Entered Pub. Library, Barbados, Sep. 1890; 5th clk. and rev. office, April 1891; ch. clk. insp.-gen's. office, 1892, also reg. of births, Dis. A.

GOODMAN, WILLIAM MEIGH.—Educated at University College; graduated B.A. (honours) at the University of London, 1867; called to the bar Middle Temple, Nov., 1870; went South East Circuit and Surrey Sessions; attorney-general, British Honduras, 1883; chief justice, 1886; commissioner to revise and consolidate the laws of the colony, 1886, for which work he received the thank of the leg. coun.; attorney gen., Hong Kong, 1890, acting colonial secretary, May, 1891, to Mar., 1892; ag. ch. just., Mar. to Aug., 1895, and Jan. to May, 1896.

GOODRIDGE, HON. A. F.—Member of the House of Assembly for Ferryland, Newfoundland, 1880 to 1882, 1882 to 1885, for Twillingate, 1885 to 1889, returned for same district 1893; member exec. coun. 1885 (without portfolio) to 1889, and again in 1894; after the resignation of the Way ministry in April, 1894, he was called upon to form a ministry and became premier of the colony, which office he held till December, 1894; was a member of the Board of Works and Railway Commissioners under the Thorne Administration (1885-1889); he is also a member of the Newfoundland Chamber of Commerce, and has been its president and vice-president.

GOOLD-ADAMS, MAJOR H. C.M.G. (1894)—Royal Scots Fus.; commanding a troop, Bechuanaland Border Police, Aug., 1885; commandant, 1888; major, 1889; again commandant 1894, commanded field force against Matabele, 1893.

raw_text: GORDON, ARTHUR JOHN LEWIS, C.M.G. (1877)—Was private sec. to the governor of Trinidad, 1866 to 1870; acting colonial secretary, Trinidad, April, 1870; private secretary to governor of Mauritius, 1870; private secretary to the governor of Fiji, 1875-80; private secretary to gov.-gen. of Canada, the Earl of Aberdeen, 1893.

raw_text: GORDON, MAJOR J. M., late Lieut. R.A.—Lieut. R.A., from the R.M. Academy, Woolwich, 1875; lieut. staff instructor R.V. force, S. Australia, Dec., 1881; on the formation of a permanent artillery force in S.A., appointed lieutenant-in-command, Sept., 1882; captain, Aug., 1883; major, Nov., 1885; acting D.A.A. General, Dec., 1885; and hon. A.D.C., May, 1886.

GORDON, WILLIAM MONTGOMERIE.—Clerk to consul-general of Western Pacific, Aug., 1877; clerk of executive council and chief clerk of lands in colony of Fiji, May, 1880; also acting secretary to lands commission; resident commissioner and stipendiary magistrate for the island of Rotumah, May, 1882; commissioner inquire into claims of Europeans to land, Nov., 1882; stipendiary magistrate, Fiji, April, 1886; private secy. to governor, Ceylon, June, 1887; clerk governor's office, Leeward, and clerk of federal exec. and leg. coun., and of the local leg. coun. of Antigua, April, 1889; actg. commr., Montserrat, Dec. 1891, to Mar., 1892, and Mar., 1894, to June, 1895.

GORDON, WILLIAM ROSE.—Born, 1848; clerk and Zulu interpreter to assistant resident magistrate, Pietermaritzburg, March, 1866; clerk and interpreter to resident magistrate, Alfred County, 1867; acting clerk of the court, sub-distributor of stamps and issuer of licences, 1869; clerk and interpreter to resident magistrate, borough and county of Durban, 1870; acting interpreter of the circuit court, Durban, combined courts, and immigration department, interpreter of the supreme and circuit courts, and native high court of Natal, 1878; registrar, court of appeal, May, 1880; acting M.L., and administrator native law, Umsinga, 1883; to, Alexandria, 1884 to 1885; mag., Mapumulo, 1884. GORMANSTON (14th VISCOUNT) (created 1478), and BARON GORMANSTON, United Kingdom, 1868; ENICO WILLIAM JOSEPH PRESTON, K.C.M.G. (1887).—Born 1837; has been chamberlain to the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland and a commissioner of national education, and deputy lieutenant-col., Dublin; served in the 60th Rifles during the Indian mutiny (medal); gov.-in-chief, Leeward Islands, 1885; of British Guiana, 1887; of Tasmania, 1893.

events.text_span: Employed Her Majesty's Legation in Central America, 1891-1895

events.text_span: acted as interpreter to Rear-Admiral Stephenson, C.B., Commander-in-Chief, Pacific Station, during occupation of Corinto, Nicaragua, Mar. and Apr., 1895

raw_text: GOTTLIEB, F. H. VALENTINE.—Entered Govt. service, S.S., Oct., 1880; ag. ch. clk. (G.P.O.), Singapore, Oct. 1883; confirmed, 1888; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1892; ag. dep. regr. sup. Singapore, Feb., 1895; confirmed, Aug., 1895.

raw_text: GOULBURN, 1ST BISHOP OF—RIGHT REV. MAC THOMAS, D.D.—Educated at Trinity College, Cambridge; graduated B.A. 1840; M.A. 1843; ordained deacon, 1840; priest, 1841; vicar Tuddenham St. Martin, Suffolk; was secretary the colonial and continental church society. Goulburn formed part of the diocese of New South Wales until its separation in 1863.

raw_text: GOWAN, JAMES ROBERT, L.L.D., C.M.G. (1893).—Lieut., 4th regt., North York, 1838; called Canadian Bar, 1839; judge of Simcoe, 1842-83; chairman of board of public instruction, 1844-1871; commissioner for consolidating statute law of Canada, 1858-59; chairman of board of laws, 1869-86; engaged in consolidation of the criminal law for the Dominion, 1869; member of the Law Reform Commission, 1871; royal commissioner to inquire into charges against the minister in reference to the Pacific railway contract, 1873; commissioner for consolidating the statutes of Ontario, 1876-77; appointed to high court of Ontario, 1882; a senator of the Dominion, 1885; chairman of Barrie Collegiate Institute, by annual election, 1881-87; called to Irish bar, 1889.

raw_text: GOWDEY, W.M. WALLACE.—Solsr., Barbados, 1891; member, House of Assembly, 1883-93; member of committee of enquiry into Dodd's dormitory and industrial schl., 1884; member of health, 1884-92; J.P., 1885; member of financial enquiry commsn., 1885; of general hosp. bd., 1889-92; pol. mag. and judge, petty debt ct., Dist. E., Barbados, 1892.

GOYDER, G. WOODROFFE, C.M.G. (1889).—Draftsman, engineer's department, South Australia, 1852; chief clerk, land office, 1853; deputy surveyor general, 1855; surveyor general, 1861; also inspector of mines and valuer of runs. GRAFTON AND ARMIDALE.—(New South Wales) 2nd BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. JAMES FRANCIS TURNER, D.D.—Educated Durham University, where he graduated B.A. 1851, M.A. 1854; consecrated in 1869; formerly rector of North Tedworth, near Marlborough; for some time chaplain of Bishop Cosin's Hall, Durham University.

GRAHAM, FREDK.—Appointed clerk in the office of the Secretary of State for the Colonies, after a competitive examination, 30th May, 1870; 2nd class clerk, 30th Sept., 1872; private secretary to Mr. Herbert, 1st Oct., 1872; to the Rt. Hon. M. E. Grant-Duff, M.P., 8th Jan., 1881, to 5th Aug., 1881; to Mr. L. H. Courtney, M.P., 6th Aug., 1881; to the Hon. Evelyn Ashley, 12th May, 1882; and to the Earl of Dunraven, 24th June, 1885; to Sir R. Herbert, 3rd Feb., 1886; and again to the Earl of Dunraven, 3rd Aug., 1886 to Feb., 1887; 1st class clerk, Dec., 1887; act. principal clk., Feb., 1892; principal clk., 11th Mar., 1896; assist. under sec. of state, 1st Mar., 1897.

events.text_span: high sheriff and taxing officer, supreme court, May, 1884

raw_text: GRAHAM, JOHN JAMES.—Clerk to C.C. and R.M., Albany, 1864; clerk to registrar eastern districts court, May, 1865; assistant registrar, Jan., 1868; assistant registrar, supreme court, Mar., 1872; registrar and master, eastern districts court, Feb., 1875; chief clerk to attorney general and clerk of peace, Cape Town, Jan., 1878; secretary, law department, July, 1882; high sheriff and taxing officer, supreme court, May, 1884; member of the colonial tender board; a J.P. for the colony; sec. to Law Dept., 1889.

events.text_span: student in theology at Paris, 1864 to 1870
preacher at St. Martin's-le-Grand, London, 1874 to 1876

events.position: acting curate of Grouville and St. Heliers (Jersey)
events.text_span: acting curate of Grouville and St. Heliers (Jersey), 1876 to 1878

events.position: senior curate St. Andrew and St. Philip's, N. Kensington
events.text_span: senior curate St. Andrew and St. Philip's, N. Kensington, 1891

raw_text: GRANDJEAN, REV. J. F. J.—Educated at Bastogne and Floreffe (Belgium); student in theology at Paris, 1864 to 1870; professor at Nice, 1870 to 1872; priest in 1871; missionary in France, 1872 to 1874; preacher at St. Martin's-le-Grand, London, 1874 to 1876; acting curate of Grouville and St. Heliers (Jersey), 1876 to 1878; curate of Melton Mowbray, 1879; civil chaplain of the Seychelles, 1880; senior curate St. Andrew and St. Philip's, N. Kensington, 1891; returned to chaplaincy, Seychelles, July, 1892.

GRANNUM, EDWD. T.—Representative of Bridgetown, Barbados Assembly, 1883; J.P., 1886; member exec. committee, 1885-89, and 1890-94; member general board of health, 1885; delegate to Washington as to tariff arrangements under McKinley Act, 1891, audr.-gen., Barbados, 1894; ag. col. sec. and member ex. coun., Aug. to Dec., 1895, and July to Nov., 1896.

GRANT, LIEUT.-COL. JAS. MURRAY.—Served as a volunteer against Tambookies in 1857, being then lieut. 85th L.I.; employed in military survey of Transkeian territory, 1861-62; retired from Imperial army in 1867. Inspector F.A.M. police, 1867; served in Basutoland, 1865, and on the frontier border, 1869; member of two special commissions of enquiry into native affairs, 1872; first class inspector, F.A.M.P., 1873; in same year commanded a column sent in pursuit of the Zulus under Langalibalele; acting chief magistrate. East Griqualand, 1876; employed in making a military survey of the country between the Kabousie and the Kei, 1877; staff officer, colonial forces in Gealekaland campaign, 1877; acting R.M. Kongha, 1878; and transferred to C.M.R., 1878, as senior captain; honorary majority, Aug., 1879; commanded C.M.R., Mohalie's Hock, Oct., 1880, and in charge of base and lines of communication, Jan., 1881, to end of campaign; commanded left wing C.M.R., Sept., 1881, to Sept., 1883; on a special mission to Pondo chief Umqikela, Nov., 1881; in command of all colonial troops east of the Kei, Sept., 1884. War medal with clasp (1877-9). Lieut.-Col., 1892.
GRATIAEN, E.—Assistant colonial surgeon, Ceylon, 1868.
GRAVES, THE HON. J. H., M.P. for Delatelo, Victoria—Commissioner of trade and customs, Victoria, 9th July, 1881, to Mar., 1883.

events.position: J.P., eastern district, county of St. George
events.text_span: J.P., eastern district, county of St. George, Trinidad, 1858

events.position: storekeeper and paymaster public works department
events.text_span: storekeeper and paymaster public works department, Feb., 1875

raw_text: GRAY, A. R.—Educated at the Edinburgh Institution; J.P., eastern district, county of St. George, Trinidad, 1858; 2nd clerk, post office, Jan., 1875; storekeeper and paymaster public works department, Feb., 1875; chief collector, railway department, Feb., 1876; and of Government tramways, 1878; traffic manager, railway dept., 1880.
GRAY, ROBERT J.—Under colonial secretary, Queensland, 1 Jan., 1880; asst commr. of railways, 1889.

events.position: Called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn
events.text_span: Called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, 1847

raw_text: GRAY, S. BROWNLOW, C.M.G. (1888).—Called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, 1847; inspector of schools, Bermuda, 1848; attorney general and advocate general, 1861.

GRAY, WILLIAM BAGGETT.—Solicitor, supreme court, Jamaica, June, 1878; notary public, Kingston; acting crown solicitor for seven months in 1882, 1885, and 1888 respectively; crown solicitor with private practice, April, 1890.

GREAVES, LIEUT.-GENERAL SIR GEORGE RICHARD, K.C.B. (1885), K.C.M.G. (1881), C.B.—Has held several important posts; was chief secretary to Government in Cyprus, July, 1878, to July, 1879; adjutant-general in India, Oct., 1879, to Oct., 1884; chief of the staff at Suakin, 1885; in command of Meerut division of the Indian army to 1892.

events.position: Superintended erection of new parliament houses
events.text_span: Superintended erection of new parliament houses, Cape, 1876-85

raw_text: GREAVES, HARRY SEDDON, F.R.I.B.A.—Superintended erection of new parliament houses, Cape, 1876-85; architectural assistant in P.W. Dept., in which capacity he has carried out other important public works.

GREAVES, WILLIAM HERBERT, Q.C.—B.A., Oxon, honours, juris., 1879; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1880; act. solr.-gen., Barbados, Aug., 1884, to July, 1885, and July, 1886, to Jan., 1887, when confirmed; J.P., Sept., 1885; escheator-general, 1887; Q.C. for Barbados, 1890; member exec. coun., 1890; acted as atty.-gen., July to Dec., 1887, and June to Dec., 1891; member of house of assembly since 1882, and of board of education; atty. gen., Mar., 1896.

events.position: medical officer, colonial forces in Basuto war
events.text_span: medical officer, colonial forces in Basuto war, 1881

raw_text: GREEN, A. PIERCE.—Surgeon, A.M.D., 1878; medical officer, colonial forces in Basuto war, 1881; regimental surgeon, left wing, C.M.R., 1881-4; medical officer, Cape railways, 1885; ditto Bechuanaland border police, 1885.

GREEN, JOHN FREDERICK NORMAN, B.A.—Scholar Emmanuel Coll. Camb., 1890; bracketed 15th Wrangler, 1893; B.A., 1893; 1st class, Part II., nat. science tripos, 1894, 2nd class clerk colonial office after competitive examn., Feb., 1896.
GREENE, W. H., M.I.C.E. (Lond.)—Asst. Engineer, Victorian rlys., 1885; district engr., 1858; resdt. engr., 1868; chief engr. of existing lines, 1882; Victorian railway commissioner 1887-92.

events.text_span: entd. col. sec. office, Mauritius, Jan. 1886
jun. copyist proc. genl's. office, Mar. 1887

raw_text: GREENER, JOHN LE SUEUR.—Ed. Roy. Coll. Pt. Louis; entd. col. sec. office, Mauritius, Jan. 1886; jun. copyist proc. genl's. office, Mar. 1887; sen. ditto, Feb. 1888; govt. translator of laws, Nov., 1888.

2nd clerk, money order branch, G.P.O., June, 1884
1st clerk, administrator's office, British Bechuanaland, Mar., 1888
act. secy. to gov., June to Sept. 1888

actg. examr. of acc., Capetown, Mar. to Sept. 1890

raw_text: GREENER, HERBERT.—Clerk office of surveyor-general, Cape, May, 1884; 2nd clerk, money order branch, G.P.O., June, 1884; principal clerk Jan., 1886; 1st clerk, administrator's office, British Bechuanaland, Mar., 1888; act. secy. to gov., June to Sept. 1888; actg. clk. Vryburg, and ag. P.M.G., Jan. to May 1889; actg. examr. of acc., Capetown, Mar. to Sept. 1890; paymaster R. I. police, Mar., 1891.

events.position: prizeman medico-psychological association medical superintendent, Graham's Town Asylum and visiting surgeon to the chronic sick hospital
events.text_span: prizeman medico-psychological association medical superintendent, Graham's Town Asylum and visiting surgeon to the chronic sick hospital, Aug., 1890.

raw_text: GREENLEES, THOMAS DUNCAN, M.B., Etc.—Surgeon-captain, Graham's Town 1st City Volunteers; late assistant medical officer Green Asylum, Carlisle, and City of London Asylum; prizeman medico-psychological association medical superintendent, Graham's Town Asylum and visiting surgeon to the chronic sick hospital, Aug., 1890.
GREENWAY, THOMAS.—Member of legislative assembly, Manitoba; president of exec. council; premier and minister of agriculture and immigration.

GREENWOOD, ALFRED CRAVEN.—Executive staff, Inventions Exhibition, 1885, and Colonial and Indian Exhibition, 1886, and Royal Jubilee Exhibition, Manchester, 1887; sec. to various charitable institutions, 1887 to 1890; private sec. to Sir A. Shea, gov. Bahamas, and clerk to council, 1891-4.

GREENWOOD, THOMAS J.—Inspr. Cyprus police, Feb., 1887; ag. local commdt. Limassol, Oct. to Dec., 1894, and Apl. to July, 1895, Br Medal Roy. Hum. Soc. for saving and attempting to save life during Limassol floods, Nov. 12, 1895.
GREGORY, AUGUSTUS CHARLES, C.M.G. (1879).—Surveyor-General of Queensland, retired 1879; trustee of the Queensland museum, 1890; member leg. coun., 1882.

GREGORY, SIR CHARLES HUTTON, C.E., K.C.M.G. (1883), (C.M.G., 1876).—Past president of the Institution of Civil Engineers, and consulting engineer for various works in the colonies of Ceylon, Trinidad, Cape, West Australia, and Malay State of Perak, &c.; lieut.-col., Engineer and Railway Volunteer Staff Corps.

GREY, THE RT. HON. SIR GEORGE, K.C.B. (Civil.) 1848., P.C. (1894).—Ensign in the 83rd regiment, 1829; lieut., 1833; and captain, 1839; left England in 1837 to explore the north-west part of Australia, the account of which will be found in a work published by him entitled "Journals of Two Expeditions of Discovery in North-west and Western Australia during 1837-8-9;" was for some time resident magistrate at Albany, West Australia; governor of South Australia, Dec. 1840; of New Zealand, 1846; and the Cape of Good Hope, 1854; re-appointed governor of New Zealand, June, 1861; is author of "Polynesian Mythology;" relieved of the government of New Zealand, end of 1867; retired on governor's pension in 1872; superintendent of the Province of Auckland, 1875; premier of New Zealand, 1877 to 1879; was one of the representatives of New Zealand at the Australasian Federation Convention, 1891.

GREY-WILSON, WM., C.M.G. (1891).—Educated at Cheltenham College; private secretary to Sir William Grey, governor of Jamaica, 1874; lieut.-governor Edwd. E. Rushworth, March, 1897. Administrator, Major-General J. R. Mann, R.E., August, 1877; to lieut.-governor, F. P. Barlee, British Honduras, Sept., 1877; and clerk of the executive and legislative councils, June, 1878; acting magistrate, Orange Walk, in charge of frontier scouts, Dec., 1879, to April, 1880, and June, 1880, to March, 1881 assistant colonial secretary and treasurer, Sierra Leone, 1883; special commissioner, Sulymah, Dec., 1883, to June, 1884; assistant colonial secretary, Gold Coast Colony, 1884; colonial secretary, St. Helena, July, 1886; acting governor, 1887; governor, 1890.

events.position: Engineering assistant of public works department
events.text_span: Engineering assistant of public works department, Cape, May, 1887

raw_text: GRIER, WM. MAGEE, M.I.C.E.—Engineering assistant of public works department, Cape, May, 1887; acting chief inspector, July, 1881; confirmed July, 1882; is also hydraulic engineer, and J.P.

events.text_span: Medical superintendent, Lunatic Asylum, Berbice, 24th Sept., 1875
acting medical officer, immigration department, Aug., 1885

raw_text: GRIEVE, R., M.D., C.M.G. (1894)—Medical superintendent, Lunatic Asylum, Berbice, 24th Sept., 1875; acting medical officer, immigration department, Aug., 1885; surgeon-general of colony, Oct., 1885.

events.text_span: 3rd clerk treas., Gibraltar (after competitive exam.), Feb. 1883
chief clerk and cashier, P.O., Nov. 1893
transferred to col. secy.'s office, July, 1895.

raw_text: GRIFFIN, EUGENE PATRICK.—3rd clerk treas., Gibraltar (after competitive exam.), Feb. 1883; active clerk, 1883; 3rd clerk, col. secy's. office, 1886; 1st class clerk, Jan. 1890; 1st class clerk, Jan., 1893; chief clerk and cashier, P.O., Nov. 1893; transferred to col. secy.'s office, July, 1895.

events.position: was in command of the police at the Devonshire Castle Riots
events.text_span: was in command of the police at the Devonshire Castle Riots, 1872

events.position: private secretary and A.D.C. to Sir J. Scott
events.text_span: private secretary and A.D.C. to Sir J. Scott, 1870-71

raw_text: GRIFFIN, FREDERICK.—Entered army from Sandhurst, 1866; served on West Coast of Africa, Barbados, and British Guiana; retired, 1871; insp. of police, British Guiana, 1871, and was in command of the police at the Devonshire Castle Riots, 1872; private secretary and A.D.C. to Sir J. Scott, 1870-71; also acted as S. of P. on two occasions, and as inspr.-gen. of police; transferred to immigr. dept., 1878.

GRIFFIN, MARTIN J.—Called to the bar in 1883 from offices of Hon. James McDonald (now chief justice of Nova Scotia) and of Hon. Wm. Meller (now speaker of Senate of Canada); edited Halifax "Express" till 1874; contested Halifax county for local legislation in 1874; edited Halifax "Herald" till 1878; appointed assistant on fishery commission under Washington Treaty in 1873; private secretary to minister of justice in 1878; secretary of Civil Service Commission in 1881; edited Toronto "Mail" till Aug. 6, 1885; when appointed Parliamentary librarian.

GRIFFIN, WILLIAM HENRY, C.M.G. (1890).—Clerk in post office, Canada, 1831; surveyor of post offices, 1835; secretary of post office on its transfer to provincial control, 1851; deputy postmaster-general, Canada, 1857; deputy postmaster-general of the Dominion, 1868; commissioner for the re-organization of the civil service, 1858; member of the Civil Service Commission, 1862; of the board of audit, 1858, and of the board of customs excise, and stamps, 1864; negotiated the postal convention with the United States, 1875; is, vice-president of the Civil Service Building and Savings Society, and chairman of the Civil Service board; retd., 1888.

GRIFFITH, CHARLES DUNCAN, C.M.G. (1877)—Was lieut. Uitenhaze Hottentot levy in Kafir war of 1846-47; lieut. Kafir police, Jan., 1848, to Dec., 1850; captain commanding the Albany Hottentot levy in Kafir war of 1851-52 (medal); several times mentioned in general orders; commandant of the second division of the armed mounted police force, Nov., 1852, second in command during the operations in 1857-58 against the Tembu chief Fadana, and the Galeka chief Kreli; captured the chief Fadana; C.C. and R.M. Albert, 1858; Queenstown, June, 1859; Albany, Nov., 1868; King Williamstown, July, 1869; governor's agent and chief magistrate, British Basutoland, July, 1871; commandant of the frontier armed mounted police force upon the outbreak of the Galeka tribe under the chief Kreli, Sept., 1877; on the 16th Jan., 1878, commandant-general of colonial forces, and from that date in command of the colonial forces in the colony until the end of the Galeka war and the Gaika rebellion; in July, 1878, received the thanks of both houses of parliament for his services; on the 26th Aug., 1878, re-appointed governor's agent in, and chief magistrate for, British Basutoland; retired, 1882; M.L.A., Cape.

GRIFFITH, FRANCIS BELL.—Inspector of inland revenue officers, Barbados, 23rd March, 1874; acted as auditor-general, in 1874, 1876, 1877, 1878; treasurer and collector of customs, St. Vincent, 8th Oct., 1879; and ex officio member of executive and legislative councils.

GRIFFITH, F. W.—Matric. Lond. Univ., 1885; super. P.O. St. Vincent, Sept., 1885; clk. P.O., Jan., 1886; clk. to col. regr., June, 1886; ag. clk. to regr. sup. ct., Jan., 1887; marshal V.A. ct., Nov., 1887; ag. regr. sup. ct., 1888; 2nd clk. gov. office, Apl., 1889; ag. col. postmr., Nov., 1889 to Oct., 1890, Dec., 1891 to Jan., 1892, and May to June, 1892; escheator-gen. and casual receiver, Nov., 1890; insp. of schools, Dec., 1890, ch. clk. govt. off. and clk. of councils, June, 1893; J.P., Apl., 1894.

events.position: confidential clerk to administrator of Lagos
events.text_span: confidential clerk to administrator of Lagos, Oct., 1880

events.position: first clerk and warehouse keeper at Lagos
events.text_span: first clerk and warehouse keeper at Lagos, 1883

events.position: attached to audit office, Accra, in
events.text_span: attached to audit office, Accra, in 1884
private secretary to governor, Gold Coast, 1885 to 1886

raw_text: GRIFFITH, HORACE MAJOR BRANDFORD.—Ed. Harrison College, Barbados; confidential clerk to administrator of Lagos, Oct., 1880; private secretary to lieutenant-governor, 1880, and clerk of legislative council, 1882; first clerk and warehouse keeper at Lagos, 1883; attached to audit office, Accra, in 1884; private secretary to governor, Gold Coast, 1885 to 1886; controller of customs, Lagos, 1889; treas. Gambia, 1894.

GRIFFITH, HON. SIR SAMUEL WALKER, G.C.M.G. (1895), K.C.M.G. (1886), Q.C.—Born at Merthyr Tydvil, 1845; ed. at Univ. Sydney, N.S.W.; B.A., 1863; Mort Travelling Fellowship, 1865; M.A., 1870; called to the Queensland bar, 1867; Q.C. 1876; entered the legislative assembly, 1872; attorney-general, 1874 to 1878; secretary for public instruction, 1876 to 1878; secretary for public works, 1878 to 1879; led the opposition in the assembly, 1879 to 1883; premier, colonial secretary, and secretary for public instruction, Nov., 1883; delegate to the Intercolonial Convention held at Sydney, Dec., 1883; resigned office of secretary for public instruction, Jan., 1885; member of the federal council of Australasia, and chairman of the standing committee of the federal council, 1886; resigned office of colonial secretary, April, 1886, and accepted newly created office of chief secretary; delegate to colonial conference, 1887; chief secretary and treasurer, August, 1887; president of federal council, 1888; resigned office after general election, June, 1888, and became leader of opposition in assembly; again premier, and chief secretary, 1890; chief justice of Queensland, 1893.

GRIFFITH, T. RISELY, C.M.G. (1892).—Auditor, Grenada, Nov., 1872, to Feb., 1878; collr. and treasurer, Gold Coast Colony, Mar., 1878; colonial secretary and treasurer, Sierra Leone, June, 1879; admin. govt. of Gambia, Nov., 1887, to June, 1888; gazetted to admin. govt. of Sierra Leone in absence of gov., 1888; administrator Seychelles, 1888; administrator St. Kitts and Nevis, 1895.

GRIFFITH, SIR WILLIAM BRANDFORD, K.C.M.G. (1887), C.M.G. (1879).—Member of legislative assembly, Barbados, 1861-74, and of numerous executive and legislative boards; auditor-general, 31st Mar., 1863; acting colonial secretary in 1874 and 1877; member of the executive council, 1876; lieut.-governor of the Gold Coast Colony, and to administer the government of Lagos, Nov., 1879; administered the government in chief, Dec., 1880 to Mar., 1881, when he averted a war threatened by the Ashantis; also 1882 and 1885; governor 6th Oct., 1885; was sent on special mission to the King of the Belgians in 1891; retired from the govt. of the G. Coast, 5th Apl., 1895, after a service of 32 yrs.

GRIFFITH, WM. BRANDFORD JUN., B.A. (Lond.), 1880.—Gilchrist scholar, 1877; called to the bar, Middle Temple, June, 1881; acting Queen's advocate, Gold Coast Colony, April to Oct., 1884, district commissioner, 1885; acting puisne judge, Lagos, Jan., and Cape Coast, May, 1885; acting Queen's advocate, 1886 and 1887; compiled ordinances of Gold Coast Colony, 1887; resdt. magistrate, Jamaica, 1888; R. M., St. Catherine. Feb., 1893; ag. atty.-gen., May to Dec., 1893.

events.position: Scholar of Corpus Christi College, Oxford: 1st class classical mods
events.text_span: Scholar of Corpus Christi College, Oxford: 1st class classical mods., 1889

events.position: 2nd class clerk in Local Government Board
events.text_span: 2nd class clerk in Local Government Board, Feb. 1893

events.position: called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn
events.text_span: called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, Nov. 1895

events.position: 2nd class clerk, after competitive examination, in the office of the secretary of state for the colonies
events.text_span: 2nd class clerk, after competitive examination, in the office of the secretary of state for the colonies, 10th Feb., 1896.

raw_text: GRINDLE, G. E. A., M.A.—Scholar of Corpus Christi College, Oxford: 1st class classical mods., 1889; 1st class in final classical school, 1891; elected fellow of C.C.C., 1891; Chancellor's English Essay, 1892; 2nd class clerk in Local Government Board, Feb. 1893; called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, Nov. 1895; 2nd class clerk, after competitive examination, in the office of the secretary of state for the colonies, 10th Feb., 1896.

GRINLINTON, SIR J. J., KT. BACH. (1894). F.R.G.S. and A.I.C.E.—Was employed for seven years on the Ordnance Survey of England; gazetted ensign in the 65th Regiment; appointed depot adjutant; served in the Crimean campaign of 1855-6 as lieutenant 4th King's Own Regt., and assistant engineer during the siege of Sebastopol; was also engaged making a military survey of the allied positions; was mentioned in despatches on the fall of Sebastopol, 8th Sept., 1855; Crimean medal with clasps; 5th class of the Order of Medjidie, and the Turkish medal; assistant surveyor-general of Ceylon, Sept., 1857; retired from the army by the sale of his commission. Oct., 1858; appointed an official councillor of the municipality of Colombo at its formation in 1866, and served for six years; resigned the public service, Oct. 1872, and subsequently followed mercantile pursuits; member of the central Irrigation Board, July 1887; a councillor of municipality of Colombo, 1887, and member Legislative Council, Ceylon (to represent the general European community), Nov., 1888; commissioner for Ceylon, Chicago Exhibition, 1893.

events.position: and member of leg. assembly for that place since
events.text_span: and member of leg. assembly for that place since 1862

raw_text: GROOM, THE HON. WM. HENRY.—Born at Plymouth, 1833; emigrated to Queensland, 1857; alderman of Toowoomba, 1861; and member of leg. assembly for that place since 1862; re-elected at general election, 1888; speaker of the assembly, 1884; has been several times mayor of Toowoomba.

events.position: compiler of handbook on labor ordes
events.text_span: compiler of handbook on labor ordes., 1890.

raw_text: GROSE, CHARLES BENJAMIN.—Magistrates' clerk, British Guiana, 1884; compiler of handbook on labor ordes., 1890.

GRUBER, JASPER WM.—Employed under late vestry of St. Ann, Jamaica, in 1854; collr., 3rd class customs and internal rev., Feb., 1868; parochial treas., May, 1870; hrbr. mr., St. Ann's Bay, Aug., 1872; 1st class collr., Montego Bay, Oct., 1875. Is also treas. parish of St. James.

events.position: coll. of land revenue, Sungei Ujong
events.text_span: coll. of land revenue, Sungei Ujong, 1885

raw_text: GUERITZ, EDWARD PEREGRINE.—Served under Sarawak Government, 1874 to 1877; assist. president, Bri. N. Borneo, 1882-84; coll. of land revenue, Sungei Ujong, 1885; coll. and mag., Jelebu, June, 1885; ag. resdt. Labuan, May, 1890.

events.text_span: Crown attorney and Queen's proctor, Mauritius, 1 July, 1883

raw_text: GUIBERT, J.—Crown attorney and Queen's proctor, Mauritius, 1 July, 1883; ag. reverser registrn. dues, June, 1896.

events.position: transferred to L. and A. dept., Somerset House
events.text_span: transferred to L. and A. dept., Somerset House, Feb., 1873

events.position: supdt. colonial audit branch of that dept
events.text_span: supdt. colonial audit branch of that dept., Feb., 1892.

raw_text: GULLICK, JOSEPH WM.—Clerk, examiner's off. Imp. customs, Dec., 1862; transferred to L. and A. dept., Somerset House, Feb., 1873; supdt. colonial audit branch of that dept., Feb., 1892.

GUN MUNRO, J. POYNTZ.—Admitted to the bar, Grenada, 1869; member for St. George and St. John in assembly until introduction of single chamber; acted as att.-gen. on three occasions; police magistrate, East. District, Sept., 1876; coroner ex officio ; commissioner of customs, 1881; commissioner, sup. ct., commissioner of wrecks, &c.

GURDON, SIR W. BRAMPTON, C.B., K.C.M.G. (1882).—Educated at Eton and at Trinity College, Cambridge, B.A. (1863); appointed a clerk in treasury, 1863; and received the K.C.M.G. for services on special missions in 1879 and 1881 to South Africa for the settlement of financial questions, resigned 1885.

GURNER, JOHN AUGUSTUS.—Educated at Whittenham College, and Jesus College (Camb.), L.L.B.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1877; parliamentary draughtsman, Victoria, May, 1882, one of the crown prosecutors.

events.position: chief clk. treasury, customs, and P.O
events.text_span: chief clk. treasury, customs, and P.O., Feb., 1891.

raw_text: GUTTERON, W. B.—Revenue offr., Brit. Honduras, April, 1883; keeper, Queen's warehouse, January, 1888; chief clk. treasury, customs, and P.O., Feb., 1891.
GWYNNE, J. W.—Puisne judge of supreme court and court of exchequer, Canada.

HABENS, REV. W. J., B.A. (LonL, 1882).—Minister, Congregational church, New Zealand, 1864-78; secretary, board of education, Christchurch, Jan., 1877, to May, 1878; inspector-general of schools, 1878; member (and secretary) of Board Commission to enquire into the operations of New Zealand University and its relation to the secondary schools, 1879-80; fellow New Zealand Univ., 1883; sec. and inspr.-gen. edn. dept., Wellington, 1888.

HADDON-SMITH, GEO. BASIL.—Lieut. Rnd Guernsey Militia, 1879; attached 32nd (D.C.L.I.) regiment, 1880; attached 107th (Royal Sussex) regiment, 1881; assist inspector, Lagos constabulary, 1886; adjutant, 1887; private secretary to acting governor and clerk of councils, 1887, 1888, and 1893; acting inspector-general and sheriff, Lagos, 1888, 1890, 1891, and 1893; acting district commissioner, 1887, 1888, 1889, and 1891; took part in Jobu expedition, 1892, mentioned in despatches (medal and clasp); political officer with Sir G. Carter's mission to Yorubaland, 1893; asst. col. sec. Lagos, 1894; ag. col. sec., 1895; priv. sec. to Sir F. Scott in Ashanti expedition, 1896; chief asst. col. sec. G. Coast, 1896.

events.position: private secretary to Sir Henry Bulwer, K.C.M.G., lieut.-governor of Natal
events.text_span: private secretary to Sir Henry Bulwer, K.C.M.G., lieut.-governor of Natal, 17th March, 1877

events.position: was chairman of Natal trade commission
events.text_span: was chairman of Natal trade commission, 1885-6

events.position: delegate to customs and railway confec., Cape Town

events.position: dep. gov. Natal and Zululand in
events.text_span: dep. gov. Natal and Zululand in Dec., 1890

raw_text: HADEN, FRANCIS SEYMOUR, B.A., C.M.G. (1890).—Educated at Westminster school, and Christ Church, Oxford; private secretary to Sir Henry Bulwer, K.C.M.G., lieut.-governor of Natal, 17th March, 1877; acting chief clerk, colonial secretary's office 17th March, 1880; assist. colonial secretary, 1st Jan., 1881; acted as col. treasurer and also as colonial secretary, 1885; colonial secretary, 1887; was chairman of Natal trade commission, 1885-6; delegate to customs and railway confec., Cape Town, 1888; dep. gov. Natal and Zululand in Dec., 1890; dep. gov. in Aug., 1891; retired, 1893, on introduction of responsible government

HAGAN, JOSEPH SAMPSON.—Ed. Wesley High Schl., Cape Coast; employed temporarily in various depts., Nov. 1883-85; powder magazine keeper, Accra, Oct. 1885; junr. clk. gov. off., Mar., 1887; dep. regr. and interpr., Akuse, June, 1888. clk gov. office, Jan., 1891; accompanied governor on various tours of inspection; qr. mr. t., Gold Coast rifle vols., April, 1894.

AGARTY, J. H., D.C.L.—Judge of the court common pleas, Ontario, Feb. 5, 1856; puisne judge of the court of Queen's bench, March 19, 1862, justice of the court of common pleas, same place, July, 1873; chief justice Queen's bench, 1878; chief justice of Ontario, 6 May, 1884.
AGGART, THE HON. JOHN G.—Postmr.-gen. Canada, 1888; minister of railways and works, 1892.

AHN, D. M., A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Realschool, Hamburg; served apprenticeship under J. A. Ratzki, architect, Altona; theoretically instructed at the Technological Institution, Hamburg; in charge govt. saw mill, Trinidad, Jan., 1877; foreman, public works, Jan., 1888; ag. st. engineer, N. Div., in 1889, 1890, and 1891; st. engineer, N. Div., 1891.
AINES, REV. FRANK WM., B.A. (Oxon.)—Christ's Coll., Brecknock, and Jesus Coll., Oxford; govt. tutor; chaplain, Selangor, 1890; Supt. of schools, Selangor, 1881.

events.position: clerk, assistant, and sergeant-at-arms, Legislative council
events.text_span: clerk, assistant, and sergeant-at-arms, Legislative council, April, 1874

raw_text: ALCOMB, FRED. M.A. (Wadham College, Oxon.)—Parliamentary librarian, S. Australia, 1870; clerk, assistant, and sergeant-at-arms, Legislative council, April, 1874; clerk, house of assembly, May, 1887.

events.position: inspector mines and asst. mag., Kinta
events.text_span: inspector mines and asst. mag., Kinta, 1885

raw_text: FALE, A.—Actg. mag., Perak, 1884; inspector mines and asst. mag., Kinta, 1885; mag. and ir., Tampin, 1887; acted for supert. Negri Sembilan, 1888.

HALKETT, FREDERICK CRAIGIE.—1872, passed in competition for Royal Indian Engineering College, lieut. 3rd Batt. Highland Light Infantry; 1873-5, lieut. and capt. southern division, R.A. Artillery; 1873-5, passed school, Woolwich, selected artillery instructor; 1876-8, organized artillery branch of Houssa Force, Gold Coast; in charge diplomatic missions to Denkera, Wassaw, and Shanti; commissioner of Dixcove and Secondi; 1879-84, stipendiary magistrate and commissioner the supreme court, Fiji; 1885-6, inspr.-gen. police, Sierra Leone; acting commandant of Freetown; 1887, represented West Africa Settlements at colonial conference; commanded expedition against Mendis, receiving thanks of govt.; 1887-8, in Yonnie expedition (medal and asp); 1889-91, provost marshal, Bahamas; member leg. coun.; on commission to revise liquor laws; organised new constabulary to replace cops; col. sec., Falklands, 1894.

HALL, CECIL G.—Volunteer colonial secretary's fire, Mauritius, Nov., 1866; clerk, general board of health, June, 1867; clerk, medical department, Nov., 1868; accountant, immigration department, June, 1872; senior clerk, Oct., 1880; acting inspector of immigrants, Dec., 1879, to Oct., 1880; and acting chief clerk several times from 1880 to 1886; confirmed inspr. of immigrants, 1886.

events.text_span: passed final examination in Malay, April, 1891
ag. 2nd magis., Penang, July to Sept., 1893

raw_text: HALL, GILBERT AMOS.—Educated at Harrow School; cadet, Straits, 1888; passed final examination in Malay, April, 1891; ag. 3rd magis., Penang, July, 1891; ag. 2nd magis., Penang, July to Sept., 1893; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Mar., 1894; supdt. of edn., Penang, Oct., 1894.

HALL, THE HON. SIR JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1882).—Born 1824; emigrated to N. Zealand, 1852; memb. leg. assem., 1855; col. secy., 1856; memb. leg. coun., 1862-6; postmr.-gen., 1866-9; col. secy., 1872-3; premier, 1879-82.

events.text_span: Clerk to Col. Surveyor, Bermuda, Aug., 1881

raw_text: HALLETT, W. CARDY.—Clerk to Col. Surveyor, Bermuda, Aug., 1881; asst. supdt. pub. wks., Jan., 1887; supdt., July, 1887; col. surveyor, Oct., 1887.

events.text_span: passed final exam. in Malay, June, 1894

raw_text: HALLIFAX, F. J.—Cadet, S.S., Mar., 1893; passed final exam. in Malay, June, 1894; ag. dist. offr. Penang, April, 1896.
HALY, AMYRALD.—Director, Colombo museum, June, 1875.

events.text_span: Entered the Royal Navy in June, 1862

treasurer and harbour master, Labuan, Aug., 1877

events.position: also surveyor and superintendent of prisons
events.text_span: also surveyor and superintendent of prisons, Jan., 1882

raw_text: HAMILTON, ARTHUR SHIRLEY.—Entered the Royal Navy in June, 1862; lieut., Sept., 1872; served on the Mediterranean, China, and East African stations; present at the bombardment and reduction of Mombasa, Jan., 1875; retired Feb., 1876; treasurer and harbour master, Labuan, Aug., 1877; also surveyor and superintendent of prisons, Jan., 1882; M.L.C.

HAMILTON, CHARLES BOUGHTON, C.M.G. (1895).—Entered the service, 1865; 2nd clerk receiver-general's office, British Guiana, 1873, 1877; colonial book keeper, 1879; acting receiver-general, 1881; and acting auditor-general, 1883 to 1886; received thanks of council on leaving for post of receiver-general, &c., Trinidad, 1886; J.P.; acting auditor-general M.E.C., and M.L.C., 1886; member of bd. of education, 1887; of bd. of health, 1888; M.L.C., 1889; chairman of com. for widows' fund, 1889; actg. col. sec., 1890; chairman harbour improvement comttee., and ag. aud. genl. and col. secy., 1891; member college coun., 1891; rec.-gen., Br. Guiana, Dec., 1892; ag. audr.-gen. and mem. ct. of policy, May, 1893, to May, 1895, and again Oct. 1895; senior comsnr., Vlissingen estate; chairman, spirits commn., "Trotman," Trust Comttee.; Director W. and O. Fd. &c.; memb. ex. coun., Dec., 1895.
HAMILTON, CHARLES EDWARD.—Born 1844; called to the bar, Ontario, 1865; elected mayor of Winnipeg, 1885; elected to Manitoba legislature, 1885; att.-gen., Manitoba, Feb., 1887-8.

events.text_span: capt., 4th Bat. Roy. I. Fus., Feb., 1893

raw_text: HAMILTON, JOHN DOUGLAS.—Entered 4th Bat. Roy. I. Fus., 1885; joined 14th (King's) Hussars, 1887; resigned 1892; capt., 4th Bat. Roy. I. Fus., Feb., 1893; assist. inspr., Lagos constab., April, 1893; ag. dist. comsnr., Lagos, Dec., 1893; adjt., April, 1894; commissr. of police, sheriff, &c., Jan., 1896.

events.position: called to the bar, Inner Temple
events.text_span: called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1895

raw_text: HAMILTON, R. W.—Ed. St. Paul's School and Trin. Hall, Camb.; B.A., 1889; M.A., 1892; Sec. Commission of Enquiry, Dominica, 1893-4; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1895; Dist. Commr., Lagos, 1895.

HAMILTON, W. A. B., C.B. (1892), C.M.G., (1887)—Educated at Harrow, appointed a junior clerk in the Colonial Office, May, 1864, after competitive examination; sent on secret service to North America, 1867; 2nd class clerk, 30th Sept., 1st class clerk, July, 1879; and private secretary to Mr. Knatchbull-Hugessen (now Lord Brabourne), M.P., 9th Oct., 1872; called to the bar (Inner Temple), 18th Nov. 1872; private secretary to Mr. James Lowther, M.P., under secretary of state for the colonies, 25th Feb., 1874, to 14th Feb., 1878; private secretary to the Right Hon. James Lowther, M.P., chief secretary for Ireland, 15th Feb., 1878; captain in the East Lothian Yeomanry Cavalry, 5th June, 1878; major, 18th June, 1883; Hon. Lt.-Col. and commanding regiment, 1894; commanding 12th Yeomanry Brigade, 1895; author of "Mr. Montenegro, a romance of the civil service"; private secretary to Mr. Stanhope, 3rd Aug., 1886; and to Sir H. Holland (now Lord Knutsford), Jan., 1887 to Aug., 1892; secretary to Colonial conference, 1887; principal clerk, 1st January, 1894; chief clerk, March, 1896.

HAMLEY, WILLIAM WYMOND. M.A., F.R. Met. Soc. — Clare Coll., Camb. (Hon. classics, 1873); 1st class prof., Royal Coll., Mauritius, Jan., 1879; senior prof., May, 1887; ax. rector, May, 1891, to July, 1892, and Apl. to Nov., 1894.

events.position: Government medical officer, Savanna Grande District
events.text_span: Government medical officer, Savanna Grande District, Trinidad, 1 Jan., 1876.

raw_text: HAMMOND, E. J. — Government medical officer, Savanna Grande District, Trinidad, 1 Jan., 1876.

HAMPDEN, 2ND VISCOUNT (creat. 1884), BARON DACRE (creat. 1507). — Dep. Lt. and J.P. for Sussex; J.P. Herts; was M.P. for Hertfordshire, 1868 to 1874; for Stroud, July, 1874 (unseated Dec. 1874); and again 1880-5; and for Mid-Gloucester, 1885-6; Surveyor-Gen. of Ordce. 1883-5; Governor N.S. Wales, 1895.

events.position: headmr. govt. gram. schl. and govt. analyst St. Kitts

raw_text: HANCOCK, E. A., F.C.S., &c.— Late Nat. Sc. Exhibn. Keble Coll. Oxon; 1st B.Sc. Lond.; headmr. govt. gram. schl. and govt. analyst St. Kitts, 1890; govt. analyst only, 1894; off. memb. leg. coun. St. Kitts and Nevis, 1895.

events.position: prof. classics and moral phil. Codrington Coll.
events.text_span: prof. classics and moral phil. Codrington Coll., Barbados, 1883

raw_text: HANCOCK, HERBERT HENRY, M.A. — Scholar St. John's Coll., Oxon; 2nd class classical mods. 1876; 2nd class classical finals, 1878; B.A., 1879; M.A., 1883; asst. mr. Cheltenham College, 1879; 2nd mr. Bosworth Schl., 1880; prof. classics and moral phil. Codrington Coll., Barbados, 1883; ag. principal, 1889-91; 2nd mr. Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad, 1892; ag. Principal, Apl. to Dec., 1894; Clk. to Coll. Coun., 1892; Lieut. Trinidad Field Artillery.

HANCOCK, WILLIAM J., M.I.E.E., A.M.I.C.E. — Educated Glasgow Univ.; supert. of telephones, Western Australia, 1885; supert. of telegraphs and telephones, 1890; hon. local secretary and treasurer for Western Australia of the Institution of Electrical Engineers of London.

raw_text: HANNAM, WILLOUGHBY, M.I.C.E. — Apprentice to M. Du Bays, C.E., Reading; asst. surveyor, Melbourne and River Murray Rly., Victoria, 1860; engineer Moreton Bay Tramway Co., Brisbane, 1861-63; district engineer (southern dist.) Queensland Govt. Rlys., 1863-8; contractor's engineer on same 1868-72; district engineer and in charge of surveys, Central Divn., Queensland Rlys., 1872-85; chief engineer, northern divn., 1885; has been engaged on survey of 1,060 miles of rly., and directed survey of 250 miles more.

HANNAY, W. M. — For ten years in Glasgow and S. W. Rly.; entered N. Zealand Govt. Rly. service May, 1876; asst. traffic manager, June, 1878; traffic manager, 1879; asst. gen. manager, 1880; rly. commsr. under Govt. Rlys. Act, 1887, June, 1889.

raw_text: HANSON, A. B. — Educated at the C. M. S. Grammar School, Sierra Leone, and Ipswich, Suffolk; tide waiter, Customs, Sierra Leone, July, 1862; landing and tide surveyor, Oct., 1863; acted as sub-collector in Sherbro, 1869; acted for the harbour master of Freetown in 1865; deputy harbour master, superintendent of quarantine, and superintendent of lighthouses, Sierra Leone, April, 1876.

raw_text: HANSON, CHAS. RASTRICK, M.I.C.E. — Cape Govt. rly. staff, 1874; Ceylon, ditto, 1879; resdt. engr. and manager, Perak State Rly., 1887.

HARBIN, JONX ALPHONSO. — Chief clerk to regr., Grenada, July, 1884; ditto to regr. sup. ct., July, 1886; marshal V. A. ct., Mar., 1887; ag. regr. sup. ct., May, 1888, to April, 1890; accountant treasy., July 1890.
HARDING, MORGAN H. M. — Lieut., North Tipperary Militia, 1878; in transport train, Trinidad, 1881; clerk, savings bank dept., 1882; 6th clerk, and office, Jan., 1888.

events.position: Secretary and registrar, province of Ontario
events.text_span: Secretary and registrar, province of Ontario, Canada, 1877.

raw_text: HARDY, A. S., Q.C. — Secretary and registrar, province of Ontario, Canada, 1877.

HARDY, WENTWORTH MARMADUKE. — Entered survey dept., S. Australia, Oct., 1865; accompanied the surveyor-general as surveyor and draughtsman to Port Darwin, Dec., 1868; rose step by step, till appointed, Jan., 1878, trigonometrical surveyor. In 1884, at the recommendation of the surveyor-general of Victoria, and to carry out his suggested reformation, appointed inspector of surveys and prof. adviser survey dept., Tasmania.

HARE, FREDERICK ARTHUR. — Ed. St. Columba's Coll., Wicklow; clerk to magistrates, Vasse, W. Australia, 1880; clerk and asst. private secretary to Sir William Robinson, during various periods from 1880, to 1882; inspector of police for the Southern Districts of the colony, 1882; private secretary and A.D.C. to Sir F. N. Broome; also government resident, Wyndham, Cambridge Oct. 1886-8; now R.M., York, W.A.

events.text_span: actg. 2nd asst. protector of Chinese, Feb., 1888
confirmed as asst. prot. Chinese, Singapore, April, 1895.

raw_text: HARE, GEO. THOMPSON. — Ed. Wadham Coll., Oxon; Cadet, Str. Settln., 1884; passed in Chinese, 1888; actg. 2nd asst. protector of Chinese, Feb., 1888; actg. asst. ditto, Singapore, 1889; dist. 5th Jan., 1890; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, June, 1890; 2nd asst. prof. Chinese, Penang, Oct., 1891; also asst. prot. Chinese, Penang, 1893; ditto, Singapore, Nov., 1893; confirmed as asst. prot. Chinese, Singapore, April, 1895.

events.text_span: Clerk in treasury, Western Australia, April, 1881

events.position: secretary, Western Australian govt. agency, London
events.text_span: secretary, Western Australian govt. agency, London, May, 1890.

raw_text: HARE, REGINALD CHARLES. — Clerk in treasury, Western Australia, April, 1881; police inspector southern districts, June, 1886; ag. govt. resdt., Wyndham, May, 1888; ditto, Roebourne, June, 1889; secretary, Western Australian govt. agency, London, May, 1890.

HARLEY, OCTAVIUS. — Clerk in treasury, Trinidad, April, 1862; cashier in that department about three years; acted as warden and coroner for the Diego Martin Ward Union, July, 1870, to Apr., 1871; Suptdt. of prisons, Oct., 1873, and inspector of industrial schools also, Oct., 1885; stipendiary J.P. for E. dist. city of St. George, and inspector of prisons and reformatories Apr., 1889; acted as stip. J.P., W. dist. St. George and town of Port of Spain, June, 1892 to Feb., 1893; chairman of committee on prison accommodation, Mar., 1893.
HARMER, HERBERT J. — Harbour master Malacca, 21st April, 1884; asst. Indian Immigrant agent, Dec., 1884; ag. dep. master attendant, Singapore, Sept., 1893, to Apl., 1896.
HARRAGIN, WILLIAM CAMPBELL. — Inspector of police B. Guiana, Sept., 1874; senior inspector Oct., 1883; acted as stip. mag. in 1885, and as stip. gen. in 1886 and 1888; dep. inspr. gen., 1891.

events.text_span: Tancered student in common law at Lincoln's Inn, 1877

raw_text: HARRIS, CHARLES ALEXANDER, B.A. — Scholar prizeman, and Porteus medallist of Christ's Coll., Cambridge; Tancered student in common law at Lincoln's Inn, 1877; 12th in the classical tripos, 1878; appointed a second class clerk, Col. Office after open competition, 1st July, 1879; sent to the West Indies Finance Commission, Dec., 1883; and asst. sec., Sugar Bounties Conference, 1884; private sec. to Mr. Buxton, M.P., Jan., 1884 to June, 1895; and to the Earl of Selborne, June, 1895; first class clerk, 1896.

HARRISON, JAMES HERBERT HUGH. — Regent's Park Coll., and New College (London) at University Coll. and Hospital; L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), F.R.C.P. District magistrate, district surgeon, Cayo, British Honduras, 1889; ag. district surgeon, Orange Walk, 1891. 1892; dist. commr. and dist. surgeon, Orange Walk, June, 1892.
HARRISON, THOMAS. — Educated in Edinburgh; crown surveyor, Jamaica, 1853; govveyor, 1867; is J.P., and director of widows' orphans' fund.

govt. analyst and chemist, Br. Guiana, Dec., 1889

raw_text: HARRISON, JOHN B.—Nat. Sc. Scholar Christ's Coll., Camb., B.A. (Nat. Sc. Tripos), 1887; M.A., 1897; Professor of Chemistry and Agricultural Science, Barbados, Sept., 1879; govt. analyst and chemist, Br. Guiana, Dec., 1889; is F.S., F.G.S., and F.I.C., and author of several papers relating to the agriculture and geology of West Indies.

raw_text: HART, ANTHONY DE LLANO.—Storekeeper, Madrid, October 4th, 1866; office of the court of appeal, 1868; office of stipendiary magistrate, 1 of Spain, 1868; marshal's office, June, 1869; left at Government House, 21st Sept., 1869; noted to the customs dep. in 1871; landing per. 1872, and inspector of Venezuelan vessels in town to other duties, 1876.

events.position: Chief clerk and acct., sheriff's dept.
events.text_span: Chief clerk and acct., sheriff's dept., Trinidad, 1869

raw_text: HART, DANIEL.—Chief clerk and acct., sheriff's dept., Trinidad, 1869; marshal V. A. ct., 1890; has acted as marshal of the island.

HART, JOHN HINCHLEY.—Superintendent, Esq House Gardens and Grounds, Jamaica, during their formation, Nov., 1875; superintendent, Government Cinchona Plantations, Oct., 1881; ag. director, public gardens and plantations, July, 1883; chief, botanical dept., Trinidad, Mar., 1887; fellow Linn. Soc.; author of a "Botanist's tale in Central America," a work on cacao pre, curing, and fermenting (1891), a report on soil conservation (1891), and numerous pamphlets relating to the cultivation of West Indian pros; engaged by the govt. of the Windward islands to inspect and report on the working of botanical stations, St. Vincent and Grenada (1890); lieut. Trinidad Rifle Vols., Mar., 1889.

HART, LOVELACE FRED. EDWARD RALPH.—Passed a first-class certificate at I. C. College in 1875; articled law student, 3rd Jan., 1876; ag. clerk of the peace the same year; Jan., 1877, joined customs department, Trinidad; clerk in it office, Sept., 1878; 7th clerk, Jan., 1883, 6th cl., 1887; 2nd clerk, registrar, Sup. Ct., 1887.

HARTLEY, E. B.—Surgeon-Major, Cape Mounted Riflemen; created D.C. for gallantry in operations against the stronghold of the Basuto & Morosi; principal medical officer, Cape colonial forces, 1878; served through the Gaika, Kosi, and Basuto wars, 1878-9-80-1; was regimental medical officer, Basutoland, 1874-7.

HARVEY, THE HON. A. W.—Member New Zealand Legis. Council; member Exec. Council (without portfolio), 1889 to 94, and again Dec., 1895 to Dec., 1895; member of official and of native delegations to London on the fisheries question in 1890 and 1891 respectively; on special mission to Madrid, 1892, in connection with commercial negotiations with Spain.

HARWOOD, JOHN AUGUSTUS.—Educated at Merchant Taylor's School; called to the bar, Middle Temple, June, 1871; private secretary to the Marquis of Salisbury, K.G., 1873; police magistrate, St. Vincent, 1877; police magistrate, Grenada, 1879; acting attorney general, May, 1880, until 1881; acting colonial secretary in Jan., 1881; registrar, supreme court, Penang, Aug., 1881; ag. attorney general, Straits Settlements, 1882-3, 1888, and 1892; compiled revised edition of laws and ordinances of that colony, 1886; ag. regr. at Singapore, Feb., 1893, to July, 1894; solr.-gen. S.S., Apl., 1895.

events.position: Clerk, Law and Volts. office, Auckland
events.text_span: Clerk, Law and Volts. office, Auckland, July 1863

raw_text: HASELDEN, CHARLES JOHN ALLAN.—Clerk, Law and Volts. office, Auckland, July 1863; capt. of Justice, Wellington, Feb., 1865; chief clerk, July, 1873; as under secy., Mar., 1882; confirmed May, 1886; also patent officer, Oct., 1882.

HASTINGS, W. C. HOLLAND.—Served in H.M.S. Navy from 1865 to 1881, when he retired with rank of Commander; inspr. of immigrants, and dep. caraner, Jamaica, 1881; chief of police and inspr. of prisons, St. Lucia, 1889; asst. harbr. mr., Hong Kong, Nov., 1889; ag. harbr. mr., Feb., 1891 to April, 1892; also ag. pol. mag. from April, 1892; ag. capt. suptdt. of Police, May, 1895; and ag. hrbr. mr., June, 1895; provl. memb. leg. coun.

events.position: Apptd. after competitive exam. clk. 2nd divn. civil service and assigned to W.O
events.text_span: Apptd. after competitive exam. clk. 2nd divn. civil service and assigned to W.O., 11 Sept., 1893

raw_text: HATCHER, C. M.—Apptd. after competitive exam. clk. 2nd divn. civil service and assigned to W.O., 11 Sept., 1893; transferred to C.O., 18 Mar., 1896.
HATHERTON, BARON.—Col. the Hon. Edward George Percy Littleton, C.M.G. (1880). Late mil. sec. to the gov.-gen. of Canada.

raw_text: HAUGHTON, HANCOCK THOMAS, B.A., Dub., Sen. Mod. in Classics.—Cadet, S.S., Nov., 1881; passed final examination in Malay, Mar., 1883; acting magistrate, Malacca, April to Sept., 1883; acting 3rd magistrate, Singapore, Oct., 1883; acting 2nd ditto, Aug. to Oct., 1884; acting collector land revenue, Malacca, April, 1885; confirmed, Nov., 1885; act. coll. land revenue, Singapore, May, 1886; confirmed Dec., 1886; act. 2nd asst. col. secy., 1888; 2nd mag., Singapore, Sept., 1889; 2nd asst. col. secy., July, 1892; ag. asst. col. sec., April to July, 1893, and Sept. 1893 to Feb., 1894; ag. 1st mag., Penang, Feb., 1894 to Aug. 1895; collr. ld. rev. and offr. in charge of treas., Malacca, June, 1896.

HAUGHTON, S.—Educated at Portora Royal School, Enniskillen, and Trin. Col. Dublin, royal scholarship, 1867; first honours in classics in Trinity and Michaelmas Terms, 1868, and in Hilary Term, 1869; writer, Ceylon civil service, Sept., 1870; acting police magistrate, Panadure, June, 1872; police magistrate, Haldummulla, and acting assistant government agent, Kandy, Sept., 1872; assistant government agent, Anuradhpura, Sept., 1873, to continue to act at Kandy; acting fiscal, central province, 1875; assistant agent, Batticaloa, 1875; acting police magistrate, Colombo, 1875; acting assistant agent, Negombo, 1876; assistant agent, Mullaittivu, 1877; ditto Mannar (actg.). 1879; ditto Negombo, 1883; deputy collector and landing surveyor of customs, Colombo, 1891; dist. judge, Negombo, Dec., 1891.

HAVELOCK, SIR ARTHUR ELIRANK, G.C.M.G. (1895), G.C.I.E. (1896), K.C.M.G. (1884).—Entered the army, 32nd Light Infantry, Jan., 1862; captain, 1873; private sec. and aide-de-camp to the officer administering the government of Mauritius from July, 1873, to Jan., 1874; acting chief civil commissioner, Seychelles Islands, from April, 1874, until Feb., 1875; member of the executive and legislative councils of Fiji, May, 1875; appointed (provisionally) colonial secretary and receiver-general, Fiji, 1st Sept., 1875; president of Nevis, 1877; administering the government of St. Lucia from June, 1878, to Aug., 1879; chief civil commissioner, Seychelles, Oct., 1879; governor, West Africa Settlements, 1881; April, 1881, sent on a mission to Paris in conjunction with Mr. (now Sir A.) Hemming, of the Colonial Office, to assist in negotiating the settlement of certain questions at issue between Great Britain and France, with regard to territory on the West Coast of Africa; appointed Her Majesty's consul for Liberia, Dec., 1881; negotiated the settlement of a conterminous boundary between Sierra Leone and Liberia, and inquired into the claims of British subjects against the Liberian Government, Mar., 1882; governor of Trinidad, Dec., 1884; governor of Natal and special commissioner for Zulu affairs, 1885; also governor of Zululand, 1887; attended international conference at Brussels on the suppression of the slave trade, 1889; governor of Ceylon, 1890; of Madras, 1895.

HAWTAYNE, GEORGE HAMMOND, F.R.G.S., C.M.G. (1886).—Was private secretary to Mr. Eyre, lieutenant-governor of St. Vincent, in 1854; police and stipendiary magistrate of the Leeward District of that colony, March, 1857; for several years a member of assembly; commanded, from 1863 to 1869, the Queen's volunteer corps which was raised by him; private secretary to the governor-in-chief of the Windward Islands, from July, 1869, to December, 1871; police magistrate, Kingston District, 1st Jan., 1872; acted as colonial secretary of St. Vincent in 1871 and in 1874; stipendiary justice of the peace, British Guiana, May, 1877; administrator-general British Guiana (ag.), Jan., 1891; confirmed, Nov., 1883; also administrator of the de Saffron and Trotman Trusts; is a corresponding member of the Zoological Society of London, and the Pharmac. Soc. of Gt. Britain; executive commissioner for British Guiana, Colonial and Indian Exhibition, 1886.

events.text_span: Deputy Queen's advocate, Eastern Province, Ceylon, 1868

raw_text: HAY, C. S.—Deputy Queen's advocate, Eastern Province, Ceylon, 1868; ditto, Northern Circuit, 1878; crown counsel, 1st January, 1884.

HAY, SIR JAMES SHAW, K.C.M.G. (1889), C.M.G. (1887)—Joined 89th Princess Victoria's regiment in 1858, served in India during the latter part of the Mutiny; was adjutant for some time, and afterwards instructor of musketry; acting adjutant to the provisional dépôt battalion at Dum Dum in 1859; adjutant to the Glasgow Highland Volunteers in 1868 at their formation; assistant inspector, Gold Coast armed native police, 1875; district commissioner of Accra, Oct., 1875, inspector-general, Gold Coast constabulary, April, 1877; assistant colonial secretary, Gold Coast Colony, 1878; had charge of several special missions to the tribes in the interior; acting colonial secretary from 10th Oct. 1878, to 14th Jan., 1880, and again, 9th Aug., 1880; inspector general of police, Mauritius; administrator of the Gambia, 1885; administered the government of the West Africa Settlements, from 5th July, 1886, to Dec., 1887; governor, Sierra Leone, Oct., 1888; served in the Yonnie Expedition, 1887-8 (medal and clasp); ag. administrator Barbados, 1891; governor, Barbados, 1892.

events.position: prothonotary and clerk of common pleas
events.text_span: prothonotary and clerk of common pleas, Barbados, Dec., 1864.

raw_text: HAYNES, ROBERT.—Registrar in chancery, clerk of the crown; prothonotary and clerk of common pleas, Barbados, Dec., 1864.

HAYNES, W. LINDSAY II.—Clerk to chief justice, Barbados, 1874-1878; clerk to commissioners appointed to investigate offences against rioters, 1876; inland revenue officer, district "B," 1878, now inspector of I.R. officers.

HAZELAND, FRANCIS ARTHUR.—Clk. to puisne judge, Hong Kong, Nov., 1878; ag. dep. reg., Mar. to Nov., 1882; clk. to chief justice, Sept., 1883; ag. ch. clk. col. sec. off., Mar. to Oct., 1890; ag. clk. of councils, Mar. to Dec. 1890; ag. dep. regr., April, 1894, to Mar., 1895; dep. regr. and acct., Jan., 1896; J.P., 1894; has passed in Chinese (higher standard).

events.position: Crown prosecutor, southern district, N. S. Wales
events.text_span: Crown prosecutor, southern district, N. S. Wales, Jan., 1878

raw_text: HEALY, P. J.—Crown prosecutor, southern district, N. S. Wales, Jan., 1878; ditto, S. W. district, 1879; crown prosecutor, Sydney, Jan., 1882.

HEARD, H. A.—Assist. official assignee and dep. regr. of deeds, Singapore, Oct., 1890; ag. official assignee and regr. of deeds, Feb., 1893, to Jan., 1894; official assignee and regr. of deeds, Penang, July, 1894.
HECTOR, SIR JAMES, M.D., F.R.S., K.C.M.G. (1887), C.M.G. (1875).—Director of geological surveys, and curator of the colonial museum, New Zealand.
HEIDENSTAM, DR. F. C., C.M.G. (1884).—Chief medical officer, Cyprus, 1882.

HELY-HUTCHINSON, THE HON. SIR WALTER FRANCIS, K.C.M.G. (1888), C.M.G. (1883).—B.A. of Trin. Coll., Camb., and has been called to the bar by the society of the Inner Temple. Accompanied Sir Hercules Robinson to Fiji as private secretary on the occasion of the cession of the islands to Great Britain in Sept.-Oct., 1874; private secretary to Sir Hercules Robinson, 10th Oct., 1874, in Fiji affairs; private secretary for New South Wales affairs, Jan., 1875; colonial secretary of Barbados, Nov., 1877; chief secretary to the governor of Malta, 1883; and lieut.-governor and chief secretary to the government, 1884; governor of Western Islands, 1889; governor of Natal, 1893.

HEMERY, PERCY.—1st clerk, govt. savings bank, Br. Guiana, July, 1880; 3rd clerk, rec. office, Jan., 1881; cashier, Aug., 1881; ag. govt. savings bank, April, 1888; actg. bookkeeper, July, 1888, and Aug., 1890; ag. rec.-gen., and actg. sub-admnr., Berbice, 1891; actg. dep. rec. gen., Apr., 1891. May, 1892, and from May, 1893; secy., cattle commission, Oct., 1891.

HEMMING, SIR A. W. L., K.C.M.G. (1887), C.M.G. (1885).—Appointed after competitive examination a clerk in the office of the secretary of state for the colonies, Feb. 1862; promoted Oct. 1864, 1st junior class; private secretary to Sir F. Rogers (now Lord Blathwayt) 20th May, 1867, till 20th May, 1871; private secretary to Mr. Herbert, 21st May, 1871, till 29th September, 1872; appointed a 2nd class clerk 30th September, 1872; 1st class clerk, November 1874; private secretary to the Earl of Cadogan, March, 1878, to 30th June, 1879; sent on a mission to Paris, April, 1879; principal clerk, July, 1879; again proceeded on special service at Paris, May, 1881; British delegate to the West African conference at Berlin, 1884; on special service at Paris, 1889-90 and 1894, in connection with the delimitation of French and English possessions on the West Coast of Africa. Governor of Guiana, Mar., 1896.

events.position: C.C. and R.M., Ald Cape Colony
events.text_span: C.C. and R.M., Ald Cape Colony, May, 1868

events.position: was clerk to C.C. and R.M., Queenstown, from
events.text_span: was clerk to C.C. and R.M., Queenstown, from 1860 to 1868

events.position: C.C. and R.M. King William's Town
events.text_span: C.C. and R.M. King William's Town, 1873

events.position: C.C. and registrar of deeds, Kimberley, till
events.text_span: C.C. and registrar of deeds, Kimberley, till 1883

events.position: C.C. and R.M. registrar of deeds, King William's Town
