events.position: commanded the bookland Division in Kaffir War
events.text_span: commanded the bookland Division in Kaffir War, 1877-8.

raw_text: HEMMING, JOHN.—C.C. and R.M., Ald Cape Colony, May, 1868; was clerk to C.C. and R.M., Queenstown, from 1860 to 1868; did so at Bathurst, 1858 to 1860; ditto at Oudtshoorn, 1855; clerk to R.M. Oudtshoorn, 1857; C.C. and R.M. King William's Town, 1873; C.C. and registrar of deeds, Kimberley, till 1883; C.C. and R.M. registrar of deeds, King William's Town, 1883; C.C. and R.M., Albany, 1885; commanded the bookland Division in Kaffir War, 1877-8.

events.text_span: Shorthand reporter, debates, legislative council, Natal, 1883.

raw_text: HENDERSON, J. T.—Shorthand reporter, debates, legislative council, Natal, 1883.
HENDERSON, JOSEPH, C.M.G. (1879), of Natal.—Was commissioner to inquire into the affairs of the Transvaal; J.P.; acting treasurer-general Transvaal, 1877; M.L.C., Natal, 1857-62.

events.position: clerk of the Kingston circuit court
events.text_span: clerk of the Kingston circuit court, June, 1871

events.position: registrar in chancery and clerk of the patents, and clerk of the supreme court and crown
events.text_span: registrar in chancery and clerk of the patents, and clerk of the supreme court and crown, 1875

raw_text: HENDRICK, THOMAS.—Is an attorney-at-law and solicitor of the supreme court, and solicitor of the vice-admiralty court, Jamaica; clerk of the Kingston circuit court, June, 1871; notary public for Kingston, Dec., 1872; registrar in chancery and clerk of the patents, and clerk of the supreme court and crown, 1875; registrar of the supreme court, 1879.

HENDY, CHARLES LOWICK.—Senior assistant inspector of prisons office, Br. ana, 1881; ag. clerk of supplies and sec. to board of prisons, July to Oct., 1885; July to Oct., 1888; and June, 1890, to Jan., 1891; issuer of vict prison, Rio Massaruni, Nov., 1890; k of supplies and sec. to board of prisons, e. 1892; ag. suptdt. Orphan Asylum, Nov. to 1895, and Feb. to July, 1896.
ENRY, R.—Entered Tasmanian service, 1852; superintendent of telegraphs, July, 1878.

ENSMAN, ALFRED PEACH.—Ed. Univ. Coll., D.; B.A., 1853; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1858; on Midland circuit, 1860; counsel Treasury at Leicester assizes, 1865; revising officer, 1876; leader of Leicestershire and Rutlandshire sessions, 1877; atty. gen. Western Australia, 1883; resigned, 1886; judge advocate et al. of that Colony, 1892. Is author of work on the English constitution.
BERT, C. St. J. S., C.B. (1863).—Some in command of militia and volunteers in New Zealand.

events.position: private secretary to the chancellor of the Exchequer (the Right Hon. W. E. Gladstone)
events.text_span: private secretary to the chancellor of the Exchequer (the Right Hon. W. E. Gladstone), 1855

events.position: called to the bar at the Inner Temple
events.text_span: called to the bar at the Inner Temple, 1858

events.position: premier of Queensland and member of legislative assembly
events.text_span: premier of Queensland and member of legislative assembly 1860 to 1865

events.position: one of the assistant secretaries of State Board of Trade from
events.text_span: one of the assistant secretaries of State Board of Trade from 1868 to Feb., 1870

events.position: under-secretary of state for the colonies
events.text_span: under-secretary of state for the colonies, May, 1871

events.position: secretary of Order of St. Michael and St. George
events.text_span: secretary of Order of St. Michael and St. George, May, 1872

events.position: chancellor of the Order of St. Michael and St. George
events.text_span: chancellor of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, 1892

raw_text: BERT, THE HON. SIR ROBERT G. W., B. (1892), K.C.B. (1882), D.C.L. (Oxon.), J. (Cam.)—Educated at Eton, and at Balliol College, Oxford, of which he was elected Scholar 1849; Hertford Scholar, 1851; Ireland Bar, 1852; Latin Verse Prize, 1852; Eldon Bar, 1854; elected Fellow of All Souls; private secretary to the chancellor of the Exchequer (the Right Hon. W. E. Gladstone), 1855; called to the bar at the Inner Temple, 1858; judicial secretary of Queensland, 1859; premier of Queensland and member of legislative assembly 1860 to 1865; one of the assistant secretaries of State Board of Trade from 1868 to Feb., 1870; acted assistant under-secretary of state for the colonies; under-secretary of state for the colonies, May, 1871; is a magistrate and deputy lieutenant of the county of Cambridge; secretary of Order of St. Michael and St. George, May, 1872; retired 1st Feb., 1892; chancellor of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, 1892; acting lieutenant-general for Tasmania, 1893-6.
HOLD, F. W.—C.C. and R.M. Victoria, Cape, 1875; ditto, Bredasdorp, Sept., 1878; ditto, Herbosch, June, 1886.

EKVEY, DUDLEY FRANCIS AMELIUS, C.M.G. —Educated at Marlborough; cadet, Cape Settlements, May, 1867; passed in May in Nov., 1868; acting private secretary governor in March, 1869; chief clerk interpreter to the lieut.-governor, Penang, 1870, in which year he accompanied H.S. "Algerine" to inquire into various cases piracy on the coast of Acheen; senior sworn commissioner supreme court, Singapore, Jan., 1871, also in bankruptcy, and J.P. and ag. magistrate, 1871; Aug., 1878, police magistrate, Malacca; accompanied various expeditions to the different states of the peninsula; J.P.; resident counsellor, Malacca, 1882; also suptdt. of Negri Sembilan, 1883-6; retired, Dec., 1893.

ETHERSETT, ANDREW LANIYONI.—Ed. S. Coll. Inst., Abeokuta; out-door officer, 1870; clerk and interpreter to a mission, under Goldsworthy, to Ondo, in April, 1872; head of harbour police, 1873; second clerk and interpreter, administrator's office, 1876; chief clerk and interpreter, 1879. On special mission to Abeokuta, 1881; on special mission of conciliation under Capt. Douglas to Ikorodu after attack by Jebu Remo refugees, 1884; accompanied Lt. Denton as interpreter on mission to Jebu Ode, 1885; and Sir G. Carter in 1892 (medal and clasps), and accompanied latter on mission, Jan. to April, 1893, to put an end to the tribal wars in Jebu; chief clerk and interpreter, Aborigines Dept. Aug., 1895.
HEWETT, MAJ.-GEN. EDWARD OSBORNE, R.E., C.M.G. (1883).—Commandant of the Royal Military College, Canada, Sept., 1875, to July, 1886.

HEWETT, ROBERT D.—Assessor and collector, Province Wellesley, 1874; secretary to resident at Perak, 1879; acted as magistrate and collector at Selama and Krian, 1880; also for a short time in 1881 as police magistrate and commissioner, court of requests, Larut; and acted as superintendent, Lower Perak, June to Sept., 1881; magistrate and collector, Kinta, Oct., 1881; acting superintendent, Lower Perak, Mar., 1884, to June, 1885; J.P. for the Straits Settlements, 1884; magistrate and collector, Krian, Jan., 1888; state auditor, 1889; ag. district magistrate, Kinta, July, 1894 to Jan., 1895; dist. magistrate, Kinta, March, 1895.
HEYWOOD, J. B.—Secretary to Treasury, New Zealand, 1890.
HICKSON, THE HON. SIR JOSEPH, KT. BACH. (1800).—Montreal.

HIGGINSON, WALTER ROBERT.—Formerly lieutenant, Dublin City Militia; served through Cape war of 1877-78 in the F.A.M.P.; lieutenant and adjutant, 3rd regt., Natal native contingent, Nov., 1878; present at the attack on Sirayo's kraal, battle of Isandhlana; mentioned in the despatches as having endeavoured to save the colours of the 1/24th regiment; captain, 3rd troop Natal Horse; present at Ulundi, and during the siege of Pretoria (medal and clasp for 1877-89); assistant inspector, G.C.C., Aug., 1881; govt. secretary and superintendent, police, Gambia, Dec., 1886; inspector of constabulary, Lagos, June, 1881; has acted several times as inspector-general, and also as district commissioner, and as treasurer and collector of customs.

HILL, CHARLES W.—Clk., lower divn., chief sec. office, Cyprus, Jan., 1886; passed in Greek, May, 1888; acted as clerk, ex. coun., Aug., 1889, to Mar., 1890; and as priv. sec. to admiral, Oct., 1889, to Mar., 1890; govt. clerk, shipping master, and clerk of councils, and J.P., Falkland Islands, 1892; also priv. sec. to governor; ag. col. treas. collector of customs postmaster, &c., 1893; member exec. and leg. councils; ag. col. secretary, &c., 1894; special commissioner on land question W. Falklands, 1895; ag. inspector of schools, 1895.

HILL, DAVID YOUNG CAMPBELL.—As sixth clerk, Treasury, British Guiana, July, 1874; acting fourth clerk, April, 1875, to Feb., 1876; acting first clerk, Oct., 1877, to May, 1878; provisional third clerk, audit office, May, 1878; acted as colonial bookkeeper, Sept., 1880, to Mar., 1881; second clerk, Treasury, 1880; chief clerk, 1881; colonial bookkeeper, 1886; acting receiver-general, 1887, and again 1888; deputy receiver general, April, 1891; acted as receiver general, Mar. to Oct., 1891, and from Mar. to Dec., 1892.

HILL, E. C. H.—Cadet, Straits Settlements, Mar., 1875; acting treasurer, Malacca, Mar. to July, 1876; acting inspector of schools, June to Oct., 1877; acting collector of land revenue, Penang and Province Wellesley, Oct., 1877; acting inspector of school Feb., 1879; superintendents of education, Penang, and acting 2nd magistrate, June, 1880; acting magistrate, Province Wellesley, 1881; inspector of schools, S.S., 1883; ag. resident councillor Malacca, April, 1895, to Feb., 1896.
HILL, GEORGE F.—Member for Charlotte in leg. council, New Brunswick; president thereof.

HILL, J. K. D.—Entered Treas., Br. Guiana, Sept., 1879; 6th clerk, Feb., 1881; 2nd clerk, keeper, aud. off., April, 1881; 1st ditto, Mar., 1882; 2nd class clk, April, 1889; 1st ditto, July, 1889; sec. P.W. dept. comsn. of enquiry, 1889; ag. ch. clk., 1890, 1891, and 1895.

HILL, P. H. R.—Clerk and book-keeper, police establishment, British Guiana, Jan., 1867; senior inspector of police, 1st July, 1868; acting inspector-general of police, 26th July, 1872, to 26th April, 1873; acting stipendiary magistrate, 14th April, 1875, to Nov., 1876, and on other occasions; appointed assistant police magistrate for Georgetown, 3rd Aug., 1880; acting sheriff of Essequibo and stipendiary magistrate, 8th Dec., 1880, to 30th Nov., 1881; acting police magistrate of Georgetown and sheriff of Demerara, 1882; chairman, poor law commission, April, 1885.

HIME, Lt.-Col. A. H., C.M.G. (1876) (late R.E.)—Entered the royal engineers in 1861; became captain in 1874; was employed in Bermuda under the colonial government from 1869 to 1871 in the construction of a causeway and iron swing bridge connecting the island of St. George with the main island, for the successful completion of which works received the thanks of the legislature, and also received the acknowledgments of the secretary of state for the colonies; colonial engineer of Natal, May, 1875; member of executive and legislative councils, 1876; employed on survey of boundary between Orange Free States and Natal, 1884-5; retired, 1893.

events.position: D.C., Accra, sheriff, and Queen's advocate
events.text_span: D.C., Accra, sheriff, and Queen's advocate, Gold Coast, 1889
ag. col. secy., Jan. to Aug., 1892

raw_text: HINDLE, EDWARD BRUCE.—Educated Stockport Grammar School and Owens College; called to the bar (Middle Temple), 1879; district commissioner, Gold Coast Colony, 1888; D.C., Accra, sheriff, and Queen's advocate, Gold Coast, 1889; ag. col. secy., Jan. to Aug., 1892; ch. justice, S. Leone, 1896; died, 1897.

HIRSCH, CHARLES T. W., L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.S.A., F.C.S., (Lond.).—District medical officer, Rewa, Fiji, 1892; member Brit. med. assn.; corrtdg. fellow Hunterian soc. (Lond.); late resident clinical asst. N.E. hospital for children (Lond); and ophthalmic and aural clinical asst., London hospital; educated at King's College School and London Hospital.

events.position: apptd. officer of legion of honour for services at Paris Exhibition
events.text_span: apptd. officer of legion of honour for services at Paris Exhibition, 1889.

raw_text: HISLOP, THE HON. T. W. THOMAS.—Col. secry., New Zealand, 1889-90; apptd. officer of legion of honour for services at Paris Exhibition, 1889.

HITCHINS, ALFRED W.—Clk., col. secy.'s office, Jamaica, 1868; clk., customs, Kingston, 1869; landing waiter, 1870; asst. inspr. of invoices, 1877; ag. asst. surveyor, June, 1877; chief tide surveyor, April, 1882; landing surveyor, May, 1883; has acted on several occasions as collector of customs, Kingston.

RIXSON, FRANCIS, late Master, R.N.—Served fifteen years on Australian station, principally in surveying and exploring service; superintendent of pilots, lighthouses, and harbours, N.S. Wales, Jan., 1863; organised N. S. W. Naval Bde., 1863, and still commands it; President, marine board, April, 1872.

events.text_span: junior asst. inspr. of schls., June, 1886

events.position: ag. warden and supervisor, Chaguanas Ward Union
events.text_span: ag. warden and supervisor, Chaguanas Ward Union, Aug., 1893.

raw_text: HOBSON, CHARLES HENRY.—Ed. Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad, and Gram. Sch., Ashford, Kent; clk. edn. off., Trinidad, Feb., 1884; clk. andt. office, June, 1884; junior asst. inspr. of schls., June, 1886; 2nd ditto, Aug., 1890; ag. warden and supervisor, Chaguanas Ward Union, Aug., 1893.

events.text_span: Clerk of the peace of Victoria county, Trinidad, Nov. 1860

events.position: acting clerk of the peace of Port of Spain
events.text_span: acting clerk of the peace of Port of Spain, May, 1864

events.position: acting clerk of the income tax office
events.text_span: acting clerk of the income tax office, April, 1865

events.position: clerk of the peace of Port of Spain
events.text_span: clerk of the peace of Port of Spain, Oct., 1867

events.position: acting stipendiary justice, county of St. George
events.text_span: acting stipendiary justice, county of St. George. Jan., 1869

events.position: stipendiary justice of Prince Town, La Breu, and Moruga
events.text_span: stipendiary justice of Prince Town, La Breu, and Moruga, June, 1869.

raw_text: HOBSON, HENRY PEMBERTON.—Clerk of the peace of Victoria county, Trinidad, Nov. 1860; acting clerk of the peace of Port of Spain, May, 1864; acting clerk of the income tax office, April, 1865; clerk of the peace of Port of Spain, Oct., 1867; acting stipendiary justice, county of St. George. Jan., 1869; stipendiary justice of Prince Town, La Breu, and Moruga, June, 1869.

events.position: Apptd. after a competitive examination, to be a div. of the lower (now second) division of the civil service and assigned to the office of the comrs. of H.M. wks. and pub. bldgs
events.text_span: Apptd. after a competitive examination, to be a div. of the lower (now second) division of the civil service and assigned to the office of the comrs. of H.M. wks. and pub. bldgs., 1st Jan., 1885
transfd. to the colonial office, 13th Apr., 1891.

raw_text: HOBSON, WILLIAM EDWARD.—Apptd. after a competitive examination, to be a div. of the lower (now second) division of the civil service and assigned to the office of the comrs. of H.M. wks. and pub. bldgs., 1st Jan., 1885; transfd. to the colonial office, 13th Apr., 1891.

HOCKING, SIR HENRY HICKS, K.T. (1895).—Educated at St. John's College, Cambridge, where he graduated B.A. in 1864 and B.C.L. in 1866; 2nd class mods., 1st class law and history; Victoria scholar, 1866; called to the bar at the Inner Temple, Nov., 1867, and joined the Home circuit; att.-gen. for Western Australia, 1872; acting chief justice, 1873; acting chief justice, Gibraltar, 1877; attorney-general, Jamaica, 1880; retired, 1883.

events.position: Was executive commissioner of the colony of Queensland at the Paris Exhibition
events.text_span: Was executive commissioner of the colony of Queensland at the Paris Exhibition, 1878

events.position: deputy-lieutenant for the county of Warwick
events.text_span: deputy-lieutenant for the county of Warwick, 13th March, 1879.

raw_text: HODGSON, SIR ARTHUR, C.M.G., K.C.M.G. (1886)—Was executive commissioner of the colony of Queensland at the Paris Exhibition, 1878; deputy-lieutenant for the county of Warwick, 13th March, 1879.

HODGSON, FREDERIC MITCHELL, C.M.G. (1891).—Entered the imperial post office, appointed after competitive exam., Feb., 1875; was employed in the sec.'s office in connection with the transfer of the telegraphs to the govt. and appointed clerk in that office, 1870; apptd. after further examination, to the 3rd class (now 2nd class) of the secretary's office, Aug., 1876; promoted to 2nd class, Aug., 1880; was senior captain of the 24th Middlesex (Post Office), R.V.; postmaster general, British Guiana, Jan., 1882; col. sec. Gold Coast, Sept., 1888; adminstd. the govt., Jan., 1889, to Feb., 1890; June to Nov., 1891; Aug., 1893 to March, 1894; is commissioner of native affairs, principal registrar of marriages, president quarantine board, a director of the public officers' guarantee fund, and major commanding the Gold Coast Rifle Volunteers, which he raised in 1892.
HOFMEYR, JAN HENDRIK.—Member of the leg. council.
HOFMEYR, JAN HENDRIK.—Member of the Stellenbosch in Cape house of assembly; member of exec. council, 1881; delegate to colonial conference, 1887, and 1894.

events.position: ag. clk. and French interpreter to the govt., Durban
events.text_span: ag. clk. and French interpreter to the govt., Durban, 1875-76

events.position: 3rd class clk. to the govt., Durban
events.text_span: 3rd class clk. to the govt., Durban, 1885

events.position: received the govt. S. bk., Durban, and field cornet
events.text_span: received the govt. S. bk., Durban, and field cornet, 1891.

raw_text: HOGARD, ALFRED HENRY.—Appointed Lieut.-Col. Durnford Langalibalele expedition, 1873; ag. clk. and French interpreter to the govt., Durban, 1875-76; served in Natal Guides in the war (medal and clasp); 3rd class clk. to the govt., Durban, 1885; 2nd class clk., 1890; received the govt. S. bk., Durban, and field cornet, 1891.

HOGGE, EDWARD.—Inspector in S.S. P&O, Sept., 1871; chief inspector and acting superintendent, 1st June, 1880; assistant superintendent, 1st Oct., 1882; acting superintendent, April, 1883; ag. supt. S.S., Sept., 1890; at Penang, Jan., 1891, to Apr., 1893; supdt. of Malacca, Apr., 1893; visiting and licensing justice, and also coroner and protector of Chinese.

HOLDEN, ROBERT.—2nd lieut., 4th bn. Worcestershire Regt., 1878; lieut., 1879; asst. inspr. of musketry, 1879-83; capt., 1883; capt. of reserve of officers, 1885; asst. secy. to royal commission on the Scottish crofters, 1883-4; member of royal commission on the housing of the poor classes, 1884-6; private secretary to Sir R. Dilke in 1884; A.D.C. to Sir Henry Bulwer in 1885. missioner, Mar., 1886; author of several books articles on military subjects.

HOLLAND, B. HORWOOD, HOS. W. H., Q.C.—Member House of Assembly, Newfoundland, District of Trinity, Oct., 1894; upon resignation of Goodridge Government, Dec., 1894, accepted office as colonial secretary under the Greene Administration; delegate from the Newfoundland Govt. to the Ottawa conference in April, 1895, on the subject of confederation with Canada; is at present member of the exec. council without portfolio.
HOSE, CHARLES.—Cadet Sarawak service, Mar., 1884; resdt. 2nd cl., 4th divn., Jan., 1891.

(K.C.M.G., 1859)— Educated at Oriel and St. John's Colleges, Oxford, of which latter college he was elected fellow; graduated B.A. in 1845, M.A. in 1847; was private secretary to Sir Wm. Molesworth, when president of the board of works in 1853, and when secretary of state for the colonies in 1855; appointed chief secretary to the government of Malta, Sept., 1855; retired 1883; executive commissioner for Malta, Col. and Ind. Exhibition, 1886.

S. general manager, Natal government railway, 27th Sept., 1879; mentioned in despatches, 1880; commissioner, Natal harbour board, 1881; thanks of Lt.-Gen. commanding troops for services of his dept., 1882; member executive council, 1890; delegate to Harrismith Railway Confer., 1890; delegate to S.A. (1879). — Was teacher in government schools, Cape of Good Hope, 1842; clerk to superintendent-general of education, 1845; clerk in colonial secretary's office, 1847 to 1856; magistrate of Riversdale, 1856-63; ditto of Uitenhage, 1863-67; C.C. and R.M., Bedford division, Oct., 1867; also inspector of roads; acting administrator of Griqualand West, 1880; under secretary for native affairs, 1881.
INNES, THE HON. J. ROSE, Q.C. — Att.-Gen., Cape, July, 1890-2.

INNES, JOHN ROBT. — Ed. Edin. and Brussels Univ.; cadet S.S., Nov., 1886; passed in Malay, 1889; dist. offr. Sth. Malacca, June, 1890; ditto, N. Malacca, June, 1890; assist. Indian immigration agent, Malacca, June, 1889; ag. collr. land rev. and magis., Malacca, June to Nov., 1892; passed exam. in Dutch, 1893; ag. collr. land rev., Singapore, Feb., 1894, acted also as mag., Oct., 1894 to July 1895, and as offl. assignee, and reg. of deeds, July to Oct., 1895; ag. asst. col. sec. Oct. to Dec., 1895; ag. collr. land rev. and offr. in charge of treas., also ag. mag. and dep. regr. sup. ct., Malacca, Mar., 1896.

IRVING, CHARLES JOHN, C.M.G. (1881). — Was in the colonial land and emigration office from June, 1852; clerk, audit office, 1853; special clerk, audit office, Mauritius, 1864; auditor-general, Straits Settlements, 1867; resident councillor, Malacca, 1879; ditto, Penang, 1882; acting lieut.-governor, Malacca, 1870 and 1871; and of Penang, 1879; acting colonial secretary, 1871, 1875, 1878, and 1888; ret'd, 1887.

raw_text: IRVING, SIR HENRY T., G.C.M.G. (1888) K.C.M.G. (1878), C.M.G. (1874).—Clerk in colonial office, Nov., 1854; promoted to 3rd class, Oct., 1859; private secretary to Sir F. Rogers, now Lord Blachford, 1862; promoted to 2nd class, April, 1863; colonial secretary of Jamaica, June, 1866; colonial secretary, Ceylon, May, 1869; governor of the Leeward Islands, 1873; governor of Trinidad, July, 1874; governor of British Guiana, 1882; retired, 1888.

ISAACS, HENRY BABER.—Clerk of petty sessions and sub-collector of taxes, district No. 3, Tobago, 1 July, 1878; acting inland revenue officer, Leeward District, Oct., 1878, to 31st Dec., 1878; acting 2nd revenue officer, 4th Mar., 1880; acting 1st revenue officer, Jan., 1881; confirmed May, 1882; revenue officer and registrar of births and deaths, Carriacou, Nov., 1884; is a J.P.

ISEMONGER, EDWIN EMPSON.—Appointed to the audit office, London, Feb., 1856, after competitive examination; chief clerk to auditor general, Straits Settlements, Sept., 1867; acting colonial treasurer, Mar., 1869, to May, 1870; assistant treasurer, Nov., 1869; in charge of Malacca treasury and stamp offices, Sept., 1872; duties of collector of land revenue added, Feb., 1874; magistrate of police and commissioner of court of requests, Malacca, June, 1874; served in native disturbances (medal); magistrate of police, and commissioner of court of requests, Prov. Wellesley, 1878; acting auditor-general, 1879; postmaster-general, Str. Settl., 1882; col. treasurer, and member exec. and leg. counes., 1888; ag. resident councillor, Malacca, 1888-9; ag. and gen. S.S., Jan. 1895; ag. audr. gen. S.S. Jan. to Dec., 1895.

JACK, R. L.—Ed. Edin. Univ.; employed on Geological Survey, Scotland, 1867-77; govt. geologist, Queensland, 1877.

JACKSON, HENRY M., C.M.G. (1892).—Capt. late Royal Artillery; A.D.C. and private sec. to Sir Henry Irving, govr. of Trinidad, 1874 to 1876; and to Sir John Glover, governor of Newfoundland, 1877 to 1879; inspector-general of police, Sierra Leone, Dec., 1880, to 1884, and A.D.C. and private secretary to governor Sir A. Havelock, 1881-4; sent to the Gold Coast on special service in connection with the threatened Ashanti invasion, 1881; private secretary and A.D.C. to Sir A. Havelock, governor of Trinidad, 1884; commissioner, Turks Islands, 1885; col. sec., Bahamas, 1890; administered govt., June to Sept., and Nov. and Dec., 1891, July to Oct., 1892, and June to Oct., 1893; colonial secy., Gibraltar, 1894.

JACKSON, MAXIMILIAN JAMES.—Special magistrate and inspector under Northern Border Protection Act, October, 1868, Cape of Good Hope; was clerk at Victoria (East), 1858 to 1864; ditto at Richmond, 1864 to 1865; clerk at Murraysburg, 1865 to 1868; C.C. and R.M., Richmond, 1872, now C.C. and R.M., Knysna division.

Institute, Jan., 1883; prof. of chemistry, Ontario, Agricultural Coll., at Guelph; once minister of agriculture, Ontario, and sec. of the bureau of industries, 1st June, 1891.

JAMES, JOHN CHARLES HORSEY.—Educated at Rugby and Oxford, B.A. 1864; called to the bar Nov., 1866, and practised on the Oxford circuit; commissioner of land titles, Western Australia, July, 1875, on the introduction of the transfer of land systems; official member, legislative council, 1887-90.

JARVIS, A. LEONARD FITZGERALD.—Entered Canadian government service 1868; private secretary to postmaster-general 1882, to minister of agriculture, 1885; secretary to dept. of agriculture, 1896.

raw_text: JEFFREYS, EDWD. A.—Officer in charge, customs dept., Sarawak, Dec., 1885; suptdt. pub. works dept., and surveyor, Sept., 1887.

JEKYLL, LIEUT.-COL. HERBERT, R.E., C.M.G. (1885).—Lieut., R.E., April, 1866; employed at War Office and Trinity House in 1868 to 1870; destruction of sunken ships, from submarine mine, under G.P.O. in the telegraph department, 1870 to 1873, and 1874 to 1876; served in the campaign of 1873-74 on the Gold Coast; private secretary to the Earl of Carnarvon, Jan., 1877, to 3rd June, 1878; secretary to the colonial defence conference, 1878; secretary to the royal commission on defence of British possessions and colonies abroad, 1879-82; specially employed to report on the defences of Singapore, 1883-4; secretary colonial defence com- 1885; private secretary to Lord Carnarvon, of Ireland, June, 1885, to Jan., 1886; secry. general commission for Melbourne centennial celebration, 1888; private secretary to Lord-lieutenant of Ireland, 1892-5. KINS, G. H., C.M.G. (1891)—Clerk, government railways, Victoria, 1861; clerk of bills in the legislative assembly, 1865; secretary to royal commission on the subject of election of members of the legislature, 1868; chairman of committees, 1870; secretary to the royal commission on the parliament buildings, 1877, and assistant of the legislative assembly, 1878; of the legislative assembly, 1882; secretary to Australasian Federation Convention, 1890. For his services was accorded a special vote of thanks by the members of that body; on 1st May, was appointed clerk of the parliaments of India; is an honorary justice of the peace for the colonies of Victoria and South Australia. On the occasion of his twenty-fifth year of service in the legislative Assembly, the members of that body presented him with a magnificent service of plate as a mark of their regard and esteem, and on his retiring from the position of clerk of the legislative Assembly, a special vote of thanks was passed unanimously for the prolonged and singularly valuable services which he had rendered to the country and its members.

NINGS, SIR PATRICK A., K.C.M.G. (1880) (G., 1879).—Executive commissioner for the Universal Exhibition, New South Wales, 1879-80; president of the legislative council, Jan. to July, colonial secretary and premier, Oct., 1885, to Nov., 1887; delegate to colonial conference, 1887. INGHAM, SIR HUBERT EDWARD HENRY, K.C.M.G. (1893), C.M.G. (1889), Lettres, Paris.—Attaché, 1866; passed examination and obtained an honorary certificate, 1866; attaché at Paris, 1867, and was referred to Constantinople, 1870; 3rd secretary, 1870; on temporary duty at Athens, and was transferred to Carlsruhe and and was transferred to Carlsruhe and Carlsruhe, 1872; acting chargé d'affaires, 1873; secretary, 1873; acting chargé d'affaires, 1875, 1876, and 1877; was transferred to Berlin, 1877; acting agent and consul-general at Aden, 1878; was unemployed from 1879 till 1881 when he resigned; M.P. for Berwick 1881-5; naval secretary, Br. Honduras, 1887; administered the govt. for three months in 1888; col. sec. at Mauritius, 1889; administered the govt., Mar., Lieut. gov., Aug., 1892; gov. and com.-in- June, 1893; gov., Trinidad, Jan., 1897. RSEY (7th EARL OF), VICTOR ALBERT GEORGE VILLIERS, G.C.M.G. (1890).—Born 1845; is lieut. co. Oxford; has been a lord in waiting Queen; governor, N.S. Wales, 1890-3; represented H.M.G. at colonial conference, Ottawa, 1894. VOIS, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR WILLIAM FRANCIS MOND, G.C.M.G. (1878), (K C.M.G., 1874), R.E., F.R.S.—Served at the Cape of Good 1841 until 1848, of which time he was six on the frontier and in Kaffraria. In 1842 he brigade-major to an expedition against the Boers; served in Kaffir war of 1846-7, and map of British Kaffraria. From 1852 to was specially employed at Alderney in the construction of that island; from 1856 to 1875 on the staff of the war office as assistant inspector-general of fortifications, and subsequently deputy-director of fortifications. From 1857 to was secretary of the committee on the defence empire, and in 1859 secretary to the royal commission on national defences; whilst in war office, carried out fortifications at Portsmouth, Plymouth, Pembroke, Portland, the Thames, Medway, and Cork; in 1863 was nominated a civil C.B.; in 1863 was sent to Canada, New Brunswick, Nova Scotia and Bermuda, to report on the defences of those provinces; and again in 1864, to confer with the Canadian government as to the defences of the dominion. His proposals were approved by the imperial and colonial authorities, and those for Bermuda and Halifax have been adopted and carried out; in 1865 was sent to Malta, and in 1866 to Gibraltar, to prepare projects for the improvement of the defences at those fortresses. He again visited Halifax and Bermuda in 1869, and made further reports on the defences; in 1871-2 was employed by the government of India to inspect and report upon the defences of Aden, Perim, Bombay, and the Hooghly. He also visited British Burmah, and subsequently submitted reports with regard to the defences of Rangoon and Moulmein. Governor of the Straits Settlements, 1875-7; successfully quelled insurrection of Malays in States of Perak and Sungei Ujong; prepared plan for government of protected Malay States; was selected by H.M.'s government to proceed to the Australian colonies to advise the respective governments regarding a scheme of defence for the Australian ports; governor, New Zealand, 1882-9, when he retired.
JETTE, L. A.—Puisne judge, Quebec, 1878.

JOHNSON, ARTHUR EDMUND.—Admitted to the colonial secretary's office, Mauritius, August, 1877, after examination; copying clerk despatch branch, April, 1880; clerk 1st July, 1880; acting private secretary to governor of Mauritius, July, 1883; private secretary, 1883-6; 3rd class clerk, Aug., 1892; 2nd class clerk, Jan., 1895; ag. clk. of councils, Aug., 1896.

raw_text: JOHNSON, J.—Chief clerk audit office, New Brunswick, 1860; auditor-general, 1865; assistant-commissioner of customs, Canada, 1867; and commissioner of customs, 1st Jan., 1875.

JOHNSON, OBADIAH, B.A., M.D., C.M. (Edin.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.S.A. (Lond.).—assoc. King's coll., Lond.; Ed. S. Leone gramm. school and Fourah Bay Coll., King's Coll., Lond., and Edin. Univ.; ag. asst. col.-Surg., Sherbro, 1888-89; asst. col. Surg., Lagos, 1889.

Aust., Jan., 1874; ag. staff surv., Nov. 1883; confirmed Feb., 1885; surveyed and triangulated Kimberley district, and discovered Elvire and Mary rivers, where goldfields have been found; in charge of constrn. of telegraph, Roebourne to Derby, 1886-9.

JOHNSTON, LEWIS AUDLEY MARSH.—Educated at Derby Grammar School; B.A., Trin. Coll., Dublin; cadet, Straits, 1888; passed final examin., Malay, Oct., 1890; attached to G.P.O., Singapore, Oct., 1890, to June, 1892; ag. dist. off., N. Malacca, June, 1892; dep. regr. deeds and official assignee, Singapore, June, 1894; asst. postmr.-gen., June, 1895; ag. postmr.-gen. S.S., Sept., 1896.

JOHNSTON, ROBERT MACKENZIE, F.L.S. (1879).—Registrar-general and government statistician of Tasmania; educated Andersonian University, Glasgow; appointed to organise and superintend the working of the accountant's and traffic audit branch of the first railway opened in Tasmania (1870); transferred to service of Tasmanian Government (1872); chief clerk in auditor-general's office (1880); appointed to reorganise and conduct the then combined offices of registrar-general and government statistician (1882); royal commissioner to inquire and report on the fisheries of the colony of Tasmania (1882); appointed salmon fisheries commissioner (1882); member of the fisheries board (1889); member of technical education board (1888-90); chairman of boundaries board of advice (1890); originated the "Tasmanian Official Record," published yearly, which he still edits; has since 1870 taken an active part in the exploration and development of the Natural history, geology, and mineral resources of the colony; and in addition to official duties, at the request of the government, he (during the four years 1884-88) undertook the preparation of a large and comprehensive work on the geology of Tasmania ("Systematic Account of the Geology of Tasmania"), which was published by the Government of Tasmania as an official publication in 1888. For this work he was highly complimented by scientific authorities and by the government and parliament of Tasmania, and received a bonus of 500L Is also author of "Field Memoranda for Tasmanian Botanists" (1874); "Systematic Catalogue of the Fishes of Tasmania" (1882); "Provisional Aid to the Study of the Tasmanian Mollusca" (1890); "Root Matters in Social and Economic Problems;" has also contributed over sixty memoirs to the principal scientific societies of Australasia on scientific, social, and economic subjects; was elected president of section F—economic and social science and statistics—at the second meeting of the Australasian Association for the Advancement of Science, held in Melbourne (1890); is a fellow of the Royal Statistical Society of London; member of council of the Royal Society of Tasmania and of the Australasian Association for the Advancement of Science; fellow of the Royal Geographical Society of Australasia; member of the Linnean Society of New South Wales, and fellow of the Linnean Society of London.

JOHNSTONE, ROBERT.—Ed. Edinburgh; temporary clerk, audit office, Jamaica, 1878; 3rd class clerk, col. secretary's office, 1879; sec. to board of supervision, poor relief, Sept. 1886. Is also sec. schools commn., and asst. gov. meteorologist; sec. to special commissions in 1889, 1890, and 1892; elected Fellow Roy. Met. Soc., 1895.

JOLICOEUR, PHILIPPE J., Q.C.—Assistant secretary of the province of Quebec.

JOLY DE LOTBINIERE, SIR HENRI GUSTAVE, K.C.M.G. (1895).—Born in France, Dec. 5th, 1829. Educated in Paris, and called to the bar of Quebec in 1855. Elected to parliament of old Canada for Lotbiniere in 1861, and continued to represent the county till 1867, when he was returned both to the parliament of the Dominion and to the Legislature of Quebec. In 1874 he retired from parliament to devote his attention to provincial affairs. In 1878, on the dismissal of the De Boucherville cabinet, he was called on to form a ministry, which he did, holding power for one session by virtue of the vote of the speaker; he resigned 1879, after defeat in the house of assembly; later he retired from the leadership of the party; and in 1886, as a protest against the Riel agitation carried on by the provincial liberal party, retired from public life. In 1877 was offered the portfolio of agriculture in the Mackenzie government, with a seat in the senate; controller of inland revenue, without a seat in the cabinet, in Mr. Laurier's govt., June, 1896.

Coll., Camb.; LL.B. honours, 1876; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1878; appointed judge of assistant court of appeal, Antigua, 1880; acting senior police magistrate, Portsmouth, Aug., 1880; one of the commissions to inquire into the condition of the police force, Barbados, Oct., 1880; stipendiary magistrate, St. Lucia, Oct., 1881; member of legislative council, May, 1881; has acted several times as chief justice, St. Lucia and of Tobago; police magistrate, Grenada, 1887; and member ex. and leg. council, resident magistrate, Jamaica, April, 1888; pensioned judge, S.S., 1896.

JOSEPH, GERARD A.—Priv. sec. to Mr. See Clarence, Ceylon, 1891; ag. librarian and y. Colombo museum, Oct., 1891, to Apl., 1892, 1 Nov., 1892, to Sept., 1893; confirmed Oct., 8; is hon. sec. Ceylon branch Royal Asiatic Soc.
DOKE, ADOLPH B.—Inspr. native taxes, Fiji. re, 1884; stip. mag. and asst. res. commr. for lo. East, and inspector of native taxes, Oct., 4.
OURDAIN, HENRY JOHN, C.M.G. (1886).— Commissioner for Mauritius at the Indian and Colonial Exhibition; formerly M.L.C.

raw_text: UDGE, EDWARD ARTHUR.—Magistrate's clerk, on's Town, May, 1856; clerk in Colonial Office, etown, Aug., 1857; acting C.C. and R.M., aqualand, 1861; C.C. and R.M., Namaqua- , 1864; C.C. and R.M., Queenstown, Dec., 6; inspector-general of chests, &c., 1875; act- C.C. and R.M., Capetown, and R.M., Wynberg. , 1877; C.C. and R.M., Worcester, Nov., 1877; and register of deeds, Kimberley, May, 1882; eld in the following commissions: boundaries ordrecht and settlement of Tambookie loca- , 1870; disturbances in Transkei, 1872; railway rs, Port Elizabeth, 1878; Northern Border dis- ances, 1879; Basutoland and Transkei war nditure, 1881.

LYAN, SIR PENROSE G., K.C.M.G. (1874), (1869).—Served as a volunteer during the Chan rebellion in 1837-8; special commissioner swis and bridges in Lower Canada in 1839; dated to the commissariat in 1845; promoted he rank of deputy assistant commissary ral, and received the thanks of the colonial mment; assistant financial secretary to the d of works in Ireland, 1848; nominated director branch of the royal mint in Australia in 1852, was employed on mint service until the war e out with Russia, when he was recalled to the assariat; was employed as an engineer during early part of the war, and then designed and intended the construction of a "Marine a Flour Mill" and a "Floating Bakery" for apply of the army in the Crimea, which was h service throughout the war. Received the al thanks of the Minister for War, and pro- d assistant commissary-general, Jan., 1856; created a C.B. in May, 1869; was appointed n Agent for the colonies in 1858; sent on a al commission of inquiry into the civil estab- lments and general expenditure of Mauritius, ; and again on a similar mission to Malta in ; retired on pension, Nov., 1879.

raw_text: ST, ALBERT WOLFGANG, B.A. (1891).—Jun. r State of Perak, July, 1892; passed final l. in Malay. Aug., 1893; asst. to dist. mag. collector of ld. rev., Krian, Jan., 1894.

ST, HARTMANN WOLFGANG, B.A. (1877).— anted, after a competitive examination, to clerk in the office of the secretary of state he colonies, 3rd June, 1878; educated at us Christi College, Oxford; was an open ar on the foundation; first class in classical rations, 1874; Taylorian exhibitioner for German, 1876; second class in final classical school, 1877; assistant private secretary to the Earl of Derby, 6th Feb., 1883, to Col. F. A. Stanley (now Earl of Derby), 24th June, 1885, and to Mr. Stan- hope, 3rd Aug., 1886; private secretary to Mr. Osborne Morgan, 6th Feb. to 3rd Aug., 1886; assis- tant private secretary to Sir H. Holland (now Viscount Knutsford), 12th Jan., 1887, and to the Marquess of Ripon, 17th Aug., 1892; assistant secretary to the colonial conference, 1887; 1st class clerk, Apl., 1895.

JUSTICE, MAJOR-GEN. WILLIAM CLIVE, C.M.G. (1881).—Entered 75th regt. in 1852, Col. 1881; has held the following appointments: adjt., 75th regt.; adjt., depot battalion; town major, western dis- trict, England; military secretary to commander- in-chief, Bombay; asst. adjt.-general, Bombay; asst. adjt. and qrmr.-general, West Indies; col. on the staff, West Coast of Africa; col. on the staff, Jamaica; served in East Indies during the mutiny, 1857-8, and was present during the siege, assault, and capture of Delhi (medal and clasp); in command of an expedition sent in anticipation of war with Ashanti in 1881; now in command of the troops in Ceylon; was president of commission on Barbados police force, 1880; senr. member ex. and leg. councils, G. Coast. 1881; senr. member ex. council, Barbados, 1883; senr. member privy and leg. councils, Jamaica, 1886, and administered the Gov.. Feb., to Mar., 1887, May to Aug., 1888, Jan. to Mar., 1889; member ex. and leg. councils, Ceylon, 1893.

KAYE, A. B. R.—Capt., 3rd Batt., Royal Irish Fusiliers; served during Indian rebellion, N.W. Territory of Canada, 1885, attached to Boulton's Mounted Infantry; present at the Fish Creek engagement, and the taking of Batock; medal; sub-inspector, British Honduras constabulary, 1887; ag. dist. mag., Orange Walk, April to June, 1889, Nov., 1890, to April, 1892; inspector B. Honduras constabulary, June, 1889; ag. inspr. commdt., April, 1892; inspr. commdt., and memb. ex. and leg. councils, Oct., 1892.

raw_text: KEEFER, THOMAS COLTRIN, C.M.G. (1878), C.E.—Engineer of several railways and other works in Canada; was executive commissioner for the dominion of Canada at the Paris Exhibn., 1878.

raw_text: KELSON, JACOB WALTER.—Clk., surveyor's dept., G. Coast, 1885; chief warder and med dresser, lunatic asy., Accra, 1887.

KENNAN, THOMAS PONSONBY K.—Served in F.A.M.P. and C.M.R., 1877 to 1882, during Griqua and Basuto wars (medal); temporary clerk to R.M., Leribe, Basutoland, 1882; sub-inspector, Basuto police, 1884; inspector, 1886; asst. commr., Quthing, 1889.

raw_text: KENNEDY, WM.—Letter carrier in gener. post office, Western Australia, 1861; third class clerk, 1867; clerk, 1874.
KENT, R. G.—Accountant, railway depart- ment, Victoria, 1891

KER, J. ERRINGTON. M.D. (Edin.)—Was asst. house surg., gen. hosp., Birmingham, and house surg., Hertford Br. hosp., Paris; asst. surg., colonial hosp., Gibraltar, 1890; is also police and port surg. and public vaccinator.
KERR, C. M. MACLEOD.—3rd class clk.; constab. off. Jamaica, Feb., 1868; 2nd class, Aug., 1869; 1st class, Oct., 1873; chief clerk, July, 1879.

KERR, MARK.—Priv. sec. to gov. Falklands, Sept., 1884, to Apr., 1888; acted also as govt. clk., clerk of councils, shipping master, regr.-gen. and receiver of wrecks from Dec., 1886, to Feb., 1888; on special service to S. Leone, Nov., 1888, to July, 1889; principal clerk col. secy's. office, G. Coast, Oct., 1889; chief clerk, July, 1891; asst. col. secy., July, 1892; ag. chief asst. col. sec., Aug., 1893, to Mar., 1894, and Nov., 1894, to Mar., 1895; clk. of councils, Aug., 1893, to Mar., 1894, and Apl. to Sept., 1895; ag. col. sec., Apl., 1896; asst. col. sec., Lagos, Aug., 1896.

KERR, THOMAS, C.M.G. (1887)—Was employed in the colonial secretary's department, Barbados, in 1856. Police magistrate, 1860 to 1869; judge of the petty debt court, Bridgetown, to 1874; judge of the assistant court of appeal, to 1880; represented the parish of St. Joseph in the house of assembly from 1862 to 1866; member of the legislative council, 1876; major 11th regiment of militia, 1854; commanding officer since 1858; a member of the militia board. Author of "Kerr on the Cultivation of the Sugar-cane and the manufacture of Sugar;" administered the government of Grenada, Aug., 1878, to April, 1879; governor, Falkland Islands, 1880-91.
KERR, THOMAS S., M.B., C.M., Edin.—Colonial surgeon for Penang, Oct., 1883; coroner 1884; ag. prin. civil med. offr., S. S., Feb., 1893, to May, 1894.

raw_text: KERSHAW, CAPT. A. E., 4th Brigade, S. I. Div. Royal Artillery.—Government service, South Australia, 1887; Canada (N.-W. territory), 1881; Colonial Engineers, Survey Departments, British Honduras, 1884; private secretary to Governor Sir R. T. Goldsworthy, K.C.M.G.; clerk of councils, 1884; ag. registrar, supreme court, 1885; A.D.C., 1886; passed School of Instruction, Woolwich, 1886; inspector, constabulary, 1886; dist. mag., Orange Walk, 1887; commandant and ex officio member exec. and leg. councils, 1888; chief commandant of police and inspr. of prisons, Cyprus, Sept., 1892.

events.text_span: legal adviser, Federated Malay States, June, 1896.

raw_text: KERSHAW, THOS. HERBERT.—B.A., Trinity College, Oxon.; 2nd class mod. classics 1872; 2nd class finals 1874; Inner Temple equity scholar 1876; called to the bar, April, 1877; registrar of deeds, Singapore, June, 1887; official assignee, Nov., 1890; ag. atty.-gen., Oct. 1894; legal adviser, Federated Malay States, June, 1896.
KEYT, F., M.D., (Abdn.)—Licentiate of medicine, Bengal College; medical assistant, Ceylon, 1863; assistant colonial surgeon, 1863 colonial surgeon, 1885.

KIDD, JONH, C.M.G. (1882).—As private secretary to the late Lord Dudley Coutts Stuart, M.P. for Marylebone, 1847; secretary on the staff of General Count L. Zamoyzki in Her Majesty's Imperial service in Turkey, 1855-56; private secretary to the Earl of Carnarvon, 1857; entered the service of the Canadian Government, 1858; first clerk, 1858; chief clerk, gov. gen. office, 1875; acting governor-general's secretary, 1876, 1877, and 1880; retired on pension.
KIDD, J.—Member legislative assembly, N.S. Wales; postmaster-general in Dibbs' ministry, 1891-4.

KILPIN, ERNEST FULLER.—Third class clerk and shorthand writer, colonial secretary's office, Cape, June, 1876. private secretary to Hon. J. Gordon Sprigg, premier and colonial secretary, 8th Feb., 1878, to June, 1880; promoted to 2nd class, 1st July, 1878; clerk assistant of the House of Assembly, 4th June, 1880; has served as secretary on various Cape Government Commissions, and acted as private secretary to premier the Hon. Sir T. C. Scanlen, Feb. and Mar., 1883; secretary to the Anglo-German Commission on Angola Pequena and West Coast claims, 1885.

KIMBERLEY, 1ST EARL OF (created 1866, JOHN WODEHOUSE, K.G., P.C. (1864); BARON WODEHOUSE, 1797; baronet, 1611.—Son of the late Hon. Henry Wodehouse; born 1826; succeeded his grandfather in 1846; educated at Christ Church, Oxford, where he was 1st in classics, 1847; appointed a deputy-lieutenant of Norfolk, 1847; was under-secretary for foreign affairs from Dec., 1852, to April, 1856; envoy extraordinary and minister plenipotentiary to Russia from May, 1856, to Mar., 1858; again under-secretary for foreign affairs from June, 1859, to May, 1861; under-secretary of state for India, 1864; went on a special mission to Copenhagen, December, 1863; lord-lieutenant of Ireland from Oct., 1864, to June, 1866, when he was created an earl; was made privy seal, Dec., 1868, to July, 1870; secretary of state for the colonies, 6th July, 1870, to 2nd Feb., 1874, and again from 28th April, 1880, to 16th Dec., 1882; secretary of state for the Colonies, Dec., 1882, to June, 1885, and from Feb. 6th to Aug. 3rd, 1886, and 17th Aug., 1892; secretary of state for foreign affairs, April, 1894 to June, 1895.

events.text_span: passed final exam. in Malay, June, 1894

raw_text: KINDERSLEY, J. M.—Ed. Repton and Hereford Coll. Oxon.; cadet, S.S., Mar., 1893; passed final exam. in Malay, June, 1894; ag. dist. comr., Penang, Mar., 1896.

KING, AE. A.—Graduated (honours) Queen's Univ., Ireland, 1862, M.A., 1882; writer in the Ceylon civil service, 1865, and in the same year acting police magistrate of Upper and Lower Doombera; district judge, &c., Badulla, 1868; district judge, Kegalla, Sept., 1872; assistant government agent, Kegalla, June, 1873; assistant agent, Nuwara Eliya, 1875; assistant agent, Badulla, 1878; government agent, Uva, 1886; chief government comsnr., Mar., 1889; dist. judge, Galle, Aug., 1893; govt. agt., N.W. Prov., May, 1895; also col. treasurer, 1896.

KING, CHARLES KEASLEY BERRY.—E. Stonyhurst Coll.; matric. Lond. Univ., 1865; 1st clerk and 3rd sub-immigration agent, British Guiana, Nov., 1871; 2nd sub-immigration agent, June, 1872; senior ditto, April, 1881; acting immigration agent-general, 1881, 1882, and 1892-3 and 1894; member of ct. of policy on two occasions; also member poor law board, acting chairman, 1889 and 1894.

raw_text: KING, F. EUSTACE.—Clerk in the colonial secretary's office, Barbados, from May, 1866; transferred to government secretary's office, British Guiana, in June, 1868; inspector of police and justice of the peace, British Guiana, Jan., 1871; commissioner of taxation, 1873; emigration agent at Barbados, British Guiana, 1875; district commissary of taxation, 1884.

KING, H. G.—Clerk to improvement commissioners, Basseterre, St. Kitts, Dec., 1867; clerk to treasury, 1869; clerk to auditor and to executive council, 1869; acting treasurer, 1871; clerk and accountant, treasury and customs, 1873; clerk and accountant, savings bank, 1879; member general, 1883; acting treasurer, 1885 and 1886.

KING, JOHN.—Clerk to attorney-general, Gibraltar, Feb., 1866, transferred to colonial secretary's office, June, 1870; second clerk, Nov., 1877; assisted in audit duties, 1875–83; chief clerk and secretary to the board of health, 1883; asst. colonial secretary, 1891.

KING, JOHN HAMPDEN.—Junr. clk., secretariat, R. Guiana, Aug., 1880; 3rd class clk., July, 1881; 1st ditto, July, 1883; 1st ditto, Dec., 1889; ag. chief clk., June to Dec., 1891; April, 1892, to Jun., 1893; and April to Oct., 1893; priv. sec. to Lt.-Gov. Sir C. Bruce, 1887 and 1888; sec. to suit industry comsns., 1890, and to comsn. to enquire as to development of country between Essequibo and Demerara rivers, April, 1891, to Aug., 1892; ag. supt. Onderneeming Reformatory bl., Aug., 1894, to Feb., 1895; ag. ch. clk. secretariat, Feb. 1895; confirmed, Sept. 1895; P., 1894.

raw_text: KING, JOSEPH CHARLES.—Ed. Mt. St. Mary's Coll., Derbyshire; super. clk. immigr. dep., B. Guiana, Jan., 1888; 5th class clk., Mar., 1893; 4th class clk., Jan., 1894.

raw_text: KING, MERTON.—Assistant commissioner, massol District, Cyprus, Nov., 1878; registrar, ch court, Mar., 1879; local commandant military force, and assistant commissary, Limassol, Mar., 1883; commissioner, Nikosia, April, 1883; is also sheriff and president of municipal and water commissioners; member leg. coun., 1893; Br. legate of Evcaf, 1894; ag. rec.-gen., June to Nov., 1896.

events.position: Entered Queensland civil service, customs department, as clerk

raw_text: KING, T. M.—Entered Queensland civil service, customs department, as clerk, 23rd Sept., 1863; passed through various grades of the department; appointed collector of customs, Sept., 1882; member the immigration board, Aug., 1884; under secy. the treasury, Nov. 1893.

raw_text: KING, T. N.—Assistant commissary of taxation, British Guiana, July, 1875; commissary, July, 1875; also inspector of weights and measures, Trinidad, June, 1880; ditto, Georgetown, April, 1884.

KINGDON, ALFRED.—Educated at Winchester; called to the bar, Inner Temple, July, 1878, and read western circuit; police magistrate, Barbados, Jan., 1881; police magistrate and coroner, Grenada, Jan., 1882; solicitor-general, British Guiana, Jan., 1887; presdt. of Br. W. I. Confec. on quarantine, held at Demerara, Oct., 1888, appointed C., Br. Guiana, Mar. 1890; has acted as att.-gen. Grenada, St. Vincent, and British Guiana.

KING-HARMAN, CHARLES ANTHONY, C.M.G. —Ed. Cheltenham Coll.; B.A., of Trinity Coll., Camb., 1872; M.A., 1879; private secretary to the governor of the Bahamas, Nov., 1874, to Nov., 1879; private secretary to high commissioner, Cyprus, Dec., 1879; assistant commissioner, Cyprus, Dec., 1879; assistant to the chief secretary, Cyprus, March, 1881; acting chief secretary and member of executive and legislative councils, June Sept., 1882; auditor-general, Barbados, 1883; acting member of house of assembly, 1884–93; acting colonial secretary and member of executive council, 1884, 1885, and 1886; colonial secretary, Mauritius, 1893; ag. governor, Jan. to Aug., 1894; and Mar. to Sept., 1895; admnr., St. Lucia, 1897.

events.position: captain of yeomen of the guard
events.text_span: captain of yeomen of the guard, 1886

raw_text: KINTORE, RT. HON. EARL OF, G.C.M.G., (1889).—Lord-in-waiting, 1885; captain of yeomen of the guard, 1886; privy councillor, 1886; gov. South Australia, 1889–95; a lord in waiting. 1893.
KIRK, SIR JOHN, M.D., G.C.M.C. (1880). K.C.B., K.C.M.G. (1881), C.M.G. (1879).—Formerly Her Majesty's political agent and consul-general at Zanzibar.

KIRKE, HENRY.—Graduated at Oxford, B.A., 1863, M.A., 1866, B C.L., 1866. Called to the bar, Inner Temple, January, 1868; went the Midland circuit, Derby and Birmingham sessions; stipendiary magistrate, British Guiana, Sept., 1872; sheriff of Essequibo, 1877; sheriff of Demerara and police magistrate, Georgetown, Aug., 1882; acted on 5 occasions as judge, sup. ct. and twice as atty.-gen.

KIRKHAM, JOHN KARKEEK.—Ag. harbr. mr., Barbados, Sept., 1877, to June, 1878; asst. harbr. mr., Dec. 1878; shipping mr. and surveyor of shipping, Jan., 1881; ag. harbr. mr., Oct., 1886, to Mar., 1887, and Aug. to Oct., 1887, when confirmed in that post.
KIRTON, G. G.—Ag. clk., P.O., St. Kitts, 1889; 2nd clk., regr's. off., 1892; ag. mag. clk., Aug., 1891, to Feb., 1892, and Apl. to Oct., 1893; 1st clk. regrs. off., May, 1895.

ag. asst. col. sec., July to Nov., 1894

events.text_span: sec. to civ. ser. comsn. and to rlys. and roads comsn., 1894.

raw_text: KNAGGS, H. L.—Clerk, San. Fdo. Hosp., Trinidad, Apl. 1885; 4th clk. col. sec. off., Oct., 1885; 3rd clk., July, 1892; ag. asst. col. sec., July to Nov., 1894; sec. to civ. ser. comsn. and to rlys. and roads comsn., 1894.

KNAGGS, S. W.—Entered Trinidad service, Aug., 1875; chief clk. col. sec. office, July, 1892, and clk. of leg. coun., Jan., 1893; secretary to commission to inquire into road system, 1886; ag. private secretary to Governor Sir W. Robinson, 1886, 1889, and 1890–91; sec. to bd. of edn. and college council, 1889–91; priv. sec. to Sir F. N. Broome, Aug. to Sept., 1891, and to the governor of Hong Kong, Oct., 1891, to July, 1892; ag. asst. col. sec. Trinidad in 1891–2–3 and 4; ag. rec.-gen. and M.L.C. in 1895 and 1896.

KNIGHT, ARTHUR.—Shorthand reporter, legislative council, Straits Settlements April, 1869; acting chief clerk, audit office, Sept., 1869; chief clerk, Oct., 1873. Accompanied Governor Sir Harry Ord on his special mission to Labuan in 1873; ag. assist. col. secy. and clerk of councils, May, 1875, to Jan., 1877; assist. auditor-gen., 1884.

KNIGHT, F. G.—Jun. clk. prothonotary's off., Barbados, 1874; 4th clk. corrdce. branch col. sec. off., 1879; 2nd clk. audit. off., 1882; ag. ch. clk. on several occasions, and as govt. acnt. to Barbados Rly. Co.; ch. clk. record branch col. sec. office, 1892; ag. clk. asst. ct. of appeal, 1894 and 1895.

KNIGHT, JOHN LOCKE.—Clerk and Zulu interpreter to the Upper Tugela magistracy, Feb., 1876, to May, 1878; ditto, Umsinga magistracy, May, 1878, to Aug., 1881, during which time he acted for the R.M. and administrator three times; Aug., 1881, clerk and Zulu interpreter, and registrar to the circuit court, Newcastle district; April, 1882, acting resident magistrate and administrator of native law, Newcastle; Feb., 1883, ditto, Umsinga division; Oct., 1883, administrator of native law, and Zulu border agent to Lower Tugela division; has held the honorary appointments of crown prosecutor and officer for the sale of stamps at the several magistracies named; commanded a native levy of over 1,000 strong during the Zulu war; acted for two months in 1884 as R.M., Lower Tugela division; sub-commissioner, Zululand, April, 1887; assist. commissr. and R.M., Entojaueni district, June, 1887; R.M., Nkandhla district, Jan., 1893.
KNIGHT, M. T.—Financial secy., Newfoundland, 1886-9; surveyor-general, 1894.

KNOLLYS, C. C., C.M.G. (1888).—Educated at Mag. Col., Ox., open scholarship in 1868; 1st class math. mod., 1870; 2nd class final schools, 1872; B.A., 1873; M.A., 1877; sub-receiver, Trinidad, 1874; auditor-general of Barbados, 1879; president of the education board, 1882-3; acting colonial secretary, 1882, confirmed 1883; administered the government of Barbados, 1883; of St. Lucia, May, 1884, to May, 1885, and Nov., 1890, to May, 1891; of Grenada, 1892; col. sec., Trinidad, 1894.

KNOLLYS, MAJOR LOUIS FREDERICK, C.M.G. (1877).—Marlborough School; ensign, 32nd regt., 1866; captain, May, 1880; major, 26th Cameromians, 1881; A.D.C. to the governor of Mauritius, 1872-74, to governor of Fiji, 1875; commanded force sent to reduce mountain tribes, 1876 (promoted to company and C.M.G.); commandant armed native constabulary; and commissioner in mountains, 1877; A.D.C. to governor, New Zealand, 1880, and to governor of Ceylon, 1883; inspector-general of constabulary and director of prisons, Jamaica, 1886; inspr.-gen. of police, Ceylon, Oct., 1891.

raw_text: KNOLLYS, REGINALD F.—Ed. Oxford Military Coll. and Heidelberg Univ.; private secretary and clerk ex. and leg. councils, Br. Honduras, July, 1887; inspr. of schools, Jan., 1889; 3rd assist. col. secy., G. Coast, Feb., 1893; member bd. of edu., chief interpreter Ashanti expedit., 1896; is author of "Brit. Honduras, outlines of its Geography."

KNUTSFORD, 1ST VISCOUNT (creat. 1895). (created BARON 1888). THE RIGHT HON. SIR HENRY THURSTAN HOLLAND, BART., G.C.M.G. (1886), K.C.M.G (1877), C.M.G. (1875).—Succeeded to the baronetcy on the death of his father in Oct., 1873; edu. at Harrow and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1849, and went the Northern Circuit; legal adviser to the secretary of state for the colonies, Jan., 1867, and assistant under secretary of state, Mar., 1870. Resigned, 3rd Aug., 1874, and elected member for Midhurst, 22nd Sept., 1874; member of the royal commission on the treatment of fugitive slaves, 1876; one of the royal commissioners on the defence of British possessions and commerce abroad, 8th Sept., 1879; secretary to the treasury, June, 1885; and vice-president of the council Sept., 1885, and again in Aug., 1886; returned to Parliament as member for the Hampstead division of Middlesex, 1885, and again 1886 and 1887; secretary of state for the colonies, Jan., 1887, to Aug., 1892; president, colonial conference, 1887.

KOCH, REV. C. A.—Assistant missionary to Bishop of Labuan, bishop's chaplain, registrar of the diocese, 1856; diocesan chaplain, Ratnapura, Ceylon, Mar., 1866; acting colonial chaplain, Jaffna, Aug., 1867; confirmed Aug., 1869; acting colonial chaplain, Colombo, Jan., 1879, to Jan., 1880, and confirmed Oct., 1880.

KOCH, HENRY C.—Admitted to Natal bar as solr., 1884, as advocate, 1887; crown prosecutor and legal adviser to Zululand Govt., Apr., 1889; is also regr. of deeds, regr.-gen. of births, deaths, and marriages.

KOCH, WILFRED VINCENT MILLER, M.B., C.M. (Edin., 1884), M.D. (Edin. 1895, highest honours, gold medal, thesis).—late asst. in eye department of Edin. Royal Infirmary; and asst. to the lectures on diseases of the eye and mental diseases, Edin. Univ.; and assist. to the Gynaecological Dept., Edin. Sc. of Medicine; acting asst. med. officer, West Riding Asylum, Sheffield, in 1884; asst. med. officer, Hull Borough Asylum, 1888; late surgeon to Monckton and Car Main Collieries, Barnsley; asst. surgeon, Col. Hospital, Port of Spain, 1889; acting med. superintendent, Leper Asylum, Trinidad, 1890; dist med. officer, Diego Martin, 1890-92; ag. med. officer, Port of Spain, 1892-93; ag. med. Leper asylum, 1893; ag. dist. med. offr. D. Martin, 1893; ag. med. suppt. lunatic asylum, to Oct., 1894; lieut. 1891; capt. 1892, comdg. of Spain art. vols.; is surgeon to permanent Trinidad local forces.

raw_text: KUFAL, ALBERT OTTO.—Extra clk. and Natal, 1889; do. col. sec. office, Feb., 1890; class clk. in same office, July, 1890; and in audit dept., Durban, Jan., 1892; sworn trans. for French and German, sup. ct., Aug., 1892; clk., registry of deeds, Mar., 1893; ag. 2nd April, 1894.

KYNERSLEY, CHAS. WALTER SNA—Ed. Rugby; cadet, S.S., April, 1872; sec. assistant lieut.-governor's office, and superintendent of schools, Penang, 1873; ass. magistrate, P.W., 1875, accompanied expdn. to Perak and Sungei Ujong in that year; 2nd magistrate, Singapore, 1881; 1st mag. comsnr., ct. of requests, Penang, 1881; is superintendent of prisons; actg. col. treasurer, res. councillor, Penang, 1889; 1st mag., Singapore, June, 1890; ag. resdt. councillor, Malacca, 1893; confirmed Jan. 1895.

KYSHE, JAMES WILLIAM NORTON.—Downing Coll. Camb.; called to the bar, Lin. Inn, 1880; held various clerical appointments in Mauritius from 1871 to 1877; deputy reg. Supreme Court, Penang, 1880; has actd. registrar on several occasions and as mag. has published an index to the ordinances of the Straits Settlements; and has reported the decisions in the Supreme Court, 1786 to 1877; sheriff, Singapore, Jan., 1892; ag. regt. sup. and mag., Malacca, June, 1893, to Feb. 1894; regt. sup. ct. offl. assignee, regt. of commerce, land offr., Hong Kong, Sept. 1895.

LABORDE, EDWARD, C.M.G. (1883)—Ch. the treasury of St. Vincent, 1850; deputy treas., 1852; acting colonial secretary, 1856; pol. magistrate, 1857; stipendiary magistrate and col. 1858; provost-marshal, 1860; colonial sec. from 1861 to 1880; commanded, until 1883, St. George's volunteers, which were raised by him in 1863; administered the government of Grenada in 1871, May to Oct., 1874; administered the government of St. Vincent July, 1872, to 1873; April to May, 1874; Oct., 1874, to 1875; May to Dec., 1877; Mar., 1878, to 1879; and Mar. to July, 1880, when he was ordered to administer the government of Tobago; administered the government of Grenada, April, 1885, to May, 1885, when he was transferred to administer the government of St. Lucia.

D.—1st clerk, audit office, T., Jan., 1882; and deputy registrar, acting confcl. clerk to administrator, and clerk of councils, 1882; 2nd clerk, administrator's office, St. L., Feb., 1883; chief clerk, Sept., 1883; acting clerk, governor-in-chief's office, Barbados, chief clerk to governor, Windward Islands. private Secy. to Governor Sendall, 1888; chief of dice and excise, St. Vincent, 1889; inspector of prisons, 1889.
LACKEY, THE HON. SIR JOHN, K.C.M.G. 1894.—M.L.C., N.S. Wales, Sec. for pub. wks., 1878, to Jan., 1883.

In addition officer of customs for the Unions, and manager, Couva savings bank, April, 1886.

LAING, JOHN.—J.P. for Stockenstrom, Cape, 1888; member of assembly for Fort Beaufort, 1874; member of ex. coun. and commissr. of own lands and public works, Feb., 1878, to May, 1881; resigned seat in parliament. 1885; again elected 1887.

raw_text: LAIRD, THE HON. DAVID.—Born 1833; member L. coun., P. E. Island, 1872-3; one of the delegates who negotiated the admission of the land into the Dominion; sworn of the Canadian privy council and minister of interior, 1873-6; lieut.-gov. N.W. Territories, 1876-8; was chief commissr. negotiating treaty with Blackfeet Indians, 1877.

LAMB, JOHN CAMERON, C.B. (1895), C.M.G. (1890).—Entered the post-office in 1864; principal clk. 1877; was one of the British delegates to the submarine cables protection confec. at Paris in May and Dec., 1886; asst. sec. to the post-office, 1889; first British delegate to the international telegraph confec. at Paris, 1890, when he was also one of the representatives of the Cape and Natal; royal commissioner on electrical communication with lighthouses and lightships, June, 1892; 3rd secy. to the post-office, Apl., 1896; senior British delegate and "président de la commission des tarifs" at international telegraph confec. at Budapest, 1896, when he was also one of the representatives of the Cape, Natal, and N. Zealand; 3rd sec. to P.O., Apl., 1896; 2nd sec., Jan., 1897.

raw_text: LAMBERT, HENRY CHARLES MILLER.—Ed. at Eton Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; first class in classical moderations, 1889; first class in final classical schools, 1891; entered as student of Lincoln's Inn; aptd. second cl. clk. in the Col. Office after open competitive exam., 1st Feb., 1892; member of committee emigrants information office 1894; chairman of the committee, 1897.

LAMBERT, RICHARD S. F.—Acting clerk of supplies to prisons and asylums and secretary to the board of prisons, British Guiana, Mar., 1874; confirmed, Feb., 1875; acted, in conjunction with his own office, as clerk of the records, government secretary's office, April to Oct., 1876; clerk to inspector of prisons, &c., July, 1877; district commissary of taxation, Aug., 1877; transferred to customs, Mar., 1879; secretary to commission to inquire into law relating to roads, 1881; acting clerk of the records, government secretary's office, 1882; acting chief clerk in same office, 1882; special duty in same office from 1882 to 1884; 2nd clerk, government savings bank, Georgetown, 1884; chief clerk, government savings bank, Berbice branch, 1885; secy. to Lands Titles Comsn., Oct., 1889, to Sept., 1891; pay and qr.-mr., B. Guiana police, with rank of cty. inspr., Sept., 1891.
LAMBERTON, STEPHEN HARBORD.—Entered postal service, N.S.W., Sept., 1852; acting secretary, Aug., 1865; confirmed Sept., 1866.

raw_text: LAMOND, GEORGE.—Postmaster at Compensation, Natal, 1853; clerk in the audit office, 1854; second clerk in the colonial secretary's office, 1859; 1st clerk, 1864; chief clerk, 1875; and registrar of deeds and distributor of stamps, and registrar-general of births, deaths, and marriages, 1878.
LAMPSON, P. E.—Ed. Wesleyan mission school, Anamaboe, G. Coast; asst. master govt. school, Accra, 1893.
LANG, WM., M.D.—Health officer Carriacon, Grenada, 1863; medical officer, St. Andrew's parish, 1867; and health officer, Port of Grenville.

raw_text: LANGEVIN, EDOUARD JOSEPH, N.P.—Admitted as a notary public, Lower Canada, 3rd Dec., 1858; a member of board of notaries of Lower Canada for several years; clerk of the Crown in Chancery, Canada, 4th Jan., 1865; to same office for dominion, 5th July, 1867; deputy registrar-general, 1st July, 1868; under secretary of state of Canada, 9th July, 1873; elected secretary to civil service board of Canada, 1876; clerk of the Senate, 1883.

LANGEVIN, THE HON. SIR HECTOR LOUIS, K.C.M.G. (1881), C.B. (1868), Q.C., L.L.D.—Born, 1826; called to the bar of Lower Canada, 1850; created a queen's counsel, 1864; mayor of Quebec from 1858 to 1861, and as such received H.R.H. the Prince of Wales on his landing in Canada, 1860; entered Canadian parliament, 1857, and held a seat, first in the old legislative assembly, and since confederation, in the house of commons of the dominion till 1892; member of the executive council, Canada, and solicitor-general for Lower Canada, 30th March, 1864; postmaster-general, Nov., 1866; at confederation, 1st July, 1867, sworn of the queen's privy council and was appointed secretary of state of Canada until 8th Dec., 1879, when appointed minister of public works, and whilst holding that office, was elected as the successor of the late Sir G. E. Cartier, Bart., as leader of the liberal conservative party of Lower Canada; resigned office, 5th Nov., 1873; postmaster-general in Sir John A. Macdonald's second administration, 19th Oct., 1878; then minister of public works; resigned 1891; was one of the framers of the present Canadian constitution, and in furtherance of the cause of confederation attended the colonial conferences at Charlottetown and Quebec in 1864, and in London in 1866, when the Act of Union was carried through the imperial parliament; created a Knight Commander of the Roman Order of St. Gregory the Great, 1870; is the author of a prize essay on Canada, and of several other works.

events.text_span: asst. storekeeper, control dept., Trinidad, Oct., 1869

raw_text: LANGFORD, CHARLES WM.—Issuer, commissariat dept., Barbados, 1868; asst. storekeeper, control dept., Trinidad, Oct., 1869; clerk, G.P.O., Trinidad, June, 1871; 2nd ditto, Nov., 1871; 6th clerk, rec. gen.'s office, Jan., 1875; 5th ditto, Feb., 1877; 4th ditto, May, 1878; 3rd ditto, May, 1879; excise officer, April, 1884.

raw_text: LANGFORD, JOHN.—Clerk, col. secy's office, Fiji, Oct., 1874; chief clerk and clerk of leg. coun., Jan., 1878; registrar, supreme court, and curator of intestate estates, June, 1884.

LANNING, CAPT. ROBERT ALFRED, R.A.—Commissary of ordnance, Cape, July, 1882; and accounting officer, colonial defence, in addition, July, 1886; asst. col. mil. sec., in addition, 1892; entered R.A., June, 1862; garrison adjt., Cape Town, July, 1870, to August, 1872; adjt. R.A. division, in India, May, 1877, to April, 1881; served in Afghan campaigns of 1878-79-80; in first as adjt. of siege train; and in second as adjt., R.A., Khyber division (medal).

LANSDOWNE, 5TH MARQUIS OF (GT. BRIT.)—Creat. 1784; K.G., G.C.M.G. (1884); G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E.—Henry Charles Keith FitzMaurice, eldest son of the 4th Marquis, born 1845; succeeded his father in 1866; was under secretary for war from April, 1872, to Feb., 1874, and under secretary India Office, May to Aug., 1880; governor-general of Canada, 1883; viceroy of India, Nov.. 1888-94; secretary of state for war, July 28th, 1895.
LA PRIMAUDAYE, C.—Commander, R.N.; capt. of port, Malta, Oct., 1888; superintendent of police, 1890.

LARNACH, THE HON. WILLIAM JAMES MUDIE, C.M.G. (1879).—Late treasurer of New Zealand, and minister of public works in Grey Government. During 1878, 1879, and part of 1880 was one of the loan agents for New Zealand in London, and during 1878 assisted in making arrangements with the Bank of England to act as agents for New Zealand Government loans; during 1885, 1886, and 1887 was member of Stout-Vogel Administration, and held portfolios of minister of mines and minister of marine; published a work on the Mineral Resources and Mines of New Zealand; in 1891 was appointed commissioner and chairman of the Royal Commission for investigating the condition of the public trust office of New Zealand.

events.text_span: passed qualifying exam. for commissn., Oct., 1886
asst. inspr. G. Coast constaby., Feb., 1891

raw_text: LARYMORE, CAPT. H. D., C.M.G. (1896).—Ed. Westminster; entered Art. Militia, 1886; passed qualifying exam. for commissn., Oct., 1886; asst. inspr. G. Coast constaby., Feb., 1891; adjt., July, 1891; served in Jebu expdn. as staff offr. and sec. to Sir F. Scott; mentioned in despatches, and received thanks of gov. and ex. and leg. councils of Lagos (medal and clasp).

events.text_span: head master, Govt. gram. schl., St. Kitts, Jan., 1894.

raw_text: LASBERY, PERCY V.—M.A. Lond.; head master, Govt. gram. schl., St. Kitts, Jan., 1894.

events.position: called to the bar, Inner Temple
events.text_span: called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1885

raw_text: LASCELLES, ALFRED GEORGE, B.A.—Univ. coll., Oxon; 2nd class mod. hist.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1885; acted on several occasions as president of district courts in Cyprus and as puisne judge; president of district court, Papho, 1892; ditto, Larnaca, 1893; ag. puisne judge, June, 1896; ag. Queen's advocate, Feb., 1896.
LASH, JAS. MILLER.—Educated upper and middle schools, Peckham; schoolmaster, Kirkle prison, Liverpool, 1876; gaoler, Grenada, 1st supt. of prisons, 1890; governor, Glenda prison, Barbados, 1894.

LATOCHE, FRANCIS PHILIP.—Elected member leg. assembly, Dominica, 1870 to 1873; representative member for Dominica in leg. co. Leeward Islands, 1872 and 1873; acted as judge, Dominica, in 1873; commissioner for revision and compiling laws of Dominica, 1875, and revision of votes in 1876; acted as solr.-gen. att.-gen., Leeward Is. on various occasions, 1886; regr. and provost-marshall and registrar of titles, Dominica, May, 1886, and dep. regr. cert., 1887; also nominated mem. leg. assembly suptdt. of crown lands; appointed regr. and provost-marshall and regr. of titles, St. Kitts Nevis, Mar., 1891.
LATOUK, G. L., M.D., M.R.C.S., E.—Col. surgeon, Grenada, 1881.
LAUDER, CHARLES R.—Clerk, president's of Dominica, Dec., 1872; 4th revenue and quartermaster officer, Nov., 1874; 3rd ditto, Feb., 1880; ditto, rum department keeper, 1883.

events.text_span: Third clerk, sur. department, Western Australia, Nov., 1882

raw_text: LAURANCE, JOHN.—Third clerk, sur. department, Western Australia, Nov., 1882; registrar, col. secy's office, Jan., 1888.

events.text_span: Puisne judge, Griqua West, Cape Colony, 1882

raw_text: LAURENCE, P. M.—Puisne judge, Griqua West, Cape Colony, 1882; judge president. 1890.

LAURIER, HON. WILFRID.—Born at St. I Quebec, Nov. 20, 1841; became a B.C.L. McGill in 1864, and a Q.C. in 1880; esp. Parliament 1871, being elected for Drummond and Arthabaska, and was re-elected 1874 for same constituency; minister of inland revenue in the Mackenzie ministry, Sept., 1877, when office he held until the resignation of that government in 1878. He was defeated at the general election of 1878, but was immediately afterwards elected for Quebec East. He was re-elected at the general elections in 1878, 1882, 1887, and 1890, and premier in June, 1896, on the resignation of the government of Sir C. Tupper.

LAW, A. F. G.—Assistant commissioner Cyprus, 1880; president, dist. ct., Famagusta, 1886; member leg. co. 1887; ag. Queen's advocate, 1889; ag. ch. se. 1890; Queen's advocate, 1892; puisne judge, Settlements, 1893.

LAW, REGINALD A.—Sec. to Sir E. O'Mulch, justice, S.S., 1890; mail asst., G.P.O., Singapore, Apl., 1892; ag. asst. P.M.G., Penang, S. 1893, to Sept., 1894; Feb., 1895, to July, 1896; from Sept., 1896.

LAWRANCE, C. J.—Clerk, public works of Barbados, Jan., 1877; secretary central poor board, Sept., 1880; and manager of government emigration funds, June, 1881; secretary to West India telegraph conference, at Barbados, May, 1882; to confidential committee to inquire into the colonial defences, May, 1883; to harbor improvements commission, Jan., 1884; and to supply commission, June, 1885; acting clerk executive committee and council, Jan., to May, 1884, and on other occasions; acting police magistrate and coroner, southern district, Grenada, 1884; secretary to local committee, West Indian Exhibition, and compiler of the Exhibition Catalogue, Nov., 1885, and census, 1891; police magistrate and judge petty debt. ct., Bridgetown, Aug. to Nov. and Dec. 1889; and in dist. B. M. 1890; and D., Aug. to Oct., 1891; clk., ct. appeal, Sept., 1891; inspr. of police, and e cptdt., Fire Brigade, July, 1892, ag. inspr.-gen., April to Dec., 1893; and again, July, 1894.

raw_text: LAWRIE, ARCHIBALD CAMPBELL.—District judge of Kandy, Ceylon, Sept., 1873; a member of the Faculty of Advocates of Scotland; called in 1860; has acted several times as puisne judge of supreme court, and as commissioner of assize; issne judge, sup. ct., July, 1892.

raw_text: LAZZARINI, MAJOR-GENERAL C. C. JAMES, M.G. (1882).—Of the Royal Malta Fencible Artillery, decorated for services in connection with the expedition to Egypt, 1882.

LAYARD, CHARLES PETER.—Ed. Cheltenham Coll., and St. John's Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, June, 1873; advocate sup. ct., Ceylon, Aug., 1873; ag. solr.-gen., April, 1878; confirmed July of that year; ag. atty.-gen., May, 1891, to July, 1892; puisne judge, sup. ct., Oct., 1892; atty.-gen., Nov., 1892.

LAYARD, EDGAR LEOPOLD, C.M.G. (1875).—Whilst Her Majesty's consul in Fiji was concerned in the negotiations which resulted in the cession of the islands to Great Britain; administered the government from the cession, in 1874, till the arrival of Sir A. Gordon, in 1875; was consul in New Caledonia to 1889.

LEACH, ANDREW JOHN.—Ed. at Sir Roger Cholmondeley's sch., Highgate, and St. John's Coll., Oxon, B.A.; called to the bar, 1876; ag. puisne judge, Hong Kong, Ap. 1887 to Feb. 1889; atty.-gen., Feb. 1889 to Mar. 1890; May, 1891 Mar., 1892; June, 1892, to Jan., 1893; Apl. to Oct., 1894; and Mar. to Apl., 1895; puisne judge, S. Apl., 1895.
LEACH, JOHN VINCENT.—Educated Stonyhurst College, England; admitted solr. supreme court, Victoria, 1881; res. mag., 1888.
LEADER, HY. FRANCIS.—Entered govt. service, S. Australia, 1870; overseer, 1879; govt. contractor, 1888.

raw_text: LAKE, GEORGE.—Called to the bar, Western Australia, May, 1880; acted as assistant clerk to legislative council, and registrar of the vice-admiralty court; acting crown solicitor and prosecuting counsel in Jan., 1881, and again Jan., 1883; confirmed Jan., 1884; acting attorney-general and member of executive council in Sept., 1886-90.

LEARMONTH, CAPT. JOHN HENRY, late capt. 5th Lancers.—Ed. at Eton; staff adjt., South Australian mounted forces, 1886-89, A.D.C. to genl. of Kintore, 1889; private secretary and D.C. to Sir C. A. Moloney; Gov., Lagos, I clk. to ex. and leg. councils, Feb., 1890; pr. Bahamas constabulary, Aug., 1891; pr.-gen. Leeward Islands police, 1895.

events.text_span: chief clerk and registrar of the circuit court, and sub-distributor of stamps, Durban, June, 1881

raw_text: LEASK, GEORGE.—Acting clerk to master of supreme court, Natal, Jan., 1874; confirmed y., 1874; junior clerk, treasury department, Nov., 1874; 2nd clerk to R.M., Durban, Mar., 1876; chief clerk and registrar of the circuit court, and sub-distributor of stamps, Durban, June, 1881; 3rd dist. waiter and searcher, Customs, 1886.

LEASK, J. T., M.B., Ch.M. (Edin.), 1879.—Colonial surgeon, Malacca, Jan., 1891; ditto, Singapore, April, 1893; ag. col. surg. resdt., Singapore, May, 1895, to Mar., 1896; ag. col. surg., Singapore, Mar., 1896.

LEE, LIONEL FREDERIC.—Ed. Brentwood Gram. Schl., and Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey; writer, Ceylon service, June, 1864; commissioner of requests, Panadura, 1866; assistant government agent, Colombo, Feb., 1871; district judge, Kegalla, Dec., 1871; police magistrate, Colombo, 1877; regr.-gen., 1879; supt. of census, 1880; postmr.-gen. and dir. of telegraphs, 1886; special mag. to repress crime, N.W.P., 1887; supt. census, 1890; dist. judge, Jaffna, May, 1895; ditto, Galle, Oct., 1895; prin. collr. of customs, 1894; ag. treas. comsur. of stps. and member of councils, May, 1895; is author of a handbook in Sinhalese and Tamil for the use of native headmen, and of Census Repts., 1881 and 1891; principal collr. of customs, Ceylon, Dec., 1895; is memb. leg. coun., comsur. Loan Board, director of Govt. Savings Bk. and of W. and O. Fund.

events.position: visiting justice general penitentiary and official visitor lunatic asylum
events.text_span: visiting justice general penitentiary and official visitor lunatic asylum, 1878

raw_text: LEE, WILLIAM.—J.P. for Jamaica, 1860; visiting justice general penitentiary and official visitor lunatic asylum, 1878; adminr., general, 1880.

assistant surveyor and engineer, Selangor, Oct., 1876
