collector and magistrate, Krian, Perak, Aug., 1881

raw_text: LEECH, H. W. CHAMBRE, J.L.D., C.E., B.L.—Educated in the University of Dublin, where he graduated in 1867; qualified there as a civil engineer; called to the Irish bar in Michaelmas term, 1883; assistant surveyor and engineer, Selangor, Oct., 1876; first assistant commissioner of Perak armed police, Jan., 1877; collector and magistrate, Kinta, Perak, Jan., 1879; collector and magistrate, Krian, Perak, Aug., 1881; state treasurer, June, 1892; also ag. secr. mag., May, 1895.

LEES, SIR CHARLES, K.C.M.G. (1883), C.M.G. (1876).—Was private secretary and aide-de-camp to the governor-in-chief of the Leeward Islands, had local rank of lieut.-colonel, and brigade-major to the militia; entered the army in June, 1854; adjutant, 2nd batt. Royal Welsh Fusiliers, 1858; sold out in Jan., 1866; and was appointed to the adjutancy of the 3rd administrative battalion, Derbyshire rifle volunteers; resigned in May, 1867; appointed civil commandant at Accra, 1869; collector of customs, Lagos, Nov., 1872; sent to the Gold Coast to act as colonial Secretary under Sir Garnet Wolseley during the Ashanti War; administered the government of the Gold Coast Settlements for a short period after the war; administrator of the government of Lagos, July, 1874; and also lieutenant-governor of the Gold Coast Colony, Nov. 1874; despatched on a special mission to Coomassie in 1874, to preserve peace between the Juabins and Ashantis; administered the general government on several occasions between 1876 and 1879, during the absence of governors-in-chief; governor of Labuan and British consul at Brunei, Sept., 1879; governor, Bahamas, 1881; governor, Leewards, 1883; governor, Barbados, 1885; governor, Mauritius, 1889; Br. Guiana, 1893-5; retired 1895.
LEFROY, A O'GRADY, C.M.G. (1878).—Treasurer of Western Australia, 1856; was private secretary to Governor Fitzgerald, 1849 to 1855; acting colonial sec., 1875, to Aug., 1879; retired, 1890.

events.text_span: actg. supert., public works and res. eng., Larut, 1885-7

events.text_span: chief surveyor and in charge survey dept., 1887.

raw_text: LEFROY, GEORGE ANTHONY, A.M.I.C.E.—Asst. surveyor, West Australia, 1879-80; priv. secy. to Sir J. H. Lefroy as admin. of Tasmania; joined public wks. depart., Perak, 1882; actg. supert., public works and res. eng., Larut, 1885-7; actg. res. eng., Perak State Railway, 1886-7; chief surveyor and in charge survey dept., 1887.

LEGGE, CHAS. HENRY GLADMAN.—Clerk to the attorney-general, Trinidad, Sept., 1867; 3rd clerk in the audit office, April, 1872; was clerk of the colonial hospital, port of Spain, Oct., 1873; 6th clerk, receiver-general's department, Nov., 1874; 2nd clerk, audit office, January, 1875; chief clerk pat. office, 1880; 2nd clerk, audit office, Demerara, May, 1880; (designation altered to "Inspector of Accounts" Jan., 1881); chief clerk, 16th Jan., 1882; acting auditor-general, 1883, 1886, 1888, and 1889.

events.text_span: Commandant and inspecting field officer, Tasmania, 1882.

raw_text: LEGGE, LIEUT.-COL. W. V.—Commandant and inspecting field officer, Tasmania, 1882.

raw_text: LE HUNTE, GEORGE RUTHVEN, Trin. Coll., Camb., 1873, M.A., 1880.—Called to the bar, Inner Temple, May, 1881; second private secretary to governor, Fiji, 1875; stipendiary magistrate, 1875; resident commissioner Colo. Viti Levu, 1877; acting commandant, A.N.C., 1878-9; acting colonial secretary, 1880; assistant colonial secretary, Jan., 1882; acting attorney-general, Nov., 1882; judicial commissioner for the high commission to Western Pacific Islands, May, 1883; acting attorney-general, May, 1884; acting receiver-general, Jan., 1886; president of Dominica, 1887; col. sec., Barbados, with dormant comsn. as admnr., 1894.

raw_text: LEICESTER, REGINALD BARNABY.—Assist. treas. and collr. of stamps, Penang, Feb., 1890.

LEIGH, J. T.—Clerical asst. treas., Lagos, 1876; clk. P.W. dept., 1877; 3rd clk. customs and treas., Jan., 1879; acted on various occasions as chief clk. and warehouse keeper; clk. and cashier savings bk., 1887; 1st clk. and statistician customs, Jan., 1891.
LEMIERE, H.—District magistrate, Mauritius, 1883

LEMON, ARTHUR HENRY.—Educated at Merchant Taylors School and Exeter Coll., Oxford (scholar); B.A., 1887; cadet, Straits, 1888; priv. sec. to governor, Sep., 1889 to Jan., 1890; attached to Chinese protectorate, Penang, Aug., 1891; sec. to currency comtee., June, 1893; ag. dist.-offr., South Malacca, Dec., 1893; passed in Malay 1894; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Ap., 1894; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., May, 1895; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of councils, June, ag. 2nd assist. col. secy., Aug., 1895; ag. dist. offr. Malacca, Oct., 1895; dist. offr. Prov. Wellesley, June, 1896, but continued to act at Malacca.

LENNOX, ARKYLL NAPIER OLIVER.—Sixth clerk, immigration department, British Guiana, 1882; 5th clerk, 1884; 4th clerk, 1885; awarded second class certificate in Hindustani, 1885; acted as 1st clk., 1887, and July, 1889, to Jan., 1890; 3rd class clk., land dept., Jan., 1891; 2nd class clk., April, 1891.

events.text_span: Atty.-gen., Cape of Good Hope, 1 July, 1882

events.position: member of leg. assembly, and of exec. council
events.text_span: member of leg. assembly, and of exec. council, 1881

raw_text: LEONARD, THE HON. JAMES WESTON—Atty.-gen., Cape of Good Hope, 1 July, 1882; member of leg. assembly, and of exec. council, 1881; Q.C.; resigned, 1884.

LETHBRIDGE, H. BRIDGMAN.—Served in 2nd Queen's Royal Regt 1877-84; asst. inspr. G. Coast Constaby., Oct., 1886; on special service in Eastern Akim, 1887; adjt. G.C.C., Oct., 1888; on special political mission to Ashanti, Gaman, and Schwi, Nov., 1888, to Feb., 1889; inspr. G.C.C., July, 1889; ag. inspr.-gen., Sept., 1889, to Sept., 1890; local commandant, Cyprus police, Aug., 1891; suptdt., Hong Kong Prison, 1892.

events.position: was secretary to the Victorian commission, and acting commissioner in charge of the Victorian exhibits at the Paris Exhibition
events.text_span: was secretary to the Victorian commission, and acting commissioner in charge of the Victorian exhibits at the Paris Exhibition, 1878

raw_text: LEVEY, GEORGE COLLINS, C.M.G. (1878).—Member legislative council, Victoria, 1861-8; was secretary to the Victorian commission, and acting commissioner in charge of the Victorian exhibits at the Paris Exhibition, 1878; secretary, Melbourne Exhibition, 1880-1; executive commissioner, Victoria, Amsterdam Exhibition, 1883; secretary, Adelaide Jubilee Exhibition, 1887, and Tasmanian Exhibition, 1894.

raw_text: LEWIS, ARTHUR KENNEDY.—Clk. to comsnr. regr. of births and deaths, West Indies, S. Leone, Apl., 1893; transferred to regr.-gen. off., Oct., 1895.

raw_text: LEWIS, E. CORNEWALL.—Entered Government office, St. Vincent, as supernumerary, May, 1877; clerk to Kingstown police magistrate, 1880; acting colonial registrar, 1883; acting provost marshal, 1884; acting colonial postmaster, 1885; acting registrar, 1886; deputy comr. Kingstown, 1886; acct. of public works, Grenada, 1887; col. postmr., St. Vincent, 1892.

events.position: office asst. to govt. agent, N.P. Jaffna
events.text_span: office asst. to govt. agent, N.P. Jaffna, 1883

raw_text: LEWIS, J. P.—Educated at Mill Hill School and Queen's Uni., B.A., 1876; M.A., 1882; entered Ceylon civil service, July, 1877; act. pol. mag. Matara, Jan., 1881; ditto, Pt. Pedro, 1883; office asst. to govt. agent, N.P. Jaffna, 1883; mag., Gampula, 1884; act. office asst. to agent, Cent. Prov., Kandy, 1885; act. sec. of requests, Colombo, 1885-6; office asst. to agent, C.P., 1887; act. dist. judge, Matara, 1889; assistant agent, Negombo, 1891; ditto Vavuniya, Kilinochchi, Apl., 1896.

LEWIS, JACOB WILLIAM.—Educated at St. George's Grammar School, Sierra Leone; served in customs' department, Jan., 1868; assistant head waiter after competitive examination, 1869; extra clerk, colonial office, Feb., 1870; assistant private secretary to governor, May, 1871; clerk of legislative council, Mar., 1872; confidential clerk and clerk of the council at Gold Coast, 1873; governor's clerk, Feb., 1874; served on the Mongray and Bargroo expedition, Dec., 1875; on an expedition to the Scarcies, April, 1878; on the Bompeh and Ribbee expedition, 1880, and on the Scarcies, Port Lokko, Sulymah expeditions, 1885; also clerk to bar education, May, 1882; West African medal clasps, Yonnie expedition, 1887-8 and Tamida expedition, 1892; clerk of exec. council, June, 1893; J.P. for the colony, 1896.

events.text_span: Probationary customs dept., S. Leone, June, 1892

raw_text: LEWIS, JACOB WM. (JUNR.).—Probationary customs dept., S. Leone, June, 1892; 2nd officer, June, 1895.

LEWIS, JOHN CHRISTOPHER.—Entered Civil service, 1861; postmaster, San Fernando, 1869; received the thanks of inhabitants for services in 1882; acting 2nd clerk, general office, 1868; acting postmaster general, 1890; 1891; commissioner for affidavits and J.P., received the thanks of the inhabitants of Fernando in 1882 for his zeal in discharge of duties, and the legislative council in Jan., 1893; passed a unanimous resolution that his services were deserving of recognition.

LEWIS, WALTER LLEWELLYN, M.A.—Medallist, Queen's University (Irel.); called to bar, Middle Temple, Jan., 1876; joined bar and afterwards sth. Wales and Chester circuit, stipendiary justice, Trinidad, Dec., 1884; registrar general and registrar of the supreme court, St. Vincent, 1886; stipendiary, Port of Spain and Western District, St. George, Sept., 1887; acting puisne judge, 1888, and other occasions; chairman of commission, 1886; of committee on agricultural tracts, 1888; and prepared agric. contracts, 1889; puisne judge, 1893; ag. chief justice, 1894.

LEYS, ANDREW K.—Priv. sec. to gov., 1882; acting col. treas. and auditor, 1882 to 1884; and judge general court, 1884-5; magistrate of requests, and postmaster-general, 1887. Oct., 1886; also shipping mastr. and coroner, 1st bvn.

LEYS, PETER, C.M.G. (1890).—Educated at the Universities of Glasgow and Edinburgh, of which latter he is a graduate in medicine; entered colonial service, Labuan, Feb., 1876; admin. the govt. 187-7; held a commission as H.B.M.'s consul-general for Borneo; retired, 1889.
LIGHTBOURN, H. C.—Port officer, Bahamas, st June, 1869; examining officer, 3rd March, 1876.

events.position: vice-president of executive-council, col. secy. and attorney-general
events.text_span: vice-president of executive-council, col. secy. and attorney-general, 1868-70

raw_text: LILLEY, SIR CHARLES, KNT. (1851).—Called to Queensland bar, Nov., 1861; M.P., 1st legislative assembly, April, 1860; attorney-general, 1865-6, and 1866-7; premier 1868-70; vice-president of executive-council, col. secy. and attorney-general, 1868-70; Q.C., Dec., 1865; puisne judge, 1874; def justice, 1879; resigned, 1893.

events.position: clerk of the peace for the parish of St
events.text_span: clerk of the peace for the parish of St. Mary, 1854

raw_text: LINDO, SOLOMON DA SILVA.—Admitted attorney supreme court, Jamaica, 1848; clerk of the peace for the parish of St. Mary, 1854; resigned pension, 1868; advocate of the supreme court, 1870, and asst. to the attorney-general, 1872.
LINGHAM, ARTHUR.—Lieut. R.N. (retd.); served in Egypt 1882 (medal and star); Hbr. mr., Guiana, 1888; chief of police, Grenada, 1892.

events.text_span: Assistant to manager of Tukva tea and cinchona estate, Darjeeling, May, 1870

events.text_span: secretary to sub-committee for silk and vegetable products, Indian and Colonial Exhibitions, 1886
conservator of forests, eastern divisions, September, 1888.

raw_text: LISTER, JOSEPH STORR.—Assistant to manager of Tukva tea and cinchona estate, Darjeeling, May, 1870; passed lower standard examination in Hindustani and an examination in land surveying and levelling; appointed to the forest department, Punjab, in charge of Changa Manga, Lahore, and Paballa plantations, 1871; transferred to Hazara, assist deputy conservator, in exploring and reporting on hill forests of Khagan; on special duty to Kulu and Kangra in charge of Nupur Division; left India owing to ill-health, 1874; Supt. of plantations, Cape, Jan., 1875; J.P. and ad magistrate, Ang., 1875; in conjunction with her duties, custodian of ex-chief Langalibalele & ex-king Cetewayo during their detention at Sylvat and Oude Moulen; established plantations at Worcester, Beaufort West, Tokai, and Eijies Kraal; secretary to sub-committee for silk and vegetable products, Indian and Colonial Exhibitions, 1886; conservator of forests, eastern divisions, September, 1888.
LISTER, HON. MARTIN.—Ed. at Cheltenham Coll.; secy. to resdt., Perak, Sept., 1884; transferred to Selangor, Nov., 1885; suptdt., Negri Sembilan, Jan., 1887; resident, 1889.

LITTLE, JOSEPH J.—Called to the bar 1859; member of the legislature, Newfoundland, 1868-83; C. 1873; attorney-general 1870; member executive council, 1870-4 and 1881-3; puisne judge, Supreme Court, 1883.

LEWELYN, ROBT. B., C.M.G. (1889).—Extra clerk, Colonial Office, London, from 1868; registrar in colonial secretary's office, Jamaica, Nov., 1869; was nominated as one of a commission appointed to inquire into prison discipline in Jamaica, 1873; private secretary to Sir J. P. Grant, July, 1873; clerk of privy council, Jamaica, 1877; commissioner for Turks Islands, 1878; administrator of Tobago, 1885; admin., St. Vincent, 1888; actg. admin., Grenada, 1888; admin., St. Lucia, 1889, with dormant com. as admin., Windward Islands; governor, Gambia, 1891; Boer war, medal with two clasps, 1891-2 and 1893-4.

minister for mines, March to August, 1877

raw_text: LLOYD, GEORGE ALFRED.—Was agent for the government of New South Wales in England in 1857, and 1858; postmaster-general of New South Wales in 1872; colonial treasurer in 1873-4; minister for mines, March to August, 1877; a member of Leg. Coun., N.S.W.

events.text_span: appointed to 3rd clerkship, customs, Barbados, Nov., 1876

raw_text: LLOYD, JAS. S.—Educated at Codrington College, Barbados; appointed to 3rd clerkship, customs, Barbados, Nov., 1876; 2nd indoor officer, customs, Dec., 1890.

events.position: Her Majesty's commissioner of woods, forests, and land revenue from
events.text_span: Her Majesty's commissioner of woods, forests, and land revenue from 1882 to 1884

raw_text: LOCH, THE RT. HON. LORD LOCH OF DRYLAW, 1ST BARON (creat. 1895). SIR HENRY BROUGHAM LOCH, G.C.B. (1892), G.C.M.G. (1887), K.C.B. (1880), C.B. (1860).—Entd. 3rd Bengal Cavalry, 1844; served Sutlej campaign 1845-6; A.D.C. to the commander-in-chief, Lord Gough; adjutant and 2nd in command, Skinner's Horse, till 1853; sent on special military service to Turkey to assist in organizing Turkish troops, with local rank of major, 1854; crossed with army from Varna to Crimea, 1854; accompanied Earl of Elgin's special embassy to China, 1857-58; attached to head-quarters of commander-in-chief during operations in the field; bearer to England of Treaty of Yeddo, 1858; secretary to Earl of Elgin's second embassy to China, 1860; attached to head-quarters of the army during military operations; was treacherously made prisoner and cruelly treated by the Chinese, while engaged in negotiations under flag of truce; brought home ratified treaty of Tien-tsin, and Convention of Pekin; private secretary to Right Hon. Sir George Grey, secretary of state for home department, 1861; lieut.-governor, Isle of Man, 1863 to 1882; colonel commandant, 4th battalion, Cheshire Regiment; Her Majesty's commissioner of woods, forests, and land revenue from 1882 to 1884; govr. of Victoria, 1881; of the Cape, 1889-95.

LOCKHART, J. H. STEWART, F.R.G.S., M.R.A.S.—Edinburgh University; Greek medalist, &c.; appointed, after competitive examination, to a Hong Kong cadetship, 1878; attached to the Colonial Office for one year, 1879; passed cadet, 1882; superintendent, opium revenue, March, 1883; assistant colonial secretary and assistant auditor-general, Aug., 1883, acting registrar general, 1884 and 1885; reg.-gen., 1887, and M.L.C., 1889; member of ex. council, 1891; ag. col. secy., April, 1894; col. secy. and regr.-gen., 24th Mar., 1895.
LOCKHART, NORMAN.—4th revenue officer, Dominica, Feb., 1880; acting postmaster, 1881; acting excise officer, May, 1882; 3rd revenue officer, 1883; quarantine officer, 1886.

events.text_span: Admitted to the bar of Western Australia, 1865

events.position: master and registrar of the supreme court and keeper of records
events.text_span: master and registrar of the supreme court and keeper of records, 1870

events.position: acting government resident at King George's Sound
events.text_span: acting government resident at King George's Sound, 1879

raw_text: LOFTIE, ROWLEY C.—Admitted to the bar of Western Australia, 1865; master and registrar of the supreme court and keeper of records, 1870; and subsequently master in lunacy and registrar of the vice-admiralty court of the colony; acting commissioner of titles, 1875; police magistrate, Perth, 1878; acting government resident at King George's Sound, 1879; govt resident, southern districts, 1881.

events.text_span: called to the bar, N.S., Sept. 1875
sworn in member of executive, July, 1884

events.position: commissioner for revising and consolidating the law and framing Judicature Act

raw_text: LONGLEY, J WILBERFORCE.—Born 1849, B.A. Acadia College, N. Scotia, 1871, M.A., 1875; called to the bar, N.S., Sept. 1875; commissioner supreme court, 1876; elected to Provincial Parliament June, 1882; sworn in member of executive, July, 1884; attorney-general, May, 1886; commissioner for revising and consolidating the law and framing Judicature Act, Oct., 1882; editorial writer "Acadian Recorder" since 1873.

events.position: appointed a judge of superior court of Quebec
events.text_span: appointed a judge of superior court of Quebec, 1882.

raw_text: LORANGER, L. O., Q.C.—Attorney-general, province of Quebec, Canada; appointed a judge of superior court of Quebec, 1882.

LORD, EDWARD WILLIAM.—Entered police department, St. Lucia, 1872; acted keeper of the prison, Oct., 1876, to Mar., 1877; sub-inspector of revenue and police, Jan., 1880; chief revenue officer and landing waiter, Jan., 1882.

LORNE, MARQUIS OF, P.C., K.T., G.C.M.G., M.P., The RT. HON. JOHN DOUGLAS SUTHERLAND CAMPBELL.—Born 1845, married, 1871, H.R.H. Princess Louise Caroline Alberta, 4th daughter of Her Majesty the Queen; sat in the House of Commons for Argyllshire from Feb., 1868, to Sept., 1878; private secretary to his father, the Duke of Argyll, at the India Office, Dec., 1868; is author of poetical and other works, including "Trip to the Tropics;" governor-general of Canada, 1878 to 1883; M.P. for Manchester, 1895.

LOVELL, EDWD. A., M.A., Ph.D.—Asst. comptroller of customs, Gold Coast Colony, Feb., 1890; collr. of customs, Lagos, July, 1891; ag. treasurer and member ex. and legisl. councils, 1893-4-5-6; ag. col. sec., 1894-5.

events.position: chief medical officer and president general board of health
events.text_span: chief medical officer and president general board of health, 1879

events.position: surgeon general and medical officer of health, and member legislative council
events.text_span: surgeon general and medical officer of health, and member legislative council, Trinidad, Sept., 1893

raw_text: LOVELL, FRANCIS HENRY, C.M.G. (1893).—Colonial surgeon, Sierra Leone, Dec., 1873; medical officer, immigration department, Mauritius, 1878; chief medical officer and president general board of health, 1879; member council of government, 1885; surgeon general and medical officer of health, and member legislative council, Trinidad, Sept., 1893; also member exec. council, 1894.

LOVETT, W.—Junior clerk, P.O., Tasmania, Feb., 1841, served there till he reached the position of chief clerk; accountant to Treasury, Nov., 1860; ag. deputy auditor, April, 1865; assistant colonial treasurer, 1867; auditor-general, 1873.

LOW, SIR HUGH, G.C.M.G. (1889) K.C.M.G. (1883), C.M.G. (1879).—Secretary to government of Labuan, 1848; police magistrate, 1850; administered the government of Labuan five times between 1885 and 1876; British resident, Perak, 1877; negotiated treaty of Protection with Sultan of Brunei, 1888; retired 1889.

LOW, WILLIAM.—Acting private secretary to Lieut.-Gov. Longden, Dominica, June, 1866, to Mar., 1867; private secretary to Lieut.-Gov. Bulwer and clerk of the patents, April, 1867, to Jan., 1869; and to Lieut.-Gov. Freeling, Feb. to July, 1869, acted as colonial secretary of Dominica, 1868; police magistrate in St. Vincent, May, 1874; acting senior police magistrate, Bridgetown, Barbados, Oct., 1878; acting police magistrate, S. District, Grenada, May, 1883; ag. Treas., Ap. to Oct., 1884; stip. mag. 1st Dist. St. Lucia, Ap. 1887; represented Windward Islands at Quarantine, Confee. Oct., 1888; visiting justice of prisons, Grenada and St. Lucia, ag. treas., Grenada, Dec., 1890, to July, 1892; commr. of Tobago, 1892.

events.position: professor of agriculture and principal agri. coll.
events.text_span: professor of agriculture and principal agri. coll., S. Australia, 1887.

raw_text: LOWRIE, W.—M.A., B.Sc.; professor of agriculture and principal agri. coll., S. Australia, 1887.

LOWTHER, RT. HON. JAMES.—Born 1840. Ed. Westminster School, and Trin. Coll., Cam., B.A., 1862, and M.A., 1866; called to the bar at the Inner Temple, 1864; is a magistrate and deputy lieutenant for the North Riding of York; parliamentary secretary to the poor law board, Aug. to Dec., 1868; parliamentary under-secretary for the colonies, Feb., 1874, to Feb., 1878. First elected for the city of York, July, 1865; privy councillor and chief secretary for Ireland, 14th Feb., 1878; sat for the city of York to Mar., 1880; M.P., North Lincolnshire, Aug., 1881, to Dec., 1885; M.P., Thanet Divn. of Kent, 1888.

events.position: first class in final class schools
events.text_span: first class in final class schools, June, 1876

raw_text: LUCAS, CHARLES PRESTWOOD, B.A.—Educated at Winchester College, and Balliol College, Oxford; open exhibitioner; first class in moderntutions, 1873, and first class in final class schools, June, 1876; Chancellor's prize for Latin essay, 1877; called to the bar by the Society of Lincoln's Inn, Easter, 1885; appointed, after a competitive examination, to be a clerk in the office of the secretary of state for the colonies, April, 1877; private secretary to Sir R. Herbert, 1st Dec., 1881; Earl Granville, Feb., 1884, and again to Sir R. Herbert, Aug., 1886; also chairman of Committee of Emigrants Information till 31st Dec., 1896; first class clerk, 1st Feb., 1892; author of Historical Geography of the British Colonies; principal clerk, 1st Sept., 1896.

LUCIE-SMITH, A. VAN W.—Educated at Rugby; solicitor, British Guiana, 1877; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1881; acting solicitor-general, British Guiana, 1882, to 1887; president, district court, Famagusta, Cyprus, 1887; Limassol, 1889; acting Queen's advocate, 1893; resident, Kingston, Jamaica, Nov., 1896.

LUCKIE, D.M., F.S.S.—Trained in a lawyer's office in Montrose, Scotland; in 1862 emigrated to N. Zealand, as editor and part proprietor of "Nelson Colonist," which he largely popularized; in 1872 was elected member of parliament for Nelson City; in 1873 became editor of "Southern Cross," and when it was amalgamated with the "New Zealand Herald," the leading journal in the colony, became chief editor of the combined papers; Govt. insurance commr., 1894.

raw_text: LUDLOW, SIR HENRY, K.T. (1890).—Ed. Christ's Hospital and St. John's Coll., Camb., 1857, subsequently M.A. and fellow of St. John's College; obtained the studentship given by the Inns of Court, 1861; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Jan., 1862; attorney-general, Trinidad, 1873; chief justice, Leeward Islands, 1885; retired, 1890.
LUKIS, W. R. FELLOWES.—Dist. law agt., Ceylon govt. rys., Jan., 1890; ag. asst. public engr., May, 1893, to June, 1895; asst. law agt., Feb., 1896.

LUMB, CHARLES FREDERICK.—Educated at Liverpool Institute (Yates exhibitioner), Downing Coll., Camb. (scholar and present gold medallist in mathematics, natural science, and law); 3rd math. tripos, 1870; M.A., and L.L.M., 1877; called to the bar, 1892; barrister of Lincoln's Inn, 1874. Practised on Northern circuit; vice-president, Liverpool Institute, and member of Liverpool school board; prosecutor for the mint, 1886; second puisne judge, Trinidad, 1887; member of educ. commsn., 1887; of bd. of educ., and of royal college council, 1888; of library committee, 1889; puisne judge, Jamaica, 1892.

raw_text: ACBRIDGE, ROBERT KNOX, C.M.G. (1890).—Superintending officer, public works department, en. 1866; irrigation assistant, 1872; provincial assistant, north central province, 1874; Uva, 1876; southern province, 1878; and central province, 1882; director of public works, 1885.

CALLUM, MAJOR HENRY EDWARD, R.E., J.P. (1887)—Entered the Royal Military Academy, Woolwich (July, 1869), passed out first twenty-two cadets (1871); after two years practical training at Chatham, proceeded to Portsmouth, and appointed superintendent of telegraphy, Portsmouth District. Transferred to the office of the inspector general of fortifications. Private secretary to Governor Sir William Jervois, S.S. and aided him on various missions to the natives of the Malay Peninsula; mentioned in despatches for the assistance given in preparing a report for the defence of Singapore, and again in (1876) for services in connection with a commission inquiry; superintending engineer military works, Hong Kong, April, 1877; April (1878) selected to proceed to Singapore to take measures for the defence of that port. Attached to the office of the inspector of works, royal naval, July, 1879, to July, 1880, when appointed military colonial engineer, Straits Settlements. Is a gold medallist of 1871; the annual Fowke medallist for 1874; is an associate of institution of engineers, etc.; colonial engineer and surveyor-general, Straits Settlements, 1884; member of executive and legislative councils. President of Singapore municipality, Feb., 1881 to July, 1886. Directed to construct new fortifications of Singapore, March, 1885; commuted Singapore vol. art., 1888. Special commr., in Pahang during disturbances, 1891; received thanks of S. of S.

Gen. since 1889; ag. ch. justice, May to Sept., 1893; escheator, 1893.

raw_text: McCARTHY, J. DESMOND, C.M.G. (1891), M.D., M.Ch.—Surgeon, royal navy, 1871; the Ashantee expedition, had charge of hospital Prahsu (medal), specially mentioned in despatches by Commodore Sir W. N. W. Hewitt, V.C., K.C.B.; retired 1874; senior resident assistant physician, royal asylum, Cheadle, 1875–7; assistant colonial surgeon, Lagos, July, 1877; colonial surgeon, Lagos, 7th January, 1889; chief medical officer, Gold Coast colony, 1st Jan. 1885; also dist. commr. and coroner for the colony, June, 1892; retired 1893.

HENRY.—2nd class clk., Imperial Customs, Belfast, Feb., 1875; served at Cork and Plymouth; selected for special service with surveyors-general, and in connection with reorganization of customs statistical dept., 1893; 2nd offr., Felkstone, 1894; collr. of customs, Trinidad, Dec., 1895.
McCORD, T.—Law clerk, Quebec assembly, 1867, puisne judge superior court, 1872; deputy judge vice-admiralty court, 1884.

McCORMACK, M. P. CORMAC.—Govt. med. offr., Jamaica, 1895; in charge of govt. reformatory, 1875; transferred to St. Ann. 1876; to Mid Clarendon, 1877; asst. med. offr., lunatic asylum, Mar., 1880. Has acted as med. supt. in 1884, 1886, and 1895.

raw_text: McCOY, SIR FREDERICK, K.C.M.G. (1891), C.M.G. (1887), D.Sc. (Cantab.), F.R.S.—Professor of natural science, Melbourne University, 1854; was chairman of goldfields commission, 1855; has been a royal commissioner for various exhibitions, and on education.

McCULLOCH, THE HON. SIR JAMES, KNT. BACH. (1870), K.C.M.G. (1874)—Was twenty years a member of the legislature of Victoria; during that time was a member of the Govt. eleven years, and premier nine years; resigned in 1877; has been a trustee of the public library and national gallery, Melbourne, for many years, and was twice presdt. of the Melbourne chamber of commerce.

MACDONALD, THE HON. ANDREW ARCHIBALD—Born at Three Rivers, Prince Edward's Island, 1829; a member of Prov. Assembly, 1854 to 1860, and of the leg. coun. from 1863, when it became elective, until July, 1873, when he resigned seat in the legislative and executive councils, and became provincial postmaster-general, until 1844, when appointed lieutenant-governor of the province, which position he occupied until 4th September, 1889; was a delegate to the Charlottetown and Quebec conference in 1864, which settled the terms of union for the Dominion of Canada; called to the senate of the Dominion 11th May, 1891.

McDONALD, DARENT H.—Supernumerary col. secy's. office, Br. Honduras, Feb., 1885; clerk to col. engineer, April, 1885; chief clerk commissariat and transport staff, June, 1885; third clerk, treasury, Nov., 1887; second ditto, Jan., 1888; chief clk., Aug., 1888; actg. clk. of councils, Oct. to Dec., 1890; priv. sec. to Admr. Melville, Oct., 1890, to Aug., 1891; auditor and registrar of births, deaths, and marriages, Feb., 1891; asst. treas., G. Coast, July, 1893; dist. commr., Feb., 1894; chief asst. treas., Apl., 1895; ag. treas. and member ex. and leg. councils, also director pub. offrs. guarantee fund, and member bd. of edu., April, 1895 to March, 1896.

events.position: snper. clk., col. sec.'s office. Brit. Honduras
events.text_span: snper. clk., col. sec.'s office. Brit. Honduras, 1887

raw_text: MCDONALD, ERNEST E.—Ed. Sydenham coll., Kent; snper. clk., col. sec.'s office. Brit. Honduras, 1887; clk. to atty.-gen., Jan., 1888; clk. col. engineer's dept., Sept., 1888; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, June, 1892.

MACDONALD, GEORGE.—1st class, Queen's scholar, 1876; snr. master, academy, Banbury, 1877; snr. mr., British schl., London, N.W., 1878; ditto, Eastbourne Schls, 1881; snr. asst. to H.M.S. inspr. of schls., Marylebone, 1883; director of edu., G. Coast, June, 1893. Is author of several schoolbooks and manuals.

MCDONALD, HON. JAMES, Q.C.—Born 1828; called to the bar of Nova Scotia, 1851; created a Q.C. 1867; was chief railway commissioner of Nova Scotia from June, 1863, to Dec., 1864, when appointed financial secretary, which office he held until the union of 1867; one of the commissioners (representing Nova Scotia) to open trade relations between the West Indies, Mexico, and Brazil, and the B.N.A. provinces, 1865-66; sworn of the privy council of Canada, 17th Oct., 1878, and appointed minister of justice of Canada; sat in Nova Scotia assembly from 1859 until 1867, and from 1871 until July, 1872, when returned to the House of Commons; now chief justice of Nova Scotia; joint commissioner to inquire into charges agst. atty.-gen. of Jamaica, Dec., 1893.

McDONNELLS, JOHN.—Clerk in the immigration office of New South Wales in 1854; promoted in 1856 senior clerk to the registrar-general; placed in charge of the compilation of the general statistics of the colony in 1858; secretary of the police department of Queensland on its separation from N.S.W., Feb., 1860; immigration agent Jan., 1866, inspector of benevolent asylum and orphanages, also visiting justice to H.M. gaol and lunatic asylum; as well as chief inspector of distilleries; under secretary and permanent head of the post and telegraph department in 1870, which office he now holds; in addition he was appointed major-commandant of the Queensland volunteer forces in 1877; lieut.-col. commandant in 1878; retired in Nov., 1879, and was placed on the staff with his rank as lieut.-col.; acting commandant of the defence forces in Feb., 1883, to 1884; member of immigration board since 1865.
MACDOUGALL, JOHN LORNE.—Auditor-general of Canada, 1st Aug., 1878.

events.text_span: lecturer on criminal law and torts to law society of Ontario, 1879-83
junior judge cty. ct., York, Mar., 1883

raw_text: MACDOUGALL, JOSEPH EASTON. — Son of Wm. McDougall, C.B.; born 1846; educated U.C. College; called to bar, Feb., 1870; lecturer on criminal law and torts to law society of Ontario, 1879-83; Q.C. 1883; junior judge cty. ct., York, Mar., 1883; senior judge, Sept., 1885; judge maritime ct., Ontario, Sept., 1885; chairman of police board for Toronto, 1886.

MACDOUGALL, THE HON. WILLIAM, C.B. (1867).—Born 1822, educated at Victoria University, Cobourg; admitted an attorney, Upper Canada, 1847; called to the bar, Hilary, 1862; founded (1848) the "Canada Farmer," subsequently merged in the "Canadian Agriculturist," which he published and edited until 1858; founded (1850) the "North American," a Reform newspaper, of which he was chief editor, until merged in the Toronto "Daily Globe," in 1857; was the leading political writer on the "Daily Globe," from 1857 to 1860; represented Canada at the New York Exhibition, 1853; was secretary of the Constitutional Reform Association of Upper Canada, 1859; a member of the executive council and commissioner of crown lands, Canada, May, 1862, to March, 1864, provincial secretary from June, 1864, and acting minister of marine from July, 1866, until the union of the provinces, 1867; sworn of the queen's privy council in Canada, and minister of public works for the dominion, from 1st July, 1867, lieut.-governor of Rupert's Land and the north-west territories, Oct., 1869; chairman of the commission appointed to open trade relations with the West Indies, Mexico, and Brazil, 1865-6; delegate to the Charlottetown Union Conference, 1864; to that at Quebec, in the same year; and to the Colonial Conference in London to complete terms of Union of B.N.A. colonies, 1865; delegate to England, with late Sir G. E. Cartier, to confer with imperial authorities on the defence of the dominion, and for the acquisition of the north-west territory, 1868; commissioner for Canada for the settlement of the north-western boundary of the province, 1871; sent to England by Canadian government, 1873, as special commissioner to confer with imperial government on subject of fisheries; returned to Canadian legislature in 1878, where he sat until the union of 1867. He returned to the house of commons; elected to Ontario assembly in May, 1875; resigned Sept., 1878, and again returned to house of commons; retired from public life, 1882, and resumed practice of his profession in Ottawa. Is a Q.C.
McEWEN, E. JAMES.—Clerk to provost-marshal, Grenada, January, 1868; colonial revenue officer, June, 1871; chief clerk, Treasury, 1880.

raw_text: McGEE, JOHN J.—Educated at St. Patrick's College, Wexford, affiliated with the Catholic University, Dublin, where he was medallist in physical science; employed by the government of Canada on special service in district of Algoma and on various surveys; subsequently professionally engaged under the Dominion government in the construction of the Intercolonial Railway, and in connection with the railway commission of the House of Commons; first-class clerk in charge of surveys branch of Dominion lands, Department of the Interior, 1879; assistant clerk of the Queen's Privy Council for Canada, January, 1880; clerk of the Privy Council, 20th May, 1882.

McGOWAN, JAMES—B.A., Lond., and a fellow of the institute of actuaries, Lond.; has acted as examiner to that institute, and was engaged for many years in life assurance business; general actuary, Cape, 1890.

raw_text: MACGREGOR, MALCOLM.—Pioneer officer (provisional), Ceylon, 14th April, 1870; superintending officer, 1st June, 1875; superintending er (fixed), 1st Jan., 1878; acting provincial stant, North-Central Province, 21st Sept., 9; superintending officer, Dikoya, Feb., 1882; 22 second financial and office assistant, Aug., 8; resumed duties as superintending officer, Dec., 1883; acting second financial and office tant, 1st Feb., 1884; appointment confirmed, il, 1885; acting financial and office assistant, Sept., 1887; resumed duties as second financial office assistant, 16th Oct., 1887; acting rial and office assistant, Feb., 1888; district eer, Gampola; passed examination, junior ion, Oct., 1874; and senior division, Oct. 1877.

MACGREGOR, SIR WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1889), G. (1881).—Educated at Aberdeen, Glasgow, in and Paris; M.B., Abdn., 1872; M.D., 1874; sea gold medallist, 1872; formerly resident em and resident physician, Glasgow Royal Mary; resident physician, Royal Lunatic Asylum, Aberdeen; assistant government medical r. Seychelles, 1873; superintendent, lunatic m. and resident surgeon, Civil Hospital, Port s. Mauritius, 1874; chief medical officer, Fiji, 1875; also receiver-general, and member of Executive and legislative councils, Jan., 1877; acted as registrar-general, agent-general of emigration, and commissioner of lands; engaged, in the suppression of the disturbances in the islands of Viti Levu, for which he was voted a city of 200l.; joint commissioner, 1877, for settlement of debts due from natives and teans, and for the settlement of all pecuniary is against the late government of Fiji; member of the Native Regulation Board, 1877; proceeded to Tonga, 1879, to report on the financial position of that country; acting colonial secretary, Nov., 1883, to June, 1884, and Oct., 1874, m., 1875; administrator of the government, acting high commissioner and consul-general for the Western Pacific, Jan. to Aug., 1880; representative of Fiji at the first session of Federal Council of Australasia, at Hobart, 1885; Albert medal of the 2nd class (1884). the Clarke gold medal of the Royal Society of Australasia in 1885, for saving wreck; dpty. admr. of Fiji, Sept., 1885, and 1886; admr., British New Guinea, 1888; Nov., 1895.

MUIRE, WM.—Appointed, after open competition, a clerk in the lower division of the civil service, and assigned to the audit office, 10th Nov., 1887; transferred to valuation office, Ireland, Nov., 1890, and to colonial office, 1st June,

MILWRAITH, THE HON. SIR THOMAS, M.G. (1882), LL.D., M.L.A.—Born at Ayr, Scotland, 1835, and educated there and at Glasgow University. He arrived in Melbourne in 1854, and was engineer to the Government railways; in 1861 engaged in squatting pursuits in Queensland, and died there in 1870; first entered Parliament in 1872 and represented the Warrego and Maranoa many years; in 1873 he joined the Macalister Ministry as minister for works, but resigned not long afterwards; in 1878 he was elected member for Mulgrave, and, on the defeat of the Douglas ministry in 1879, he formed a new Government, in which he took the position of premier; in consequence of two adverse votes, resigned, Nov., 1883. Among his most prominent acts was the annexation of New Guinea, a step that did not receive the sanction of the Imperial Government; in 1883 the freedom of his native place was conferred on him; he was made an Associate of the Institute of Civil Engineers in 1881, and had the dignity of LL.D. conferred upon him by his Alma Mater. Sir Thomas retired from public life in June, 1886, but re-entered in 1888, when he was elected member for North Brisbane. The result of the general election in 1888 was the defeat of the Griffith ministry; Sir Thomas was then called on to form a government, which he succeeded in doing, taking the position of premier, treasurer, and chief secretary, which he resigned on account of ill health in 1889; col. treasurer in Griffith ministry, 1890; premier, chief sec. and minister of rlys., 1892; resigned, 1893.

McINNIS, LIEUT.-COL. EDWARD BOWATER, late 9th Lancers, C.M.G. (1890).—Entered the army, Mar., 1865; was adjt. 9th Lancers for several years, including campaigns in Afghanistan, 1878-9-80; present at the operations near Kabul, and on Sir Frederick Roberts' march from Kabul to Kandahar, and battle of Mazra (Kandahar), 1st Sept., 1880; bronze star for Kabul, Kandahar march; medal and clasps for Kabul and Kandahar; inspr.-gen., G. Coast constab., Mar., 1887, commandant of local forces, Trinidad, 1890; inspector-general, Br. Guiana, 1891.

(1891).—Cornet, Inniskilling Dragoons, Oct., 1871; A.D.C., Bengal, 1880; served in Transvaal war, 1881; staff officer, Zululand war (Eshowe column), 1883; D.A.A. and Q.M.G., South Africa, 1884; actg. D.A.A.G., Natal, 1886; surveyor to Zululand and New Republic Boundary Com., 1886; assist. commr., Zululand, 1887; commr. of Basuto levies in Zulu disturbances, 1888; was honourably mentioned and made brevet lieut.-col. for services; asst. mil. sec. to gov., Malta, 1889.

MCKINNEY, W. J., C.M.G. (1890).—Postmaster, British Honduras, 1859; served as ensign, Belize volunteers, in 1866-67, against the hostile Indians of Yucatan; mentioned in despatches, and received the thanks of the legislative assembly for his services; acting police magistrate, 1875; treasurer, Sept., 1880.
MACKINNON, LOUIS FULLERTON.—Accountant, savings bank, Jamaica, 1870; manager of government railway 1879.
MCLELLAN, DAVID.—Member of House of Assembly and exec. coun., New Brunswick, provincial sec. and receiver gen.; president of exec. council, 1890.

AUGUSTUS.—Entered Cape civil service after examination, 1857; inaugurated a system of deeds registry for Griqualand East, 1872; chief clerk, office of regtr. of deeds, 1879; assistant regtr. of deeds, Nov., 1891; Lieut. and Capt. in D.E.O.V.R., 1882-5; is a J.P. for Cape Town and District.
MACLEOD, LT.-COL. JAMES FARQUHARSON. C.M.G. (1870).—Served in Red River Expedition, 1870; judge of Albert District, N.W. territories, Canada.

raw_text: MCLEOD, NEIL.—Called to the bar, Prince Edward Island, 1872; member of house of assembly since 1879; provincial secretary and treasurer, 1879 to 1882; atty.-gen. and premier, 1889.

raw_text: MACLEOD, P. F., M.D.—Medical officer, parish of St. George, Grenada, 21 July, 1881.

MCLEOD, WILLIAM.—Sub-inspector, Jamaica constabulary, Dec., 1866; 3rd class inspector, Jan., 1869; 2nd class, Oct., 1871; 1st class, Oct., 1873; acting inspector-general, Sept. to Dec., 1883, May to Oct., 1885, and April to Sept., 1886; June to Sept., 1888; May to Oct., 1890; June to Dec., 1891; and May to Nov., 1894.
MACLURE, JOHN STEVENSON GRANT.—Clk. Leg. Coun., Bahamas, Mar., 1888; acted for a time as clk in col. sec. off. and regtr. of deeds off.; clk. police office, Aug., 1890.

MACLURE, WILLIAM MALCOM GREY, M.D., M.R.C.S., England.—Coroner, Nassau, N.P., Bahamas, 1865; physician to prison and police force, and health officer, 1873; medical inspector and chairman board of health, 1880; member of house of assembly from 1866 to 1879, when appointed to Legislative council.

MCNAIR, J. F. A., MAJOR, Royal (late Madras) Artillery (retired), C.M.G. (1879).—Educated at King's College, London, and student at the School of Mines; entered the Madras artillery in 1845; served in India, the Straits Settlements, and Labuan; private secretary to the governor, Straits Settlements, 1857, and subsequently executive engineer and superintendent of convicts, Singapore; chief engineer, Straits Settlements, on their transfer to the crown in 1867; accompanied the governor to Siam, Aug., 1867; surveyor-general, Straits Settlements, 1872; chief commissioner of Perak, throughout the disturbances in that country in 1875-6; and engaged in the affair at Kotah Lamah, on the Perak river; special commissioner to Selangore, to inquire into piracy, and to Perak in connection with the Pangkor treaty, 1874; again to Siam, in Aug. of that year, on special mission; and again, early in 1875, with Sir A. Clarke, in connection with unsettled state of affairs there; special mission to the State of Sungei Ujong, 1878; secretary to Sir W. Robinson's mission to Siam in Nov., 1878, to present the G.C.M.G. to the king; author of "Perak and the Malays," &c.; act. lieut.-governor and resident councillor of Penang from Feb., 1881, to Aug., 1884, when he retired.

MCNEIL, MAJOR-GENERAL SIR JOHN CAE-STAIRS, K.C.M.G. (1880), (K.C.B., 1892), (C.M.G., 1870), V.C.—Served during the campaign of 1857-58 in India as A.D.C. to Sir F. Lugard during the siege and capture of Lucknow; in 1861-5 A.D.C. to Sir D. Cameron in New Zealand; commanded the Tipperary flying column during the Fenian disturbances, 1866-67; served on the staff of the Red River expedition under Sir G. Wolseley in 1870, and in the Ashanti war, 1873-74; A.D.C. to H.R.H. the Duke of Cambridge, Jan., 1874, and an equerry to Her Majesty in Aug. following; served in the Egyptian expedition, 1882, personally attached to the Duke of Cambridge; served in the Soudan Expedition, 1885; commanded troops at the action of Tobrek, in which clasp was granted.

raw_text: MCTURK, MICHAEL.—Revenue officer and surveyor, Essequibo, 1872; commissioner of auction, Essequibo district, 1873; special mag. of suptdt. cr. lds. and forests, Essequibo, 1880; govt. gold mining officer, Br. Guiana, 1887.
MADDEN, SIR JOHN, Q.C., KT. BACH. (1893).—Chief justice of Victoria, 1893; administered the govt., 1893 and 1895.
MADDEN, W.—President of the Board of Land and Works, and commissioner, crown lands and survey, Victoria; 19th Aug., 1881, to 8th Mar., 1893.
MAGRATH, THOS. HENRY.—Junior clk. P.O., Tasmania, 1853; chief clerk, 1869; comptroller of money orders, 1879, and of savings bank, 1882; sec. to P.O. 1888.
MAHONY, LIEUT.-COL. JOHN, C.M.G. (1895).—Paymaster, army pay department, in the Zulu war; retired with the honorary rank of Lieut.-Col. Mar., 1884.

MAINWARING, CAPTAIN K. H. A., R.N.—Entered royal navy Sept., 1850; lieut. May, 1856; served in Mediterranean as flag lieut. to Sir Arthur Fanshawe and Sir Henry Codrington; served in China 1863 till 1868; commander, Dec., 1867, on board H.M.S. "Aboukir," guard-ship at Jamaica, Oct., 1868, till July, 1871, when he was appointed to the "Chameleon," in the Pacific; retired as captain in Oct., 1873; stipendiary magistrate, Jamaica, April, 1874; acting inspector general of police, 1875 to 1876; harbor master, Kingston, 1876; and asst. pol. mag., 1876-8; pol. mag., 1888-91, when that office was abolished.

events.text_span: 2nd clerk to suptdt. of prisons, Dec., 1886

events.text_span: priv. sec. to Sir W. Robinson, 1888

raw_text: MAJOR, F. W.—5th clerk, Treas., Trinidad, Oct., 1885; 2nd clerk to suptdt. of prisons, Dec., 1886; 9th clerk, audit office, Aug., 1887; ditto, 1888; priv. sec. to Sir W. Robinson, 1888; asst. controller of customs, 1891.

MALCOLM, ORMOND DRIMMIE.—Called to the bar of the Bahamas, July, 1861; acting pm magistrate in 1864 and 1866; elected member of the house of assembly in 1865, speaker, 1868; acting judge, court of common pleas, 1875; Q.C., counsel in 1876; acting attorney-general, 1880, and again in 1881; confirmed, Mar., 1882; ag. chief justice, July, 1893, to Feb., 1894; ag. adv. June, 1895.

MALLING, CAPTAIN IRWIN CHARLES, C.M.G. (1892).—Educated at Wimbledon School and Royal Military Academy; late 23rd Royal Welsh Fusiliers; deputy magistrate and collector, Nuddea district, Bengal, 1857; served throughout the Indian mutiny, 1857-59; present at many engagements (medal); ensign, 1861, lieut. Princess Victoria's Regiment; private secretary to A.D.C. to the governor, West Coast of Africa, 1868; lieut. 35th Royal Sussex Regt., 1870; private secretary to the Marquis of Normanby, governor of Queensland, 1871, and New Zealand, 1874; colonial secretary and registrar-general of Canada, 1879; administered the government in 1882, 1884, 1885, and 1886-7; actg. col. sec.,eward Islands, 1888-9; and representative at Quarantine conference at Demerara, 1888; actg. min., 1889; admin. of St. Vincent, 1889; retired 1903.

raw_text: MALSCH, CHARLES CHRISTIAN, A.M.I.C.E.—Sightsman, works department, admiralty, 1866; dist. engineer, German admiralty works, Kiel, 1873; surveyor state engrs. dept., California; surveying Sacramento and Fraser Rivers, &c., California and British Columbia, 1875; exploring mosa for Chinese govt., 1881 to 1883; assist. engineer, P.W. Dept., Hong Kong, 1884; suptdt. r. lds., 1892; ag. assessor of rates, 1894; J.P., 1904.

events.text_span: Clerk in chief secretary's office, Cyprus, 1880

raw_text: LAMO, LOUIS ARNETT.—Clerk in chief secretary's office, Cyprus, 1880; higher division clerk, 1882; senior clerk, receiver-general's department, 1885; acted as treasurer, 1888-89; clk. chief sec. 1894.

LAN, COLONEL ALEXANDER.—Served with field force under the late Brigadier Murray, 1860; employed on the 30-mile radius round Shanghai during the summer of 1863 (medal); with the Anglo-Chinese contingent in Colonel Gordon's Taiping campaigns of 1860-64, being present during the operations before Soochow, and siege and storming of Chang-chow (Chinese medal and Precious Star 2nd class); in 1868-69 stationed in Formosa, and assisted the U.S. consul and Mr. W. A. Pickering, C.M.G., in coming a friendly settlement with the savage tribes; took part in the negotiations at Fort Zelandia and withdrawal of British force (thanked in despatches); selected in 1873 to raise a corps of military police for the Treaty district of Newchwang, northern Manchuria, at that time infested by armed banditti, and subsequently commanded force embodied (thanks of the British and French ministers, and of the Imperial High Commissioner, with the brevet of colonel in the Chinese army, and the cross of the Italian Crown, 4th class); served with the Nile expedition of 1884-45, as staff officer and staff officer, and afterwards as commandant at Dal (medal with clasp and Khedive's star); A.D.C. to General Valentine Baker, B.A. in 1885, and actg. dep. inspr.-general of gendarmerie in 1886-7-8 (thanks of Egyptian Government, and Order of the Osmanieh, 4th class); lt. of local forces, Trinidad and Tobago,
ANISTY, J. F.—Traffic superintendent, Natal government railways, 1878; dep. prot. of Indian immigrants, 1892.

events.text_span: Clerk in insolvency office, South Australia, 1862

raw_text: ANN, JOHN.—Clerk in insolvency office, South Australia, 1862; clerk of local court, 1868; secretary of land titles office, 1870; to commissioners of public works, 1875.

raw_text: ANN, MAJOR-GENERAL J. R., R.E., C.M.G.—Entered R.E., 1840; surveyor-general of Mauritius, 1856 to 1861; director of roads surveyor-general of Jamaica, 1867; resigned, and re-appointed in Feb., 1874; official C.C., 1874; retired, 1886.

raw_text: MANNING, F. N., M.D.—Med. suptdt. hospital for insane, Gladesville, N.S. Wales, Nov., 1867; inspector-general of the insane, N.S. Wales, 1879.

MANSEL, GEORGE, C.M.G. (1891).—Served in the Weenen Yeomanry through the Langalibalele rebellion in 1873; served as sub-inspector and inspector in the Natal Mounted Police, through the Zulu war, 1879 (medal with clasp), and Boer war, 1880 and 1881; raised and commanded the Reserve Territory Carbineers from 1883 to 1887, when the force was changed into the Zululand police; served in this force through all disturbances in Zululand from 1883 to 1888; twice mentioned in despatches as having shown conspicuous coolness and gallantry in action.

raw_text: MANSERGH, CORNEWALL LEWIS WARWICKSHIRE.—Clerk, crown lands office, Cape Town, May, 1880; 2nd class clk. and private secretary to commissioner of crown lands and public works, May, 1884; first class clerk, April, 1892; chief clerk to the assistant commissioner of railways and public works, October, 1892.

MARCHAND, FELIX GABRIEL.—Member of the legislative assembly, Quebec, for the county of St. John's in 1867, re-elected in 1871, 1875, 1878, 1881, and in 1886; entered the cabinet as provincial secretary in 1878, and minister of crown lands in 1879; chosen speaker of the assembly in 1887; in Jan., 1862, organized the 21st bat. of infantry, and was aptd. lieut.-col., Canadian militia, 1866; during the Fenian invasions of 1866 and 1870, took active service with his batn., and commanded a brigade on the frontier; is an author of works in prose and verse; has received the decoration of officer of public instruction of France, and is F.R.S. of Canada, and of many other literary institutions.

events.text_span: 1st class with distinction classical tripos, Part II, and B.A., 1895

raw_text: MARSH, EDWARD HOWARD, B.A.—Ed. at Westminster and Cambridge; Scholar, Trin. Coll., 1891; 1st class classical tripos, 1893; senior chancellor's medal for classics, 1895; 1st class with distinction classical tripos, Part II, and B.A., 1895; aptd. after competitive examn. 2nd class clk., Colonial Office, Sept. 29, 1896.

raw_text: MARSH, SIR WILLIAM HENRY, K.C.M.G. (1887), C.M.G. (1881).—Clerk to procureur-general, Mauritius, Feb., 1848; crown solicitor, April, 1854; chief clerk, colonial secretary's office, Oct. 1859; special immigration commissioner to India, May, 1865; junior assistant colonial secretary, April, 1866; senior assistant colonial secretary, 1867; sole assistant, 1870; acting colonial secretary, 1875-6; auditor-general, April, 1876; colonial secretary and auditor-general, Hong Kong, 1879; acting auditor and accountant-general in Cyprus, 1881; administered government Hong Kong on six occasions; retired 1887.
MARSHAL, FRED. WM.—Supervisor of customs, G. Coast, May, 1892; principal clk. col. sec. office, Sept., 1892; priv. sec. to ag. gov., Dec., 1893, to March 1894; local auditor, Lagos, June, 1894.

clerical assistant to colonial secretary, Tobago, 1879

raw_text: MARSHALL, ARTHUR LACY.—Clerical assistant, immigration department, British Guiana, Dec., 1875; supernumerary clerk, 1876; clerical assistant to colonial secretary, Tobago, 1879; acting 2nd revenue officer, 1881; confirmed, May, 1882; provisional 1st revenue officer, Nov., 1884; con. April, 1885; chief clerk, treas., July, 1885.

events.position: For services rendered in the African war

raw_text: MARSHALL, LIBUT.-GENERAL FREDERICK, C.M.G. (1879).—For services rendered in the African war, 1879.
MARTIN, JOSEPH.—Member of leg. assembly, and exec. council, Manitoba; attorney-general and commr. of railways and lands.

raw_text: MARTIN, LOUIS. — Volunteer in surveyor-general's department at Mauritius. Dec. 1859; clerk in the audit office, Jan. 1860; junior examiner, Mar., 1862; senior examiner, Sept., 1869; chief clerk, immigration department, Mar., 1880; colonial postmaster and supt. of telegraphs, Sept., 1892.

MARTIN, COL. SIR RICHARD EDWARD ROWLEY, K.C.M.G. (1895), C.M.G. (1888), Inniskilling Dragoons. — Commanded mounted infantry at Cetywayo's installation, Zululand, 1883; commandant of general depot, Bechuanaland field force, 1884; major Bechuanaland border police, 1885 to 1887; was Br. commissr. presdt. Portuguese-Swazi Boundary Commission, and Br. commissr. on special mission to Tongaland, 1888; proceeded on visit of inquiry to Swaziland, 1889; secretary to British commission to Swaziland, 1889; British member of temporary joint commission of admin., 1889-95, commandant of police and dep. comsr., Rhodesia, Mar., 1896.

events.text_span: 6th class offr., customs, G. Coast, Sept., 1890

raw_text: MARTIN, S. B. — Ed. Wesleyan Mission Sch., Cape Coast; 6th class offr., customs, G. Coast, Sept., 1890; clk. to director of telegraphs and storekeeper, Aug., 1891; clk. and acctnt., July, 1893.
MARTINS, FRED. G. — Ed. C.M.S. coll. inst., Lagos; junior bailiff, supreme court, June, 1884; clerk to queen's advocate, Aug., 1886; storekeeper, P.W. dept., Jan., 1891; audit clk., Jan., 1896.

MARTINS, G. N. — Ed. C.M.S. Gram. Schl., Lagos; messenger, audit, and P.O., 1889; customs, outdoor officer, 1890; ag. clk., P.O., 1893; 4th clk. of customs, 1893; 3d. clk., and statistician, Mar., 1894.

MARTIN, PEDRO JOSIAH. — Supernumerary clerk, treas., Accra, Oct., 1883; junior clerk, governor's office, 1884; storekeeper, P.W. dept., Lagos, 1887; audit clerk, Jan., 1889; in charge of audit office, Apr. to Nov., 1889, Dec., 1889 to Aug., 1890; Aug., 1891, to Mar., 1892, and again Aug., 1893.
MASKEW, W. W. — Resident magistrate, Middleburg, Cape Colony, 1875; civil commissioner, Clan William, Aug. 22, 1884.
MASON, ARTHUR WIER, B.A. — Puisne judge sup. ct., Natal, 1896.

MASON, CLAYTON T., M.I.C.E., J.P. — Educated King Edward's School, Birmingham; from 1874 to 1877 was engaged in the railway branch of the department of public works, New South Wales; resident engineer of the Geraldton and Northampton railway, Western Australia, 1877; superintended the construction of Point Moore Lighthouse and other public works in the Victoria district; afterwards resident engineer for four years of the Eastern Railway in the same colony; acting commissioner of railways on two occasions during 1882 and 1883; was provisionally appointed in 1884 director of public works and commissioner of railways; and member of the executive and legislative councils, general manager and maintenance engineer, June, 1885; commissioner of railways and member of exec. conn., Jan., 1890; after the introduction of responsible government, was appointed collector of customs, June, 1891.

MASON, J. D. — Educated at "The College," Llandovery, and the London University; a writer in the Ceylon civil service, 1872; acting police magistrate, Panadura, Sep. 1872; acting assistant government agent, Galle, Nov. 1872; police magistrate, Balapitymodara, June, 1873, to continue to act at Galle; acting police magistrate, Galle, 1876; landing surveyor, Galle, 1876; acting district judge, Matara, 1876; acting police magistrate, 1877; assistant collector of customs, Jaffna, 1877; police magistrate, Colombo, 1878; commissioner of requests and police magistrate, Galle, 1879; police magistrate, Colombo, 1888; dep. collr. of customs, Colombo, Dec., 1891; pol. mag., Colombo, May, 1894.
MASON, L. H. — Protector of Indian immigrants, Natal, 1883.

MASSIAH, J. P. — Police magistrate of Bridgetown and St. Michael, Barbados, Jan., 1872; parochial treasurer of St. John's, 1872 to 1873; acting coroner of St. John's and St. Philip's several occasions; acting police magistrate St. John's and St. Philip's, 1880. to Feb., 1881; senior police magistrate, Bridgetown, 1881 to 1885.

MASSON, THE HON. LOUIS FRANCOIS BOUTIQUE. — Born 1833; called to the bar of Lower Canada, 1859; was brigade-major, 8th militia district of Lower Canada, from 1863 to 1867; holds the rank of lieut.-colonel in the militia; entered Canadian parliament, 1867; sworn of privy council of Canada and appointed minister of militia and defence, 19th Oct., 1878; retired; called to the senate, 1882, and to the legislative council of the province of 1890. Quebec, 1890; governor of Quebec, 1884-7; senator, 1891.

B. A. Taylor, from 22nd September to 16th December, 1879; acting second clerk and book-keeper, Mar., acting collector customs, 1883; 2nd clerk and bookkeeper, 1891.
MATHIEU, M. — Puisne judge, superior court Quebec, 1881.

raw_text: MATTHEWS, MAYNARD REGINALD NESS. — Jun. clk., telegraph office, Durban, 1878; postmaster, Bothas Hill, Verulam, and Stanger, 1879; senior clk., R. M.'s off., Durban, 1880; field officer, Durban, and sec. wreck commsr's. ct., 1886; senior cl. clk., 1886; ag. clk., mag. ct. and regnr. ct., 1887; ct., Durban and Umlazi, 1886-7; clk., R. M.'s regnr. circuit ct., subdistrib. of stps., and dep. justice of peace, Klip River, Dec., 1887; J. P., Klip River divn., 1894; has acted frequently as R. M. S. River; ag. R. M., Inanda divn., Feb., 1895.

MAUNSELL, HORATIO EDMUND. — B.A., 1865; and M.Ch., Trinity College, Dublin, 1867; resident medical officer, public hospital, Kingston, Jan. to Nov., 1876; served on army medical staff in 1868, to Oct., 1872, during three years of which period served in Jamaica; appointed to government medical service, Jamaica, Sept., 1876; appointed St. Ann's Bay, Feb., 1881.

MAURICE, MORGAN SYDNEY. — Passed his service examination, 2nd grade, in Mauritius, 1874; confidential clerk to the royal commissioner to enquire into the treatment of Indian immigrants in Mauritius, in 1875; acted as senior assistant district clerk in the district and stipendiary magistrate of Black River in 1874; clerk and interpreter to the British consul, Island of Reunion, resigned; a sworn translator of Tamil in supreme court, Mauritius, 1878; employed a short time in the colonial engineer's department, Natal, in 1879; and subsequently served as confidential clerk on the staff of Major-General Hon. Sir H. H. Clifford; audit clerk in the office of the general manager of telegraphs, Cape, Oct., 1880, and shortly thereafter transferred as confidential clerk to Sir George Strahan; 3rd class clerk in establishment, July, 1881; second-class clerk Oct., 1882; was also employed in the classification of papers and appendices in connection with the report of the royal commissioners appointed for the settlement of Transvaal affairs.

events.position: mission chaplain to Bishop of London
events.text_span: mission chaplain to Bishop of London, 1886

raw_text: MAURITIUS, 5th Bishop of, WM. WALSH, M.A., D.D.—Of St. Alban's Hall and Merton Coll., Oxford; asst. secy., C.M.S., 1865-70; chaplain in one, 1878-9; vicar of St. Mary, Newington, 1879-86; mission chaplain to Bishop of London, 1886; prebendary of St. Paul's, 1889; Bishop of Mauritius, 1890.

MAVROGORDATO, ALEXANDER STEPHEN—Dispenser Civil Hospital, Nicosia, Cyprus, June, 1879; clerk to principal forest officer; June, 1881; acted as forest officer, May, 1883, to Dec., 1884; was engaged on the locust destruction as paymaster, 1883 to 1886; and as asst. to commr., Limassol, 1889-92; examiner of accounts, audit pt., May, 1886; transferred to rec. gen.'s department, April, 1891; asst. in survey and forest points., Dec., 1892; also president survey limitation commsn.; superintendent locust destruction operations, 1895-6; asst. in forest and agricultural depts., June, 1895; financial asst., W. Dept., Oct., 1896.

events.position: clerk and interpreter to Nicosia government, police
events.text_span: clerk and interpreter to Nicosia government, police, 1882

raw_text: MAVROGORDATO, THEODORE E.—Born 1871; educated at Berlin; clerk to commissioner, Limassol, 1881; clerk and interpreter to Nicosia government, police, 1882; asst. inspr., police, Limassol, 1883; inspector, police, Kyrenia, Sept., 1884; actg. commdt. of police, June, 1895.

events.text_span: resdt., Lang Lupai and Sarebas, Jan., 1876

events.position: resdt., class, 1st divn. (Sarawak proper), and member supreme Council
events.text_span: resdt., class, 1st divn. (Sarawak proper), and member supreme Council, Oct., 1881

raw_text: MAXWELL, FRANCIS R. O.—Cadet, Sarawak force, June, 1872; asst. resdt., 1872; resdt., Lang Lupai and Sarebas, Jan., 1876; resdt., class, 1st divn. (Sarawak proper), and member supreme Council, Oct., 1881; retired June, 1895; ag. consul for Sarawak, Brunei, and North Borneo, and ag. resident, Labuan.
MAXWELL, JOSEPH P. M. Inst. C. E.—Public works department, New Zealand, 1874; general manager, N. Z. railways, 1880; rly. commissioner under Rlys. Act, 1887, Jan., 1889.

MAXWELL, JOSEPH RENNER, M.A., B.C.L.—Educated at Sierra Leone grammar school and Trinity Coll., Ox.; 2nd class honours, jurisprudence; B.A., 1879, M.A., 1880, B.C.L. (honours), 1884; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, in 1880; and practised for three years at the bar of the Gold Coast Colony; Queen's advocate, Gambia, Mar., 1884; also registrar of deeds, and colonial registrar; acting superintendent, police, Aug., 1885, April, 1886; member of leg. coun., 1886; chief magistrate, 1887.

events.text_span: schoolmaster, St. Thomas primary school, Barbados, Aug., 1855
store and warehouse keeper, Barbados, Oct., 1865.

raw_text: MAXWELL, WILLIAM D.—Educated at Codrington College, Barbados; schoolmaster, St. Thomas primary school, Barbados, Aug., 1855; of Anglican school, Dominica, April, 1863; store and warehouse keeper, Barbados, Oct., 1865.

MAXWELL, SIR WILLIAM EDWARD, K.C.M.G. (C.M.G. 1885).—Educated at Repton; employed in supreme court, Penang and Singapore, 1865 to 1869; qualified as an advocate of the local bar in 1867; police magistrate and commissioner court of requests, Penang, L., 1869; ditto, Malacca, Feb., 1870; ditto, Singapore, Aug., 1871, ditto Prov. Wellesley, 1872; honorary judge of the supreme court, Penang, 1874; assistant government agent, province Kessley, Sept., 1874; deputy commissioner with Larut field force on the expedition to Perak to punish the murderers of the British resident, in 1875 (medal), mentioned in despatches; assistant resident, Perak, and member of the state council, Feb., 1878; called to the bar (Inner Temple), 1881; commissioned to visit the Australian colonies, to report on the Torrens land registration system, 1882; commissioner of lands titles S.S. (1882), and member of executive and legislative councils, 1883; employed by the foreign office on a mission to the West Coast of Atchin in 1884, when the release of the 18 survivors of the crew of the "Nisero" was obtained after they had been for 10 months in captivity; for this service received thanks of H.M.'s government, and was made a C.M.G.; actg. res. councillor, Penang, 1884-9; British resident, Selangor, 1889; colonial secretary, Straits Settlements, 1892; ag. gov., Sept., 1893, to Jan., 1894; Governor Gold Coast, 1895.

MAY, ALFRED JOHN.—Ed. St. Saviour's Gram. Schl., New Shoreham, and Training Schl., Hurstpierpoint; head master St. Paul's Coll., Hong Kong, Jan., 1875; principal, govt. normal schl., Sept., 1881; 3rd master, govt. central schl. (now Victoria Coll.), Sept., 1883; 2nd master, 1888; ag. headmr., April, 1890, to Oct., 1891; J.P., 1890.
MAY, CECIL.—Clerical asst., treas., Br. Guiana; fourth class clerk, April, 1889; third class, June, 1892.

MAY, FRANCIS H., C.M.G. (1895).—Educated at Harrow and Trin. Coll., Dub.; first honour-man and prizeman in classics and modern languages; appointed after competitive examination to a Hong Kong cadetship, 1881; acting-assistant registrar-gen., April, 1886; ag. asst. col. secy., Jan., 1887; secy. to bd. of examiners, Jan., 1889; asst. col. secy., Jan., 1891; private secy. to Gov. Sir W. Des Voeux, to Admnr. Sir F. Fleming, and Maj.-Gen. Barker, April, 1889, to Dec., 1891; ag. col. treas., April to Oct., 1892; member leg. coun., May, 1895.
MAY, HENRY FREDERIC PORTER.—Extra clerk, treasury, British Guiana, 1877; clerk, savings bank, 1880; acting accountant, 1887.

MAYER, GEORGE CLIFFORD.—Called to the bar June, 1860; Dec., 1864, district and stipendiary magistrate, Seychelles; May, 1868, district magistrate, Pamplemousses; Mar., 1881, ditto, Plaines Wilhems; Jan., 1887, senior district magistrate; ag. master, sup. ct., Feb., 1890, to Mar., 1892; Dec., 1892, to Feb., 1893; May to July, 1896.

MEADE, HONORABLE SIR ROBERT HENRY, G.C.B. (1897), K.C.B. (1894), C.B. (1885).—Is an M.A. of Exeter Coll., Oxford; passed an exam. before the civil service commsrs., May 9th, 1859; junior clerk in the foreign office, June 1st, 1859; was attached to Lord Dufferin's special mission to Syria, from July 31, 1860, till Sept., 1861; accompanied the Prince of Wales during His Royal Highness's tour in the East, Germany, and Italy, in 1862; accompanied Earl Russell to Germany when his lordship was in attendance on Her Majesty, in the autumn of 1862; and accompanied Earl Granville to Germany on a similar occasion, in 1863, and again in 1865; was appointed groom of the bedchamber to H.R.H. the Prince of Wales, Nov. 27th, 1862; and was private secretary to Earl Granville from June, 1864, till July, 1866; private secretary to Lord Granville when secretary of state for the colonies from 9th Dec., 1868, till July, 1870, and when secretary of state for foreign affairs, 1870; assistant under secretary, colonial office, May, 1871; appointed registrar of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, May, 1877; secretary, 1892; British delegate to the West African conference at Berlin, 1884; under secretary of state, 1st Feb., 1892; retired, 28th Feb., 1897.

raw_text: MEADEN, C. W.—14th Hussars and 5th Dragoon Gds., 1870-76; convict service, England, 1877; suptd. of Chaguanas convict depot, Trinidad, 1885; J.P. for county Caroni, 1887; asst. suptd. prisons, 1890; member central agr. bd.; carried out special agricultural experiments by prison labour, 1887 to 1892; acted twice as suptd. of govt. farm and pastures; acted twice as sec. central agr. bd.; and as warden of Chaguanas; lieut. and adjt. Trinidad Light Horse, Dec. 1894; manager govt. stock farm and supt. of pastures, Mar., 1895; lt. and adjt., Trinidad Light Horse, Dec., 1894.

raw_text: MELDRUM, CHARLES, LL.D., F.R.S., C.M.G. (1886).—Educated at Marischal Coll., and Univ. Aber.; Lord Rector's prizeman, 1844, and first graduate of that year (hon. LL.D., 1876; entered the Bombay educational department, 1846; professor of mathematics, Royal College, Mauritius, 1848; secretary and one of the founders of the Meteorological Society of Mauritius, 1851; government meteorological observer, 1862; director, Royal Alfred Observatory, 1875; member of the council of government, 1886; retired Oct., 1896.

MELVILLE, SAMUEL.—Served as captain commanding George native levy, Jan. to Sept., 1851; and lost arm in action at Amatola Basin, 28th June, 1851; 2nd asst. surveyor-general, July, 1882; railway expropriation commr., Aug., 1889; was commissioner of Lichtenberg, and agent for native affairs on the south-west boundary of the Transvaal, July, 1873, to Oct., 1874; and surveyor-general from latter date to April, 1877, under the government of the S. A. Republic; and surveyor-general from April, 1877, to Aug., 1881, under British rule; first assistant surveyor-general, Cape, July, 1892.

MELVILLE, GEORGE, C.M.G. (1891)—Late lieut., Kent Art. Militia; ed. Edinburgh Academy; clerk in the solicitor's department, inland revenue, after competitive examination, June, 1862; afterwards in the legacy and succession duty department; chief clerk, treasury, Sierra Leone, Nov., 1874; acted as colonial treasurer, Dec., 1874, to Feb., 1876, during which period also acted as colonial secretary, July to Sept., 1875; acted also as collector of customs and colonial surveyor, and held commissions as deputy coroner and J.P.; first clerk, government sect.'s office, British Guiana, 1877; assist. govt. sect. 1879; acted as govt. sect., 1881, 1882, 1887 and 1888, and also as audt. gen.; col. secy., audt. gen., and reg. gen. of British Honduras, 1889; admr. of govt., 1890 and 1891; admr. of govt. of Falklands, 1893; col. secy., Bahamas, 1894; admn. govt., 1894 and 1895; col. sec., Leewards, 1895; admn. govt., 1895 and 1896.

events.position: barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, ag. stip. and circuit mag. and judge ct. of common pleas
events.text_span: barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, ag. stip. and circuit mag. and judge ct. of common pleas, Bahamas, 1892

raw_text: MENENDEZ, M. RAMON.—Ed. Emman. Coll. Camb.; L.L.B., Camb., 1890; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, ag. stip. and circuit mag. and judge ct. of common pleas, Bahamas, 1892; dist. commr., Lagos, Aug., 1894.

MENSAH, ALFRED.—Temporary clerk, customs, Gold Coast, Feb., 1875; superintendent K.E. labourers during Ashanti war, Dec., 1873, to May, 1874; 3rd clerk of customs, Cape Coast, Dec., 1874; 2nd clerk, July, 1875; clerk and warehouseman, Mar., 1878; sub-collector of customs, Winnebah, Oct., 1880; Appam, Oct., 1883; and Cape Coast, Feb., 1886.

MERCER, WILLIAM HEPWORTH.—Open scholar and Hody exhibitioner, Wadham College, Oxford; 2nd class, classical moderations; 2nd class, final classical school; 2nd class clerk in the colonial office after open competition, 30th June, 1879; awarded first prize in common law offered by Council of Legal Education, 1883, and a pupil scholarship of 100 guineas, in real property, by the Society of the Inner Temple, 1885; called to the bar, 1886; sec. to the Earl of Jersey, when acting as British delegate at colonial confce., Ottawa, 1894; on special mission to Hawaii with Mr. Sandford Fleming, Sep. to Nov. 1894, negotiating lease of landing station of proposed Pacific cable; asst. priv. sec. to Lord Ripon, 1st April, 1895; and to Mr. Chamberlain, 28th June, 1895; 1st class clerk, 1st May, 1896; secy. to Pacific Cable Committee, 1896.

MERCIER, GUY, H. V.—Ed. Antigua Gram. Schl., senr. Camb. certificate, 1893; clk., tr., Antigua, 1894.

raw_text: MEREWETHER, EDWARD MARSH.—Educated Harrow; cadet, S.S., Oct., 1880; passed final examination in Malay, May, 1882; acting collector of land revenue, Singapore, and J.P. S.S., May, 1882; confirmed Jan., 1883; Malay interpreter to H.M.S. "Pegasus" to Tenom in connection with the "Nisero" case, June, 1884; ag. mag. and dist. officer in charge treas., Malacca, 1886; Dist. offr. of Jennings, 1886; actg. 2nd mag., Penang, 1888; actg. 1st mag., Penang, 1888; 2nd asst. col. sec., 1890; suptdt. of census, 1891; ag. asst. col. sec., Aug., 1891; collr. of land rev., and in charge of treas., Malacca, July, 1892; ag. resdt. councillor, May, 1893; Sept. to Dec., 1893; inspr. of prisons, 1893; ag. asst. col. secy. and clk. of councils, 1893-1896.

METHUEN, MAJ.-GEN. LORN, C.B., D.C.M.G. (1886).—Entered army, 1864; served in Ashantee campaign, 1873-4; in Egypt, 1882; in the Bechuanaland expedition, 1884-5; inspr. of Bechuanaland police, 1890.

Belize fire-brigade, June, 1889 to Feb., 1893; clk. to auditor, Dec., 1891; 4th clk. Treas. 1892; ag. 3rd clk., Nov., 1893; audit examiner, col. sec. dept., Feb., 1894.

Norfolk and South Eastern circuits; acting queen's advocate, Gold Coast, Jan., 1882; president of district court Limassol, Cyprus, Sept., 1882; puisne judge, Supreme court, 1892; ag. ch. justice, June and July, 1893; ag. judge of Consular Court, Constantinople, May to July, 1894; ag. ch. just., May, Oct., 1895; recd. thanks of S. of S. (1893). — V.D. accountant to the treasury, 1859-72; treasury officer of accounts, 1872-88; asst. comptroller and auditor-general, 20 Sept., 1888; comptroller and auditor-general, 29 Apl., 1896; late lieut.-col. commdg. hon. col.) 12th Middlesex (Civil Service) R. MISSO, W. E., M.R.C.S. — Assistant colonial surgeon, Ceylon, 1869; dist. med. offr., Badulla, Jan., 1883; seur. med. offr., 1886; asst. col. surg., Galle, 1889.

MITCHELL, LIEUT.-COL. SIR C. B. H. (late R.M.), G.C.M.G. (1895), K.C.M.G. (1883), C.M.G. (1880). — Colonial secretary, Br.
