raw_text: MITCHELL, G. P. S. — Asst. clerk, ed. dept., Trinidad, Aug., 1880; 2nd clerk to asst. dir. of pub. works, Feb., 1884; clerk, northern division of the P.W.D., May, 1886; clerk to warden of St. Ann's and Diego Martin Ward Unions, July, 1893.

MITCHELL, H. HASTINGS — Entered col. sec.'s office, Grenada, 1878; 2nd clerk, treasury, 1879; acted as revenue officer, 1880; in audit office, St. Lucia, April, 1881; acted chief clerk, treasury, 1881-2; 2nd clerk and checking officer of invoices, treasury, 1882; acted as auditor, 1882 and 1883-6; chief clerk, treasury, 1887; acting chief clerk, col. sec.'s office, Grenada, 1889.
MITCHELL, HENRY STUART, C.M.G. (1882). — Agent-general of immigrants, Trinidad; appointed 1853. Was superintendent from 1852 to 1853; retired on full pay, 1883.

MITCHELL, P. — Born 1824, called to the bar of New Brunswick, 1848; sat in N.B. legislative assembly from 1856 to 1860, when appointed to the legislative council; in 1867, called to the senate of the dominion, where he remained until 1872, when he resigned and was elected to the house of commons; a member of the executive council of New Brunswick from 1858 to 1865, and from April, 1866, to the union in 1867 (in his own administration); was minister of marine and fisheries for the dominion July, 1867, until Nov., 1873, when the Macdonald gov. retired.

MITCHELL, ROBERT W. S., C.M.G. (1892). — Assistant clerk, immigration department, Trinidad, 1858; warden of the district of Montserrat, and commissioner of crown lands, 1867; sub-intendant of crown lands, 1869; was a J.P., member of the central road board and of the board of health; assistant protector of immigrants, Mauritius, 1872. Received the thanks of Her Majesty's royal commissioners for special services rendered during their inquiry into the treatment of Indian immigrants in that colony; acted as agent-general of immigration in Trinidad in 1873; government emigration agent in India, Oct., 1873; acting emigration agent for Jamaica, 1876; agent-general for immigration, British Guiana, 1881; emigration agent for British Guiana in Calcutta, 1884; in 1885 modified emigration rules under Act of 1883, and in 1886 obtained extension of Indian P.O. money order system to British Guiana and other West Indian colonies. MITCHELL - INNES, NORMAN G.—Edinburgh academy and Repton; Hong Kong cadet, 1880; attached to the colonial office for one year, 1881; passed cadet, 1884; acting assistant colonial secretary; assistant-registrar-general, 1884; acting police magistrate and coroner, 1886; actg. supert. gaol, 1887; called to bar (Middle Temple), 1889; ag. regr. gen. 1889-90; treasurer, and member ex. and leg. councils, 1891.

events.text_span: Entered public works department, Trinidad, Jan., 1882
asst. col. secy. and treas., Sierra Leone, May, 1886

events.position: acted as audit.-gen. collr. of customs and commt. of Sherbro, treas.
events.text_span: acted as audit.-gen. collr. of customs and commt. of Sherbro, treas., Gambia, 1889
ag. col. secy., Jan. to Oct., 1892
deputy gov., Mar., and again May, 1892.

raw_text: MITFORD, CHARLES B.—Entered public works department, Trinidad, Jan., 1882; asst. col. secy. and treas., Sierra Leone, May, 1886; acted as audit.-gen. collr. of customs and commt. of Sherbro, treas., Gambia, 1889; ag. adminr., June to Nov.; col. treas., Sierra Leone, 1891; ag. col. secy., Jan. to Oct., 1892; deputy gov., Mar., and again May, 1892.

MOFFAT, REV. JOHN SMITH, C.M.G. (1890).—Native commissioner, N.W. Border, Transvaal, 1880-1; R.M., Basutoland, Mar., 1882, to April, 1884; res. mag., Taungs, British Bechuanaland, Oct., 1885; asst. comr. for the protectorate, 1887.

MOIR, ALEXANDER WILSON, C.M.G. (1877).—In customs department, Jamaica, Feb., 1843; presiding magistrate of Bay Islands, Dec., 1855; treasurer of Honduras, July, 1860; acting colonial secretary with a dormant commission as superintendent, June, 1861; president, Turks and Caicos Island, Aug., 1862; president, Virgin Islands, Oct., 1869; president of Dominica, June, 1872; president of St. Kitts, Feb., 1873; member of the executive council, Leeward Islands (1871), and a nominated member of the general council; retired, 1883.
MOLESWORTH, H.—Judge, court of insolvency, Victoria, 1886; formerly county ct. judge.

MOLONEY, SIR C. ALFRED, C.M.G. (1882), K.C.M.G. (1890).—Entered military college, Sandhurst, 1866; ensign, Feb., 1867; lieut., Dec., 1868; capt., Nov., 1874; acted as civil commandant of the Quiah district, Sierra Leone, 1867-8; A.D.C. to Sir James Walker, C.B., governor, Bahamas, Feb., 1871; private secretary to Capt. G. C. Strahan, administrator, Bahamas, April, 1871, to May, 1873; private secretary and A.D.C. to Governor Hennessy, June to Nov., 1873, when he embarked for service on the Gold Coast; served in the Ashantee campaign, 1873-4 (medal); private secretary and A.D.C. to Governor Strahan, July, 1874; acting auditor, G.C. colony, Feb., 1875; acting inspector-general of constabulary, Mar., 1875; assistant colonial secretary, 1877; colonial secretary, 1879; administered government of Lagos, 1878 to 1880, and again in 1881; administrator of the government of the colony, 1882; resumed the administration of Lagos, 1882-3; administrator of the Gambia, 1884; administrator of Lagos, 1885; governor, 1887; governor, British Honduras, 1891; gov. of Windwards, 1897; author of the "Forestry of West Africa."

events.text_span: regr. in bankruptcy and marshal, Jan., 1894.

raw_text: MONIER-WILLIAMS, CYRIL FAITHFULL, M.A. (Oxon).—Of Lincoln's Inn, barrister-at-law; educated at Haileybury Coll.; served for more than 20 years in the master's office, ct. of Queen's Bench, and afterwards in the central office of sup. ct. of Judicature, England; regr. sup. ct. of Trinidad and Tobago; regr. in bankruptcy and marshal, Jan., 1894.
MOOR, THE HON. F. R., M.L.A.—Secy. for native affairs, Natal, 1893.

MOORE, MAJOR ALEX. McDONNELL.—2nd lieut. Roy. Nth. Down Rifles, 1876; 3rd lieut. 89th P. V.'s Regt.; Suakin campaign, El Teb, &c., (medal and clasp and Khedive's star); adjt. depot Armagh, 1884; capt. 2nd bat. R. I. Fus. 1885; inspr. S. Leone police, Feb. 1890; raised S. Leone frontier police; inspr.-gen., Jan., 1891; commanded expedition in Tambacca country, 1892 (medal and clasp); inspr.-gen. of police, Mauritius, Sept., 1892; resigned, 1895.

MOORE, GEORGE.—Surveyor to commission for quieting land titles, Sept., 1875; first government surveyor, colony of Fiji, Oct., 1876; staff surveyor, January, 1880; acting crown surveyor, July, 1884, to Feb., 1885.

MOORE, CAPTN. S.—Late Roy. Irish Regt.; served in Afghanistan, 1879-80, in charge of regimental transport; acted as adjt. to rear-guard at evacuation of Lundi Kotal; served in Nile campaign, 1884-85, crossing the desert to Metemneh; was adjt. 1st Norwich Volr. Batt.; Inspector, S. Leone Frontier Police, 1894.

MOORE, W.—Minister of lands and works, Tasmania, 4th Aug. 1873, to 20th July, 1876; from 9th to 13th Aug. 1877; colonial secretary, 13th Aug. 1877, to 20th Dec. 1878, and again Oct., 1879, to 1881; now president of leg. council.
MORCOM, RICHARD F.—Assistant clerk of peace and prosecutor, native high court, Nov., Jan., 1879; clerk of peace for several courts, Feb., 1880, and attorney for war department.
MOREHEAD, B. D.—Postmaster-general, Queensland, 17th Dec., 1880, to 3rd Aug., 1882; col. secy., 13th June, 1888; premier, 1889-90.
MORETON, THE HON. BERKELEY BASIL.—Postmaster-general, Queensland, Mar., 1891; minister of public instruction, April, 1895-8.

events.position: chief regr. sup. ct. and regr. of deeds
events.text_span: chief regr. sup. ct. and regr. of deeds, Oct., 1897

raw_text: MORGAN, STANLEY WM.—Ed. Broomsgrove School; Dist. Commr., G. Coast, June, 1892; chief regr. sup. ct. and regr. of deeds, Oct., 1897; confirmed, Jan. 1893.

MORINE, ALFRED B., L.L.B.—Born in Nova Scotia; removed to Newfoundland in 1883; became editor of the "Mercury" in 1883, and in 1890 of the "Herald," both published in St. John's; was admitted to bar of Nova Scotia 1894, and in same year to the bar of Newfoundland; was elected M.H.A. for Bonavista, Newfoundland, in 1888, 1889, 1893; was a member of the people's delegation to Great Britain, in 1890, on French treaty question, and a member of the legislative delegation to Great Britain, in 1891, on the same quest.; colonial secretary, April to December, 1894.

events.text_span: second clerk to R. M., Durban, May, 1868

events.text_span: acting clerk of the court, &c., Klip river division, Mar., 1881

raw_text: MORLING, ARTHUR.—Entered the customs department, Natal, Mar., 1862; second clerk to R. M., Durban, May, 1868; acting clerk of the court, &c., Klip river division, Mar., 1881; acting clerk of the court registrar of the circuit court, &c., Durban, Mar., 1872; acting clerk of the court, &c., city division, P. M. Burg, June, 1874; third landing waiter, customs, Jan., 1876; second ditto, May, 1879; and first ditto, Jan., 1881; magistrate, Lion's River divn., 1886.
MORRAH, A.—Secretary for lands, Victoria, 1st June, 1878; comsnr. of audit, 1890.

MORRIS, DANIEL, M.A., D.Sc., C.M.G. (1890)—Ed. at Cheltenham and Royal School of Music, London; late senior moderator and first gold medallist natural science honours, Trin. Coll., Dub.; B.A. (1876), M.A. (1880); assistant, Botanical Gardens, Ceylon, Aug., 1877; on special duty, "coffee disease inquiry," Ceylon, 1879; received the thanks of the "Planters' Association of Ceylon" for services in connection therewith, as also special commendation from Sir Michael Hicks-Beach, and honorarium equal to one year's salary; director public gardens and plantations, Jamaica, Nov., 1879; on special mission to Trinidad and Grenada to report on the cultivation of cacao, 1882; reported upon the economic resources of the colony of British Honduras, 1882; chairman, Jamaica committee, Amsterdam exhibition, 1883; reported the resources of St. Helena, 1883; prepared report upon "planting enterprise in the West Indies" presented to parliament, 1884; chairman, board of governors of the institute of Jamaica, 1885-86; commissioner for the West Indies at World's Exposition, New Orleans, 1885; chairman of local commission for Colonial and Pan Exhibition, 1886; assistant director, Royal Museums, Kew, May, 1886; represented India at World's Conference, at Paris, 1888 and 1889; on mission to West Indies to organise botanical stations in Leeward and Leeward Islands, 1889-90. Visited Bahamas in 1895-6, with the object of advising aiding the Colonial Government. Is author numerous pamphlets on pure and applied science, and of papers and reports relating to the distribution and cultivation of tropical economic plants.
MORRIS, E. P. — Member, Newfoundland executive (without portfolio), 1889 to 1894; ag. atty.-gen. 1890.
MORRISON, HON. D., Q.C. — Delegate to Canada, Newfoundland Treaty shore question, 1893; M.H.A. for Bonavista, 1888, re-elected, 1892; attorney-general and member ex. Coun., till to December, 1894.

events.position: Clerk financial secretary's department, War Office
events.text_span: Clerk financial secretary's department, War Office, Mar., 1878

events.position: clerk in inner and accountant general's office
events.text_span: clerk in inner and accountant general's office, Cyprus, Dec., 1879

raw_text: MORTON, ALBERT. — Clerk financial secretary's department, War Office, Mar., 1878; clerk in inner and accountant general's office, Cyprus, Dec., 1879; assistant collector of customs, Nov., 1882; acting chief collector, 1883 to 1884, and 1886; collector of customs, Larnaka, 1891; isld. comr., 1894.

MOSELEY, C. H. HARLEY. — Temporary assistant colonial secretary and treasurer, Sierra Leone, Aug., 1881; sub-treasurer and collector, Freetown, July, 1882; J.P. for Sierra Leone, Aug., 1883; commissioner of the court of requests, Freetown, Aug., 1882; assistant colonial secretary and treasurer, Sierra Leone, 1884; civil comdt. Sherbro, 1885; treasurer, Gambia, 1891; member ex. and leg. coun. and J. P. for the colony; act. of govt. in 1891-2 and 3; on mission to West Africa, 1891; treasurer, Lagos, 1894.

MOSELEY, FRANCIS ARNOLD. — Admitted Sierra Leone bar, 1874; acting clerk, colonial secretary's department, Bahamas, April, 1872; private secretary to Governor Pope Hennessy, Nov., 1873 (also clerk of executive council); and to Administrator J. D'A. Dumaresq, June to Dec., 1874; assistant private secretary to Governor Robinson, Dec., 1874, to June, 1879; private secretary to Administrator E. B. A. Taylor, June, 1879; to Governor Robinson, Dec., 1879, to June, 1880; also to Mr. Taylor, from that date to Dec., 1880; J.P. for the colony, and acting police magistrate for the island of New Providence Sept., 1880; registrar of titles, West Australia, 1881; admitted to West Australia bar, 1881; deputy commissioner of titles, 1881, 1882, 1883, and 1888; 1883; reg. supreme court, 1889.

events.position: was chairman of Canadian Pacific Railway to
events.text_span: was chairman of Canadian Pacific Railway to 1888.

raw_text: MOUNTSTEPHEN, BARON. — Sir George Stephen (Bart., 1886); was chairman of Canadian Pacific Railway to 1888.
MOURITZ, GEORGE A. — Treasurer, Melbourne, harbour trust, 1877; secretary, 1884.

events.text_span: senator and minister of justice (Canada), June, 1896.

raw_text: MOWAT, SIR OLIVER, K.C.M.G. (1892), LL.D., Q.C. — Attorney-general and premier, Ontario, 25th Oct., 1872; senator and minister of justice (Canada), June, 1896.

events.position: Commissioner of emigration from Canada to Ireland
events.text_span: Commissioner of emigration from Canada to Ireland, 1869-72

raw_text: MOYLAN, J. G. — Commissioner of emigration from Canada to Ireland, 1869-72; director of penitentiaries, Canada, 1872; inspector of penitentiaries, 1875.

acting assistant government agent, Batticaloa, July, 1873

raw_text: MOYSEY, HENRY LUTTRELL. — Educated at Cheltenham Coll.; a writer in the Ceylon civil service, Sept., 1870; police magistrate, Kayts, Oct., 1872; acting assistant government agent, Kurunégala, April, 1873; acting assistant government agent, Batticaloa, July, 1873; assistant agent, Kurunégala, 1876; police magistrate, Kandy, 1876; assistant agent, Matale, 1878; dist. judge, Kurunégala, Dec., 1891.

raw_text: MOYSEY, COLONEL CHARLES JOHN, R.E., C.M.G. (1884). — Ed. at Cheltenham Coll.; special commissioner Keate Award district, South Africa, 1880-1; assisted in defining the South Western boundary of the Transvaal, under the Pretoria convention of 1881; assistant director of works, War Office, 1884-9.

MUGLISTON, THOMAS CRAIGHTON, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. and L.M. (Edin.) — Entered R.N. medical service, 1876-81; medical officer to French and United States consulates, Singapore; J.P. and coroner 1883; colonial surgeon, S. S., 1886; ag. prin. med. offr., Singapore, Feb. to Aug., 1890, and July and Aug., 1896.

events.position: sub-warden, St. Leonard's College Hall, St. Andrew's
events.text_span: sub-warden, St. Leonard's College Hall, St. Andrew's, 1869

events.position: examiner in mathematics and natural philosophy, St. Andrew's Univ
events.text_span: examiner in mathematics and natural philosophy, St. Andrew's Univ., 1884-86

raw_text: MUIR, THOMAS, M.A. (Glas., 1869), LL.D. (Glas.), F.R.S.E., F.R.G.S. — Member London and Edinburgh Mathematical Societies, &c.; sub-warden, St. Leonard's College Hall, St. Andrew's, 1869; asst. prof. mathematics, Glasgow Univ., 1871; head mathematical and science master Glasgow high school, 1874; examiner in mathematics and natural philosophy to Glasgow Univ., 1880-84; Keith medallist, R.S.E., 1881; examiner secondary schools, Scotch education department, 1885-87; examiner in mathematics and natural philosophy, St. Andrew's Univ., 1884-86; superintendent-general of education, Cape, April, 1892.

MULOC, THE HON. WILLIAM. — Born at Bond Head, Ont., Jan. 19, 1843; graduated at Toronto univ., 1863; gold medal in modern languages; called to the Ontario bar, 1868; vice-chancellor of Toronto univ. in 1881; sat in Parliament since 1882, representing North York; postmast.-gen. in Mr. Laurier's govt., June, 1896.

events.text_span: appointed to the survey department, Trinidad, Feb., 1882

events.position: office of registrar of supreme court
events.text_span: office of registrar of supreme court, Sept., 1884

raw_text: MUNN, CECIL AUGUSTUS. — Educated at Felstead College, Essex, and afterwards in Switzerland; appointed to the survey department, Trinidad, Feb., 1882; public works department, Jan., 1883; education office, June, 1884; office of registrar of supreme court, Sept., 1884; audit office, May, 1886; chief clerk, savings bk., Jan., 1888.

events.position: agent-general for the colony in London
events.text_span: agent-general for the colony in London, 1892-3.

raw_text: MUNRO, THE HON. JAMES. — Premier and treasurer of Victoria, 1891; agent-general for the colony in London, 1892-3.

clerk in the marshal's office, Dec., 1868

raw_text: MURRAY, URIEL. — Acting clerk to water commissioners, Antigua, May, 1867; second clerk, treasury department, Nov., 1867; clerk in the marshal's office, Dec., 1868; acted as first clerk in registrar's office, June, 1870; second revenue officer, Dominica, Feb., 1871; first revenue officer, Nov., 1874; admeasurer of shipping, July, 1878.
MURRAY, ALEXANDER, C.E. (Glasgow Univ.), A.M.I.C.E.—Pioneer officer, P.W.D., Ceylon, 1871; superintendent, 1871; chief assistant head quarters, 1874; provincial engineer, 1886.

events.text_span: Member of legislative assembly, Manitoba, 1874 to 1878

raw_text: MURRAY, ALEXANDER—Member of legislative assembly, Manitoba, 1874 to 1878; police magistrate, East Marquette, 1878; member of house of assembly, 1879; speaker, 1883 to 1886; municipal commissioner, Winnipeg, 1887.

events.position: assistant government agent, Kurunégalla, and acting landing surveyor, Galle
events.text_span: assistant government agent, Kurunégalla, and acting landing surveyor, Galle, 1870

raw_text: MURRAY, C. A.—Writer, Ceylon civil service, 1866; acting police magistrate, Dumbara, in the same year; commissioner of requests, Balapitimodara, Oct., 1867; assistant government agent, Kurunégalla, and acting landing surveyor, Galle, 1870; acting assistant agent, Jaffna, 1874; acting police magistrate, Galle, 1874; acting commissioner of requests, Colombo, 1875; police magistrate, Kandy, 1876; assistant agent, Nuwara Eliya, 1877; ditto, Hambantota, 1887; govt. agt., Badulla, 1895.

events.text_span: Crown prosecutor, S.W. district, N.S.W., June, 1875

raw_text: MURRAY, C. E. R.—Crown prosecutor, S.W. district, N.S.W., June, 1875; district court judge, N.W. district, 1878; of N. district, 1880.

raw_text: MURRAY, T., JUNR.—Government medical officer, Tacarigua district, Trinidad, 1 Jan., 1876

raw_text: MURRAY, THE HON. THOMAS KENRICK, C.M.G. (1895).—Memb. leg. ass., Natal; minister of lands and works, Natal, 1893.

raw_text: MURRAY, W. SHEPHERD.—Dep. fiscal., N. Prov., Ceylon, 1868; asst. suptdt. police, 1873; ag. pol. mag. and commis. of requests, 1873; suptdt. of police, 1873.

MUSGRAVE, ANTHONY, JUNR.—Private secretary to Governor Musgrave, in Newfoundland, from July, 1868, to July, 1869; served in same capacity in British Columbia, August, 1869, to April, 1872; private secretary and clerk of executive council Natal, July, 1872 to 1873; private secretary and clerk of executive council in South Australia, June, 1873; private secretary to governor of Jamaica, Aug., 1877, to June 1883; also acted as secretary to the local committee of the International Fisheries Exhibition, 1883; private secretary to Sir A. Musgrave in Queensland, Nov., 1883; assistant deputy commissioner, New Guinea, 1885; govt. secy., 1888, and local auditor, Nov., 1890; member exec. and leg. councils.

MUSGRAVE, CHRISTOPHER.—Junior clerk, registrar's office, Antigua, Dec., 1874; clerk to magistrate district A, April, 1877; acted as chief clerk, registrar's office, Jan., 1879; acted as magistrate and coroner for district B, July, 1882; deputy-coroner for district A, Aug., 1882; clerk colonial secretary's office, Mar., 1883; private secretary to the admir., Leeward Islands, Aug., 1883, to Jan., 1884; acted magistrate and coroner for district B, Dec., 1884, and for district A, Feb. and June, 1885; secretary to local committee of the Colonial and Indian Exhibition, Jan., 1886; acting magistrate, Nevis, 1886; member of infirmary board, 1886; ag.-mag. and coroner, Barbuda Oct., 1888 to Nov., 1889; and for dist. A. Antigua. Dec., 1889 to June, 1890; ch. clk., regr.'s office, Antigua, Aug., 1890; regr. and pro-marshal, Dominica, July, 1892.

events.text_span: admitted solicitor, sup. ct., Jamaica, Oct., 1875

raw_text: MUSSON, JOHN TURNBULL.—Ed. King's Coll. Schl. (Lond.); admitted solicitor, sup. ct., Jamaica, Oct., 1875; notary public, 1883; resdt. mag., St. Andrew, Nov., 1888.

MUSSON, SAMUEL PAINTER.—Clerk in the governor's secretary's office, Jamaica, April, 1865; second-class clerk in the office of the final secretary, Sept., 1866; first-class clerk, colonial secretary's office, Oct., 1872; chief clerk, 1884; has repeatedly acted as assistant secretary and auditor-general, June, 1885, to May, 1896; treasurer, Jamaica, 1896.

MYLNE, THOMAS.—Registrar of titles, Queensland, 1884; clerk, registrar-general's office, 1872; chief clerk 1873; second deputy, 1878; principal deputy registrar, 1879; member civil service board, 1890.

raw_text: NAUDI, ALFREDO, LL.D. (1877).—Received the Bar, Malte, 1878; elected member coun. of govt., 1889; prof. of law, Malta Univ., 1890; crown advocate and govt. legal adviser, with seat in exec. coun. and council of govt., 1895.

raw_text: NAUDI, SIR SALVATORE (KT. BACH. ISS. C.M.G. (1888), LL.D.—Judge of the court of appeal, Malta, appointed in 1859.
NAZ, SIR VIRGILE, K.C.M.G. (1880). (C.M.G. 1874).—Member of the council of government of Mauritius; retired, Dec., 1895.
NEGUS, FRANCIS H. D.—Local auditor, la. Dec., 1893; ditto, G. Coast, June, 1894.

NEILL, A. S.—Railway commissioner, South Australia. Was appointed 1888; born in Scotland 1837; mercantile manager at Port Adelaide, 1832 to 1888; vice-consul for Sweden and Norway at Port Adelaide, Aug., 1881, and resigned same May, 1888.
NELSON, THE HON. HUGH.—Lieut.-govr., British Columbia, 8th Feb., 1887; resigned, 1892.

raw_text: NEVILL, EDMUND NEVILLE, F.R.A.S., F.L.S. &c.—Govt. astronomer, Natal, 1882; also govt. chemist and official assayer, 1886.

events.text_span: Superintendent of public buildings, Newfoundland, Jan., 1864

raw_text: NEVILL, J. T.—Superintendent of public buildings, Newfoundland, Jan., 1864; also inspector of lighthouses, Jan., 1872.

events.text_span: Asst. clerk, queen's advocate's dept., G. Coast, 1879

events.text_span: accompanied Governor Sir S. Rowe to Prahsue as special service clerk, 1881

raw_text: NEVILLE, G. STANLEY.—Asst. clerk, queen's advocate's dept., G. Coast, 1879; extra col. secy.'s office, 1880; accompanied Governor Sir S. Rowe to Prahsue as special service clerk, 1881; clerk in regr. gen.'s office, 1881; extra clerk, governor's office, 1881-7; acted as governor's clerk, clerk of leg. coun., and clerk to bd. of rev., 1887; second clerk, col. secy.'s office, 1889.

NEVILLE, HUGH.—Was at Magdalene College, Cambridge. Writer, Ceylon civil service, 1869; police magistrate, Point Pedro and Chs. kachcheri, July, 1871; commissioner of requests, Colombo, 1st Sept., 1879; district judge, Matara, 1st Jan., 1885; fiscal for central province and assistant to government agent, Trincomalee, 1891; dist. judge, Batticaloa, Dec., 1895. NEWCASTLE (Australia), BISHOP OF.—Founded, 1847, RIGHT REV. JOSIAH B. PEASE, D.D., educated at Chesterfield Grammar School and St. John's College, Cambridge, of which he became fellow and tutor; took a first class in the moral sciences tripos, 1864; vicar of Newcastle, 1874-80; consecrated, 1880.

NEWEY, JOSEPH.—M. Inst. C.E.; engineer, staff of chief insp., Cape Town, Nov., 1872; in charge of Buffalo Committee's, and Great K bridges, and reconstruction of all eastern province bridges destroyed by 1874 flood; special service in military engineering works during Gaika and Basuto wars and Tembu rebellion; in charge of Aliwal North and Kraai River bridges and other works and surveys, Nov., 1877; engineer-in-chief, Cape Town, 1880. sistant, Cape Town, Sept., 1881; district inspector, 1882; chief inspector, June, 1893.

NEWLAND, HARRY OSMAN.—Asst. supt. police, Singapore, Dec., 1882; ag. supt. of police, Singapore, April, 1894, to Apl., 1895.

raw_text: NEWMAN, GEORGE HENRY.—J.P., Queensland, 1877; trustee in insolvency, 1878; curator of estate estates and in insanity, 1885; commanded rs of engineers, 1876-88; is a lt.-col., Queensland defence forces.

medical officer No. 2 district, St. Vincent, 1872 to 90

raw_text: NEWSAM, WILLIAM FORBES, M.D.—Superintendent of lunatic, pauper, and leper asylum, and voter, St. George's, Grenada, 1868 to 1871; medical officer No. 2 district, St. Vincent, 1872 to 90; acting colonial surgeon, 1879; appointed to . 1 district (Kingstown), 1880; acting colonial surgeon, 1881, and again 1886; col. surgeon, ne, 1887; is a J.P. and chairman medical rd.

events.text_span: Assistant colonial secretary of Mauritius, May, 1859
lieut.-governor and colonial secretary, Jamaica, Nov., 1877

raw_text: NEWTON, SIR EDWARD, K.C.M.G. (1887), M.G. (1875).—Assistant colonial secretary of Mauritius, May, 1859; acting auditor-general, pt., 1863; auditor-general, April, 1866; colonial secretary, 1868; lieut.-governor and colonial secretary, Jamaica, Nov., 1877; on several occasions ministered the governments of Mauritius and Jamaica; retired, 1883.

NEWTON, GEORGE.—Attorney-at-law in Mauritius, in 1873; acted as secretary to two committees of attorneys, to report on the tariff of attorneys' fees, and to make observations on a draft ordinance judicial sales (1880-81); was placed in charge of the arrangements for bringing into operation the Seychelles Mortgage Act, 1882; conservator of mortgages, Seychelles, 1882; legal adviser to the Seychelles Government, 1883; accountant in bankruptcy, Mauritius, 1884; is also official liquidator under the companies winding up order; was member of commission to inquire into the working of the bankruptcy laws, Mauritius, 1885-6; author of "Treatise of the Bankruptcy Law." Official assignee in insolvency, Sept., 1896. NEW ZEALAND (Auckland), 2nd BISHOP of.—RIGHT REV. W. G. COWIE, D.D. Consecrated June, 69; was chaplain of the 3rd division of Sir Colin Campbell's army at the capture of Lucknow March, 1858; was present at the actions of Coorae, Rooyah, Aliginje, and Bareilly, with Sir Robert Napier's column in April and May of the same year; was chaplain to the Viceroy of India's camp the autumn of 1863; to Sir John Garrock's column against the Afghan tribes, Nov. and Dec., 63, and present at the storming of Laloo and the fight at Chumlah. Indian and Afghan medals, with clasps for Lucknow and Umbeyla.

events.position: Appointed, after a competitive examination, to be a clerk of the lower (now second) division of the civil service, and assigned to the colonial office
events.text_span: Appointed, after a competitive examination, to be a clerk of the lower (now second) division of the civil service, and assigned to the colonial office, 1st Sept., 1879

raw_text: NIBLETT, CHARLES HERBERT.—Appointed, after a competitive examination, to be a clerk of the lower (now second) division of the civil service, and assigned to the colonial office, 1st Sept., 1879; promoted to higher grade 1st April, 1891.

NICHOLLS, HENRY ALFRED ALFORD, C.M.G. (1896), M.D., C.M., Abdn.—M.R.C.S., Eng.; F.I.S.; C.M.Z.S.; corresponding member New York Academy of Sciences and Jamaica Inst.; medical superintendent Dominica Yaws Hospitals, 1877; surgeon, Roseau Infirmary, 1879; medical officer public institutions, 1880; chairman Poor Law Board, 1885; crown nominee in the legislative assembly of Dominica, 1875-7; local commissioner, Colonial and Indian Exhibition, 1886; ag. curator, Dominica Botanic Station, May to Oct., 1890; gained prize of £100 for best text book on Tropical Agriculture offered by Govt. of Jamaica; special commissioner to enquire into prevalence of Yaws in West Indies, 1891, for report on which received thanks of S. of S., 1894.

NICHOLSON, SIR CHARLES, 1st BART. (Creat. 1859), KNT. BACHEL. 1852, D.C.L.—Emigrated to Australia in 1834, and practised there as a physician; was elected a member of the first legislative council of New South Wales in 1843, and was three times chosen for the office of speaker (1845 to 1856); is provost of the university of Sydney.

events.position: Member faculty of advocates, Scot., and called to the bar
events.text_span: Member faculty of advocates, Scot., and called to the bar, July, 1882

raw_text: NICOLL, WM., M.A. (1879), LL.B. and Forensic Prizeman, Edin., 1882.—Member faculty of advocates, Scot., and called to the bar, July, 1882; asst. prof., civil law, Edin., 1883; stip. magistrate, Br. Guiana, Ap., 1888; acg. chief justice Br. Honduras, 1891-2; ag. puisne judge, Br. Guiana, 1893-4; ag. solr.-gen. 1894.

events.position: British delegate of Evkaf, in addition to his other duties
events.text_span: British delegate of Evkaf, in addition to his other duties, June, 1886

raw_text: NICOLLE, H. C.—Assistant auditor, Cyprus, 17th Feb., 1880; auditor-general 1883; British delegate of Evkaf, in addition to his other duties, June, 1886; local auditor, Hong Kong, Jan., 1890.

events.text_span: Commissioner of public works, Victoria, Feb., 1886-90.

raw_text: NIMMO, JOHN.—Commissioner of public works, Victoria, Feb., 1886-90.

NOBLE, JOHN, C.M.G. (1895).—Clerk of house of assembly, Cape, elected May, 1865; confirmed by H.M., Feb., 1866; is a J.P. for the colony; was sec. to the comn. of 1880-82, appointed to report upon native laws and customs; and to the conference of delegates from the O. F. State, Cape and Natal, at Cape Town, in 1888, for establishment of a S. African customs union; author of "Descriptive Handbook, Cape Colony" (1875); "Official Handbook, Cape and South Africa" (1878); "South Africa Past and Present"; "a Short History of the European Settlements at the Cape"; and edited the Official Handbook of the Cape for the Indian and Colonial Exhibition, 1886.

events.position: accountant special pub. works and rd. loan bd
events.text_span: accountant special pub. works and rd. loan bd. Sept., 1890.

raw_text: NOCK, W. C.—Clerk public works department, Trinidad, 1881; road accountant, Sept., 1883; storekeeper, May, 1886; accountant special pub. works and rd. loan bd. Sept., 1890.

events.position: called to the bar, Inner Temple
events.text_span: called to the bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1877

raw_text: NOEL, ARTHUR BAPTIST.—Eldest son of Judge Noel, Victoria; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1877; acted for some time as a Crown prosecutor in Queensland; district court judge, Queensland, 1883

NORMAN, GEORGE W.—Educated at the high school and college of Edinburgh; landing waiter, Antigua, 1854; lieut. of fire brigade, 1855; harbour master, June, 1856; adj., Antigua yeomanry cavalry, 1859; in 1865 accompanied Sir Stephen John Hill to Dominica, as confidential secretary; acting provost marshal, Oct., 1865, to May, 1866; immigration agent, 1867, also notary public; acting auditor general, 1869, of the Leeward Islands; on several occasions acting excise officer; inspector of weights and measures, and secretary to the Board of Health, July, 1869, to Feb., 1870; commissioner of water works and fire brigade, and escheator general, in 1869; in 1872 commissioned to examine the warehouses, and to report on the working of the treasury and revenue departments of Antigua; in June, 1873, J.P. and member of the Board of Health; chairman of this board, May, 1877; captain of forts and fortifications, and in charge of signal stations, June, 1873; acting resident magistrate of St. John's, and puisne justice of Antigua, Feb. to Mar., 1873; visiting justice of the gaol, Mar., 1873; in July, 1874, nominated member of the legislative council of Antigua; acting surveyor of public works, 1876; postmaster general, Mar., 1876; harbour master, Trinidad, Oct., 1877, and J.P., county of St. George, Jan., 1878; is also registrar of births and deaths for the waters of the colony, coroner for the Gulf, and secretary to the Quarantine Board.

NORMAN, GEO. WM., jun.—Clerk, regr.'s office, Antigua, May, 1871; chief clerk, marshal's office, Dec., 1874; ditto regr.'s office, Oct., 1877; ag. prov. marshal and regr. in 1878 and 1880; marshal V. A. Ct., Aug., 1870; secy. to law library, supreme court, 1878-80; chief clerk G.P.O. Trinidad, April, 1880; surgeon-gen.'s dept. May, 1865; ag. warden of St. Ann's and Diego Martin ward union, July, 1888, to Jan., 1889; supervisor for the colony, Feb., 1889; 2nd lieut., Trinidad rifle volunteers, Sept., 1890.

NORMAN, GENERAL SIR HENRY WYLIE, C.B. (1859), K.C.B. (1873), G.C.B. (1887), G.C.M.G. (1887), C.I.E. (1878).—Joined the Bengal Army in 1844; served as adjutant to the 31st native infantry throughout the Punjab campaign in 1848-49, including the passage of the Chenab, action of Sordoolapore, battles of Chilianwalla and Goojerat and pursuit of the Sikhs and Afghans; as brigade major or assistant adjutant-general, engaged in numerous affairs and expeditions on the Peshawur frontier in the years 1850-51-52-53-54; in 1855 in the Sonthal campaign in command of a detachment; in the mutiny campaigns of 1857-58-59, as assistant adjutant-general or deputy adjutant-general, and the greater part of the time as adjutant-general to the army in the field; served throughout the siege of Delhi; in all the actions under Greathed and Grant from Delhi to Lucknow; relief of Lucknow; operations at Cawnpore; action at Khodagunge, and re-occupation of Futteghur; siege and capture of Lucknow, March, 1858; campaign in Rohilcund (wounded at the action of Bareilly); campaign in Oude, cold season of 1858-59, including several actions; in 1860, assistant military secretary at the Horse Guards; in 1862, military secretary to the Government of India; in 1870, member of the viceroy's council, and in 1878, member of the Council of India in London; A.D.C. to the Queen from 1863 to 1869; in 1883, governor of Jamaica; governor, Queensland, 1889; viceroy of India, but did not take up appointment, 1893; retired, 1895; agent-general for Queensland, Nov., 1896; memb. of royal commission to enquire into condition of West Indies, Dec., 1896.

NORONHA, HENRIQUE LOURENÇO.—Member of the government board of examiners for the Hong-Kong civil service from 1878 to Sept., 1879; superintendent government printing office, Singapore, Sept., 1879; compiled the "Straits Civil Service List" for 1883 and 1884.

NORQUAY, HON. J.—Provincial treasurer and premier, province of Manitoba Canada, June, 1878; railway commissioner and premier until 1887. NORTH. M.K.A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. at Marlborough and Freiburg (Germany); pupil to Ed. Easton & Co., Westminster; employed on Brighton, Hastings, Herts, Essex, and other waterworks, and on drainage wks. in Norfolk and Lincolnshire; engr. Sta. Fé and Cordoba Ct. Sths. Rly. Argentine, 1888; ch. engr. for contractors' line extension, Central Argentine Rly., Rosario to Peirano, 1889-91; asst. col. civ. engr., B. Aires, 1892; ag. col. civ. engr. and memb. Ct. of Poly. 1893.

NORTHCOTE, ERNEST AUGUSTUS.—Ed. at Westminster, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; I.L.B. 1873; called to the bar, Middle Temple, in 1877; Oxford circuit and the Worcester and U'sk Sessions, stipendiary magistrate, Br. Guiana, 1882; sh. of Essequibo, 1884; acting puisne judge, Apr. 1884, to Sept., 1885; puisne judge, Jamaica, 1891; ag. chief justice, June, 1892, Sept., 1893, to Jan., 1894, and Aug., 1894, to Apl., 1895, and on subsequent occasions.

NORTON, 1ST BARON (United Kingdom), created 1878, RIGHT HON. SIR CHARLES BOWYER AMLEY, K.C.M.G. (1869).—Educated at Christ Church, Oxford, B.A., 1838; was president of the board of health and vice-president of the board of education from March, 1858, till June, 1859; M.P. for Staffordshire North, for which he was first returned in 1866, until Dec., 1868; president of the Royal Commission on the sanitary laws, 1868; president of the Board of Trade, 1874 to 1878.

events.position: clerk of federal council of Australasia, first session
events.text_span: clerk of federal council of Australasia, first session, 1886

raw_text: NOWELL, EDWIN CRADOCK.—Clerk, commissariat department, Tasmania, 1852; clerk to governor, 1857; to executive and legislative council, 1864; government statistician, 1867 to 1882; clerk of federal council of Australasia, first session, 1886, and second session, 1888; is author of a parliamentary handbook.

events.position: assistant to government agent, eastern province
events.text_span: assistant to government agent, eastern province, 1883

events.position: asst. govt. agt. and dist. judge, Chislaw
events.text_span: asst. govt. agt. and dist. judge, Chislaw, Jan., 1888.

raw_text: NOYES, EDWARD THOMAS.—Writer, C. S., 1874; police magistrate, Kalpitiya and Puttalam, 1877; assistant to government agent, eastern province, 1883; asst. govt. agt. and dist. judge, Chislaw, Jan., 1888.
NUNES, W. G.—clerk in secretariat, Jan., 1875 to 1878, clerk, internal revenue, 1880, clerk of parochial boards, 1882; inspector of po. St. Ann's, 1886.
OBAFENI, EDWIN HARRISON.—Messr. P.O., Lagos, 1869; asst. ldg. waiter, customs, 1872; asst. examing. offir., 1876; second ch. 1891.

events.position: wrote Ceylon service, attached to colonial secretary's office
events.text_span: wrote Ceylon service, attached to colonial secretary's office, 1867
acting second assistant colonial secretary, Feb., 1870

raw_text: O'BRIEN SIR GEORGE THOMAS MICHAEL, K.C.M.G. (1894), C.M.G. (1889).—Educated at Westminster and Trin. Coll., Camb.; wrote Ceylon service, attached to colonial secretary's office, 1867; additional police magistrate, Kurnegalla, June, 1867; police magistrate, Harrispar, Jan., 1869; acting second assistant colonial secretary, Feb., 1870; acting police magistrate, Colombo, Oct., 1870; assistant government agent, &c., 1871; second assistant colonial secretary, 1874; principal assistant, Sept., 1876; treasurer, Ar., 1886; acted several times as col. secy.; and lt. gen., 1890; col. secy., Cyprus, 1891; ditto, Hong Kong, 1892-5; retired, 1895; gov. Fiji, 1897.

O'BRIEN, H. ARTHUR.—Cadet, Straits Settlements, 1875; passed his final examination, 2 Nov., 1878; attached to office of Lieut.-Gov., Penang, Mar., 1879; assistant magistrate for Penang, Province Wellesley, 1879; commissioner and master te, Malacca, Jan., 1883; acting resident, Sungei Ujong, May, 1884, to June, 1885; acting resident and magistrate, Malacca, July to Sept., 1885; acting magistrate, Singapore, April, 1886; postmaster, Str. Settlements, 1888; ag. col. treasurer, 1891; audr.-gen. S.S., May to Nov., 1892, and Sep., 1893, to Sep., 1894; 1st mag., Penang, Apr., 1895; died 1897.

BRIEN, H. L.—Ed. Queen's Royal College, Maid, and Royal Naval School, New Cross, England; 3rd clerk, registrar-general's office, Maid. April, 1881; 2nd clerk to the assistant director of public works, July, 1881; extra clerk, Western Division, Aug., 1881, to July, 1882; acting same period as clerk to the general superintendent of railways; first clerk to assistant director of public works in July, 1882; and transferred to financial branch, Oct., 1883; 2nd clerk, registrar-general's office, Sept., 1884; act. chief clerk, Mar., April, 1888; chief clk., regr.-gen. office, Oct., ag. dep. regr.-gen., March and April, and to Nov., 1892, and Jan. to Apr., 1893; t., Trinidad Light Infantry Volrs., Feb.,

BRIEN, LIEUT.-COL. SIR J. TERENCE N., G. (1879, K.C.M.G. (1887).—Ensign 67th regt., 11th Sept., 1847; transferred to 70th foot, 1848; lieutenant 5th May, 1850; captain 5thers, 2nd Feb., 1858; transferred to 20th regt., 12th Feb. 1858; brevet-major, 26th April, major, unattached, 19th May, 1868; brevet-colonel, 3rd June, 1870; embarked with his regt. for India in 1849, in which country he d in the native languages, and also as a eyor and a civil engineer; became assistant the revenue survey, from which he was transferred to the public works, in which he rose executive engineer; served during the whole of Indian mutiny; was, in 1855, at Peshawur, in the Ensofaize expedition (medal and 5); served throughout the Oudh campaign 1858-59, as deputy-assistant quartermaster-general to a column in the field; present at the capture of Rampore Kussia; took part in operations under Lord Clyde, leading to surrender of Amcathie, evacuation of Shunkur, and flight of Beni Madhoo; proceeded with column under Brigadier Taylor, C.B., and Sir C. Grant to Fyzabad; present at the crossing the Gosra Affairs of Muchleegawn and Kundana, and pursuit of the rebels into Nepaul (mentioned and thanked in despatches, medal and lt.-major); in 1860 proceeded to Ceylon as assistant military secretary; appointed to the staff of the Bengal army as brigade-major, for district, 1863; inspector-general of police, Ceylon, May, 1867; poor-law commissioner and chair of the Orphan Asylum, 1870; governor of Heligoland, 1881; of Newfoundland, 1888; retired on pension, 1895.
CARROLL, M.—Colonial surgeon, district 3, Vincent, 1st May, 1879.
CONNOR, C. Y., C.E.—Under secretary, public works department, New Zealand; marine engineer, 1890; engineer-in-chief, W. Australia,

CONNOR, CAPT. JOHN THOMAS.—Clerk toer commanding No. 5 Company, F.A.M.P., at William's Town, 27th April, 1867; served British Basutoland in 1868, under Colonel Ker, acting as postmaster, collector of revenue, in addition to his regimental duties; sub-inspector of F.A.M.P., July, 1870; commanded 3 Troop at the Diamond Fields, Nov., 1871, to 1873; J.P. for that territory, Sept., 1872; commanded No. 3 and subsequently No. 2 Troop, June, 1873, to Aug., 1875; acted as magistrate in Tambockieland, Aug., 1875; commanded F.A.M.P. in East Griqualand, July, 1876, to Mar., 1880, and served during the Griqua rebellion in 1878; also at Moirosis mountain in 1879, and commanded the whole field force after the taking of that stronghold, until the disbandment of the auxiliary forces; Kaffir war medal and clasp; inspector, F.A.M.P. July, 1877; captain C.M.R., Aug., 1878; R.M., Bonvanaland, Mar., 1880; ditto, Umzimkulu district, Nov., 1881; despatched by the Cape government on three special missions to the Pondo chief Umqikela, between Sept., 1884, and April, 1885, all of which were successful, and elicited the express thanks of the Cape government; R.M., Tsolo, East Griqualand, Sept., 1886; now R.M., Nqwanakwe, Transkei.

events.text_span: Crown solicitor and administrator-general, Trinidad, 1st May, 1869.

raw_text: O'CONNOR, LEON D.—Crown solicitor and administrator-general, Trinidad, 1st May, 1869.

O'CONNOR, OWEN L.—Inspector of Police, Mauritius, Oct., 1862; poor law guardian, district of Moka, 1866; was hon. secretary of the "Moka special fund committee" for the relief of the sick poor, during the fever epidemic of 1867, and was presented with silver plate by inhabitants for his services; poor law guardian, district of Flacq, Jan., 1872; ditto ditto, Grand Port, Jan., 1874; sanitary guardian, Savanna, June, 1874; poor law guardian, Grand Port, Jan., 1875; acting inspector of immigrants, 1877; inspector of immigrants, Feb., 1883; member of district prison committee, 1868-76, and of local board of health, 1868-74; director, widow and orphans' fund, Jan., 1885; acting protector of immigrants and member of council of education, 1889; chairman, Rodriguez Inquiry Comm., 1889; acting protector of immigrants, May, 1891

events.position: Minister of justice in Dibbs' ministry, N.S. Wales
events.text_span: Minister of justice in Dibbs' ministry, N.S. Wales, 1891-94.

raw_text: O'CONNOR, THE HON. RICHARD E., M.A.—Minister of justice in Dibbs' ministry, N.S. Wales, 1891-94.

events.position: in Melbourne, secy. of Royal Commission on Federation of Australian Colonies in
events.text_span: in Melbourne, secy. of Royal Commission on Federation of Australian Colonies in 1870

raw_text: O'DONOVAN, DENIS, C.M.G. (1893), F.R.S.I., F.R.G.S., &c.—Ed. partly in Ireland, partly in Paris; for some time on the French press, and professor in one of the colleges of the university of France; in Melbourne, secy. of Royal Commission on Federation of Australian Colonies in 1870; parliamentary librarian of Queensland, 1874; is author of "Memories of Rome," and other literary works, and has produced an analytical and classified index of the library over which he presides, which has attracted much attention, not only in England and America, but on the continent of Europe; is a fellow of the Incorporated Society of Authors (England), a member of the Society of Art (England), a member of the Library Association of the United Kingdom; fellow of the Society of Literature (England); and hon. member of the Société d'Anthropologie of Paris; also a corresponding member (causé honoris) of the Sociétés de Géographie Commerciale of Paris and Havre.

events.position: Member Newfoundland executive council and receiver-general
events.text_span: Member Newfoundland executive council and receiver-general, 1889-93

raw_text: ODWYER, RICHARD H.—Member Newfoundland executive council and receiver-general, 1889-93; ag. commissioner of poor, 1893.

OFFORD, JAMES ALBERT, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—Surgical scholarship, Lond. hosp., 1889; late suptdt. Forston Asylum, and house-surgeon, surgical asst., and asst. in dept. of skin diseases, Lond. hosp., and resdt. med. offr., Bradford children's hosp.; asst. govt. med. offr., Fiji, 1894.

raw_text: O'HALLORAN, JOSEPH CLANFERGAEIL.—First clerk and cashier, crown lands department, S. Australia; 2nd clerk to the protector of immigrants; 3rd accountant, surveyor-general's department, 1865-8; inspector of constabulary, Mauritius, 1st Sept., 1870; poor law guardian of Flacq district in 1874, and of Plaines Wilhems district in 1875; twice received the thanks of the government for services rendered while in the police; police magistrate, Rodrigues, 1879; civil commissioner, 1882; resident justice and collector, Governor's Harbour, Bahamas, March, 1892; ditto, Harbour Island, July, 1893; ditto, Inagua, Sept., 1895; J.P. and coroner, Tobago, Nov., 1896.

assistant secretary, royal colonial institute, June, 1881

raw_text: O'HALLORAN, JOS. SYLVESTER, C M.G. (1895).—Clerk in audit office, South Australia, 1859; clerk of executive council, and clerk to the court of appeals, 1869; acted also as private secretary to the Right Hon. Sir Jas. Fergusson, Bt., Governor of South Australia from Feb. to May, 1870; assistant secretary, royal colonial institute, June, 1881, and secretary, Jan., 1884.
OKEDEN, W. E. P.—Formerly immigration agent, Brisbane; under colonial secretary, Queen's Island, 1890.

events.position: C. C. and resident magistrate, Willowmore division

raw_text: OKES, H.—C. C. and resident magistrate, Willowmore division, Cape Colony, 1st April, 1880.

OLIVIER, SYDNEY, B.A.—Educated at Tonbridge School; open exhibitioner, Corpus Christi College, Oxford; second class classical moderations, 1878; second class in final classical school, 1881; appointed after competitive examination, a clerk in the office of the Secretary of State for the Colonies, 17th April, 1882; honourably mentioned for Cobden prize essay, Oxford, 1883; ag. col. sec. Br. Honduras, Oct., 1890, to April, 1891; ag. auditor-gen., Leeward Is., Oct., 1895, to Feb., 1896; private secy. to the Earl of Selborne, 1st Sept., 1896; sec. to the royal commissn. appointed to inquire into condition of the West Indies, Dec., 1896.

O'LOGHLEN, THE HON. SIR BRYAN, BART.—Attorney-general, treasurer and premier of Victoria, Australia, 9th July, 1881; retired, 1883; M.L.A. for Port Fanny, 1889; member of Patterson ministry, 1893-4, as attorney-general.

O'MALLEY, SIR EDWARD LOUGHLIN, K.T. (1891).—Ed. at Trin. Coll. Cam., graduated B.A., 1864; M.A., 1868; called to the bar (Middle Temple), 1866; joined the Norfolk circuit; attorney-general of Jamaica, 1876; attorney-general, Hong Kong, Nov., 1879; chief justice, Straits, 1889; retired, 1892; chief justice, British Guiana, 1895.

events.position: Third assistant clerk, the registrar's office
events.text_span: Third assistant clerk, the registrar's office, British Guiana, 1868

events.position: first sworn clerk and notary public
events.text_span: first sworn clerk and notary public, 1882
actg. provost marshal, Br. Guiana, Oct., 1889

raw_text: O'MEARA, WILLIAM.—Third assistant clerk, the registrar's office, British Guiana, 1868; second assistant clerk, 1872; first ditto, 1872; sworn clerk and notary public, 1876; first sworn clerk and notary public, 1882; actg. provost marshal, Br. Guiana, Oct., 1889; confirmed, June, 1892.

OMMANNEY, SIR MONTAGU FREDERICK, C.M.G. (1882), K.C.M.G. (1890).—Late captain R.E.; ed. at Cheltenham coll., and R.M.A., Woolwich; entered the royal engineers, Jan., 1864; employed under the War Office and Admiralty, and at R.M.A., from 1867 to 1874; private secretary to the Earl of Carnarvon, secretary of state for the colonies, 4th March, 1874, to 1st January, 1877, when he was appointed a Crown Agent for the colonies; a commissioner for the colonial exhibition, 1887.

ONGLEY, PERCY ALGERNON.—Clerk, commrs. office, Nicosia, Cyprus, 1879, and sub. coll. customs and excise, 1882; ag. local commdt. of police, 1893. ONslow, EARLOP, G.C.M.G. (1889). K.C.M.G. (1887), WILLIAM HILLIER ONSLOW, VISCOUNT CRANLEY, 4TH EARL (created 1801).—Educated at Eton and Exeter College, Oxford; J.P. and deputy-lieutenant of Surrey, and High Steward of Guildford; parliamentary under-secretary of state for the colonies, 16th Feb., 1887; parliamentary secy. of bd. of trade, Feb., 1888; vice-president of the colonial conference, 1887; a delegate to sugar customs conference, 1887; gov., N. Zealand, 1889; resigned 1892; under sec. of state for India, 1895. ONSlow, SIR ALEXANDER CAMPBELL, K.BACH. (1895).—Ed. Westminster and Trin. Coll. Camb., B.A. in 1864; called to the bar, Lin. Temple, 1868, Home Circuit and Surrey Sessions; attorney-general British Honduras in 1878; Mr. Western Australia, in 1880; chief justice, July, 1883; administered the Government, 1885; again in 1891 and 1895.

O'REILLY, HON. C.—Minister of lands & works, Tasmania, Aug., 1876, to Aug., 1877; Dec., 1878, to Nov., 1882; stipendiary magistrate, Ringarooma, and commissioner of gold fields, N.E. district, Jan., 1883.

ORGILL, BERNARD CHURTON.—2nd class internal revenue department, Jamaica, Oct., 1869; retired, Oct., 1871; J.P. for the parish of Portland, June, 1872, and member of the parochial board of that parish, Jan., 1873, to Nov., 1875; first clerk, collector-general's office, Nov., 1875; clerk, Feb., 1878; supervisor of rev. offices, 1889.

ORMSBY, R. D.—Superintending officer, planters' wks. dept., Ceylon, 1866; acting provincial assistant, N.W.P., July, 1870, to May, 1871; provincial assistant, Ratnapura, 1876; ditto, Adamantapuria, 1877; financial and office assistant director, Aug., 1877; acting provincial assistant W.P., May, 1879; ditto, Uva, 1880; ditto, C.B., 1883-4; ag. director of P.W., May, 1887, to Nov., 1888.

O'RORE, THE HON. SIR GEORGE MATTHEW KNIGHT BACHELOR (1880).—Educated at Trin. College, Dublin, of which he is a graduate, emigrated to Victoria in 1852, and settled in New Zealand in 1854; elected to the House of Representatives in 1861, for the Town of Onehunga, and sat continuously for that until it was merged in the elective district of Manukau, for which he has been since 1882; elected speaker of the House of Representatives, July, 1879; and a dissolution taking place in Aug., was re-elected speaker in Sept. following; on both occasions was elected unanimously; previous to this he had been elected chairman of committees of the House of Representatives, viz., in 1871, 1875, and 1877; has been also speaker of the Auckland provincial council for twelve years, from 1865 to the abolition of the province of Auckland in 1876; entered the Waterhouse ministry in 1872, as secretary of crown lands, and minister for immigration Waterhouse resigning the premiership in 73, he continued in the ministry of Sir Julius Wel until 1874, when he resigned; is a member of the New Zealand bar, and in 1879-80 was chairman of the royal commission to inquire into the conditions of the university of New Zealand, and secondary schools of the colony in their relation to the university; was for the 3rd and 4th time unanimously elected speaker of the House of Representatives. May, 1882, and Aug., 1884, respectively; again in 1887; was elected as first chairman of the council of the Auckland University College founded in 1883, and still holds that office; he is also chairman of the board of governors of the Auckland grammar school.

H.—Temporary clerk to C.C., Griquatown Division, Cape Colony, 1872; clerk, post office, Barkly, and thence clerk to surveyor-general, 1873; clerk to surveyor-general, on the re-establishment, Griqualand West, 1874; secretary to commission on Griqualand claims (on special duty), Feb. to June, 1875; clerk to C.C., Barkly, 1876; re-appointed to surveyor-general's office, 1st Jan., 1876; secretary to special commissioner on native claims and locations (special duty), Feb. to Dec., 1877; served by special permission as a volunteer in the One Star Diamond Contingent, 1878; in the Intelligence Department, Griqualand West, May to Dec., 1878; South African war medal; clerk (special duty) in staff paymaster's office, March, 1879; inspector of native locations, mission of May, 1882; inspector of natives, Herert division, 1883.

RPEN, HENRY MARTYN HEBERT.—Captain commanding Colesberg native levies, from Jan., 1851 to Aug., 1853, during the Kaffir war also district adjutant of North Victoria from April, 1852 to March, 1853; held several other military appointments during the same period; Kaffir war medal; appointed to the customs department at Port Elizabeth, Dec., 1853; landing surveyor, August, 1857; sub-collector and surveyor and controller of H.M. customs and navigation laws, Cape Town, October, 1867; temporary charge customs department, April, 1868, assistant treasurer, accountant-general, and stamping commissioner, May, 1876; receiver-general and paymaster-general, 1881.

raw_text: OSBORN, SIR MELMOTH, K.C.M.G. (1893), C.M.G. (1882).—Clerk and native interpreter to resident magistrate, Inanda, Natal, 1854; clerk to R.M., Pietermaritzburg, 1855; J.P., clerk, and sub-accountant, Ladysmith, 1860; clerk to attorney-general, resident magistrate, and administrator of native law, Newcastle, 1865; captain commanding Newcastle mounted rifles; accompanied Sir T. Shepstone, Her Majesty's special commissioner in South Africa, as secretary to the mission, Dec., 1876; on the mission to the chief Mukukuni, March, 1877; secretary to government, Transvaal, July, 1877; British resident in Zululand, 1880; commissioner in the Zulu reserve, 1882; resident commissioner and chief magistrate, Zululand, 1887; retired, 1893.
OSLER, B.—Resident magistrate, Cathcart division, Cape Colony, Feb., 1881. OSLER. FEATHERSTON.—Puisne judge, Ontario, 1879; promoted to court of appeal, 1884.
OSMENT, DAVID S.—Colonial engineer, St. Vincent, 25th Feb., 1876.

O'SULLIVAN, ARTHUR WARREN SWETE, B.A., University scholar and medallist, Trin. Coll. Dublin.—Cadet, S.S., Oct., 1883; passed final exam. Mar., 1885; ag. dist. offr. S.W. dist., Penang, 1886, confirmed, Jan., 1888; district officer, Balik Pulau, Penang, 1888; actg. collr. land rev., Singapore, 1889; ag. postmr.-gen., S.S., Oct., 1889, to Jan., 1890; coll. ld. rev., Penang, June, 1890; ag. Indian immigr. agt. Ap. to Oct., 1892; ag. snr. dist. offr., P.W., May to Nov., 1895; insp. of prisons, S.S., Apl., 1896.

OTWAY, LOFTUS JOHN BRIDGEWATER.—Postmaster-gen. Grenada, 1874; was speaker house of assembly in 1875; on change of constitution official member; leg. ass. ag. pol. mag. west dist., Apr., 1876; pol. mag. and coroner. N. dist., Sept., 1876; ag. pol. mag., W. dist., July, 1884, confirmed as such, also as coroner and regr. births and deaths, Dec., 1884; ag. pol. mag., south dist., April, May, and Oct. to Dec., 1888; co-manager district savings Bk. of St. John's and St. Mark's in W. dist., Mar., 1889, in addition to magisterial duties.
OUIMET, G. D. C. L., Q. C.—Premier of Quebec, 1873; superintendent of public instruction 1876.

D.—5th clerk, customs, Cape Town, April, 1865; 4th, 1867; 3rd, 1870; Port Elizabeth, 4th examining officer, April, 1871; 3rd officer, 1874; 2nd ditto and measuring surveyor, 1879; East London, chief examining officer, 1880; measuring surveyor of shipping, 1882; assistant surveyor of customs, 1883; special officer of customs on staff of Sir B. On special service in Orange Free State in reference to customs union, June-Aug., 1889, Oct., 1890, and Sept., 1891; acted as collector of customs, Jan. to Mar., 1891. On special service in S. A. Republic, June-July, 1892.

PALMER, THE HON. SIR ARTHUR HUNTER, K.C.M.G. (1881).—Colonial secy.; secy. for works, Queensland; secretary for lands, Queensland, 1867-68; colonial secretary and premier, May, 1870, to Jan., 1874; colonial secretary and secretary for public instruction, Jan., 1879, to Dec., 1881, when appointed president, legislative council; administrator of the government, May to Nov., 1883, April to Dec., 1886, and Oct., 1888; was a member of legislative assembly for sixteen years.

PARKER, FREDERICK HARDYMAN, M.A. Edin., 1877, F.R.G.S.—Scholar in property law, Middle Temple, Trin., 1879; called to the bar., Trin., 1880; employed in Canada; admitted to the bar, British Honduras, 1881; deputy att.-gen. Corosal circuit, 1882; notary and standing conveyancing counsel, 1883; registrar of the supreme court, keeper of the records and provost marshal, Sept., 1884, also registrar Admty., lands, titles, a J.P., &c.; acted as dist. mag. and coroner, Belize, and as att.-gen., 1883-5-6-7, and 9; acting chief justice, 1886-7; presdt., dist. ct., Papho, Cyprus, 1890; ag. presdt., Limassol, 1892, and Larnaka, 1893, 4, and 5; presdt., Famagusta, 1892; passed in modern Greek, 1894.

PARKES, J. C. ERNEST.—Educated at the Catholic Institution, Freetown, and at Albert College, Suffolk; assist. clerk, Sherbro, Sierra Leone, 1882, third clerk, colonial secretariat, employed in the aborigines dept. from Mar. 1885, to Dec., 1888; superintendent of the same, 1889, now called sec. for native affairs; J.P.

PARR, LT.-COL. HENRY HALLAM, C.M.G. (1893).—Military secretary to Sir B. Frere at the Cape; served in the Egyptian expedition, 1882; ag. assistant adj. and quartermaster-general, 1884; A.D.C. to Her Majesty.
PARRY-OKEDEN, W. E.—Inspector of police, Queensland, 1870; police magistrate, immigration agent, Brisbane, 1886.
PARSONS, J. L.—Minister of education, S. Australia, 24th June, 1881, to 12th Mar., 1883; government resident northern territory, 1889; resigned, 1890; M.L.A. for northern territory, 1890.

events.text_span: Assistant inspector, education department, Trinidad, 1st Jan., 1882.

raw_text: PASEA, H. A.—Assistant inspector, education department, Trinidad, 1st Jan., 1882.

PASLEY, MAJOR-GENERAL CHARLES, late R.E.—Employed in 1850 at Bermuda in strengthening and improving the entrance to St. George's Harbour by the colonial government; in 1852 appointed colonial engineer of Victoria, and in 1854 was nominated a member of the legislative council; was despatched by Governor Sir Charles Hotham on a special mission to Ballarat during the gold rush in the same year; on the proclamation of the new constitution in 1855 he joined Mr. Haines' cabinet as commissioner of public works, and was returned to the assembly for the district of South Barwon; resigned with Mr. Haines in 1857, and on the construction of his cabinet in the same year as professional head of the department of works till 1860, when, on the outbreak of the New Zealand war, he volunteered to serve in that country on the staff of Major-General Pratt; was severely wounded at the capture of the Kaitote Pa in October; was mentioned in despatches, and promoted to brevet-major for his services there; returned to England in 1861, and was special commissioner in that country for the government of Victoria from 1864 to 1868; superintended, on behalf of the government, the equipment of the "Nelson," and the design, construction, armament, and despatch of the "Taranaki"; was director of works of the navy from 1873 to 1882; was acting agent-general for Victoria between the years 1880 and 1882.

PATERSON, HON. WILLIAM.—Born in Ontario, Out., Sept., 9, 1839; elected member town of Brantford, 1868; deputy reeve in 1869-70-71; mayor in 1872; he was first returned to Parliament at the general election in 1872, and was re-elected in 1874, 1878, 1882, 1887, and 1891; member for Ontario, and Comptroller of Cash in Mr. Laurier's govt., June, 1896.
PATRICK, ALFRED, C.M.G. (1882).—Born in Canada of English parents; late clerk, House of Commons of Canada; superannuated, 1881, after 54 years' service.

PAUL, G. W.—District court judge, Queens, 3rd Aug., 1874.

PAUNCEFOTE, THE RT. HON. SIR JOHN, G.C.B. (1892), G.C.M.G. (1885), K.C.B., K.C.M.G. (1879), C.B. (1880), (Kt. Bach., 1874).—Called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1852; attorney-general of Hong Kong, May, 1865; was ex officio member of executive and legislative councils; acting chief justice in 1869 and again in 1872; received the honour of knighthood, for his public services to the colony; chief justice of the Leeward Islands, 1873; assistant under-secretary of state for the colonies, 28th Sept., 1874, to 29th June, 1876; assistant under-secretary of state for foreign affairs, th June, 1876; permanent under-secretary of state for foreign affairs, 1882; minister at Washington, 1889; ambassador, 1893; privy councillor, 1894.

raw_text: PECK, CAPT. HERBERT W.—Served in Pultim's rifles, Zulu War (Medal); cadet, Sarawak service, Feb., 1884; acting commandant, Mar., 1884; capt. and commandant, April, 1885; principal inspector of police, July, 1886; acting Sarawak consul for Brunei and Labuan, June, 1888; administrator of Brooketon and vice-consul for Brunei and Labuan, June, 1889; resdt. 2nd class, 1st divis., July, 1892.

PEEL, RIGHT HONOURABLE SIR FREDERICK, Privy Councillor (1857), K.C.M.G. (1869). 2nd son of the late Sir Robert Peel, 2nd Bart.—Edu. Harrow and at Trin. Coll., Camb., 1st class in classics, 1845; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1849; under-secretary for the colonies, Nov., 1851, to March, 1852, and Dec., 1852, to Feb., 1855; under-secretary for war, 1855 to 1857; is a deputy-lieut. of Warwickshire; was M.P. for Leominster, Feb., 1849, to July, 1852, and for Bury, Lancashire, July, 1852, to April, 1857; re-elected for Bury, May, 1859, and appointed secretary to the treasury, 1860; railway commissioner, 1873.

raw_text: PEIRCE, T. ESTWICK.—Ed. Harrison's Coll., Barbados; clk. to col. secy., Barbados, 1880; 5th clk., col. secy.'s office, Jan., 1881; 4th clk., aud.-gen.'s office, Mar., 1881; excise offr., St. Lucia, 1882; special clk., aud.-gen.'s office, Barbados, 1884; officer of customs, 1887; supervisor of customs, G. Coast, Feb., 1892; ag. travelling and inspecting supervisor, May to Aug., 1892; cashier, treas., 1893; asst. treasurer, Feb., 1894; asst. comptroller of customs, G. Coast, Nov., 1895; ag. treas. and collr. of customs, Gambia, July, 1896; collr. of customs, Gambia, Sept., 1896.

PELLETIER, CHARLES ALPHONSE PANTALEON, C.M.G. (1878).—President of the executive committee for the Dominion of Canada at the Paris Exhibition, and late minister of agriculture and statistics, and commissioner of patents for the Dominion; member of the Privy Council for Canada.

raw_text: PELLETIER, PHILLIPPE.—Called to the bar, Quebec, 1875; member of the council of the bar of Montreal, and secretary, 1878; a fire commissioner for city of Montreal, 1879 to 1887; unsuccessfully contested St. John's in provincial elections, 1886, and Beauharnois in dominion elections, 1887; chief clerk to sec. of state, Canada, 1888.

raw_text: PEMBERTON, SHOLTO RAWLINS.—Ed. Downing Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1882.; called to the bar (Inner Temple), 1883; admitted to the bar, Leeward Islands, 1883; elected memb. leg. ass., Dominica, and of gen. leg. coun., Leewards, 1885 to 1895; memb. edn. commsn., 1886; of road bd., Dominica, 1889; nominated memb. leg. ass., Dominica, and memb. ex. coun., 1895; comsnr. of valuation, 1895; comsnr. of assessed taxes, 1896; J.P., 1896; frequently prosecutes for the crown, and presents indictments for the attorney-general.

events.text_span: marshal of admiralty ct., nautical assessor, port captain, and shipping master, 1879

raw_text: PENFOLD, HUGH MARCHANT.—Commander R.N.R.; J.P., Cape Colony; marshal of admiralty ct., nautical assessor, port captain, and shipping master, 1879; suptdt. of convict station, 1886; post capt., Table Bay, 1889.
PENNEFATHER, LT.-COL. E. G.—Inspr.-gen. of police, S.S., April, 1895.

PENNELL, CHARLES HENRY. — Clerk, ex-council, and confidential clerk to governor, Cape; entered civil service, Cape, 1868; and was specially selected in 1879 for employment in the governor's office; has acted as priv. sec. to the following offrs. when adming. the govt. of the Cape: Maj.-gen. the Hon. Sir H. Clifford, 1880; Gen. Sir Wm. Cameron, 1892, and Lieut.-gen. W.H. Goodenough, 1896; J.P. for Cape Town dist., 1886.

PENNEY, F. GORDON. — Ed. Trin. Coll., Glenalmond, and Edin. Univ., M.A., 1876; cadet, S.S. Sept., 1876; magistrate for Singapore and a J.P. for the colony, Sept., 1878; acting coroner, Dec., 1878; acting coll. land rev., Singapore, Jan., 1879; coll. land rev., Penang, 1880; acting 2nd mag., Penang, 1882; act. sup. education, Penang, 1882; 2nd mag. and comr., court of requests, Singapore, May, 1883; acting sen. mag. 1884-5; acting 2nd ass. col. sec., 1886 and 1888; is a licensing justice; 2nd asst. col. secy., 1888; mag., Malacca, Jan., 1890; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of coun., Apr., 1890; senr. dist. offr. P.W., June, 1890.

PENNYCUICK, CHARLES EDWARD DUCAT. — Ed. Cheltenham Coll.; writer, Ceylon civil service, Nov., 1866; acting landing surveyor, Galle, March, 1867; police magistrate, &c., Panvilla, Feb., 1868; assistant government agent, Batticaloa, March, 1871; acting district judge, &c., Badulla, May, 1871; district judge, Tangalle, 1873; acting police magistrate, Kandy, 1874; district judge, Badulla, 1874, acting assistant agent, Puttalam, 1876; ditto, Kegalla, 1876; acting district judge, Kurunegala, 1877; assistant government agent, Matara, Dec., 1883; ditto, Trincomalee, May, 1886, ditto, Puttalam, 1891; chairman municipal coun. and mayor, Colombo, Oct., 1892; postmr.-gen., June, 1896.

events.position: agent-general for the Colony in England
events.text_span: agent-general for the Colony in England, 1891 to 1896

events.position: called to the bar, Middle Temple
events.text_span: called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1878

events.position: was a Royal Commissioner for Chicago Exhibition
events.text_span: was a Royal Commissioner for Chicago Exhibition, 1893

raw_text: PERCEVAL, SIR WESTRY BROOK, K.C.M.G. (1894). — Was member for Christchurch City in legislative assembly of New Zealand; chairman of committees in 1890; agent-general for the Colony in England, 1891 to 1896; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1878; was a Royal Commissioner for Chicago Exhibition, 1893; agt.-gen. for Tasmania, 1896.
PEREGRINE, LAWSON N. — District commissioner, Gold Coast, 1888.
PERHAM, REV. J. — Missionary, S.P.G., in Sarawak, 1868-88; col. chaplain, Singapore, 1891; archdeacon, 1892.

PERKINS, H. INNES, F.R.G.S. — Clerk to director of surveys, Trinidad, 1880-81; 3rd assistant, crown surveyor, British Guiana, Dec., 1881; 2nd ditto, Sept., 1884; accompanied E.F. Im Thurn on an expedition to ascend Mount Roraima, in 1884-5, and furnished from actual survey a map of the mountain and the country in its vicinity to the Royal Geographical Society; is a J.P. author of papers on natural history, geography and mining industries of Br. Guiana; asst. crown surveyor, July, 1889; ag. crown surveyor, 1890, 92 and 93; is member of institution of mining and metallurgy, London.

events.text_span: Forester, wds. and for. dep., S. Australia, 1880

raw_text: PERRIN, GEO. SAMUEL, F.L.S., F.R.S. (Tasm.). — Forester, wds. and for. dep., S. Australia, 1880; chief forrester, Wirrabura, 1885; conservator of forests, Tasmania, 1886; ditto, Victoria, June, 1888.

PERRY, GEORGE McREDDIE, M.B., C.M., Glasgow and St. Mary's Hospital, London, F.R.G.S. — Asst. col. surg., Lagos, Oct., 1893; ag. col. surg., Feb.-Mar., 1894; ag. asst. col. secy., May, 1894; medical officer to expedition to Idanan Mountains; sen. asst. col. surg., Aug., 1895; ag. col. surg., 1895.

events.text_span: called to the bar (Inner Temple), 20th Jan., 1878

raw_text: PERRY, HAROLD ARTHUR. — Educated at Eton; Newcastle scholar, 1869; scholar of King's Coll., Camb., 1870; Sir William Browne's medallist, 1873; proxime accessit Craven scholar, 1875; 1st class classical tripos, 1874; fellow of King's Coll., 1875; called to the bar (Inner Temple), 20th Jan., 1878; commissioner of the high court for the Macclesfield savings bank enquiry, April, 1891; resident magistrate, Jamaica, 22nd Dec., 1891.

events.text_span: Matriculated at Magdalen Coll. Oxon., Oct., 1891

raw_text: PERRY, J.F., M.A. — Matriculated at Magdalen Coll. Oxon., Oct., 1891; 1st class, classical moderations, 1893; 2nd class Lit. Humane, 1895; Lothian Prize Essay, 1896; fellow of the Souls, 1896; apptd., after competitive exam., 2nd class clk., colonial office, Sept., 1896.
PETERS, JOSHUA HENRY. — Officer, Army gaol, 1853; superintendent of convicts, June, 1866; governor of the gaol, 1868; keeper of Gardiner Island, 1871.

events.position: Probation clerk, printing department, West Australia
events.text_span: Probation clerk, printing department, West Australia, Apr., 1876

raw_text: PETHER, CARLTON R. — Probation clerk, printing department, West Australia, Apr., 1876; sen. clerk, audit office, 1878; acting second clerk, May, 1880; confirmed, 1881; railway storekeeper, Feb., 1889; asst. govt. storekeeper, Jan., 1894; sen. storekeeper, Jan., 1895.

PETHER, RICHARD. — Clerk to magistrate, Perth, W. Australia, Oct., 1832, to Aug., 1855; temporary clerk treasury, 1865; clerk, G.P.O., 1865; 3rd class clerk, colonial secretary's office, 1865; general post office, 1867; again in col. secretary's office, 1867; govt. printer, 1870.

events.position: relieving C.C. and R.M. of the colony

raw_text: PETT, F. P. — Entered civil service, Ceylon Colony, 1858; C.C. and R.M., Carnarvon, 1860; Murraysburg, 1878; Prince Albert, 1883; relieving C.C. and R.M. of the colony, 1885; C.C. and R.M., Humansdorp, 1886.

events.position: successfully contested the Honiton division of Devon in
events.text_span: successfully contested the Honiton division of Devon in 1885

raw_text: PHEAR, SIR JOHN BUDD, KNT. BACH. (born 1877), M.A., F.G.S. — Educated at Pembroke Coll., Camb., and graduated 6th wrangler in 1859; afterwards fellow and assistant tutor of the College; called to the bar 1854; puisne judge, high court, Calcutta, 1864; retired 1876; chief justice of Ceylon, 1877; resigned 1879; J.P. and chairman of quarter sessions, Devonshire; successfully contested the Honiton division of Devon in 1885, and the Tavistock division in 1886; author of "Elementary Hydrostatics," "The Aryan Village," "International Trade," and other works.

PHILLIPS, GEO. B. — Writer, colonial secretary's office, Western Australia, 1851; third clerk, 1852; second clerk, 1856, and was also employed in the governor's private office; assistant district registrar of births, deaths, and marriages, 1856; acting chief clerk, 1865; chief clerk, May, 1866; also held the offices of registrar-general, registrar of titles, and registrar of deeds; assistant colonial secretary, 1873; acting colonial treasurer, 1875 to 1877, and 1878 to 1880, with a seat in the executive council; J.P., 1878; assistant colonial secretary, 1880; acting colonial secretary, 1882 and 1883; now commissioner of police.

PHILLIPS, H. DENBIGH. — Supernumerary clerk, col. sec. office, Br. Honduras, 1887; const. clerk, col. engineer's office, 1888; ag. rev. officer, July, 1888, to Feb., 1889; ag. clerk to atty.-gen., Mar., 1889, confirmed May; ag. clerk to reg. sup. ct., Mar., 1889; Nov. to Dec., 1890; June to Aug., 1891; and from March, 1892, confirmed 1894.
PHILLIPS, JOSEPH HENRY, C.M.G. (1892). — Unofficial member of executive council, Br. Honduras.

events.position: aff., G. Coast, and in charge of prisons
events.text_span: aff., G. Coast, and in charge of prisons, 1891

raw_text: PHILLIPS, J. R.—Ed. Uppingham and Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1886; solr. (unadmitted); aff., G. Coast, and in charge of prisons, 1891; Queen's advocate, 1892.

TUCKERING, WILLIAM ALEXANDER, C.M.G.—Resided in Formosa, 1863 to Dec., 1870, during which time received the thanks of the United States government for assisting in the negotiation of a treaty with the savages at the south of the island; also thanked by the British minister at Peking for securing Spanish subjects from slavery; Chinese interpreter, Straits Settlements, Dec., 1873; during 1874 was engaged in the native states, twice received the thanks of the secretary of state for the colonies for conducting the negotiations which terminated in the pacification of Borneo, and for services in Sungei Ujong; J. P. and resident magistrate, Singapore, Mar., 1874; protector of Chinese, April, 1877; retired, 1889.

events.position: Private secretary to Mr. (afterwards Sir) W. W. Cairns, lieut.-governor of British Honduras
events.text_span: Private secretary to Mr. (afterwards Sir) W. W. Cairns, lieut.-governor of British Honduras, 28th July, 1871
