events.text_span: chief clerk to the colonial secretary and to the legislative council of British Honduras, 28th Mar., 1872
acted as colonial secretary. to Oct., 1877
acting additional magistrate, western district, Oct., 1877

raw_text: CKWOAD, ROBERT WILLIAMS.—Private secretary to Mr. (afterwards Sir) W. W. Cairns, lieut.-governor of British Honduras, 28th July, 1871; chief clerk to the colonial secretary and to the legislative council of British Honduras, 28th Mar., 1872; acted as colonial secretary. to Oct., 1877; acting additional magistrate, western district, Oct., 1877; magistrate, northern district, April, 1878; district magistrate, Orange River, 1885; ditto, Corosal, Aug., 1886.
EREZ, G., M.B., C.M.—Medical officer, Gua, 8 Feb., 1882.

events.text_span: Clerk to Commissioner, Larnaca, Cyprus, Dec., 1884

raw_text: EKIDES, LUKE, G. Z.—Clerk to Commissioner, Larnaca, Cyprus, Dec., 1884; treasury, Larnaca, June, 1887; is an official member of administrative council.

ERRE, LOUIS PHILIP.—Clerk of the peace of the county of Caroni, Trinidad, Nov., 1860; of the peace of the county of Victoria, with the town of San Fernando, in 1869; clerk of the peace of Port of Spain and the western district of the county of St. George in June, 1869; acting stipendiary magistrate of the county of St. David, ward of Blanchisseuse in the county of St. George, and warden of the Toco Ward Union, in 1872; and in Jan., 1874, stipendiary magistrate of the county of St. David and ward of Blanchisseuse, warden of the Toco Ward Union, acting stipendiary magistrate of Arouca and Arima; stipendiary magistrate of the town of Arima and the ward of Arima, 1878.

events.text_span: Resident magistrate, Murraysville division, Cape Colony, 1883

events.position: master and registrar of eastern districts court
events.text_span: master and registrar of eastern districts court, Feb., 1880

raw_text: ERS, W. R.—Resident magistrate, Murraysville division, Cape Colony, 1883; master and registrar of eastern districts court, Feb., 1880; resident magistrate, Peddie, 14 Aug., 1884.

events.position: legal adviser to prime minister, Japan
events.text_span: legal adviser to prime minister, Japan, 1887

events.position: secy. to Attorney-General Sir C. Russell (now Lord Russell of Killowen) during Suez Sea arbitration
events.text_span: secy. to Attorney-General Sir C. Russell (now Lord Russell of Killowen) during Suez Sea arbitration, 1893-4

raw_text: GOGOTT, FRANK TAYLOR.—Barrister at law; employed by foreign office on special service in China; legal adviser to prime minister, Japan, 1887; secy. to Attorney-General Sir C. Russell (now Lord Russell of Killowen) during Suez Sea arbitration, 1893-4; procureur-general, Mauritius, 1894; ag. chief justice, 1895; author "Foreign Judgments," "Services out of the Jurisdiction," "Exterritoriality," "Law of Torts."

GUENIT, JAMES GEORGE.—Acting assistant master at grammar school, St. Christopher, 1865; clerk to secretary to government, 1866; clerk to board of health, 1867; clerk to the President as auditor, 1871; notary public, 1873; acting magistrate, Dominica, 1878; 1st clerk, registrar's office, Antigua, 1880; clerk to President and executive council, St. Kitts, 1882. Locher, Ernest G.—Ent. P. W. dept. N. Zealand, June 1874; record clk. rly. dept. Oct.; chief clk. ap. 1882; sec. to N. Zea. rly. surr. Jan., 1889. (C)
PILE, SIR GEO. CLARKE, Kt. Pach. (1892).—President of legislative council and member of executive council, Barbados.

PILE, THEODORE CONRADE.—Clk. col. sec. off., Trinidad, June, 1892; asst. sec. judicial enquiry comsn., 1892; priv. sec. to governor, Aug. to Dec., 1892; sec. to road loan bd., 1892-5; to Chicago exposn. comsn., 1893-4; 2nd asst. hrbr. mr., Aug., 1893, to Dec., 1894; ag. sec. to quarantine bd., June to Oct., 1894; asst. hrbr. mr., Jan., 1895.

events.text_span: Minister of lands and works, Tasmania, Oct., 1888-92.

raw_text: PILLINGER, THE HON. A. T.—Minister of lands and works, Tasmania, Oct., 1888-92.

PILOT, CHAS. LOUIS HENRY.—Copyist, coll. sec. off., Mauritius, 1882; addl. offr. of civil status, Plaines Wilhems, 1884; also dist. cashier, Curepipe from Feb. 1890. Dist. cashier, Savanne, Feb. 1894; called to the bar, Middle Temple, June, 1894.
PILOT, G.—District magistrate, Mauritius, 4th Sept., 1882.

events.text_span: sub-collector of customs, Simon's Town, Cape Colony, 1838

events.position: chairman of Table Bay harbour commission
events.text_span: chairman of Table Bay harbour commission, 1868

raw_text: PINNEY, F. B.—Clerk, legacy duty department, London, 1830; sub-collector of customs, Simon's Town, Cape Colony, 1838; resident magistrate, 1847; collector of customs, 1865; chairman of Table Bay harbour commission, 1868; retired, 1868; member of executive council.

PIRBRIGHT, THE RT. HON. LORD, 1st Baron, created 1895, BARON HENRY DE WORMS.—Born 1840; edu. King's Coll. Lond., fellow in 1863; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1863, S. Eastern Circuit; is magistrate and dep. lieut. for Middlesex; M.P. for Greenwich, 1880-5, and for Toxteth div. Liverpool since 1885; parly. sec. to board of trade 1885, and again 1886; parly. under secretary of state for the colonies, 1888 to 1892; president of sugar bounties comce., 1887-8; author of "The Earth and its Mechanism," 1863, "The Austro-Hungarian Empire," 1872, "England's Policy in the East," 1877, "Memoirs of Count Beust," 1887; privy councillor, 1888.

PISANI, PROFESSOR S. L., C.M.G. (1895), M.D.—Graduated Univ. Malta, 1850, Univ. Edinburgh, 1853, L.R.S.C.E., 1853; professor of anatomy and histology, Malta university, Mar., 1858; professor of anatomy and midwifery, Dec., 1859, of anatomy and surgery, Oct., 1869, of surgery, Oct., 1876; chief government medical officer, July, 1885.
PITTS, HON. JAMES S.—Appointed to leg. coun., Newfoundland, 1883; member of exec. council, 1888 and 1889, and again April to December, 1894.
PLAMONDON, M. A.—Puisne judge, superior court, Quebec, 1874.

PLAYFORD, THE HON. THOMAS.—Member house of assembly, South Australia; held office as commissioner of crown lands and immigration at various periods between 2nd Feb., 1876, and 24th June, 1881; commissioner of public works, 16th June, 1884, to 4th Feb., 1885; commissioner of crown lands and immigration, 4th Feb., 1885, to 16th June, 1885; treasurer and premier, 1887-9, and again 1890 to 1892; member of federation convention, 1891; member of Kingston ministry, 1893. Agent General for South Australia, 1894, and representative of colony at colonial conference, Ottawa, 1894.

events.position: Clk. and shorthand writer, col. sec. off.
events.text_span: Clk. and shorthand writer, col. sec. off., Natal, 1890

raw_text: PLOWMAN, G. T.—Clk. and shorthand writer, col. sec. off., Natal, 1890; 1st clk, 1891; chief clk., 1894; sec. to civ. ser. bd.

events.text_span: 3rd class clk., col. sec.'s office, Gibraltar, Feb., 1883
1st class clk., col. sec. office, Nov., 1893

events.position: 1st class clk. trans. and port dept., and regr. marines

raw_text: POGUE, ROBERT H.—3rd class clk., col. sec.'s office, Gibraltar, Feb., 1883 (after competitive exam.); transferred to police office, Jan., 1886; 3rd class clk., P O., 1889; 2nd class, Jan., 1891; 1st class clk., col. sec. office, Nov., 1893; 1st class clk. trans. and port dept., and regr. marines, July, 1895.

POIRIER, FRÉDÉRIC.—13th April, 1870, volunteer, procureur-general's office, Mauritius; 1870, copying clerk, ditto; 1872, acting clerk, ditto; 1873, assistant clerk, junior district magistracy; 1874, second clerk, procureur-general's department; chief clerk, 1886; in 1876, secretary to a committee to inquire as to the central gaol; in 1882, secretary to a committee of inquiry as to the working of the central civil status departments; curator of vacant estates, June, 1890.

events.position: president of legislative council on introduction of responsible government
events.text_span: president of legislative council on introduction of responsible government, 1893.

raw_text: POLKINGHORNE, THE HON. J. T.—Member Leg. Council, Natal, 1868 to 1879; of the executive council from 1872; colonial treasurer, 1879-93; is J.P.; president of legislative council on introduction of responsible government, 1893.

POPE, JOSEPH.—Entered Civil Service of Canada in 1887 as priv. sec. to Minister of Marine and Fisheries; priv. sec. to the late Sir John A. MacDonald, prime minister from 1882 till his death in 1891; apptd. asst. clerk of the Queen's Privy Council for Canada, 1889; under sec. of state and deputy regr.-gen. of Canada, 1896; is author of the Life of Sir John A. MacDonald.

events.text_span: Super. clerk, Crown Land Office, Gibraltar, Nov., 1862

ag. contracting officer, June to Oct., 1892

raw_text: PORRAL, ALBERT.—Super. clerk, Crown Land Office, Gibraltar, Nov., 1862; 2nd class clerk, Jan., 1875; 1st class clerk, Jan., 1883; secretary to commission to enquire into the organization of the police force, 1887; act. contracting officer in 1890; secretary to the Crown Land Board, with a seat at that board, Jan., 1891; ag. contracting officer, June to Oct., 1892; commissioner of Crown lands 1894.

PORRAL, JOHN.—Clerk, port office, Gibraltar. Aug. to Sept., 1874, and July, 1877, to May, 1878; writer in H.M.'s dockyard, Sept., 1874, to July, 1877; 3rd clerk, colonial sec.'s office, June, 1878; 2nd clerk, 1883; 1st class clerk, Jan., 1890; chief clerk, Dec., 1891.
PORTELLI, COL. A. M., of the Royal Malta Fencible Artillery.—C.M.G. (1882), for services in connection with the Egyptian expedition, 1882.

PORTELLI-CARBONE, ALFONSO, M.D. (Malta Univ., 1883).—L. M. (Rot. Hosp. Dub.), 1890; police physician for Zurrico, Safi, Crendi, and act. police physician for Micabiba and Chircop, and sanitary inspector for the 6th country district, Aug., 1884; district medical officer for Valetta, Sept., 1885; is a member of the British medical association (Mediterranean branch), 1888; fell. Brit. Gynaecol. soc., and memb. Internl. Med. Cong. Berlin, 1890, and Rome, 1894 (obst. sec.)

events.position: dist. mag. and coroner, W. dist
events.text_span: dist. mag. and coroner, W. dist., Sept., 1889

events.position: ag. dist. mag. and coroner, Orange Walk
events.text_span: ag. dist. mag. and coroner, Orange Walk, 1886

raw_text: PORTER, ALEX.—J.P., Br. Honduras, 1879; foreman, pub. wks., Dec., 1886; dist. mag. and coroner, W. dist., Sept., 1889; ditto, Stann Creek, July, 1890; ex-officio judge, civil jurisdiction et al., and dist. regr. births, deaths, marriages; ag. dist. mag. and coroner, Orange Walk, 1886; W. dist. 1887; and Toledo, 1888-9.

PORTER, SIR NEALE, K.C.M.G. (1894). C.M.G. (1888).—Capt. 1st Warwickshire reg. (Mil.); pres. mag. in charge of Anguilla, Mar., 1869, to May, 1871; ag. col. sec. of Antigua, May, 1871, to Oct., 1871; acting lieut.-governor Dominica, Oct., 1871, to Mar., 1872; acting president, April to June, 1872; president, Montserrat, June, 1872; acting president, Dominica, June to Nov., 1882; colonial secretary, Leeward Islands, April, 1883; administered government June, 1883, to Jan., 1884, July to Sept., 1884; and July to Dec., 1887; colonial secretary, Jamaica, 1887-95.

PORTER, W. HENRY.—Clerk, president's office, Dominica, Nov., 1874; treasury clerk and excise officer, Nevis, 1878; 1st landing waiter and quarantine officer, St. Kitts, May, 1881; acting treasurer, Dominica, Feb., 1884 to Oct., 1887; ag. mag. dist. F., Nov. 1887 to Feb. 1888. Treasurer, Dominica, July, 1890.
POTBURY, J. A.—St. John's Coll., Camb. B.A.; Math. Tripos, 1881, M.A., 1886; head master, Queen's Coll., B. Guiana, Oct. 1882; acted as principal, Apr., 1884, to Oct., 1885, and Apr. to Sept., 1888.

POTTS, MOSES A.—Ed. S. Leone Gram. School served in H.M. commissariat at S. Leone June, 1864 till Jan., 1866; acting 2nd clerk, treasury, Feb. 1865, to Jan., 1866; extra clerk, Jan., 1866; law and land-tax clerk, Aug., 1869; 2nd clerk, war, after a competitive examination, Sept., 1870; revenue master, 1871; chief clerk and cashier, 1877; acting auditor-general, Dec., 1886, to Feb., 1888; charity commissioner, 1890.
POWELL, COL. W.—Adjutant-general at Malta at headquarters, Canada.

events.text_span: Collector of customs, New South Wales, 1st Aug., 1881.

raw_text: POWELL, JAMES.—Collector of customs, New South Wales, 1st Aug., 1881.
POWER, CAPT. W. LE POER.—Local commandant of police, Cyprus, 1892.

events.position: called to the bar (Middle Temple) of England, in
events.text_span: called to the bar (Middle Temple) of England, in 1857

events.position: admitted a barrister of the supreme court in New Zealand, in
events.text_span: admitted a barrister of the supreme court in New Zealand, in December, 1862

raw_text: PRENDERGAST, SIR JAMES, KNT. BRT. (1881).—Is a graduate of the University of Cambridge; called to the bar (Middle Temple) of England, in 1857; admitted a barrister of the supreme court in New Zealand, in December, 1862; attor.-gen., 1865; ch. justice of New Zealand, 1875.

PRICE, FERDINANDO HAMLYN.—Educated at Rossall and Queen's Coll., Camb.; open scholar, 1875; writer to the government of Ceylon, Mar., 1878; attached to the Colombo Kachcheri, June, 1878, to the Kandy Kachcheri, Dec., 1878; extra office assistant to the government agent of the Central Province, Sept., 1879; acting assistant to government agent, Central Province, Oct., 1880; also at Nuwara Eliya, Nov., 1881; assumed duties at Trincomalee, Jan., 1882; private secretary to lieutenant-governor Mar., 1883; public office assistant to government agent, Central Province, June, 1881; assistant government agent, Negombo, Dec., 1883; assistant colonial treasurer and chairman, Colombo municipality, Jan., 1885; acting office assistant, C.P., Dec., 1885; private assistant government agent, Kegalla, May, 1886; office asst. to govt. agent, North Central Province, May, 1886; ag. chairman, municipal committee, mayor, Colombo, Sept., 1889; ag. asst. govt. agent, Kegalla, Dec., 1889; confirmed, Dec., 1891; chairman, municipal council and mayor Colombo, 1896.

events.text_span: 2nd class clerk, library and museum, Jamaica, Oct., 1873

raw_text: PRIEST, HENRY.—2nd class clerk, library and museum, Jamaica, Oct., 1873; librarian, institution of Jamaica, Apr., 1879; sec. to governors' institute, Oct., 1879.

events.position: ag. clk. and draftsman in lands dept
events.text_span: ag. clk. and draftsman in lands dept., 1868

events.position: priv. sec. and A.D.C. to admr
events.text_span: priv. sec. and A.D.C. to admr., Nov., 1870

events.position: priv. sec. and A.D.C. to admr

raw_text: PRINSEP, HENRY CHARLES.—Ed. Cheltenham Coll.; went to W. Australia, 1866; apptd. 2nd lieut., 1867; ag. clk. and draftsman in lands dept., 1868; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to admr., Nov., 1870; June, 1885; ch. draftsman, lands dept., 1890; clerk, 1892; under secy. for mines, 1894.

PROBYN, LESLIE.—Ed. at Charterhouse afterwards in France and Germany, called to bar, Middle Temple, 1884 Joint author of siction and Practice of the Mayor's Court," other law books, attorney-general, British Guiana, 1893.

DOCTOR, S. F., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.E., and —Educated University Edinburgh and St. Thomas' Hospital, London; appointed assistant medical officer, Grenada, June, 1878; house surgeon, Colonial Hospital, Grenada, Nov., 1879; resigned Sept., 1880; acting medical officer, Trinidad, June, 1881, to May, 1882, during yellow fever epidemic; assistant colonial surgeon and immigration medical officer, St. Lucia, Oct., 1882.
ROUT, W. T., M B., C.M., Edin.—Asst. poor law offr., Mauritius, 1885; asst. col. surg., Sierra Leone, 1888; col. surg., Gambia, 1893; ditto, Sierra Leone, 1894.
ROWSE, D. W.—Central district court judge, Newfoundland, 1869; author of a "History of Newfoundland," 1895.

RIOR, WM. BURGESS.—Member, provisl. N. neo assocn.; resd. E. Coast dist. Br. N. Borneo, 1881; prot. of coolies, E. Coast, 1884; mbr E. Coast Coun., 1883; Br. Con. Agt., 1887; resdt. and mag. Gold Field dist., Feb., 1887. CARTEY, R. E.—Outdoor offr. customs, Bra. 1881; 4th clk. customs and treas., 1883; clk. and off., 1885; 1st clk., 1887; audit clk. local auditor, Jan., 1889; has several times been local auditor.

UEBEC, 4TH BISHOP OF.—Dr. J. W. WILLIAMS, B.—Educated at Crewkerne school, and Pemb. Coll., Ox.; B.A., 1851, 3rd class classics; curate High Wycombe, Bucks, 1851; proceeded to MA to open grammar school in connection with MP College, Lennoxville; is a professor in college; consecrated 4th Bishop of Quebec L.
VENTRALL, THOMAS.—Mining engineer, Berley, Cape, July, 1889; inspr. of mines, 1891; appointed to fixed establishment, 1892.

ADFORD, HENRY WYAT.—Clerk assistant, Native assembly, Queensland; clerk of Native council, 1881. AE. JAMES MACLURE.—Admitted to the Samoa bar, 1877; notary public same year; 1878; resident justice, Crooked Island dist., Oct., 1878; assistant commissioner at Cock-a-harbour, Turks, and Caicos Islands, July, 1880; acting judge of the supreme court, and ex offio auditor of the Turks and Caicos Islands, 1882, to May, 1883, and Mar., 1884, to Oct., 1885; assistant commissioner, Grand Turk, 1885; auxiliary magistrate, Bahamas, Nov., 1887. Ag. -gen., July, 1893 to Feb. 1894; ag. chief justice, Feb. and Mar. 1894. AE. JOHN, M.A.—Educated Univ Aber.; M.A., 1832; law student from 1831 to 1839; arrived in New South Wales, 1839; town clerk of Sydney, 1853; a commissioner for the city of Sydney, 1854; secretary to the railway department, N.S.W., 1857; secretary for public works and commissioner railways, 1861; under secretary for public works, 1869; retired, Mar., 1888.

events.position: Civil commissioner and resident magistrate, Tulbagh division
events.text_span: Civil commissioner and resident magistrate, Tulbagh division, Cape, 1883.

raw_text: AINIER, G. G.—Civil commissioner and resident magistrate, Tulbagh division, Cape, 1883.

AMA-NATHAN, PONNAMBALAM, C.M.G. (1899).—Educated at the Academy, Colombo, and Sidney College, Madras; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; advocate, supreme court, Ceylon, 1876; edited several volumes of law reports, received the thanks of the judges and congratulations of the Government; member of the Native council, 1879; member of the law commission appointed to consider and report upon certain codes dealing with the laws of Ceylon, 1879; served also on the commission appointed at his instance to report upon the Thoroughfares Ordinance, 1881; agitated for, and obtained the introduction of Post Office Savings Bank in Ceylon, 1881-83; framed and carried through Ordinance No. 11 of 1882, to provide for the more speedy and effectual recovery of the possession of "small tenements" unlawfully held over; was one of the select committee of the legislative council appointed to report upon the means of retrenching the public expenditure of the island, 1882-83; a member of the commission to report upon the abuses alleged to exist in the working of the minor courts of the island, 1884; a member of the council of legal education, 1884; was instrumental in obtaining the passing of an ordinance to establish reformatories and industrial schools, 1886; a member of the Ceylon commission of the Colonial and Indian Exhibition, 1886; a member of the Central Irrigation Board, 1888; and of the Select Committee to report upon the incidence of the Grain Taxes, 1889; framed and passed through the legislative council a complete scheme of legal education for students seeking admission as advocates and proctors, 1889; and, together with the attorney-general, received the special acknowledgments of the Secretary of State for the part he had taken in the preparation of certain codes of law relating to the courts of the island and their procedure; member of the commission appointed to inquire into the extension of the railway to the northern parts of the island, 1890; author of papers on "The Ethnology of the Moors of Ceylon, &c.," read before the Ceylon branch of the Royal Asiatic Society, of the council of which he is a member. He is also a member of the committee of the Ceylon Museum; solicitor-general of Ceylon, 1892, ag attorney-general, 1894.
RANFORD, HENRY SAMUEL—Contract surveyor, Western Australia, 1872; employed on admiralty survey, 1875; staff-surveyor, 1884; senior ditto, 1886.

ag. clk. to aud. gen., Sept., 1894

raw_text: RANNIE, CLARENCE RUPERT.—Clk. to col. engr., Antigua, Mar., 1891; ag. clk. to aud. gen., Sept., 1894; 2nd clk. to aud. gen., Leeward Is., Jan., 1895.

RAPINET, CHARLES.—Entered the colonial service, October, 1853; was a clerk in the office of controller of charitable institutions, Malta; transferred to the chief secretary's office in 1869; chief clerk in audit office in 1877; acted as auditor-gen., May to Nov., 1878; and as aud. gen. and director of contracts, June to Oct., 1880, Oct. to Nov., 1881, May to Nov., 1882, and July to Dec., 1883; commissary of the Monte di Pieta and Savings Bank, 1885.

events.position: transferred to Grenada as 2nd clerk in governor's office on separation of Barbados from Windward Islands

ag. controller of customs, May to Sept., 1892

raw_text: ATTRAY, W. WALLACE M.—3rd clerk, governor's office, Windward Islands, 1883; transferred to Grenada as 2nd clerk in governor's office on separation of Barbados from Windward Islands, June, 1885; supervisor of customs, G. Coast, Nov., 1891; ag. controller of customs, May to Sept., 1892, and Feb. to May, 1894.

RAWSON, SIR RAWSON WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1875), C.B. (1858).—Educated at Eton; clerk in the board of trade, Jan., 1829; served as private secretary to the vice-president, Mr. C. Poulett Thomson, 1830-34; to the president, Mr. Alex. Baring, 1834; and to the vice-president, Mr. W. E. Gladstone, 1841-42; acted as secretary to the pilotage commission, 1834-36; civil secretary to the governor-general of Canada, Aug., 1842; treasurer, Mauritius, Jan., 1844; colonial secretary, Cape of Good Hope, May, 1854; governor, Bahamas, Nov., 1864; received, Nov., 1865, a dormant commission as lieutenant-governor of Jamaica; governor-in-chief of Windward Islands from 1868 till 1875; retired on pension.

events.text_span: Asst. offr., civil status, Mauritius, Jan., 1874
asst. clerk, dist. ct., Port Louis, July, 1875
ag. supdt. inland rev., May to Oct., 1895.

raw_text: RAWSTORNE, HENRY CLANCY.—Asst. offr., civil status, Mauritius, Jan., 1874; clerk, stipendiary court, Flacq, March, 1875; asst. clerk, dist. ct., Port Louis, July, 1875; joint clerk, ditto, Oct., 1876; inspr. of police force, May, 1877; inspr. incl. rev., March, 1880; and supdt. of distilleries, March, 1893; ag. supdt. inland rev., May to Oct., 1895.

events.text_span: Resident magistrate, Wodehouse division, Cape Colony, May, 1880.

raw_text: RAWSTORNE, L. G.—Resident magistrate, Wodehouse division, Cape Colony, May, 1880.

ag. puisne judge, Trinidad, Dec., 1892 to Sept., 1893

raw_text: RAYNER, THOMAS CROSSLEY.—Educated at Owens college; matriculated, London, 1878; barrister, mid. temple, 1882; district commissioner, Gold Coast Colony, 1887; acted as judge, 1890; magistrate, Trinidad, 1890; ag. puisne judge, June to Oct., 1891; chairman of commission of enquiry into registry of supreme court, Jan., 1892; ag. commr., Tobago, Sept. to Dec., 1892; ag. puisne judge, Trinidad, Dec., 1892 to Sept., 1893; puisne judge, Gold Coast, Jan., 1894; chief justice, Lagos, 1895.

raw_text: READ, EDWARD INSKIP, F.R.C.S.I.—Entered govt. service, Trinidad, Feb., 1882; ag. dist. med. officer, S. Naparima and town, and Port of San Fernando, 1882-3; asst. surg. col. hospital, Port of Spain, 1884; ag. surg. in charge San Fernando hospital, 1886; confirmed 1887; ag. health officer of shipping; inspector of immigrants and dist. med. officer, Port of Spain, 1891; in temporary charge of col. hospital, Port of Spain, April, 1892; confirmed Nov., 1892; clinical asst., Roy. Westminster Ophthal. Hosp., 1893.

raw_text: READ, HERBERT JAMES, B.A.—Brazenose Coll., Oxford; 1st class math. mods., and 1st class math. finals; clerk, higher div., War Office, Feb., 1887; 2nd class clerk, Colonial Office, Jan., 1889; asst. priv. secy. to Mr. Chamberlain, 1 Sep., 1895.

raw_text: READ, HORATIO.—Ensign, 40th regt., Mar., 1867; lieut., 1870; exchanged to 2nd W. I. regt., 1871; retired, 1873; private secretary to Sir James Longden, governor, Br. Guiana, 1875 to 1877; sub-agent of immigration, west coast district, British Guiana, 1877; stipendiary magistrate, 1884; is a J.P.; student, Middle Temple, Easter, 1885; ag. inspr. of prisons, June, 1886 to May, 1887, and Aug. to Oct., 1888.

READ, THE VEN. ARCHDN. J. H., D.D.—1st ARCHDEACON OF PRINCE EDWARD ISLAND, 1860—Ecclesiastical commissary, 1858; formerly of St John's College, Cambridge, and B.D., 1852; D.D., 1857, of King's College, Frederickton, New Brunswick; rector of Milton, 1865.
READ, WILLIAM HENRY MACLEOD, C.M.G. (1886).—M.L.C., Straits Settlements, 1867-85.
REAL, C.—2nd puisne judge, Queensland, 1890.

REDWAR, H. W. HAYES.—Called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Nov., 1881; dist. comssnr., G. Coast, Oct., 1887, at Axim, Nov., 1887; at Salt Pond and Anamaboe, April, 1888; at Accra, Oct. 1888; also dep. sheriff there; again at Saltpond and Anamaboe, May, 1889; ag. cr. prosecr., Cape Coast, June and July, 1889; ag. Queen's advocate, Jan. to July, 1891; ag. puisne judge, July to Dec., 1891, and Aug., 1892 to June, 1893, when confirmed.
REEVE, FREDERICK ST. AUBYN.—Clerk, post office, Barbados, 1873; 2nd clerk, 1875; resigned 1878; landing waiter, St. Lucia, 1883; colonial postmaster, St. Lucia, 1885.

events.position: acting curate of St. Michael's Cathedral, Barbados, and private secretary to Bishop Parry
events.text_span: acting curate of St. Michael's Cathedral, Barbados, and private secretary to Bishop Parry, 1872

events.position: assistant master of Harrison College, and curate of St. Michael's Cathedral
events.text_span: assistant master of Harrison College, and curate of St. Michael's Cathedral, 1877

raw_text: REECE, REV. J. E.—Educated at St. John's College, Cambridge; B.A. in 1868; M.A. in 1874; acting curate of St. Michael's Cathedral, Barbados, and private secretary to Bishop Parry, 1872; assistant master of Harrison College, and curate of St. Michael's Cathedral, 1877; curate of St. Bartholomew's, 1878; curate of St. Luke's, 1882; bishop's chaplain, 1884; inspector of schools, 1885.

REECE, J. RICHARD.—Educated at St. John's College, Cambridge; B.A. in 1870; called to the bar at the Inner Temple in 1871; J.P., Barbados, 1873; coroner for parishes of Christ Church and St. George, 1873; acting provost-marshal and serjeant-at-arms, 1874; acting police magistrate, St. Michael's parish in 1875; acting judge and assistant court of appeal, 1875; appointed to investigate the preliminary charges preferred against rioters in 1876; acting judge of the assistant court of appeal in 1877, 1878, and 1879; a commissioner of probates in 1877; acting solicitor-general, 1880; registrar of friendly and benevolent societies, 1880; acting judge of the assistant court of appeal in 1880-82; commissioner to report on the sea police magistrates' courts and petty debt court (Barbados) in Oct., 1882; acting attorney-general of Grenada, 1883; sole commissioner to report on a complaint made by the vestry of St. Michael's parish against the Bridgetown Water Work Company in Oct., 1883; judge of the assistant court of appeal, 1883; acting chief justice of St. Vincent, 1884 and 1885; local commissioner of the Inland Revenue Estates Court (St. Vincent) in Oct., 1885; resident magistrate, Jamaica, 1888.

events.position: Called to the bar, Middle Temple
events.text_span: Called to the bar, Middle Temple, 21st Nov., 1861

events.position: on the South Wales and Chester Circuit, was a leading barrister on that circuit from
events.text_span: on the South Wales and Chester Circuit, was a leading barrister on that circuit from 1865 to 1877

raw_text: REES, SIR JOSIAH, Kt. (1891), F.R.A.S.—Called to the bar, Middle Temple, 21st Nov., 1861; on the South Wales and Chester Circuit, was a leading barrister on that circuit from 1865 to 1877; justice, Bermuda, 1878; judge of the vice-admiralty court, and president of the council.

REEVE, HENRY.—Assoc. Mem. I.C.E.—Joined Victorian survey department, 1873; calet. N.S. Wales survey department, 1874; surveyor, 1878; surveyor, Fiji, survey department, May, 1880; surveyor, Jan., 1881; acting commissioner of works, July, 1884, to Feb., 1885; colonial engineer, St. Lucia, 1887; director of survey, Windward Islands, 1891; mem. leg. coun., St. Lucia, 1891; special member exec. coun., St. Lucia and St. Vincent, 1891; colonial engineer, Gambia, 1892; chief English commisnr., Anglo-French commisn., and memb. ex. coun., 1895-6.

REEVES, SIR WILLIAM CONRAD, Kt., Q.C.—Called to the bar at the Middle Temple, 6th June, 1863; acted as attorney-general of St. Vincent from May to December, 1867; member of the House of Assembly, Barbados, 1868; solicitor-general and also escheator-general, Barbados, Aug., 1874; acted as attorney-general, Aug., 1874, to 19th Feb., 1875; resigned ditto April, 1876; nominated member of the executive committee on passing of "The Executive Council Act, 1881;" appointed attorney-general, 8th Oct., 1882; ex officio member of the executive council from February, 1882; Queen's counsel, July, 1885; chief justice, Barbados, 1886; ex officio president, Windward Islands, court of appeal.

REIBEY, THE HON. T.—Speaker of House Assembly, Tasmania, member for Western district, member of the executive council, July 20th, 1875; was leader of the opposition, Mar. 13th, 1876; July 20th, 1876; and Aug., 1877, to Dec. 20th, 1878; premier and colonial secretary, July 20th, 1876, to Aug. 29th, 1877; colonial secretary, r. Crowther's ministry, Dec. 20th, 1878, to Oct. 1879; elected speaker, July, 1887.
REID, HON. G. H.—Minister of public instruction, N.W., 5th Jan., 1883, to 1st Mar., 1884; M.L.A. v. E. Sydney; leader of op., 1891; premier, 1894.

REID, IRVINE KEMPT.—M.R.C.M., 1884, M.D., D.P.H. (1st class honours in Practical Medicine), 1891; Aberdeen University, member of y. Med. Soc., Edin.; member of soc. of med. rs. of health; house surg. and secy., West Norfolk and Lynn Hosp., King's Lynn, 1886; gen. resident medical staff, colonial hospital, Georgetown, British Guiana, 1887-89; government medical officer, island of Wakenaam, Rio Essequebo, 1889; med. off., Penal Settlement, Massaruni; resdt. surgeon, pub. hosp., Bartica, 1892; med. . Mara district, 1893; ditto, Peter's Hall dict. and member medical board, 1894; med. . Cotton Tree dist., 1895.

events.position: Is a barrister of the same court of New Zealand, admitted in
events.text_span: Is a barrister of the same court of New Zealand, admitted in 1865

raw_text: REID, WALTER SCOTT.—Is a barrister of the same court of New Zealand, admitted in 1865; assistant law officer to government in 1871; and editor-general (non-political) in 1875.

REINHOLD, APOLPHUS.—Clerical asst treas., G. Coast, Jan. to June, 1885; ditto audit dept., y. 1887, to Dec., 1888; 3rd class clk. col. secy.'s de. 1889; 2nd class clk., Aug., 1891; also agt. t. interpreter, 1892.

RENNER, WILLIAM, M.D. (Brux.), M.R.C.S. (co.), L.K. and Q.C.P., L.M. (IRELAND).— Educated at the Liverpool College, and University College, London and Liverpool, Rotunda, Dublin, St. Jean Hospital, Brussels; a member of the Biological Society of London; late ophthalmic assistant to Prof. Streatfield, University College Hospital, London; acting surgeon, Sierra Leone, 1882 and 1883; assistant colonial surgeon, 1884; charge of lunatic asylum, and incurable and ill-pox hospital, Kissy; ag. col. surg., 1887.

events.position: mill. clk. to Hon. John Norquay, premier and col. treas., Manitoba
events.text_span: mill. clk. to Hon. John Norquay, premier and col. treas., Manitoba, 1885 to 1889

events.position: ag. sanitary st., and secy., sanitary bd.
events.text_span: ag. sanitary st., and secy., sanitary bd., Hong Kong, 1893.

raw_text: RENNIE, ALFRED HERBERT.—Ed. Hamilton um. Schl., and Upper Canada Coll., Toronto; mill. clk. to Hon. John Norquay, premier and col. treas., Manitoba, 1885 to 1889; delegate to England and New York to negotiate a loan Manitoba, 1887; correspondce. clk. pub. wks. st., Hong Kong, 1890; ag. asst. harbr. mr. I ag. supt., water police, 1892; ag. sanitary st., and secy., sanitary bd., Hong Kong, 1893.
RENNIE, E. A.—Auditor-general, New South Wales, 1883; inspector of accounts, Jan., 1856.

RENWICK, SIR ARTHUR, Kt., Bach (1894), A. (Sydney), M.D. (Edin.), F.R.C.S.E.—Minister public instruction, N.S.W., 26 Feb., 1886; secretary for mines, 12 Oct., 1881, to 4 Jan., 1883; creative commissioner for N.S.W. at Melbourne International Exhibition, 1880-81; vice-president, Amsterdam Exhibition, 1883; member of medical bd of N.S.W., 1873; president, state children's bd of department, 1881; fellow of the senate, Sydney university, 1872; president, Sydney Hospital, benevolent society of N.S.W., and of deaf, dumb, and blind asylums; commissioner for S.W. at Chicago exhibition, 1893.

events.text_span: 4th clerk customs department, Natal, May to July, 1881
clerk to b-auditor railway department, Jan., 1883.

raw_text: REVELL, THOMAS, F.—4th clerk customs department, Natal, May to July, 1881; clerk to b-auditor railway department, Jan., 1883.

REYNOLDS, GEORGE ALEXANDER.—Entered lit office, Cape, 1865; transferred to office of C. Colesberg, Jan., 1868; 2nd class certificate law and jurisprudence; asst. to C.C. and R.M., Namaqualand, Feb., 1869; acted as C.C. and R.M. 1869-70-71; as sub-collr., Port Nolloth, in 1871; ad as R.M. and sub collr., Hondeklip Bay, in 1871; investigated state of R.M.'s office, Hondeklip Bay, 1875, and took over duties Feb. to May transferred to audit office as inspector and acctt. of the general and revenue branches, Aug., 1875; ag. C.C. and R.M., Namaqualand, Nov., 1875; chief clerk, office of master of supreme court, Dec., 1889.

REYNOLDS, THE HON. W. H.—Member of provincial parliament, Otago, New Zealand, 1853 to 1876, having been eight years a member of the provincial executive, and four years speaker; member of colonial parliament from 1863 to 1878; member of legislative council since 1878; has held various offices in colonial ministry; delegate to Australasian conference in 1873; is now M.L.C.

events.position: managing director of chartered British South Africa Co., and of De Beer's Mining Co
events.text_span: managing director of chartered British South Africa Co., and of De Beer's Mining Co., 1889

resigned office in Cape Govt., Jan., 1896

raw_text: RHODES, THE RT. HON. CECIL JOHN.—M.L.A. for Barkly, Cape; member of executive council, 1889; managing director of chartered British South Africa Co., and of De Beer's Mining Co., 1889; premier and commissr. of cr. lds. and pub. wks., July, 1890; afterwards premier, and minister for native affairs; member of H.M.'s Privy Council, 1895; resigned office in Cape Govt., Jan., 1896; resigned directorship of B.S.A. Co., 1896.

events.text_span: 2nd clerk, colonial secretariat, Gold Coast, Feb., 1879

raw_text: RIBEIRO, JOSE FRANCISCO.—2nd clerk, colonial secretariat, Gold Coast, Feb., 1879; acted as deputy registrar, supreme court, Accra, 1880.

events.text_span: Money order clerk, P.O., Accra, July, 1883

raw_text: RIBEIRO, M. F.—Money order clerk, P.O., Accra, July, 1883; clerk and sorter, Dec., 1883; chief clerk, G.P.O., G. Coast, 1890.

RICCARD, HERBERT JACKSON HARMAR.—Inspector of police, Mauritius, mounted constabulary, 1869; assistant suptdt. of police, Penang, June, 1878; J.P., 1879; suptdt. police, 1882; ditto, Malacca, 1887; actg. ditto, Penang, 1889; suptdt. of police, Penang, Dec., 1890; ag. inspr.-gen. of police, April, 1891, to April, 1892.

RICE, CECIL N.—Ed. Harrison coll., Barbados; super. clk. admnr. off., St. Vincent, 1890; ag. 2nd clk., April to July, 1891; Dec., 1892, to Feb., 1893; 2nd clk., June, 1893; is also secy. to gen. bd. of health; ag. ch. clk., 1895.

RICHARDS, ED. H.—Ed. Charterhouse and Oscott; English solr., qualified 1878; admitted 1880; solr. and advocate, G. Coast, 1880; dist. comsr., Lagos, 1886; ag. judge, 1886 and 1888; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1889; ag. ch. justice, 1889, 1891, and 1893; suptdt. of census, 1891; chairman of comsn. on P.O., and of bd. of enquiry on acctg. system of P.W. dept., 1893; ag. ch. just., 1895; puisne judge, G. Coast, 1895.

RICHARDS, T. H. HATTON, F.R.G.S.—Associate to chief justice of Western Australia, 1881; clerk to chief judicial commissioner, Western Pacific, Sept., 1883; associate to chief justice, and clerk supreme court, Fiji, Nov., 1883; marshal, vice admiralty court, 1884; acting private secretary to Sir W. Robinson, Governor of South Australia, July, 1885; A.D.C., May, 1886; and also in Melbourne, March, 1889; private secy. to Sir W. MacGregor, admin. of New Guinea, Aug., 1889, and R.M. in the same year; treasurer and collr. of customs, and a member of the exec. and leg. councils, 1890, also regr.-gen., 1891; ag. govt. secy., Feb. to Sept. 1892; asst. col. sec., Gold Coast, 1893; ag. chief asst. col. sec., Mar. to July, 1894; and clk. exec. and leg. councils, Mar. to June, 1894.

RICHARDSON, EDMUND L.—Junior clerk, colonial secretary's office, Tobago, May, 1874; chief clerk, Oct., 1879; acting registrar of deeds, supreme court, July, 1880, to June, 1881; chief clerk, audit office, July to Dec., 1881; 2nd revenue officer and landing waiter, St. Lucia, Dec., 1881.

RICHARDSON, THE HON. EDWARD, C.M.G. (1879).—Entered the New Zealand parliament in 1871 as representative for the city of Christchurch; minister for public works, 1872; held that office through various changes of ministries till January, 1877, when he was compelled to retire on account of ill-health; again took office as minister of public works, Sept., 1884, to Oct., 1887.

raw_text: RICHEY, MATTHEW HENRY, Q.C., D.C.L.—Mayor of Halifax, Nova Scotia, 1864-67, and again 1875-78; sat in the commons for Halifax from 1878-83; lieut.-governor of Nova Scotia. 1883-8.

RICHMOND, JAMES, M.L.C.E.—Engineer irrigation works Jamaica, 1874-90; district engineer, 1876-90; asst. director pub. wks., 1890; acted as director, on several occasions; is a J.P., and also managing commsr., Kingston improvement works.
RICKETTS, OSWALD F.—Cadet, Sarawak service, Oct, 1881; asst. resdt., 4th divn., June, 1881; resdt., 2nd class, 4th divn., Jan., 1888.

3rd class clk., col. sec. off., Jan., 1893.

raw_text: RICKETTS, S. G.—Educated Wesleyan High School, Cape Coast; temp. out door offr. customs, Accra., Aug., 1890; 6th class offr. of customs, Feb., 1891; clerical asst. col. secy's off., Sep., 1891; 3rd class clk., col. sec. off., Jan., 1893.
RIDLEY, HENRY NICHOLAS.—M.A. (Exeter Coll., Oxford); director, botanical gardens, Singapore, 1888.

events.text_span: retired from the army with the rank of Colonel, 1889

raw_text: RIDGEWAY, THE RT. HON. SIR J. WEST, K.C.B., K.C.S.I., P.C. (Ireland).—Born 1844; retired from the army with the rank of Colonel, 1889; under-secretary foreign dept., India, 1881-5; commissioner for delimitation of Afghan frontier, 1885-7; under-secretary to Lord Lieutenant of Ireland, 1887; lt. gov. of the Isle of Man, 1893; governor of Ceylon, Dec., 1895.

events.position: Acting clerk and interpreter to resident magistrate Alexandra county

interpreter to resident magistrate, Durban, Nov., 1879.

raw_text: RIDGWAY, H. V.—Acting clerk and interpreter to resident magistrate Alexandra county, Natal, 1874 to 1876; interpreter to resident magistrate, Durban, Nov., 1879.

RIPON, 1ST MARQUESS OF (created 1871). GEORGE FREDERICK SAMUEL ROBINSON, K.G. (1869).—Privy councillor (1863), G.C.S.I., C.I.E., son of 1st Earl of Ripon; born 1827; succeeded his father as Earl of Ripon and Viscount Goderich, 1859, and his uncle as Earl de Grey and Baron Grantham the same year; created a marquess in 1871, in recognition of his services as chairman of the Joint High Commission which arranged the Treaty of Washington in 1871; is lord lieutenant and C.A., North Riding of York; deputy lord lieutenant of Yorkshire and Lincolnshire; under-secretary of state for war, June, 1859; removed to India Board, Feb., 1861; returned to War Office, July, 1861; and was secretary of state for war, April, 1863, to Feb., 1866, when he resumed his position at the India Board; finally resigned, June, 1866; lord president of the council, Dec., 1868, to Aug., 1873; governor-general of India, 1880-4; first lord of the Admiralty, Feb. to Aug., 1886; secretary of state for the colonies, Aug., 1892, to June, 1895; was M.P. for Hull, 1852-3, for Huddersfield, 1853-7, and for the West Riding of York from 1857-59; hon. D.C.L., Oxford, 1879.
RITCHIE, JOSEPH NORMAN, M.A.—Educated at King's Coll., Windsor, Nova Scotia, 1857; Q.C. 1872; was recorder of Halifax; is lieut.-col. militia; asst. judge, Nova Scotia, 1885.

ROBERTS, SIR ALFRED, KNT., 1883.—Hon. and consulting surgeon, Prince Alfred Hospital, Sydney, N. S. Wales; consulting surgeon, Royal Hospital for convalescents; president, Royal Society of S. W.; chairman, bd. of official visitors of asylums for the insane; trustee, Australian Museum and Technological museum.

events.position: 1st class classical mod., and a 2nd class in classical scholarship, and was awarded the "Gaisford" prize in
events.text_span: 1st class classical mod., and a 2nd class in classical scholarship, and was awarded the "Gaisford" prize in 1879

raw_text: ROBERTS, A. TEMPLE, M.A.—Ed. Coll., Oxford; 1st class classical mod., and a 2nd class in classical scholarship, and was awarded the "Gaisford" prize in 1879; assistant master in Winchester College, 1883; 1st class professor, Royal College, Mauritius, 1886.

events.text_span: Late R.A., commanding artillery brigade, New South Wales, 1876.

raw_text: ROBERTS, MAJOR-GENERAL C. P., C.M.G. (1885).—Late R.A., commanding artillery brigade, New South Wales, 1876.

ROBERTS, CHARLES JAMES, C.M.G. (1890).—Born 1846; educated at Sydney Grammar School; alderman of Sydney, 1877; J.P., 1878; mayor and chief magistrate 1879; commissioner at Sydney International Exhibition, 1879-80; commissioner for New South Wales at Melbourne Exhibition, 1880-81; at Amsterdam Exhibition, 1883; at Calcutta Exhibition, 1883-84; at Colonial and Indian Exhibition, 1886-7; M.L.C. 1885 and 1888; postmaster-general, 1887, in Parke's ministry; member Centennial Celebrating Commn. N.S.W., 1888.

ROBERTSON, ABRAHAM FAURE.—Acting clerk to C.C. and R.M., Swellendam, June, 1872; clerk, audit office, Feb., 1872; 2nd clerk to C.C. and R.M., George (acting as chief clerk), Dec., 1872; clerk to C.C. and R.M., Prince Albert (acting as chief clerk), Dec., 1872; ditto, Brodsworth, 1874; assistant accountant, treasury, Jan., 1875; ditto, colonial secretary's office, Jan., 1877; native affairs office, April, 1879; accountant, 1879; acted as C.C. and R.M. at George in 1873 and 1874; at Prince Albert in 1873 and 1874; at Brodsworth in 1874, 1875 and 1876; at Stellenbosch in 1876; at Robertson in 1878; was secretary to the board, April, 1877, to April, 1879; was member of the second departmental committee on system of books and accounts in 1879; at request of imperial government acted as C.C. and R.M. at Vryburg, British Bechuanaland, Oct., 1885, to March, 1886, when he resumed duties as accountant, native affairs office; C.C. and R.M., Colesberg, 1880; ditto, Robertson, 1890.

events.text_span: Superintending officer, public works department, Ceylon, July, 1878.

raw_text: ROBERTSON, J.—Superintending officer, public works department, Ceylon, July, 1878.

events.position: Postmaster, telegraph gaoler, and officer for the sale of ammunition, Exopo
events.text_span: Postmaster, telegraph gaoler, and officer for the sale of ammunition, Exopo, Natal, Nov., 1883

raw_text: ROBINSON, T. M.—Postmaster, telegraph gaoler, and officer for the sale of ammunition, Exopo, Natal, Nov., 1883; sub-district commissioner, Nov., 1885.

events.position: Resident surgeon-superintendent of New Providence asylum
events.text_span: Resident surgeon-superintendent of New Providence asylum, Bahamas, 1883.

raw_text: ROBINSON, WILLIAM.—Resident surgeon-superintendent of New Providence asylum, Bahamas, 1883.

ROBINSON, SIR WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1883), K.M.G. (1877), F.R.G.S.—Clerk in the colonial service, 1 Jan., 1854; assistant junior, 3rd class, 1854; 3rd class, June, 1858; was private secretary to Mr. Herman Merivale, from 1858, to May, 1860; to Lord Blackford, in May, 1860, to March, 1862; when he was promoted to be an assistant senior. Private secretary to the Right Hon. Edward Cardwell, from April June 17, 1864; appointed, Nov. 1869, to represent Colonial Office on the East African Slave Trade Commission; senior clerk, 1st July, 1870; and a principal clerk, 30th September, 1872; one of the official committee for the Vienna universal exhibition of 1873, and special commissioner for the own colonies, and superintendent of the colonial part at that exhibition; edited the report upon British colonies represented there; was one of the committee for securing a permanent colonial museum in London; lieutenant-governor the Bahama Islands, Oct. 1874, and governor, 1875; lieut.-governor, Windward Islands, 1880, vt., 1881; govr. of Trinidad, 1885; govr. of Hong Kong, 1891; received thanks of bd. of trade, 1877, and of H.M.'s Govt., Nov., 1878, in reference to administration of Bahamas; received thanks of H.M.'s Govt., Dec., 1887, for satisfactory settlement of Venezuelan difficulty arising out of the "Henrietta" and "Josephina"

ROBINSON, SIR WILLIAM C. F., G.C.M.G. (1877), K.C.M.G. (1877) C.M.G. (1873).—Was private secretary to Sir Hercules Robinson in the governments of St. Kitts and Hong Kong, from 1855 to 1860; president of Montserrat, 1862; administered the government of Dominica, Jan. Oct., 1865; governor and commander-in-chief the Falkland Islands, 23rd May, 1866; governor Prince Edward Island, 5th July, 1870, to Nov., 1873; governor-in-chief Leeward Islands, 1874; governor W. Australia, Oct., 1874; governor Straits Settlements, Aug., 1877; proceeded to Bangkok in 1878, on a special mission to invest the King of Siam with the G.C.M.G., on which occasion his majesty conferred upon him the Grand Cross of the Order of the Crown of Siam, which the Queen authorised him to accept and wear; governor W. Australia, Jan., 1880; governor S. Australia, Nov., 1882; ag. governor Victoria, 1889; gov. W. Australia, 1890; retired, 1895; died, 1897.

ROBINSON, WILLIAM VALENTINE.—Clerk of the legislative assembly of Victoria; was educated at T. C. Lockwood's school, Leeds, and at the Leeds Grammar School reporter for the "Leeds Intelligencer" (now "Yorkshire Post"); short-hand writer, and occasional reporter for, as well as correspondent for and contributor to various Yorkshire newspapers; provincial correspondent of the "Morning Herald," "Standard," and other London journals; left England for Melbourne, Australia, Oct., 1861, under engagement with the proprietors of The "Argus" to be one of their staff of parliamentary reporters; resigned his connexion with that newspaper at the beginning of 1866, to assist in the production of official reports of the debates and proceedings of both houses of the Victorian parliament; appointed by the governor in council, on the nomination of the speaker, clerk-assistant of the legislative assembly, May 1, 1889, and promoted to be the clerk May 11, 1891.
ROBITAILLE, THE HON. THEODORE.—Lieutenant-governor of province of Quebec, Canada, July, 1879, to 1884; senator, 1885.

ROCHA, J. G. DA.—Entered the post office, Hong Kong, Feb., 1865; accountant, July, 1872; acting assistant postmaster, Sept., 1881, to May, 1883. ROCHÉ, S. E.—Colonial revenue officer, Carriacou, Grenada, 1864; district registrar of births and deaths, 1866; police magistrate and coroner, 1876; acting ditto southern district, 1880-1; police magistrate, northern district, 1881.

events.position: Appointed, after a competitive examination, to be a clerk of the lower (now second) division of the civil service, and assigned to the Colonial Office
events.text_span: Appointed, after a competitive examination, to be a clerk of the lower (now second) division of the civil service, and assigned to the Colonial Office, 9th November, 1880

raw_text: ROCKETT, E. D.—Appointed, after a competitive examination, to be a clerk of the lower (now second) division of the civil service, and assigned to the Colonial Office, 9th November, 1880; supt. of printing dept., 1894.

raw_text: ROCKWOOD, W. G., M.D., M.R.C.S., M.R.C.P.—Medical assistant, civil medical department, in Ceylon, 1866; assistant colonial surgeon, 2nd class, 1867; 1st class, 1874; surgeon, general hospital, Colombo, 1878.

RODEN, ROBERT B.—Clerk, public library, Antigua, 1880; 2nd clerk to the governor, Leeward Islands, 1881; has acted on several occasions as chief clerk, and as clerk to the legislative and executive councils, and as a dist. mag., Antigua; also acted as priv. sec. to gov., Leeward Islands, in 1883 and 1890; mag. Nevis, 1890; member St. Kitts and Nevis leg. coun., 1891.

raw_text: RODGER, JOHN PICKERSGILL.—Educated at Eton and Christ Church, Oxon.; called to the bar, Inner Temple (certificate of honour), Jan., 1877; chief magistrate and commissioner of lands, Selangor, Sept., 1882; acting British resident, Mar., 1884; Br. resdt., Pahang, Sept., 1888; ag. resdt., Selangor, April to Dec., 1891; ag. secy. to govt., Perak, July, 1894; ag. resdt., Selangor, Oct., 1894; confirmed, July, 1896.

raw_text: ROE, J. B.—Junior clerk, survey department, Western Australia, 1851; senior clerk, July, 1851; registrar of brands, 1865; 1st class clerk and keeper of records, land office, 1876; sheriff, 1877, and inspector of prisons, 1886.

ROGER, CAPTAIN ARCHIBALD.—Ensign 2nd royal Lanark militia, Aug. 1859; gazetted to the 2nd West India regiment, Nov. 1860; lieutenant, Aug. 1861, captain, Oct. 1866; exchanged to the 1st battalion 17th regiment, Feb. 1867; sold out of the service, Jan. 1870; was private secretary to Mr. Bayley, governor of the Bahamas, Dec. 1860, to July, 1863, and fort adjutant, Jamaica, Dec. 1863, to Sept. 1864; acting police magistrate of the rural districts, St. Christopher, Dec. 1872; confirmed, June, 1873; district magistrate, May, 1874.

ROGERS, CHARLES HENRY, A.M.I.C.E. (1889).—Chief road officer, P. W. D., Trinidad, 1878; ag. 2nd engineer, 1878-79; ag. 1st engineer, 1881-82, 1884-85, also 1886 and 1888; special duty in connection with Port of Spain water wks. and lightlys., 1890-92; ag. engineer, S. Province, 1892.

events.position: called to the bar, Inn. Temp., Hil
events.text_span: called to the bar, Inn. Temp., Hil. 1885

raw_text: ROHRWEGER, FRANK.—Educated Radley and Brasenose; called to the bar, Inn. Temp., Hil. 1885; joint author of "Parochial Assessment Rules"; a dist. comsnr., Lagos, 1894; ag. Queen's adv., 1894; D.C., E. dist., 1895; pol. mag., 1896.
ROLLESTON, LOFTUS JOHN, C.M.G. (1880),—Late commandant, Diamond Fields Horse, Griqualand West, Cape.

ROLLESTON, THE HON. WILLIAM.—Was educated at Rossall; graduated at Emmanuel College, Cambridge; 2nd class in the classical tripos in 1855; was appointed a member of an education commission which framed the educational system adopted in the province of Canterbury, New Zealand, 1863; provincial secretary of that province and member of the board of education, 1864; under-secretary, native department, and inspector of native schools, 1865; elected superintendent of the province of Canterbury, 1868; was again elected superintendent at two consecutive elections, and held the office at the time of the abolition of the provinces, 1876; sat in the colonial parliament from the year 1868 to 1887; is a member of the senate of the university of New Zealand; minister for education, lands, and immigration, 1880; also temporarily minister for native affairs, 1881; minister for lands, mines, and immigration, 1882 and 1883, and till the accession of the Stout ministry in 1884; leader of the opposition, 1891-93.

RONAYNE, THOMAS.—Ed. Wakefield, Yorks; apprentice at Inchicore works, Dublin, of Gt. Southern and West. Ry. of Ireland; employed subsequently at Atlas loco. works, Manchester; emigrated to N.Z., 1875; employed in various capacities on N.Z. rlys. up to 1886, when apptd. resdt. and loco. engr. on Wellington section, where he effected improvements on the Rimutaka incline; subsequently transferred as loco. engr. to Addington, and later to Greymouth to put the Grey-Brunner line into working order. Is now General manager. N.Z. rlys

raw_text: ROOKS, CHARLES JOHN.—Capt. Trinidad Light Infantry Vol.; 2nd clk., col. sec. office; ag. chief clk., June to July, 1892; and ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. ex. and leg. councils, July to Sept., 1892; first appointment (col. sec.'s office), Oct., 1881; ag. warden St. Ann's and Diego Martin Ward Unions, April to July, 1893; has held the following appointments: hon. sec. to council for management of Queen's Roy. Coll.; sec. to bd. of special loan for public works and roads; sec. to bd., civil service widows and orphans pension fund, hon. sec. Trinidad branch, St. John's ambulance association, and to commsn. on rly. extension; colonial A.D.C. to gov., June, 1894; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to admnr., July to Nov., 1895.

School, Lond.; writer in the Ceylon Civil Service, Sept., 1872; police magistrate Panadure, 1875; assistant agent, 1877; district judge, Chilaw, 1883; Tangalla, May, 1887; police mag., Galle, Nov., 1887; ag. commnr. of requests, Colombo, July, 1890; ag. dist. judge, Tangalla, Oct., 1890; ditto, Batticaloa, 1893; dist. judge, Negombo, 1895.

raw_text: ROPER, H. R.—Clerk in treasury, and secretary to government's office, Grinqualand West, 1873; acting postmaster general, 1874; C.C. and R.M., Hay, 1876; ditto, Herbert, 1878; acting R.M., Kimberley, 1881; acting C.C. and registrar of deeds, 1882; chairman of municipal and mining areas commission, 1882; inspector of Sihune's Diggings, 1883; held inquiry into detective department at Kimberley, 1884; chief of detective department, and commissioner of police, Kimberley, 1885; commssnr. of police, 1888; insp.-gen. of gaols and convict stations, 1888.

events.text_span: on the introduction of the new audit system, under Act No. 30 of 1875

raw_text: ROSE, JOHN F. B.—Temporary clerk, customs, at Cape Town, June, 1845; transferred to fixed establishment, Feb., 1848; 2nd clerk (second class), Oct., 1853; 2nd clerk (first class), audit office, April, 1860; 1st clerk, Oct., 1867; chief clerk, Aug., 1875; and 1st Jan., 1876; on the introduction of the new audit system, under Act No. 30 of 1875; appointed assistant controller and auditor, 1875; acting comptroller and auditor-general, 1890.

ROSMEAD, 1ST BARON (creat. 1896), THE RIGHT HON. SIR HERCULES G. R. ROBINSON, BART. (1891), G.C.M.G. (1875), (KNIGHT BACHELOR (1859), (P.C., 1883).—Served for some time in the 87th Fusiliers; on his retirement from the army was actively engaged during the Irish famine, 1846-9, under the commission of public works and poor law board in Ireland; chief commissioner to inquire into the fair markets of Ireland, 1852; president of Munster, 1854; lieutenant-governor of St. Christopher, 1851; with which he held the dormant commission of governor-in-chief of the Leeward Islands; governor of Hong Kong, June, 1859. Was a member of the commission appointed in the autumn of 1863, to inquire into the financial condition of the Straits Settlements; governor of Ceylon, May, 1865; governor of New South Wales, 1872; employed on special service in 1874-5 in connection with the cession of the Fiji Islands to Great Britain; governor, New Zealand, 1877; governor of the Cape of Good Hope, Griqualand West, and H.M.'s high commissioner in South Africa, 1880; president of the Royal Commission on the settlement of the affairs of the Transvaal, 1881; governor of British Bechuanaland in conjunction with governorship of the Cape Colony, 1885; on special mission to Mauritius, Oct., 1886, when he assumed the government for a few days; resigned, 1889; reappointed 1895.

ROSS, A. C.—Clerk of the legislative and executive councils, and confidential clerk, colonial secretary's office, Trinidad, June, 1868; also secretary to Board of Education and to the Council for the Management of the Queen's Royal College, 1880; acting receiver general, Nov., 1885, to Feb., 1886; entered the service Feb., 1864; now as col. sec.

events.text_span: sav. bk. clk., rec.-gen.'s dept., Nov., 1880

raw_text: ROSS, A. E. CLARENCE.—2nd clerk, surg.-gen.'s off., Trinidad, 1875; clerk to dir. pub. wks., 1876; sav. bk. clk., rec.-gen.'s dept., Nov., 1880; acctt. sav. bk., Oct., 1882; sec. to trade taxes commission, 1886; acctt., rec.-gen.'s dept., May, 1889.

ROSS, DAVID ALEXANDER.—Admitted to the bar, Quebec, 1848; Q.C., 1873; is a member legislative assembly for county of Quebec; member of the executive council, attorney-general, and in 1887-8 member legislative council.

raw_text: ROSS, G. W., LL.D.—Held seat in house of com- mons, Ontario, 1872 to 1883; minister of education 1883; was inspector of public schools from 1871 to 1883; commissioner for Ontario and also for the dominion to the Ind. and Col. Exhibn., 1886.

ROSS, JOHN KENSETH MURRAY.—Clerical est. native dept., Fiji, Sept., 1879; ag. stip. mag., Aug. 1880; confirmed, 1886; ag. resdt. commsr., Gold West, Oct., 1882; magistrate, Tailevu and Ba, April, 1887; ditto, Lauraivitu, Aug., 1888; seconded in July, 1893, to Rarotonga and Penrhyn Island as special judicial commsr. under the C.P. high commsr; collr. of customs, chief spr. of distilleries, &c., Dec., 1896.

raw_text: ROSSELLOTTY, J. C.—Medical officer of the Essex district, Western Australia, June, 1864; resident magistrate and medical officer of Williams and Kajaimp districts, 1876.

events.position: Called to the bar, Middle Temple
events.text_span: Called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1858

events.position: graduate of the Faculty of Laws, Paris
events.text_span: graduate of the Faculty of Laws, Paris, 1860

raw_text: ROUILLARD, JOHN.—Called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1858; graduate of the Faculty of Laws, Paris, 1860; edited a complete edition of the laws of Mauritius; district magistrate, Mauritius, 1865; senior district magistrate, Port Louis, 1881; chairman, Forest Lands Purchase Commission, 1881; master, supreme court, 1884; puisne judge, 1886.
ROULEAU, CHARLES B.—Educated at Laval seminary school, Quebec; advocate, Quebec, 1868; st. mag., Ottawa, 1876; stip. mag., N.W. Territories, 1883; judge, N.W. Territories, 1887.

ROUND, FRANCIS R., M.A., C.M.G. (1887).—Educated at Marlborough and Balliol Coll., Oxford, where he obtained a double second class in moderations, and a second in classics in the final examination 1868; appointed after a competitive examination, clerk in the office of the secretary of state for the colonies, Feb., 1869; 2nd class clerk, 30th Sept., 1872; accompanied Sir P. Julyan to Mauritius in 1873 as secretary on special mission of inquiry into the civil establishment and general expenditure of that colony; assistant private secretary to the Rt. Hon. Sir Michael Hicks Beach, 7th March, 1878; and private secretary, 1st April to 27th April, 1880; private secretary to Mr. Herbert, 4th Jan., 1881; first class clerk, and financial clerk, 1st Dec., 1881; on special service to British Bechuanaland and Mauritius, 1886; acted as colonial secretary, Mauritius, 1886-7; principal clerk, Mar., 1896.

ROUTLEDGE, ROBERT M.—F.R. at Abdn. Univ.; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Nov., 1879; judge and magistrate, Falklands, June, 1891; judge, magistrate and col. secy., Dec., 1891; stipendiary magistrate, Trinidad, 1893; acted as puisne judge in 1894-5-6.

HOWELL, THOMAS IRVINE, C.M.G. (1890), M.D. C.M., Aberdeen.—Principal civil medical officer, Straits Settlements, July, 1877; and health officer, port of Singapore; J.P.; had acted as colonial surgeon on several occasions from May, 1868, to Mar., 1876; accompanied Sir W. Robinson on mission to invest king of Siam with G.C.M.G., Nov., 1878; registrar of births and deaths, Dec., 1882, and of Mohammedan marriages, Dec., 1883; resdt., fisheries inquiry commission, 1887; pres., Singapore municipal commn., 1888-9; retd., 1890.

events.text_span: Assistant colonial surgeon, Gold Coast Colony, 15 May, 1880

raw_text: ROWLAND, J. W.—Assistant colonial surgeon, Gold Coast Colony, 15 May, 1880; district commissioner, Lagos, 1887; colonial surgeon, 1887.

events.text_span: passed prelim. exam., R.M.C., Sandhurst, Apl., 1880
and qualifying exam. for line, July, 1881

events.position: asst. colr. ld. rev., magistrate and J.P., Perak
events.text_span: asst. colr. ld. rev., magistrate and J.P., Perak, Dec., 1890.

raw_text: ROWLEY, THOS. WM.—Ed. Cheltenham Coll.; 2nd Lieut., 2nd L. I. Regt. Roy. Guernsey Militia; attached to 105th Regt., L.I., at Guernsey, for two trainings, and passed school; lieut., Mar., 1879; passed prelim. exam., R.M.C., Sandhurst, Apl., 1880; and qualifying exam. for line, July, 1881; joined armed constab., N.Z., 1885; transferred to mtd. pol., Jan., 1886, and to N.Z. Art., June, 1886; resigned, Aug., 1888; inspr. of roads, Perak, Nov., 1889; asst. colr. ld. rev., magistrate and J.P., Perak, Dec., 1890.

ROXBURGH, T. LAWRENCE.—Ed. Edin. Univ.; clk. petty sessions, parish of St. Elizabeth, and asst. clk. to dist. ct., Jamaica, Jan., 1882; clk. of cts., St. Elizabeth ap., 1888, 1st class clk. col. sec. office, Feb., 1890.
ROYAL, THE HON. JOSEPH.—Lt.-gov., N.W. Territories, Canada, 1888-93.

events.position: accompanied Sir A. Clarke to Scángor, as clk., and subsequently clk. to consrns. to enquire in piracy case at Langat
events.text_span: accompanied Sir A. Clarke to Scángor, as clk., and subsequently clk. to consrns. to enquire in piracy case at Langat, 1874
ch. clk., police ct., Penang, Apl., 1896

events.position: compiler of general index to proceedings of leg. coun.
events.text_span: compiler of general index to proceedings of leg. coun., S.S., 1867-82.

raw_text: ROZELLS, BENJ. B. J.—Clk. col. sec. off., Singapore, 1871; accompanied Sir A. Clarke to Scángor, as clk., and subsequently clk. to consrns. to enquire in piracy case at Langat, 1874; ch. clk., resdt. off., Larut, 1875; clk. to gov., 1878; 4th clk., col. sec. off., 1881; 3rd clk., 1883; ch. clk., pol. off., Feb., 1886; ch. clk., police ct., Penang, Apl., 1896; compiler of general index to proceedings of leg. coun., S.S., 1867-82.

events.text_span: Clerk, and Dutch and Zulu interpreter, and postmaster, Weenen, Natal, 1855

events.text_span: Landdrost, Utrecht, and commissioner of Swazi and Zulu borders, Jan., 1874

raw_text: RUDOLPH, GERHARDUS M., C.M.G. (1882).—Clerk, and Dutch and Zulu interpreter, and postmaster, Weenen, Natal, 1855; J.P., 1870; Landdrost, Utrecht, and commissioner of Swazi and Zulu borders, Jan., 1874; rendered valuable services to British commanders in Zulu war; member, Transvaal-Swazi boundary commission, 1880, (mentioned in despatches); R.M., Klip River, Natal, June, 1881.

events.text_span: commissioner of Volta district, Gold Coast, May, 1880

events.position: acting assistant colonial secretary Lagos, and district commissioner
events.text_span: acting assistant colonial secretary Lagos, and district commissioner, Jan. to July, 1883

events.position: acting assistant colonial secretary, Gold Coast, Jan
events.text_span: acting assistant colonial secretary, Gold Coast, Jan., Feb., 1884
deputy governor, Lagos, Mar. to Aug., 1884

raw_text: RUMSEY, ROBERT MURRAY.—Entered R.N., Sept., 1862; lieut., April, 1873; served on North American, West Indian, East Indian, Pacific, and Home stations; commissioner of Volta district, Gold Coast, May, 1880; retired commander, R.N., Jan., 1882; acting assistant colonial secretary Lagos, and district commissioner, Jan. to July, 1883; acting assistant colonial secretary, Gold Coast, Jan., Feb., 1884; deputy governor, Lagos, Mar. to Aug., 1884; assistant harbour-master, Hong Kong, 1884; harbour-master, 1888.

events.position: clerk of legislative council and clerk of parliaments
events.text_span: clerk of legislative council and clerk of parliaments, 1856

raw_text: RUSDEN, GEORGE WILLIAM.—Under secretary of Victoria, 1851; clerk of legislative council and clerk of parliaments, 1856; retired, 1882; author of "A History of Australia," "A History of New Zealand," "Moyarra," and other works.

RUSSELL, MAJ.-GEN. SIR BAKER CREED, K.C.M.G. (1880), C.B., 13th Hussars.—Was in command of the troops employed against Siku-kuni, in South Africa; aide de-camp to Her Majesty, 1880; assistant Adjutant-General for cavalry in the expedition to Egypt, 1882, where he also commanded the 1st Cavalry brigade.

RUSSELL, H. C., B.A., F.R.S., C.M.G. (1890)—Government astronomer, New South Wales, July 12, 1870; author of memoirs on Australian eclipse expedition, 1875, and of numerous other papers on astronomical subjects, and has designed a variety of instruments for use in the observatory.

RUSSELL, JAMES JOHN.—Entered colonial office, Mar., 1867. Appointed a clerk of the lower (now second) division, 28th Nov., 1879; promoted to higher grade, 1st Apr., 1890; superintendent of copying branch, Feb., 1892.

events.text_span: headmaster government high school, Durban, Natal, May, 1866

raw_text: RUSSELL, ROBERT.—Educated at Edinburgh Univ. and Church of Scotland training college; headmaster government high school, Durban, Natal, May, 1866; inspector of schools, Jan., 1875; superintending inspector, Jan., 1878.
RUSSELL, THOMAS, C.M.G. (1877).—Formerly defence minister, New Zealand; also controller of customs and navigation laws.

events.text_span: professor of mathematics, Royal College, Mauritius, 1886.

raw_text: RUSSELL, WESTERN F.—Ch. Ch., Oxford. B.A. (1884); professor of mathematics, Royal College, Mauritius, 1886.

events.text_span: C.C and R.M., Piquetberg division, Cape, 1882.

raw_text: RUSSOUW, J. W. H.—C.C and R.M., Piquetberg division, Cape, 1882.

events.position: also controller of customs and navigation laws
events.text_span: also controller of customs and navigation laws, 1854

raw_text: RUTHERFORD, G., C.M.G. (1888).—Landing waiter, Grenada, 1841; landing surveyor, St. George's, 1845 to 1847; sub-collector of customs, Falmouth, Jamaica, 1850; collector of customs, Natal, 1853; also controller of customs and navigation laws, 1854; retired, 1889.
RUTHERFORD, H.—Excise Department, England, from Mar., 1864; inspector of distilleries, Natal, Feb., 1882; acting controller of excise, May, 1882, confirmed, Jan., 1885.
RUTLEDGE, THE HON. A.—Barrister, Queensland, 1878; member of legislative assembly since 1878; attorney-general from 1883 to 1888.

events.position: Appointed assistant examiner Imperial audit office
events.text_span: Appointed assistant examiner Imperial audit office, 4th March, 1851

events.position: junior clerk in H.M. treasury, Whitehall
events.text_span: junior clerk in H.M. treasury, Whitehall, 25th Feb., 1852

events.position: appointed to audit office as secretary
events.text_span: appointed to audit office as secretary, 6th May, 1865

events.position: was honorary auditor of certain crown colonies (see p. 13), acting on behalf of H.M. secretary of state for the Colonies, retired
events.text_span: was honorary auditor of certain crown colonies (see p. 13), acting on behalf of H.M. secretary of state for the Colonies, retired 1896.

raw_text: RYAN, SIR CHARLES LISTER, K.C.B. (1887), C.B. (1881), J.P. Berks.—Appointed assistant examiner Imperial audit office, 4th March, 1851; junior clerk in H.M. treasury, Whitehall, 25th Feb., 1852; was private secretary to Mr. Disraeli, Sir Stafford Northcote, and Mr. Gladstone; appointed to audit office as secretary, 6th May, 1865; assistant comptroller and auditor, 22nd March, 1873; comptroller and auditor-general, 12th June, 1888; was honorary auditor of certain crown colonies (see p. 13), acting on behalf of H.M. secretary of state for the Colonies, retired 1896.

SABBEN, FREDERICK.—Ed. Royal Naval Schl.; government agt., Fiji, July, 1882; inspr. of immigrants, Jan., 1883; boarding offr., Nov., 1883; bondkeeper, 2nd class clk. and magazine keeper, Jan., 1884; inspr. of pol. and offr. in charge of gaol, May, 1886; gaoler, aug., 1887. ST. AUBYN, GEOFFREY PETER.—Called to the bar, Middle Temple, April, 1880; western circuit, pol. mag., Kingstown, St. Vincent, 1891; member leg. coun., 1891; ag. chief justice, June, 1893; ag. treas., Nov., 1893; ag. atty.-gen., Dec., to May, 1894. ST. HELENA, 2nd Bishop of (founded 1859)— RT. REV. THOMAS EARLE WELBY, D.D.—Archdeacon of George Town, Cape, 1856; bishop of St. Helena, 1862; also col. chaplain, 1862-86. ST. QUINTIN, C.—Clerk to A.A.G., Griqualand West, 1879; border police, 1880; acting sub-inspector, Cape police, 1884; sub-inspector, Bechuanaland police, 1884; lieutenant, Bechuanaland border police, 1885; inspector of native reserves and J.P., 1887; storekeeper, 1889.
SALOMONS, SIR JULIAN EMMANUEL, Q.C., Kt. Bach. (1891).—Vice-president, executive council, N.S. Wales, 1891; resigned 1893; member of legis. council.

SAMUEL, LIONEL L.—Admitted solicitor, Supreme Court, Jamaica, June, 1878, acted as clerk of petty sessions, clerk of St. Ann's circuit court, 1879; assistant clerk Port Antonio (now eastern) district court, and clerk of Bath circuit court, Oct., 1879; now clk. of cts., St. Ann's; is a J.P.

SAMUEL, THE HON. SIR SAUL, K.C.M.G. (1882), C.M.G. (1874), C.B. (1886).—Late postmas.-gen. of New South Wales; vice-pres. of the exec. coun. and representative of the govt. in the legislative council of that colony; has also held office in four previous administrations as minister for finance and trade; was first elected in Oct., 1854, in the old legislative council, before the introduction of responsible government in the colony; he afterwards represented various constituencies in the legislative assembly, his last election being for the metropolitan electorate of East Sydney, and continued in that branch of the legislature until May, 1872, when he resigned to accept a seat in the legislative council to represent the government in that house; in 1873 appointed special commissioner to visit New Zealand, England, and America, to make arrangements for the establishment of a mail service between the United Kingdom and the Australian colonies by way of the United States; resigned in 1875, with the rest of the Parkes ministry; ag. general in London for New South Wales, 1880; royal commissioner Indian and Colonial Exhibition, 1886; and representative commissioner for N.S. Wales to colonial conference, 1887.

SAMUELSON, S. O.—Acting registrar high court, Natal, Feb., 1881, to April, 1882; acting Zulu interpreter, supreme court, May, 1882; clerk and Zulu interpreter to Umlazi division, Oct., 1882; Zulu interpreter supreme ct., Feb., 1888; under sec. for native affairs, 1893; member of civil service bd.

SANDERSON, FREDERICK JAMES.—Chart. treasury, South Australia, 26th June, 1864; secretary to Lands Titles Commissioners, 1st Jan., 1862; and secretary to attorney-general, 1st Jan., 1870; acting under secretary and government statist, Jan., 1878, to June, 1879; collector of customs and chief inspector of distilleries, 1st July, 1879; is a special magistrate.

SANDERSON, JAMES.—Clk. to ch. jt. Barbados, 1867; clk. to general bd. of Barbados, Apl., 1871, also clk. to quarantine bd., 1880; solr., attorney, and proctor, Barbados, 1873; was delegate for Barbados to quarantine conference at Demerara in 1888; govt. director bd. of Barbados Rly. Co. in 1891.

SANDFORD, THE RIGHT REV. CHARLES WALDEGRAVE, D.D.—A bishop exercising episcopal functions over the same region as the former bishop of Gibraltar; appointed 1874; educated at Rugby and Christ Church, Oxford, where he was tutor and senior censor; M.A., 1851; hon. canon of Canterbury, 1871; rector of Bishopstbourne, Kent, 1870 to 1873.

SANGSTER, C. F. A.—Clerk and accountant police department, Hong Kong, 1860; 2nd clerk registrar-general's office, 1864; clerk of the superior court, April, 1874; sheriff, 1876; acting deputy registrar, 1877; official trustee, 1880; deputy registrar, Admiralty Court, 1880; acting registrar official assignee in bankruptcy, and official administrator 1881 to 1882; deputy registrar, 1883; died 1890; official trustee and regt. of companies July, 1890, to Jan., 1891, April to Dec., 1891; April, 1892, to March, 1893; ag. regr. land office official trustee and regr. of companies, April to Dec., 1894.

SANGUINETTI, FREDERICK S.—First class clerk in colonial secretary's office, Jamaica, 1878; clerk in office of main road commissioner, Sept., 1863; clerk in office of executive council, Feb., 1864; second-class clerk in financial secretary's office, 1886, and now in colonial secretary's office; was secretary to a commission appointed "to inquire into the condition of the juvenile population of Jamaica," 1877; one of the compilers of the "Handbook of Jamaica, 1881"; acting commissioner, Turks and Caicos Islands, 1883; and again, 1885; ag. col. sec., treas., pol. mag., Falklands, July, 1890-1; admr. of govt. for a short time in 1891; ag. col. sec., Jamaica, 1894.

events.position: called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn
events.text_span: called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1848

raw_text: SARGENT, SIR CHARLES, KNT. BACH. (1874).—Educated at Trinity College, Cambridge, B.A. 5th wrangler; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1848; formerly chief justice of the Ionian Islands; puisne judge, Bombay, 1876; chief justice, 1883.
SARGOOD, LIEUT.-COL. THE HON. SIR FREDERICK THOS., M.L.C., K.C.M.G. (1890), K.M.G. (1885).—Minister of defence, Victoria, Nov. 13, 1883, to 1886; and again in 1891.

SARTORIUS, LIEUT.-COL. REGINALD WILLIAM, M.G., B.C. (1874).—6th regiment of Bengal Cavalry, appointed to the Order of St. Michael and St. George for service in the Ashantee war, 1873-4, under Sir J. H. Glover, and received the Victoria Cross for an act of conspicuous gallantry in saving wounded sergeant-major of Houssas.

events.text_span: Secretary for native affairs, Cape Colony, 9th May, 1881

raw_text: SAUER, THE HON. J. W.—Secretary for native affairs, Cape Colony, 9th May, 1881; retired 1884; dl. sec., July, 1890 to Jan. 1896.

events.text_span: passed final exam. in Chinese, Feb. 1895
ag. 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, Feb., 1895

raw_text: SAUNDERS, CHARLES J., B.A.—Ed. Merchant Taylors and St. John's Coll., Oxon; cadet S., 1891; passed final exam. in Chinese, Feb. 1895; ag. 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, Feb., 1895; z. 3rd mag., Singapore, May, 1895.

events.text_span: Clerk of recourt and Zulu interpreter to the R.M., Alexandra County, Natal, Nov. 1876

events.text_span: Zulu interpreter, supreme and circuit courts, Dec., 1883
